```text
1402	// Bulk delete users
1403	router.post('/bulk-delete', requirePermission('USERS_DELETE'), async (req, res) => {
1404	  try {
1405	    const { userIds, reason } = req.body;
1406	
1407	    if (!Array.isArray(userIds) || userIds.length === 0) {
1408	      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'No user IDs provided' });
1409	    }
1410	
1411	    let deleted = 0;
1412	    let failed = 0;
1413	    const errors = [];
1414	
1415	    for (const userId of userIds) {
1416	      try {
1417	        const validation = validateUUID(userId, 'user ID');
1418	        if (!validation.valid) {
1419	          failed++;
1420	          errors.push(`Invalid ID: ${userId}`);
1421	          continue;
1422	        }
1423	
1424	        const user = await User.findByPk(validation.value);
1425	        if (!user) {
1426	          failed++;
1427	          errors.push(`User not found: ${userId}`);
1428	          continue;
1429	        }
1430	
1431	        if (user.id === req.user.userId) {
1432	          failed++;
1433	          errors.push(`Cannot delete your own account`);
1434	          continue;
1435	        }
1436	
1437	        if (!canManageRole(req.user.role, user.role)) {
1438	          failed++;
1439	          errors.push(`Access denied for user: ${user.name}`);
1440	          continue;
1441	        }
1442	
1443	        if (user.role === 'administrator' && req.user.role !== 'global_admin') {
1444	          failed++;
1445	          errors.push(`Only Global Administrators can delete Administrators: ${user.name}`);
1446	          continue;
1447	        }
1448	
1449	        const deletedUserName = user.name;
1450	        const deletedUserEmail = user.email;
1451	        const deletedUserRole = user.role;
1452	
1453	        await user.destroy();
1454	
1455	        await logActivity({
1456	          type: ActivityType.USER_DELETED,
1457	          userId: req.user.userId,
1458	          userEmail: req.user.email,
1459	          userRole: req.user.role,
1460	          action: `Deleted user ${deletedUserName} (${deletedUserEmail})`,
1461	          details: {
1462	            deletedUserId: userId,
1463	            deletedUserName,
1464	            deletedUserEmail,
1465	            deletedUserRole,
1466	            reason: reason || '',
1467	            bulkOperation: true
1468	          },
1469	          companyId: null,
1470	          companyName: null,
1471	          ipAddress: req.ip || req.connection.remoteAddress,
1472	          userAgent: req.get('user-agent')
1473	        });
1474	
1475	        deleted++;
1476	      } catch (err) {
1477	        failed++;
1478	        errors.push(`Error deleting ${userId}: ${err.message}`);
1479	      }
1480	    }
1481	
1482	    res.json({ deleted, failed, errors: errors.length > 0 ? errors : undefined });
1483	  } catch (error) {
1484	    console.error('[Bulk Delete Users] Error:', error.message);
1485	    res.status(500).json({ message: error.message });
1486	  }
1487	});
1488	
1489	// Delete user
1490	router.delete('/:id', requirePermission('USERS_DELETE'), async (req, res) => {
1491	  try {
1492	    const userId = req.params.id;
1493	    
1494	    // Debug logging
1495	    console.log('[Delete User] Request params:', { id: userId });
1496	    console.log('[Delete User] Current user:', req.user ? { userId: req.user.userId, role: req.user.role } : 'no user');
1497	    
1498	    const validation = validateUUID(userId, 'user ID');
1499	    if (!validation.valid) {
1500	      console.error('[Delete User] Validation failed:', validation.error);
1501	      return res.status(400).json({ message: validation.error });
1502	    }
1503	    
1504	    const validatedUserId = validation.value;
1505	    
1506	    // Validate req.user.userId exists
1507	    if (!req.user || !req.user.userId) {
1508	      return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Authentication required' });
1509	    }
1510	    
1511	    const user = await User.findByPk(validatedUserId);
1512	    
1513	    if (!user) {
1514	      console.error('[Delete User] User not found:', validatedUserId);
1515	      return res.status(404).json({ message: 'User not found' });
1516	    }
1517	    
1518	    console.log('[Delete User] Target user:', { id: user.id, name: user.name, role: user.role });
1519	    
1520	    // Prevent deleting yourself
1521	    if (user.id === req.user.userId) {
1522	      console.log('[Delete User] Blocked: User trying to delete themselves');
1523	      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'You cannot delete your own account' });
1524	    }
1525	    
1526	    // Check if current user can manage this user (global admins can manage all roles)
1527	    const canManage = canManageRole(req.user.role, user.role);
1528	    console.log('[Delete User] Permission check:', { 
1529	      currentRole: req.user.role, 
1530	      targetRole: user.role, 
1531	      canManage 
1532	    });
1533	    
1534	    if (!canManage) {
1535	      console.error('[Delete User] Access denied: Cannot manage this user');
1536	      return res.status(403).json({ message: 'Access denied' });
1537	    }
1538	    
1539	    // Only Global Admins can delete Administrators
1540	    if (user.role === 'administrator' && req.user.role !== 'global_admin') {
1541	      console.error('[Delete User] Access denied: Only Global Administrators can delete Administrators');
1542	      return res.status(403).json({ message: 'Only Global Administrators can delete Administrators' });
1543	    }
1544	    
1545	    const deletedUserName = user.name;
1546	    const deletedUserEmail = user.email;
1547	    const deletedUserRole = user.role;
1548	    const deleteReason = req.body?.reason || '';
1549	    
1550	    await user.destroy();
1551	    console.log('[Delete User] Success: User deleted');
1552	    
1553	    await logActivity({
1554	      type: ActivityType.USER_DELETED,
1555	      userId: req.user.userId,
1556	      userEmail: req.user.email,
1557	      userRole: req.user.role,
1558	      action: `Deleted user ${deletedUserName} (${deletedUserEmail})`,
1559	      details: { 
1560	        deletedUserId: validatedUserId,
1561	        deletedUserName: deletedUserName,
1562	        deletedUserEmail: deletedUserEmail,
1563	        deletedUserRole: deletedUserRole,
1564	        reason: deleteReason
1565	      },
1566	      companyId: null,
1567	      companyName: null,
1568	      ipAddress: req.ip || req.connection.remoteAddress,
1569	      userAgent: req.get('user-agent')
1570	    });
1571	    
1572	    res.json({ message: 'User deleted successfully' });
1573	  } catch (error) {
1574	    console.error('[Delete User] Error:', error.message);
1575	    console.error('[Delete User] Stack:', error.stack);
1576	    res.status(500).json({ message: error.message });
1577	  }
1578	});
1579	
1580	// Remove 2FA from user (admin only)
1581	router.delete('/:id/two-factor', canManageUsers, async (req, res) => {
1582	  try {
1583	    const userId = req.params.id;
1584	    const validation = validateUUID(userId, 'user ID');
1585	    if (!validation.valid) {
1586	      return res.status(400).json({ message: validation.error });
1587	    }
1588	    
1589	    // Validate req.user.userId exists
1590	    if (!req.user || !req.user.userId) {
1591	      return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Authentication required' });
1592	    }
1593	    
1594	    const currentUser = await User.findByPk(req.user.userId);
1595	    if (!currentUser) {
1596	      return res.status(404).json({ message: 'Current user not found' });
1597	    }
1598	
1599	    // Only global_admin, administrator, and manager can remove 2FA
1600	    if (!['global_admin', 'administrator', 'manager'].includes(currentUser.role)) {
1601	      return res.status(403).json({ message: 'Access denied' });
1602	    }
1603	
1604	    const targetUser = await User.findByPk(userId);
1605	    if (!targetUser) {
1606	      return res.status(404).json({ message: 'User not found' });
1607	    }
1608	
1609	    // Check if current user can manage this user (global admins can manage all roles)
1610	    if (!canManageRole(currentUser.role, targetUser.role)) {
1611	      return res.status(403).json({ message: 'Access denied' });
1612	    }
1613	
1614	    // Remove 2FA
1615	    targetUser.twoFactorSecret = null;
1616	    targetUser.twoFactorEnabled = false;
1617	    targetUser.twoFactorVerified = false;
1618	    targetUser.twoFactorMethod = null;
1619	    targetUser.emailTwoFactorCode = null;
1620	    targetUser.emailTwoFactorExpires = null;
1621	    await targetUser.save();
1622	
1623	    // Log 2FA removal
1624	    await logActivity({
1625	      type: ActivityType.TWO_FACTOR_REMOVED,
1626	      userId: req.user.userId,
1627	      userEmail: req.user.email,
1628	      userRole: req.user.role,
1629	      action: `Removed 2FA for user ${targetUser.name} (${targetUser.email})`,
1630	      details: { 
1631	        targetUserId: targetUser.id,
1632	        targetUserName: targetUser.name,
1633	        targetUserEmail: targetUser.email
1634	      },
1635	      companyId: null,
1636	      companyName: null,
1637	      ipAddress: req.ip || req.connection.remoteAddress,
1638	      userAgent: req.get('user-agent')
1639	    });
1640	
1641	    res.json({ message: '2FA removed successfully' });
1642	  } catch (error) {
1643	    res.status(500).json({ message: error.message });
1644	  }
1645	});
1646	
1647	// Configure multer for file uploads (for import)
1648	const upload = multer({
1649	  storage: multer.memoryStorage(),
1650	  limits: {
1651	    fileSize: 10 * 1024 * 1024 // 10MB limit
1652	  },
1653	  fileFilter: (req, file, cb) => {
1654	    const allowedMimes = [
1655	      'text/csv',
1656	      'application/vnd.ms-excel',
1657	      'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet'
1658	    ];
1659	    if (allowedMimes.includes(file.mimetype) || 
1660	        file.originalname.endsWith('.csv') || 
1661	        file.originalname.endsWith('.xls') || 
1662	        file.originalname.endsWith('.xlsx')) {
1663	      cb(null, true);
1664	    } else {
1665	      cb(new Error('Invalid file type. Only CSV, XLS, and XLSX files are allowed.'));
1666	    }
1667	  }
1668	});
1669	
1670	/**
1671	 * Parse and validate import file (CSV, XLS, XLSX)
1672	 * Returns array of row objects
1673	 */
1674	async function parseUserImportFile(file) {
1675	  let rows = [];
1676	  const fileExtension = file.originalname.split('.').pop().toLowerCase();
1677	
1678	  if (fileExtension === 'csv') {
1679	    const csvText = file.buffer.toString('utf8');
1680	    const result = Papa.parse(csvText, {
1681	      header: true,
1682	      skipEmptyLines: true,
1683	      transformHeader: (header) => header.trim()
1684	    });
1685	    
1686	    if (result.errors.length > 0) {
1687	      throw new Error(`CSV parsing errors: ${result.errors.map(e => e.message).join(', ')}`);
1688	    }
1689	    
1690	    rows = result.data;
1691	  } else if (fileExtension === 'xls' || fileExtension === 'xlsx') {
1692	    const workbook = XLSX.read(file.buffer, { type: 'buffer' });
1693	    const sheetName = workbook.SheetNames[0];
1694	    const worksheet = workbook.Sheets[sheetName];
1695	    rows = XLSX.utils.sheet_to_json(worksheet, { 
1696	      defval: null,
1697	      raw: false
1698	    });
1699	  } else {
1700	    throw new Error('Unsupported file format. Use CSV, XLS, or XLSX.');
1701	  }
1702	
1703	  if (!rows || rows.length === 0) {
1704	    throw new Error('File is empty or contains no data');
1705	  }
1706	
1707	  return rows;
1708	}
1709	
1710	/**
1711	 * Parse boolean value from various formats
1712	 */
1713	function parseBooleanValue(value) {
1714	  if (value === undefined || value === null || value === '') return false;
1715	  const v = String(value).trim().toUpperCase();
1716	  return v === 'TRUE' || v === 'YES' || v === 'Y' || v === '1';
1717	}
1718	
1719	/**
1720	 * Process a single user row and return preview data
1721	 */
1722	// Helper function to check if user data has actually changed
1723	function hasUserDataChanged(existingUser, newData, newEmail, newCompanyIds = null) {
1724	  // Compare basic fields
1725	  if ((existingUser.name || '').trim() !== (newData.name || '').trim()) {
1726	    return true;
1727	  }
1728	  if (existingUser.role !== newData.role) {
1729	    return true;
1730	  }
1731	  if (existingUser.isActive !== newData.isActive) {
1732	    return true;
1733	  }
1734	  if (existingUser.allCompanies !== newData.allCompanies) {
1735	    return true;
1736	  }
1737	  
1738	  // Compare email (handle case sensitivity)
1739	  const existingEmail = (existingUser.email || '').toLowerCase();
1740	  const newEmailLower = (newEmail || '').toLowerCase();
1741	  if (existingEmail !== newEmailLower) {
1742	    return true;
1743	  }
1744	  
1745	  // Compare email notification preferences
1746	  if (existingUser.sendInvoiceEmail !== newData.sendInvoiceEmail) {
1747	    return true;
1748	  }
1749	  if (existingUser.sendInvoiceAttachment !== newData.sendInvoiceAttachment) {
1750	    return true;
1751	  }
1752	  if (existingUser.sendStatementEmail !== newData.sendStatementEmail) {
1753	    return true;
1754	  }
1755	  if (existingUser.sendStatementAttachment !== newData.sendStatementAttachment) {
1756	    return true;
1757	  }
1758	  if (existingUser.sendEmailAsSummary !== newData.sendEmailAsSummary) {
1759	    return true;
1760	  }
1761	  if (existingUser.sendImportSummaryReport !== newData.sendImportSummaryReport) {
1762	    return true;
1763	  }
1764	  
1765	  // Compare company assignments if provided
1766	  if (newCompanyIds !== null) {
1767	    // Get existing company IDs
1768	    const existingCompanyIds = existingUser.companies 
1769	      ? existingUser.companies.map(c => c.id).sort()
1770	      : [];
1771	    const newCompanyIdsSorted = [...newCompanyIds].sort();
1772	    
1773	    // Compare arrays
1774	    if (existingCompanyIds.length !== newCompanyIdsSorted.length) {
1775	      return true;
1776	    }
1777	    for (let i = 0; i < existingCompanyIds.length; i++) {
1778	      if (existingCompanyIds[i] !== newCompanyIdsSorted[i]) {
1779	        return true;
1780	      }
1781	    }
1782	  }
1783	  
1784	  return false;
1785	}
1786	
1787	async function processUserRowForPreview(row, rowNum, existingUsersMap, existingUsersByIdMap, existingCompaniesMap, manageableRoles) {
1788	  const result = {
1789	    rowNum,
1790	    status: 'valid',
1791	    errors: [],
1792	    warnings: [],
1793	    action: 'create',
1794	    data: {},
1795	    existingData: null,
1796	    emailChangeRequired: false,
1797	    companyAccountNumbers: []
1798	  };
1799	
1800	  try {
1801	    // Extract fields from row (support multiple column name formats)
1802	    const userId = row['id'] || row['ID'] || row['Id'] || row['user_id'] || null;
1803	    const name = row['name'] || row['Name'] || row['NAME'] || '';
1804	    const email = row['email'] || row['Email'] || row['EMAIL'] || '';
1805	    const role = (row['role'] || row['Role'] || row['ROLE'] || 'external_user').toLowerCase();
1806	    const active = parseBooleanValue(row['active'] || row['Active'] || row['ACTIVE'] || row['isActive'] || 'TRUE');
1807	    const allCompanies = parseBooleanValue(row['all_companies'] || row['allCompanies'] || row['All Companies'] || 'FALSE');
1808	    const companyAccountNumbersRaw = row['company_account_numbers'] || row['companyAccountNumbers'] || row['Company Account Numbers'] || row['companies'] || '';
1809	    
1810	    // Email notification preferences
1811	    const sendInvoiceEmail = parseBooleanValue(row['send_invoice_email'] || row['sendInvoiceEmail'] || 'FALSE');
1812	    const sendInvoiceAttachment = parseBooleanValue(row['send_invoice_attachment'] || row['sendInvoiceAttachment'] || 'FALSE');
1813	    const sendStatementEmail = parseBooleanValue(row['send_statement_email'] || row['sendStatementEmail'] || 'FALSE');
1814	    const sendStatementAttachment = parseBooleanValue(row['send_statement_attachment'] || row['sendStatementAttachment'] || 'FALSE');
1815	    const sendEmailAsSummary = parseBooleanValue(row['send_email_as_summary'] || row['sendEmailAsSummary'] || 'FALSE');
1816	    const sendImportSummaryReport = parseBooleanValue(row['send_import_summary_report'] || row['sendImportSummaryReport'] || 'FALSE');
1817	
1818	    // Validate required fields
1819	    if (!name || !name.trim()) {
1820	      result.status = 'error';
1821	      result.errors.push('Name is required');
1822	      return result;
1823	    }
1824	
1825	    if (!email || !email.trim()) {
1826	      result.status = 'error';
1827	      result.errors.push('Email is required');
1828	      return result;
1829	    }
1830	
1831	    // Validate email format
1832	    const emailRegex = /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/;
1833	    const trimmedEmail = email.trim().toLowerCase();
1834	    if (!emailRegex.test(trimmedEmail)) {
1835	      result.status = 'error';
1836	      result.errors.push(`Invalid email format: ${email}`);
1837	      return result;
1838	    }
1839	
1840	    // Validate role
1841	    const validRoles = ['global_admin', 'administrator', 'manager', 'credit_senior', 'credit_controller', 'external_user', 'notification_contact'];
1842	    if (!validRoles.includes(role)) {
1843	      result.status = 'error';
1844	      result.errors.push(`Invalid role: ${role}. Valid roles: ${validRoles.join(', ')}`);
1845	      return result;
1846	    }
1847	
1848	    // Check if current user can manage this role
1849	    if (!manageableRoles.includes(role)) {
1850	      result.status = 'error';
1851	      result.errors.push(`You cannot import users with role: ${getRoleLabel(role)}`);
1852	      return result;
1853	    }
1854	
1855	    // Validate email preferences
1856	    if (sendInvoiceAttachment && !sendInvoiceEmail) {
1857	      result.warnings.push('Invoice attachment enabled without invoice email - will be ignored');
1858	    }
1859	    if (sendStatementAttachment && !sendStatementEmail) {
1860	      result.warnings.push('Statement attachment enabled without statement email - will be ignored');
1861	    }
1862	    if (sendEmailAsSummary && !sendInvoiceEmail && !sendStatementEmail) {
1863	      result.warnings.push('Email summary enabled without any email types - will be ignored');
1864	    }
1865	
1866	    // Parse company account numbers
1867	    const companyAccountNumbers = [];
1868	    if (companyAccountNumbersRaw && !allCompanies) {
1869	      const parts = String(companyAccountNumbersRaw).split(',').map(s => s.trim()).filter(s => s);
1870	      for (const part of parts) {
1871	        const refNo = parseInt(part);
1872	        if (!isNaN(refNo)) {
1873	          companyAccountNumbers.push(refNo);
1874	          // Check if company exists
1875	          if (!existingCompaniesMap.has(refNo)) {
1876	            result.warnings.push(`Company with account number ${refNo} not found`);
1877	          }
1878	        } else {
1879	          result.warnings.push(`Invalid company account number: ${part}`);
1880	        }
1881	      }
1882	    }
1883	
1884	    // Determine if this is a create or update
1885	    let existingUser = null;
1886	    let matchMethod = null;
1887	
1888	    // First try to match by ID if provided
1889	    if (userId) {
1890	      const validation = validateUUID(userId, 'user ID');
1891	      if (validation.valid) {
1892	        existingUser = existingUsersByIdMap.get(validation.value);
1893	        if (existingUser) {
1894	          matchMethod = 'ID';
1895	        }
1896	      } else {
1897	        result.warnings.push(`Invalid user ID format "${userId}". Attempting to match by email.`);
1898	      }
1899	    }
1900	
1901	    // Fall back to email matching
1902	    if (!existingUser) {
1903	      existingUser = existingUsersMap.get(trimmedEmail);
1904	      if (existingUser) {
1905	        matchMethod = 'Email';
1906	      }
1907	    }
1908	
1909	    // Build user data
1910	    const userData = {
1911	      name: name.trim(),
1912	      email: trimmedEmail,
1913	      role: role,
1914	      isActive: active,
1915	      allCompanies: allCompanies,
1916	      sendInvoiceEmail: sendInvoiceEmail,
1917	      sendInvoiceAttachment: sendInvoiceEmail ? sendInvoiceAttachment : false,
1918	      sendStatementEmail: sendStatementEmail,
1919	      sendStatementAttachment: sendStatementEmail ? sendStatementAttachment : false,
1920	      sendEmailAsSummary: (sendInvoiceEmail || sendStatementEmail) ? sendEmailAsSummary : false,
1921	      sendImportSummaryReport: sendImportSummaryReport
1922	    };
1923	
1924	    result.data = userData;
1925	    result.companyAccountNumbers = companyAccountNumbers;
1926	
1927	    if (existingUser) {
1928	      // Get company IDs for comparison
1929	      const companyIds = [];
1930	      if (!allCompanies && companyAccountNumbers.length > 0) {
1931	        for (const refNo of companyAccountNumbers) {
1932	          const company = existingCompaniesMap.get(refNo);
1933	          if (company) {
1934	            companyIds.push(company.id);
1935	          }
1936	        }
1937	      }
1938	      
1939	      // Check if data has actually changed
1940	      const hasChanged = hasUserDataChanged(existingUser, userData, trimmedEmail, companyIds.length > 0 ? companyIds : null);
1941	      
1942	      if (hasChanged) {
1943	        result.action = `update (matched by ${matchMethod})`;
1944	      } else {
1945	        result.action = 'no_change';
1946	      }
1947	      
1948	      result.existingData = {
1949	        id: existingUser.id,
1950	        name: existingUser.name,
1951	        email: existingUser.email,
1952	        role: existingUser.role,
1953	        isActive: existingUser.isActive,
1954	        allCompanies: existingUser.allCompanies
1955	      };
1956	
1957	      // Check if email is being changed
1958	      if (existingUser.email.toLowerCase() !== trimmedEmail) {
1959	        result.emailChangeRequired = true;
1960	        result.warnings.push(`Email will change from ${existingUser.email} to ${trimmedEmail}. User will receive validation email.`);
1961	      }
1962	
1963	      // Check if role is being changed
1964	      if (existingUser.role !== role) {
1965	        result.warnings.push(`Role will change from ${getRoleLabel(existingUser.role)} to ${getRoleLabel(role)}`);
1966	      }
1967	    } else {
1968	      result.action = 'create';
1969	      // Check if email already exists (duplicate in file)
1970	      const emailCheck = existingUsersMap.get(trimmedEmail);
1971	      if (emailCheck) {
1972	        result.status = 'error';
1973	        result.errors.push(`Email ${trimmedEmail} already exists in the system`);
1974	        return result;
1975	      }
1976	    }
1977	
1978	    if (result.warnings.length > 0 && result.status !== 'error') {
1979	      result.status = 'warning';
1980	    }
1981	
1982	  } catch (error) {
1983	    result.status = 'error';
1984	    result.errors.push(error.message);
1985	  }
1986	
1987	  return result;
1988	}
```

 `./backend/utils/sessionToken.js`

```text
1	const crypto = require('crypto');
2	const jwt = require('jsonwebtoken');
3	const { redis } = require('../config/redis');
4	
5	// Use Redis for session tokens (supports distributed systems)
6	// Falls back to in-memory Map if Redis is unavailable
7	const useRedis = process.env.REDIS_HOST || process.env.REDIS_URL;
8	const sessionTokens = new Map(); // Fallback for when Redis is unavailable
9	
10	/**
11	 * Generate a temporary session token for 2FA setup
12	 * This allows us to avoid passing passwords around
13	 */
14	const generateSessionToken = async (userId, email) => {
15	  const token = crypto.randomBytes(32).toString('hex');
16	  const expiresAt = Date.now() + 10 * 60 * 1000; // 10 minutes
17	  const sessionData = {
18	    userId,
19	    email,
20	    expiresAt,
21	    createdAt: Date.now()
22	  };
23	  
24	  if (useRedis) {
25	    try {
26	      // Store in Redis with expiration
27	      const ttl = Math.ceil((expiresAt - Date.now()) / 1000); // Convert to seconds
28	      await redis.setex(`session:${token}`, ttl, JSON.stringify(sessionData));
29	      return token;
30	    } catch (error) {
31	      console.error('Redis error in generateSessionToken, falling back to memory:', error.message);
32	      // Fall through to in-memory storage
33	    }
34	  }
35	  
36	  // Fallback to in-memory storage
37	  sessionTokens.set(token, sessionData);
38	  cleanupExpiredTokens();
39	  
40	  return token;
41	};
42	
43	/**
44	 * Verify a session token (without consuming it)
45	 * Returns user data if valid, null if invalid/expired
46	 */
47	const verifySessionToken = async (token, consume = false) => {
48	  let session = null;
49	  
50	  if (useRedis) {
51	    try {
52	      const sessionData = await redis.get(`session:${token}`);
53	      if (sessionData) {
54	        session = JSON.parse(sessionData);
55	        
56	        // Check expiration
57	        if (Date.now() > session.expiresAt) {
58	          await redis.del(`session:${token}`);
59	          return null;
60	        }
61	        
62	        // Consume token if requested
63	        if (consume) {
64	          await redis.del(`session:${token}`);
65	        }
66	        
67	        return {
68	          userId: session.userId,
69	          email: session.email
70	        };
71	      }
72	      return null;
73	    } catch (error) {
74	      console.error('Redis error in verifySessionToken, falling back to memory:', error.message);
75	      // Fall through to in-memory check
76	    }
77	  }
78	  
79	  // Fallback to in-memory storage
80	  session = sessionTokens.get(token);
81	  
82	  if (!session) {
83	    return null;
84	  }
85	  
86	  if (Date.now() > session.expiresAt) {
87	    sessionTokens.delete(token);
88	    return null;
89	  }
90	  
91	  // Only consume token if requested (for one-time use scenarios)
92	  if (consume) {
93	    sessionTokens.delete(token);
94	  }
95	  
96	  return {
97	    userId: session.userId,
98	    email: session.email
99	  };
100	};
101	
102	/**
103	 * Clean up expired tokens
104	 */
105	const cleanupExpiredTokens = () => {
106	  const now = Date.now();
107	  for (const [token, session] of sessionTokens.entries()) {
108	    if (now > session.expiresAt) {
109	      sessionTokens.delete(token);
110	    }
111	  }
112	};
113	
114	// Clean up expired tokens every 5 minutes
115	setInterval(cleanupExpiredTokens, 5 * 60 * 1000);
116	
117	module.exports = {
118	  generateSessionToken,
119	  verifySessionToken
120	};
121	
122
```

```text
1	const { Sequelize } = require('sequelize');
2	// Load .env file - check both root and backend directories
3	const fs = require('fs');
4	const path = require('path');
5	const rootEnv = path.join(__dirname, '..', '..', '.env');
6	const backendEnv = path.join(__dirname, '..', '.env');
7	if (fs.existsSync(rootEnv)) {
8	  require('dotenv').config({ path: rootEnv });
9	} else if (fs.existsSync(backendEnv)) {
10	  require('dotenv').config({ path: backendEnv });
11	} else {
12	  require('dotenv').config(); // Fallback to default behavior
13	}
14	
15	// PostgreSQL connection
16	const sequelize = new Sequelize(
17	  process.env.DB_NAME || 'invoice_portal',
18	  process.env.DB_USER || 'postgres',
19	  process.env.DB_PASSWORD || 'postgres',
20	  {
21	    host: process.env.DB_HOST || 'localhost',
22	    port: process.env.DB_PORT || 5432,
23	    dialect: 'postgres',
24	    logging: process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development' ? console.log : false,
25	    pool: {
26	      max: parseInt(process.env.DB_POOL_MAX) || 20, // Configurable, default 20 for production
27	      min: parseInt(process.env.DB_POOL_MIN) || 2, // Keep minimum connections alive
28	      acquire: parseInt(process.env.DB_POOL_ACQUIRE) || 60000, // Increased timeout to 60 seconds
29	      idle: parseInt(process.env.DB_POOL_IDLE) || 10000,
30	      evict: 1000 // Check for idle connections every second
31	    },
32	    // Better error handling for connection issues
33	    dialectOptions: {
34	      connectTimeout: 10000, // 10 second connection timeout
35	      requestTimeout: 30000, // 30 second request timeout
36	      ...(process.env.DB_SSL === 'true' && {
37	        ssl: {
38	          require: true,
39	          rejectUnauthorized: false
40	        }
41	      })
42	    },
43	  }
44	);
45	
46	// Test connection with retry logic
47	const testConnection = async (retries = 3) => {
48	  for (let i = 0; i < retries; i++) {
49	    try {
50	      await sequelize.authenticate();
51	      console.log('✅ PostgreSQL Connected Successfully');
52	      return true;
53	    } catch (error) {
54	      console.error(`❌ PostgreSQL Connection Error (attempt ${i + 1}/${retries}):`, error.message);
55	      if (i < retries - 1) {
56	        console.log(`   Retrying in 2 seconds...`);
57	        await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 2000));
58	      } else {
59	        console.error('   Host:', process.env.DB_HOST || 'localhost');
60	        console.error('   Port:', process.env.DB_PORT || 5432);
61	        console.error('   Database:', process.env.DB_NAME || 'invoice_portal');
62	        console.error('   User:', process.env.DB_USER || 'postgres');
63	        console.error('   Password:', process.env.DB_PASSWORD ? '***SET***' : 'NOT SET');
64	        console.error('   Error Message:', error.message);
65	        if (error.original) {
66	          console.error('   Original Error:', error.original.message);
67	          console.error('   Error Code:', error.original.code);
68	        }
69	        // Common error codes and solutions
70	        if (error.original && error.original.code) {
71	          const code = error.original.code;
72	          if (code === 'ECONNREFUSED') {
73	            console.error('\n   💡 Solution: PostgreSQL service is not running.');
74	            console.error('      Windows: Open Services (services.msc) and start PostgreSQL service');
75	            console.error('      Or run: Get-Service | Where-Object {$_.DisplayName -like "*PostgreSQL*"} | Start-Service');
76	          } else if (code === '28P01') {
77	            console.error('\n   💡 Solution: Invalid password. Check your .env file DB_PASSWORD');
78	          } else if (code === '3D000') {
79	            console.error('\n   💡 Solution: Database does not exist. Create it with: CREATE DATABASE invoice_portal;');
80	          }
81	        }
82	      }
83	    }
84	  }
85	  return false;
86	};
87	
88	// Sync database (creates tables if they don't exist)
89	// Use with caution in production - prefer migrations
90	const syncDatabase = async (force = false) => {
91	  try {
92	    // Import models to ensure they're registered
93	    require('../models');
94	    
95	    // Sync without alter first (creates tables if they don't exist)
96	    await sequelize.sync({ force: false, alter: false });
97	    console.log('✅ Database synchronized');
98	    return true;
99	  } catch (error) {
100	    console.error('❌ Database sync error:', error.message);
101	    if (error.original) {
102	      console.error('   Original error:', error.original.message);
103	    }
104	    return false;
105	  }
106	};
107	
108	module.exports = {
109	  sequelize,
110	  Sequelize,
111	  testConnection,
112	  syncDatabase
113	};
114	
115
```

```text
1	/**
2	 * Local PDF text extraction using pdf-parse
3	 * No cloud costs - extracts embedded text directly from PDFs
4	 */
5	
6	const pdfParse = require('pdf-parse');
7	const fs = require('fs');
8	
9	/**
10	 * Extract text from PDF file
11	 * @param {string} filePath - Path to PDF file
12	 * @returns {Promise<Object>} - Extracted text and metadata
13	 */
14	async function extractTextFromPDF(filePath) {
15	  try {
16	    const dataBuffer = fs.readFileSync(filePath);
17	    const data = await pdfParse(dataBuffer);
18	    
19	    return {
20	      text: data.text,
21	      numPages: data.numpages,
22	      info: data.info,
23	      metadata: data.metadata,
24	      version: data.version
25	    };
26	  } catch (error) {
27	    throw new Error(`Failed to extract text from PDF: ${error.message}`);
28	  }
29	}
30	
31	/**
32	 * Extract text and basic invoice fields using simple patterns
33	 * @param {string} filePath - Path to PDF file
34	 * @returns {Promise<Object>} - Parsed invoice data
35	 */
36	async function extractInvoiceData(filePath) {
37	  const extracted = await extractTextFromPDF(filePath);
38	  const text = extracted.text;
39	  
40	  // Basic field extraction using common patterns
41	  // These will be replaced by supplier-specific templates
42	  const invoiceNumber = extractInvoiceNumber(text);
43	  const date = extractDate(text);
44	  const amount = extractAmount(text);
45	  const accountNumber = extractAccountNumber(text);
46	  const customerName = extractCustomerName(text);
47	  const customerPO = extractCustomerPO(text);
48	  const goodsAmount = extractGoodsAmount(text);
49	  const vatAmount = extractVatAmount(text);
50	  
51	  return {
52	    fullText: text,
53	    invoiceNumber,
54	    date,
55	    amount,
56	    accountNumber,
57	    customerName,
58	    customerPO,
59	    goodsAmount,
60	    vatAmount,
61	    numPages: extracted.numPages,
62	    metadata: extracted.metadata,
63	    rawText: text
64	  };
65	}
66	
67	/**
68	 * Extract invoice number using common patterns
69	 * Improved patterns to handle various formats including "Invoice No.5942501" and "Invoice No. 5942480"
70	 */
71	function extractInvoiceNumber(text) {
72	  const patterns = [
73	    // "Invoice No. 5942480" or "Invoice No.5942501" or "Invoice No: 5942501" or "Invoice No 5942501"
74	    // This pattern specifically handles the space after "No." and captures the number
75	    /invoice\s+no\.?\s+:?\s*(\d{4,}[A-Z0-9\-_]*)/i,
76	    // "Invoice #5942501" or "Invoice #: 5942501"
77	    /invoice\s*#\s*:?\s*([A-Z0-9\-_]+)/i,
78	    // "Invoice Number: 5942501" or "Invoice Number 5942501"
79	    /invoice\s+number\s*:?\s*([A-Z0-9\-_]+)/i,
80	    // "INV-5942501" or "INV 5942501"
81	    /(?:^|\s)(INV[-\s]?[A-Z0-9\-_]+)/i,
82	    // "OC1_INV_05942483" format
83	    /(?:^|\s)([A-Z]{2,}[-\s_]?INV[-\s_]?\d+[A-Z0-9\-_]*)/i,
84	    // Generic: "Invoice 5942501"
85	    /invoice\s+([A-Z0-9\-_]+)/i,
86	    // Fallback: long numeric sequences that might be invoice numbers (7+ digits)
87	    /(?:^|\s)(\d{7,}[A-Z0-9\-_]*)/i
88	  ];
89	  
90	  for (const pattern of patterns) {
91	    const match = text.match(pattern);
92	    if (match && match[1]) {
93	      const invoiceNum = match[1].trim();
94	      // Validate it looks like an invoice number
95	      // Must be at least 4 characters (to avoid matching "No" or short numbers)
96	      if (invoiceNum.length >= 4 && invoiceNum.length <= 50) {
97	        // Don't match if it's just "No" or common words
98	        if (!/^(no|yes|na|n\/a)$/i.test(invoiceNum)) {
99	          return invoiceNum;
100	        }
101	      }
102	    }
103	  }
104	  
105	  return null;
106	}
107	
108	/**
109	 * Extract date using common patterns
110	 */
111	function extractDate(text) {
112	  // Common date patterns
113	  const patterns = [
114	    /(\d{1,2}[\/\-]\d{1,2}[\/\-]\d{2,4})/, // DD/MM/YYYY or DD-MM-YYYY
115	    /(\d{4}[\/\-]\d{1,2}[\/\-]\d{1,2})/, // YYYY/MM/DD or YYYY-MM-DD
116	    /(\d{1,2}\s+(?:Jan|Feb|Mar|Apr|May|Jun|Jul|Aug|Sep|Oct|Nov|Dec)[a-z]*\s+\d{2,4})/i, // DD MMM YYYY
117	    /((?:Jan|Feb|Mar|Apr|May|Jun|Jul|Aug|Sep|Oct|Nov|Dec)[a-z]*\s+\d{1,2},?\s+\d{2,4})/i // MMM DD, YYYY
118	  ];
119	  
120	  for (const pattern of patterns) {
121	    const match = text.match(pattern);
122	    if (match && match[1]) {
123	      return match[1].trim();
124	    }
125	  }
126	  
127	  return null;
128	}
129	
130	/**
131	 * Extract amount/total using common patterns
132	 * Handles formats like "Invoice Total£0.00" or "Total: £0.00"
133	 */
134	function extractAmount(text) {
135	  // Look for currency symbols and amounts
136	  const patterns = [
137	    // "Invoice Total£0.00" or "Invoice Total: £0.00" - most specific
138	    /invoice\s+total\s*:?\s*[£$€]?\s*([\d,]+\.?\d{2})/i,
139	    // "Total: £0.00" or "Total £0.00"
140	    /(?:^|\s)total\s*:?\s*[£$€]?\s*([\d,]+\.?\d{2})/i,
141	    // "Amount Due: £0.00"
142	    /(?:amount\s+due|balance\s+due|grand\s+total)\s*:?\s*[£$€]?\s*([\d,]+\.?\d{2})/i,
143	    // Currency symbol followed by amount (get the last one, usually the total)
144	    /[£$€]\s*([\d,]+\.?\d{2})/g
145	  ];
146	  
147	  // Try specific patterns first
148	  for (let i = 0; i < patterns.length - 1; i++) {
149	    const pattern = patterns[i];
150	    const match = text.match(pattern);
151	    if (match && match[1]) {
152	      const amountStr = match[1].trim().replace(/,/g, '');
153	      const amount = parseFloat(amountStr);
154	      if (!isNaN(amount)) {
155	        return amount;
156	      }
157	    }
158	  }
159	  
160	  // For currency pattern, get all matches and return the last one (usually the total)
161	  const currencyPattern = patterns[patterns.length - 1];
162	  const matches = [...text.matchAll(currencyPattern)];
163	  if (matches && matches.length > 0) {
164	    // Return the last match (usually the total/invoice total)
165	    const lastMatch = matches[matches.length - 1];
166	    if (lastMatch && lastMatch[1]) {
167	      const amountStr = lastMatch[1].trim().replace(/,/g, '');
168	      const amount = parseFloat(amountStr);
169	      if (!isNaN(amount)) {
170	        return amount;
171	      }
172	    }
173	  }
174	  
175	  return null;
176	}
177	
178	/**
179	 * Extract account number from text
180	 * Looks for common patterns like "Account Number:", "Account:", "Acc No:", etc.
181	 * IMPORTANT: Excludes bank account numbers (usually longer, near "Bank Details")
182	 */
183	function extractAccountNumber(text) {
184	  // First, try to find customer account number (usually shorter, near top)
185	  // Exclude bank account numbers which are usually longer and near "Bank Details"
186	  const bankAccountSection = text.toLowerCase().indexOf('bank details');
187	  const textBeforeBank = bankAccountSection > 0 ? text.substring(0, bankAccountSection) : text;
188	  
189	  const patterns = [
190	    // "Account No.: 999999" or "Account No.999401" or "Account No: 999401" or "Account No 999401"
191	    // This pattern handles the colon after "No." and captures the number
192	    /account\s+no\.?\s*:?\s*(\d{4,}[A-Z0-9\-]*)/i,
193	    // "Account #999401" or "Account #: 999401"
194	    /account\s*#\s*:?\s*([A-Z0-9\-]+)/i,
195	    // "Account Number: 999401" or "Account Number 999401"
196	    /account\s+number\s*:?\s*([A-Z0-9\-]+)/i,
197	    // "Acc No.999401" or "Acc No: 999401"
198	    /acc\s+no\.?\s*:?\s*(\d{4,}[A-Z0-9\-]*)/i,
199	    // "Account Code: 999401"
200	    /account\s+code\s*:?\s*([A-Z0-9\-]+)/i,
201	    // "Customer Account: 999401"
202	    /customer\s+account\s*:?\s*([A-Z0-9\-]+)/i,
203	    // "Account ID: 999401"
204	    /account\s+id\s*:?\s*([A-Z0-9\-]+)/i
205	  ];
206	  
207	  // Search in text before bank details section first (customer account is usually at top)
208	  for (const pattern of patterns) {
209	    const match = textBeforeBank.match(pattern);
210	    if (match && match[1]) {
211	      const accountNumber = match[1].trim();
212	      // Validate it looks like an account number
213	      // Must be at least 4 characters (to avoid matching "No")
214	      // Usually customer accounts are shorter than bank accounts
215	      if (accountNumber.length >= 4 && accountNumber.length <= 20) {
216	        // Don't match if it's just "No" or common words
217	        if (!/^(no|yes|na|n\/a)$/i.test(accountNumber)) {
218	          return accountNumber;
219	        }
220	      }
221	    }
222	  }
223	  
224	  // Try to find account number in common positions (near top of document, before bank details)
225	  const lines = textBeforeBank.split('\n').slice(0, 20); // Check first 20 lines
226	  for (const line of lines) {
227	    // Look for lines that might contain account numbers
228	    // Pattern: alphanumeric, usually starts with letters then numbers
229	    const accountMatch = line.match(/\b([A-Z0-9]{4,20})\b/);
230	    if (accountMatch && accountMatch[1]) {
231	      const potentialAccount = accountMatch[1];
232	      // Check if line contains account-related keywords
233	      if ((line.toLowerCase().includes('account') || line.toLowerCase().includes('acc')) &&
234	          !line.toLowerCase().includes('bank')) {
235	        // Validate it looks like an account number (not just numbers, reasonable length)
236	        if (potentialAccount.length >= 4 && potentialAccount.length <= 20) {
237	          return potentialAccount;
238	        }
239	      }
240	    }
241	  }
242	  
243	  return null;
244	}
245	
246	/**
247	 * Extract customer name using common patterns
248	 */
249	function extractCustomerName(text) {
250	  // Look for "Bill To", "Customer", "Client" labels
251	  const patterns = [
252	    /(?:bill\s*to|customer|client)[\s:]*\n?([A-Z][A-Za-z\s&,\.\-']+)/i,
253	    /(?:sold\s*to)[\s:]*\n?([A-Z][A-Za-z\s&,\.\-']+)/i
254	  ];
255	  
256	  for (const pattern of patterns) {
257	    const match = text.match(pattern);
258	    if (match && match[1]) {
259	      const name = match[1].trim();
260	      // Take first line only (usually the company name)
261	      return name.split('\n')[0].trim();
262	    }
263	  }
264	  
265	  return null;
266	}
267	
268	/**
269	 * Extract Customer PO (Purchase Order) number
270	 * Handles both numeric POs and text-based POs like "DROP OFF"
271	 * Also handles cases where PO value is on a different line
272	 */
273	function extractCustomerPO(text) {
274	  // First, try patterns that match on the same line
275	  const sameLinePatterns = [
276	    // "Customer PO: DROP OFF" or "Customer PO: ABC123" - handles multi-word POs
277	    /customer\s+po\s*:?\s*([A-Z0-9\s\-_]{2,50})/i,
278	    // "PO Number: ABC123" or "PO: DROP OFF"
279	    /po\s+(?:number\s*)?:?\s*([A-Z0-9\s\-_]{2,50})/i,
280	    // "Purchase Order: ABC123"
281	    /purchase\s+order\s*:?\s*([A-Z0-9\s\-_]{2,50})/i,
282	    // "PO #: ABC123"
283	    /po\s*#?\s*:?\s*([A-Z0-9\s\-_]{2,50})/i
284	  ];
285	  
286	  for (const pattern of sameLinePatterns) {
287	    const match = text.match(pattern);
288	    if (match && match[1]) {
289	      const po = match[1].trim();
290	      // Validate it looks like a PO (at least 2 chars, max 50)
291	      // Can contain spaces for multi-word POs like "DROP OFF"
292	      if (po.length >= 2 && po.length <= 50 && !/^(no|yes|na|n\/a)$/i.test(po)) {
293	        return po;
294	      }
295	    }
296	  }
297	  
298	  // If no match on same line, try to find "Customer PO:" and then look for value on next line(s)
299	  const poLabelMatch = text.match(/customer\s+po\s*:?\s*/i);
300	  if (poLabelMatch) {
301	    const afterLabel = text.substring(poLabelMatch.index + poLabelMatch[0].length);
302	    // Look for PO value in the next 200 characters (could be on next line)
303	    const nextSection = afterLabel.substring(0, 200);
304	    // Try to find a valid PO value (alphanumeric, possibly with spaces/hyphens)
305	    const poValueMatch = nextSection.match(/\b([A-Z0-9\s\-_]{2,50})\b/);
306	    if (poValueMatch && poValueMatch[1]) {
307	      const po = poValueMatch[1].trim();
308	      // Validate it's not just whitespace or common words
309	      if (po.length >= 2 && po.length <= 50 && 
310	          !/^(no|yes|na|n\/a|order|date|packing|list|number)$/i.test(po) &&
311	          !po.match(/^\d{1,2}[\/\-]\d{1,2}/)) { // Not a date
312	        return po;
313	      }
314	    }
315	  }
316	  
317	  // Standalone "PO ABC123" or "PO-ABC123" or "DROP OFF" near "Customer PO"
318	  const standaloneMatch = text.match(/(?:^|\s)(?:PO[-\s]+|)([A-Z][A-Z0-9\s\-_]{1,49})/);
319	  if (standaloneMatch && standaloneMatch[1]) {
320	    const po = standaloneMatch[1].trim();
321	    // Check if it's near "Customer PO" in the text
322	    const poIndex = text.toLowerCase().indexOf('customer po');
323	    const matchIndex = standaloneMatch.index;
324	    if (poIndex >= 0 && Math.abs(matchIndex - poIndex) < 500) {
325	      if (po.length >= 2 && po.length <= 50 && 
326	          !/^(no|yes|na|n\/a|order|date|packing|list|number|invoice|account)$/i.test(po)) {
327	        return po;
328	      }
329	    }
330	  }
331	  
332	  return null;
333	}
334	
335	/**
336	 * Extract Goods/Net Amount (subtotal before VAT)
337	 * Handles formats like "Goods£0.00" or "Goods: £0.00"
338	 */
339	function extractGoodsAmount(text) {
340	  const patterns = [
341	    // "Goods£0.00" or "Goods: £0.00" or "Goods £0.00"
342	    /goods\s*:?\s*[£$€]?\s*([\d,]+\.?\d{2})/i,
343	    // "Net Amount: £0.00"
344	    /net\s+amount\s*:?\s*[£$€]?\s*([\d,]+\.?\d{2})/i,
345	    // "Subtotal: £0.00"
346	    /subtotal\s*:?\s*[£$€]?\s*([\d,]+\.?\d{2})/i,
347	    // "Goods Value: £0.00"
348	    /goods\s+value\s*:?\s*[£$€]?\s*([\d,]+\.?\d{2})/i,
349	    // Look for "Goods" followed by currency amount on same or next line
350	    /goods\s*:?\s*[£$€]?\s*([\d,]+\.?\d*)/i
351	  ];
352	  
353	  for (const pattern of patterns) {
354	    const match = text.match(pattern);
355	    if (match && match[1]) {
356	      const amountStr = match[1].trim().replace(/,/g, '');
357	      const amount = parseFloat(amountStr);
358	      if (!isNaN(amount)) {
359	        return amount;
360	      }
361	    }
362	  }
363	  
364	  return null;
365	}
366	
367	/**
368	 * Extract VAT Amount
369	 * Handles formats like "VAT£0.00" or "VAT: £0.00" or "VAT £0.00"
370	 */
371	function extractVatAmount(text) {
372	  const patterns = [
373	    // "VAT£0.00" or "VAT: £0.00" or "VAT £0.00"
374	    /vat\s*:?\s*[£$€]?\s*([\d,]+\.?\d{2})/i,
375	    // "VAT Amount: £0.00"
376	    /vat\s+amount\s*:?\s*[£$€]?\s*([\d,]+\.?\d{2})/i,
377	    // "Tax Amount: £0.00"
378	    /tax\s+amount\s*:?\s*[£$€]?\s*([\d,]+\.?\d{2})/i,
379	    // "Tax: £0.00"
380	    /tax\s*:?\s*[£$€]?\s*([\d,]+\.?\d{2})/i
381	  ];
382	  
383	  for (const pattern of patterns) {
384	    const match = text.match(pattern);
385	    if (match && match[1]) {
386	      const amountStr = match[1].trim().replace(/,/g, '');
387	      const amount = parseFloat(amountStr);
388	      if (!isNaN(amount)) {
389	        return amount;
390	      }
391	    }
392	  }
393	  
394	  return null;
395	}
396
```

```text
1	const ftp = require('basic-ftp');
2	const Client = require('ssh2-sftp-client');
3	const crypto = require('crypto');
4	const fs = require('fs');
5	const path = require('path');
6	
7	/**
8	 * FTP/SFTP connection utilities
9	 */
10	
11	/**
12	 * Get FTP config with database settings priority
13	 * Priority: Database settings > Environment variables (fallback only)
14	 * This ensures that changes made in the Settings UI take effect immediately
15	 */
16	function getFtpConfig(ftpConfig) {
17	  return {
18	    ...ftpConfig,
19	    // Use database settings first, fall back to environment variables only if database value is empty
20	    username: ftpConfig.username || process.env.FTP_USERNAME || process.env.SFTP_USERNAME || '',
21	    password: ftpConfig.password || process.env.FTP_PASSWORD || process.env.SFTP_PASSWORD || '',
22	    host: ftpConfig.host || process.env.FTP_HOST || process.env.SFTP_HOST || '',
23	    port: ftpConfig.port || process.env.FTP_PORT || process.env.SFTP_PORT || (ftpConfig.type === 'sftp' ? 22 : 21)
24	  };
25	}
26	
27	/**
28	 * Test FTP/SFTP connection
29	 */
30	async function testConnection(ftpConfig) {
31	  const config = getFtpConfig(ftpConfig);
32	  const { type } = config;
33	  
34	  if (type === 'sftp') {
35	    return await testSFTPConnection(config);
36	  } else {
37	    return await testFTPConnection(config);
38	  }
39	}
40	
41	/**
42	 * Test FTP connection
43	 */
44	async function testFTPConnection(ftpConfig) {
45	  const { host, port, username, password, directory, secure, passive } = ftpConfig;
46	  const client = new ftp.Client();
47	  
48	  try {
49	    client.ftp.verbose = false; // Set to true for debugging
50	    
51	    await client.access({
52	      host,
53	      port: port || 21,
54	      user: username,
55	      password,
56	      secure: secure ? 'implicit' : false, // 'implicit' for FTPS
57	      secureOptions: secure ? { rejectUnauthorized: false } : undefined
58	    });
59	    
60	    // Test directory access
61	    if (directory && directory !== '/') {
62	      await client.cd(directory);
63	    }
64	    
65	    // List files to verify access
66	    const files = await client.list();
67	    
68	    // Close connection safely
69	    try {
70	      const closeResult = client.close();
71	      if (closeResult && typeof closeResult.then === 'function') {
72	        await closeResult.catch(() => {});
73	      }
74	    } catch (closeError) {
75	      // Ignore close errors
76	    }
77	    
78	    return {
79	      success: true,
80	      message: `Successfully connected to FTP server. Found ${files.length} item(s) in directory.`,
81	      fileCount: files.length
82	    };
83	  } catch (error) {
84	    // Close connection safely on error
85	    try {
86	      const closeResult = client.close();
87	      if (closeResult && typeof closeResult.then === 'function') {
88	        await closeResult.catch(() => {});
89	      }
90	    } catch (closeError) {
91	      // Ignore close errors
92	    }
93	    
94	    // Provide user-friendly error messages
95	    const errorMessage = error.message || '';
96	    const errorCode = error.code || '';
97	    
98	    // Check for TLS/SSL errors
99	    if (secure && (
100	      errorMessage.includes('SSL routines') ||
101	      errorMessage.includes('TLS') ||
102	      errorMessage.includes('wrong version number') ||
103	      errorMessage.includes('tls_validate_record_header') ||
104	      errorCode === 'ECONNRESET' ||
105	      errorCode === 'EPROTO'
106	    )) {
107	      return {
108	        success: false,
109	        message: `FTP server does not support TLS/SSL encryption. Please disable "Use FTPS (FTP over TLS)" in your FTP settings and try again.`,
110	        error: errorMessage,
111	        suggestion: 'Try connecting without TLS/SSL enabled.'
112	      };
113	    }
114	    
115	    // Check for authentication errors
116	    if (errorMessage.includes('530') || errorMessage.includes('authentication') || errorMessage.includes('login')) {
117	      return {
118	        success: false,
119	        message: `FTP authentication failed. Please check your username and password.`,
120	        error: errorMessage
121	      };
122	    }
123	    
124	    // Check for connection errors
125	    if (errorMessage.includes('ECONNREFUSED') || errorMessage.includes('ENOTFOUND') || errorMessage.includes('ETIMEDOUT')) {
126	      return {
127	        success: false,
128	        message: `Cannot connect to FTP server at ${host}:${port || 21}. Please check the host address and port number, and ensure the server is accessible.`,
129	        error: errorMessage
130	      };
131	    }
132	    
133	    // Generic error with helpful context
134	    return {
135	      success: false,
136	      message: `FTP connection failed: ${errorMessage}`,
137	      error: errorMessage
138	    };
139	  }
140	}

142	/**

145	async function testSFTPConnection(ftpConfig) {
146	  const { host, port, username, password, directory } = ftpConfig;
147	  const client = new Client();
148	  
149	  try {
150	    await client.connect({
151	      host,
152	      port: port || 22,
153	      username,
154	      password
155	    });
156	    
157	    // Test directory access
158	    if (directory && directory !== '/') {
159	      await client.cwd(directory);
160	    }
161	    
162	    // List files to verify access
163	    const files = await client.list(directory || '.');
164	    
165	    try {
166	      await client.end();
167	    } catch (endError) {
168	      // Ignore end errors
169	    }
170	    
171	    return {
172	      success: true,
173	      message: `Successfully connected to SFTP server. Found ${files.length} item(s) in directory.`,

175	    };
176	  } catch (error) {
177	    try {
178	      await client.end();
179	    } catch (endError) {
180	      // Ignore end errors
181	    }
182	    return {
183	      success: false,

195	  const { type } = config;

204	/**
205	 * Download file from FTP

207	async function downloadFromFTP(ftpConfig, remotePath, localPath) {
208	  const { host, port, username, password, directory, secure, passive } = ftpConfig;
209	  const client = new ftp.Client();
210	  
211	  try {
212	    console.log(`🔽 [FTP] Connecting to ${host}:${port || 21}...`);
213	    await client.access({
214	      host,
215	      port: port || 21,
216	      user: username,
217	      password,
218	      secure: secure ? 'implicit' : false,
219	      secureOptions: secure ? { rejectUnauthorized: false } : undefined
220	    });
221	    console.log(`✅ [FTP] Connected successfully`);
222	    
223	    if (directory && directory !== '/') {
224	      console.log(`📁 [FTP] Changing to directory: ${directory}`);
225	      await client.cd(directory);
226	    }
227	    
228	    // Ensure local directory exists
229	    const localDir = path.dirname(localPath);
230	    if (!fs.existsSync(localDir)) {
231	      console.log(`📁 [FTP] Creating local directory: ${localDir}`);
232	      fs.mkdirSync(localDir, { recursive: true });
233	    }
234	    
235	    console.log(`⬇️  [FTP] Downloading ${remotePath} to ${localPath}...`);
236	    await client.downloadTo(localPath, remotePath);
237	    console.log(`✅ [FTP] Download complete: ${localPath}`);
238	    
239	    // Verify file was actually written
240	    if (fs.existsSync(localPath)) {
241	      const stats = fs.statSync(localPath);
242	      console.log(`✅ [FTP] File verified: ${localPath} (${stats.size} bytes)`);
243	    } else {
244	      console.error(`❌ [FTP] File not found after download: ${localPath}`);
245	      throw new Error(`File not found after download: ${localPath}`);
246	    }
247	    
248	    // Close connection safely
249	    try {
250	      const closeResult = client.close();
251	      if (closeResult && typeof closeResult.then === 'function') {
252	        await closeResult.catch(() => {});
253	      }
254	    } catch (closeError) {
255	      // Ignore close errors
256	    }
257	    
258	    return { success: true, localPath };
259	  } catch (error) {
260	    console.error(`❌ [FTP] Download error for ${remotePath}:`, error.message);
261	    // Close connection safely on error
262	    try {
263	      const closeResult = client.close();
264	      if (closeResult && typeof closeResult.then === 'function') {
265	        await closeResult.catch(() => {});
266	      }
267	    } catch (closeError) {
268	      // Ignore close errors
269	    }
270	    throw error;
271	  }
272	}

274	/**
275	 * Download file from SFTP
276	 */
277	async function downloadFromSFTP(ftpConfig, remotePath, localPath) {
278	  const { host, port, username, password, directory } = ftpConfig;
279	  const client = new Client();
280	  
281	  try {
282	    console.log(`🔽 [SFTP] Connecting to ${host}:${port || 22}...`);
283	    await client.connect({
284	      host,
285	      port: port || 22,
286	      username,
287	      password
288	    });
289	    console.log(`✅ [SFTP] Connected successfully`);
290	    
291	    const fullRemotePath = directory && directory !== '/' 
292	      ? path.posix.join(directory, remotePath)
293	      : remotePath;
294	    console.log(`📁 [SFTP] Remote path: ${fullRemotePath}`);
295	    
296	    // Ensure local directory exists
297	    const localDir = path.dirname(localPath);
298	    if (!fs.existsSync(localDir)) {
299	      console.log(`📁 [SFTP] Creating local directory: ${localDir}`);
300	      fs.mkdirSync(localDir, { recursive: true });
301	    }
302	    
303	    console.log(`⬇️  [SFTP] Downloading ${fullRemotePath} to ${localPath}...`);
304	    await client.fastGet(fullRemotePath, localPath);
305	    console.log(`✅ [SFTP] Download complete: ${localPath}`);
306	    
307	    // Verify file was actually written
308	    if (fs.existsSync(localPath)) {
309	      const stats = fs.statSync(localPath);
310	      console.log(`✅ [SFTP] File verified: ${localPath} (${stats.size} bytes)`);
311	    } else {
312	      console.error(`❌ [SFTP] File not found after download: ${localPath}`);
313	      throw new Error(`File not found after download: ${localPath}`);
314	    }
315	    
316	    try {
317	      await client.end();
318	    } catch (endError) {
319	      // Ignore end errors
320	    }
321	    
322	    return { success: true, localPath };
323	  } catch (error) {
324	    console.error(`❌ [SFTP] Download error for ${remotePath}:`, error.message);
325	    try {
326	      await client.end();
327	    } catch (endError) {
328	      // Ignore end errors
329	    }
330	    throw error;
331	  }
332	}
333	
334	/**
335	 * List files in FTP/SFTP directory
336	 */
337	async function listFiles(ftpConfig, pattern = null) {
338	  const config = getFtpConfig(ftpConfig);
339	  const { type } = config;
340	  
341	  if (type === 'sftp') {
342	    return await listSFTPFiles(config, pattern);
343	  } else {
344	    return await listFTPFiles(config, pattern);
345	  }
346	}
347	
348	/**
349	 * List files in FTP directory
350	 */
351	async function listFTPFiles(ftpConfig, pattern = null) {
352	  const { host, port, username, password, directory, secure } = ftpConfig;
353	  const client = new ftp.Client();
354	  
355	  try {
356	    await client.access({
357	      host,
358	      port: port || 21,
359	      user: username,
360	      password,
361	      secure: secure ? 'implicit' : false,
362	      secureOptions: secure ? { rejectUnauthorized: false } : undefined
363	    });
364	    
365	    if (directory && directory !== '/') {
366	      await client.cd(directory);
367	    }
368	    
369	    let files = await client.list();
370	    
371	    // Apply pattern if provided (no file type filtering here - let route handlers do it)
372	    if (pattern) {
373	      const regex = new RegExp(pattern, 'i');
374	      files = files.filter(file => regex.test(file.name));
375	    }
376	    
377	    // Close connection safely
378	    try {
379	      const closeResult = client.close();
380	      if (closeResult && typeof closeResult.then === 'function') {
381	        await closeResult.catch(() => {});
382	      }
383	    } catch (closeError) {
384	      // Ignore close errors
385	    }
386	    
387	    return files;
388	  } catch (error) {
389	    // Close connection safely on error
390	    try {
391	      const closeResult = client.close();
392	      if (closeResult && typeof closeResult.then === 'function') {
393	        await closeResult.catch(() => {});
394	      }
395	    } catch (closeError) {
396	      // Ignore close errors
397	    }
398	    throw error;
399	  }
400	}
401	
402	/**
403	 * List files in SFTP directory
404	 */
405	async function listSFTPFiles(ftpConfig, pattern = null) {
406	  const { host, port, username, password, directory } = ftpConfig;
407	  const client = new Client();
408	  
409	  try {
410	    await client.connect({
411	      host,
412	      port: port || 22,
413	      username,
414	      password
415	    });
416	    
417	    const dir = directory || '.';
418	    let files = await client.list(dir);
419	    
420	    // Filter out directories (keep only regular files)
421	    files = files.filter(file => file.type === '-');
422	    
423	    // Apply pattern if provided (no file type filtering here - let route handlers do it)
424	    if (pattern) {
425	      const regex = new RegExp(pattern, 'i');
426	      files = files.filter(file => regex.test(file.name));
427	    }
428	    
429	    try {
430	      await client.end();
431	    } catch (endError) {
432	      // Ignore end errors
433	    }
434	    
435	    return files;
436	  } catch (error) {
437	    try {
438	      await client.end();
439	    } catch (endError) {
440	      // Ignore end errors
441	    }
442	    throw error;
443	  }
444	}
445	
446	/**
447	 * Move/rename file on FTP/SFTP server
448	 */
449	async function moveFile(ftpConfig, sourcePath, destinationPath) {
450	  const config = getFtpConfig(ftpConfig);
451	  const { type } = config;
452	  
453	  if (type === 'sftp') {
454	    return await moveFileSFTP(config, sourcePath, destinationPath);
455	  } else {
456	    return await moveFileFTP(config, sourcePath, destinationPath);
457	  }
458	}
459	
460	/**
461	 * Move file on FTP server
462	 */
463	async function moveFileFTP(ftpConfig, sourcePath, destinationPath) {
464	  const { host, port, username, password, directory, secure } = ftpConfig;
465	  const client = new ftp.Client();
466	  
467	  try {
468	    await client.access({
469	      host,
470	      port: port || 21,
471	      user: username,
472	      password,
473	      secure: secure ? 'implicit' : false,
474	      secureOptions: secure ? { rejectUnauthorized: false } : undefined
475	    });
476	    
477	    // Navigate to base directory if specified
478	    if (directory && directory !== '/') {
479	      await client.cd(directory);
480	    }
481	    
482	    // Ensure destination directory exists
483	    const destDir = path.posix.dirname(destinationPath);
484	    if (destDir && destDir !== '.' && destDir !== '/') {
485	      try {
486	        // Try to change to directory - if it fails, create it
487	        try {
488	          await client.cd(destDir);
489	        } catch (cdError) {
490	          // Directory doesn't exist, create it
491	          const parts = destDir.split('/').filter(p => p);
492	          let currentPath = '';
493	          for (const part of parts) {
494	            currentPath += '/' + part;
495	            try {
496	              await client.cd(currentPath);
497	            } catch {
498	              await client.uploadFrom(Buffer.from(''), currentPath + '/.keep');
499	              // Remove the .keep file
500	              try {
501	                await client.remove(currentPath + '/.keep');
502	              } catch {}
503	            }
504	          }
505	        }
506	      } catch (dirError) {
507	        // Directory might already exist or creation failed, continue
508	        console.warn(`Could not ensure directory exists: ${destDir}`, dirError.message);
509	      }
510	    }
511	    
512	    // Move/rename the file
513	    await client.rename(sourcePath, destinationPath);
514	    
515	    // Close connection safely
516	    try {
517	      const closeResult = client.close();
518	      if (closeResult && typeof closeResult.then === 'function') {
519	        await closeResult.catch(() => {});
520	      }
521	    } catch (closeError) {
522	      // Ignore close errors
523	    }
524	    
525	    return { success: true, destinationPath };
526	  } catch (error) {
527	    // Close connection safely on error
528	    try {
529	      const closeResult = client.close();
530	      if (closeResult && typeof closeResult.then === 'function') {
531	        await closeResult.catch(() => {});
532	      }
533	    } catch (closeError) {
534	      // Ignore close errors
535	    }
536	    throw error;
537	  }
538	}
539	
540	/**
541	 * Move file on SFTP server
542	 */
543	async function moveFileSFTP(ftpConfig, sourcePath, destinationPath) {
544	  const { host, port, username, password, directory } = ftpConfig;
545	  const client = new Client();
546	  
547	  try {
548	    await client.connect({
549	      host,
550	      port: port || 22,
551	      username,
552	      password
553	    });
554	    
555	    // Build full paths
556	    const fullSourcePath = directory && directory !== '/' 
557	      ? path.posix.join(directory, sourcePath)
558	      : sourcePath;
559	    
560	    const fullDestPath = directory && directory !== '/' 
561	      ? path.posix.join(directory, destinationPath)
562	      : destinationPath;
563	    
564	    // Ensure destination directory exists
565	    const destDir = path.posix.dirname(fullDestPath);
566	    try {
567	      await client.mkdir(destDir, true); // true = recursive
568	    } catch (dirError) {
569	      // Directory might already exist, continue
570	    }
571	    
572	    // Move/rename the file
573	    await client.rename(fullSourcePath, fullDestPath);
574	    
575	    try {
576	      await client.end();
577	    } catch (endError) {
578	      // Ignore end errors
579	    }
580	    
581	    return { success: true, destinationPath: fullDestPath };
582	  } catch (error) {
583	    try {
584	      await client.end();
585	    } catch (endError) {
586	      // Ignore end errors
587	    }
588	    throw error;
589	  }
590	}
591	
592	/**
593	 * Calculate file hash (SHA256)
594	 */
595	function calculateFileHash(filePath) {
596	  const fileBuffer = fs.readFileSync(filePath);
597	  return crypto.createHash('sha256').update(fileBuffer).digest('hex');
598	}
599	
600	module.exports = {
601	  testConnection,
602	  downloadFile,
603	  listFiles,
604	  moveFile,
605	  calculateFileHash
606	};
607	
608
```

```text
1	const express = require('express');
2	const { PendingRegistration, Settings, User, Company } = require('../models');
3	const { sendTemplatedEmail } = require('../utils/sendTemplatedEmail');
4	const { logActivity, ActivityType } = require('../services/activityLogger');
5	const { Op } = require('sequelize');
6	const recaptchaMiddleware = require('../middleware/recaptcha');
7	const router = express.Router();
8	
9	// Get registration form configuration (public) - kept for backwards compatibility
10	router.get('/form-config', async (req, res) => {
11	  try {
12	    res.json({
13	      customFields: []
14	    });
15	  } catch (error) {
16	    console.error('Error fetching registration form config:', error);
17	    res.status(500).json({ message: error.message });
18	  }
19	});
20	
21	// Submit registration (public)
22	router.post('/submit', recaptchaMiddleware({ minScore: 0.5 }), async (req, res) => {
23	  try {
24	    const { firstName, lastName, companyName, accountNumber, email } = req.body;
25	    
26	    // Validate required fields
27	    if (!firstName || !lastName || !companyName || !email) {
28	      return res.status(400).json({ 
29	        message: 'First Name, Last Name, Company Name, and Email are required' 
30	      });
31	    }
32	    
33	    // Validate email format
34	    const emailRegex = /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/;
35	    if (!emailRegex.test(email)) {
36	      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Invalid email address' });
37	    }
38	    
39	    // Check if user with this email already exists
40	    const existingUser = await User.findOne({ 
41	      where: { email: email.toLowerCase().trim() } 
42	    });
43	    if (existingUser) {
44	      return res.status(400).json({ 
45	        message: 'An account with this email address already exists' 
46	      });
47	    }
48	    
49	    // Check if there's already a pending registration with this email
50	    const existingPending = await PendingRegistration.findOne({
51	      where: { 
52	        email: email.toLowerCase().trim(),
53	        status: 'pending'
54	      }
55	    });
56	    if (existingPending) {
57	      return res.status(400).json({ 
58	        message: 'A registration request with this email is already pending review' 
59	      });
60	    }
61	    
62	    // Create pending registration
63	    const registration = await PendingRegistration.create({
64	      firstName: firstName.trim(),
65	      lastName: lastName.trim(),
66	      companyName: companyName.trim(),
67	      accountNumber: accountNumber?.trim() || null,
68	      email: email.toLowerCase().trim(),
69	      customFields: {},
70	      status: 'pending',
71	      metadata: {
72	        submittedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
73	        ipAddress: req.ip || req.connection.remoteAddress,
74	        userAgent: req.get('user-agent')
75	      }
76	    });
77	    
78	    // Get settings for email sending
79	    const settings = await Settings.getSettings();
80	    
81	    // Get all global admins and administrators to notify
82	    const admins = await User.findAll({
83	      where: {
84	        role: { [Op.in]: ['global_admin', 'administrator'] },
85	        isActive: true
86	      },
87	      attributes: ['id', 'name', 'email']
88	    });
89	    
90	    // Send notification email to each admin
91	    const { getFrontendUrl } = require('../utils/urlConfig');
92	    const frontendUrl = getFrontendUrl();
93	    const reviewUrl = `${frontendUrl}/users/pending-accounts/${registration.id}`;
94	    
95	    for (const admin of admins) {
96	      try {
97	        await sendTemplatedEmail(
98	          'registration-request',
99	          admin.email,
100	          {
101	            userName: admin.name,
102	            applicantName: `${firstName} ${lastName}`,
103	            applicantFirstName: firstName,
104	            applicantLastName: lastName,
105	            applicantEmail: email,
106	            applicantCompanyName: companyName,
107	            accountNumber: accountNumber || 'N/A',
108	            reviewUrl: reviewUrl,
109	            registrationId: registration.id
110	          },
111	          settings,
112	          { 
113	            ipAddress: req.ip, 
114	            userAgent: req.get('user-agent'),
115	            userId: null // Public registration
116	          }
117	        );
118	      } catch (emailError) {
119	        console.error(`Failed to send registration notification to ${admin.email}:`, emailError);
120	        // Don't fail the registration if email fails
121	      }
122	    }
123	    
124	    // Send confirmation email to the user
125	    try {
126	      await sendTemplatedEmail(
127	        'registration-submitted',
128	        email,
129	        {
130	          userName: `${firstName} ${lastName}`,
131	          userEmail: email,
132	          userCompanyName: companyName,
133	          accountNumber: accountNumber || null,
134	          companyName: settings.companyName
135	        },
136	        settings,
137	        { 
138	          ipAddress: req.ip, 
139	          userAgent: req.get('user-agent'),
140	          userId: null // Public registration
141	        }
142	      );
143	    } catch (emailError) {
144	      console.error(`Failed to send registration confirmation to ${email}:`, emailError);
145	      // Don't fail the registration if email fails
146	    }
147	    
148	    // Log activity
149	    await logActivity({
150	      type: ActivityType.USER_REGISTRATION_SUBMITTED,
151	      userId: null,
152	      userEmail: email,
153	      userRole: null,
154	      action: `New registration request submitted: ${firstName} ${lastName} (${email})`,
155	      details: {
156	        registrationId: registration.id,
157	        firstName,
158	        lastName,
159	        companyName,
160	        accountNumber,
161	        email
162	      },
163	      companyId: null,
164	      companyName: companyName,
165	      ipAddress: req.ip || req.connection.remoteAddress,
166	      userAgent: req.get('user-agent')
167	    });
168	    
169	    res.status(201).json({
170	      success: true,
171	      message: 'Registration submitted successfully. You will receive an email once your account has been reviewed.',
172	      registrationId: registration.id
173	    });
174	  } catch (error) {
175	    console.error('Error submitting registration:', error);
176	    res.status(500).json({ message: error.message });
177	  }
178	});
179	
180	// Get pending registration by ID (for review page)
181	router.get('/:id', async (req, res) => {
182	  try {
183	    const registration = await PendingRegistration.findByPk(req.params.id, {
184	      include: [{
185	        model: User,
186	        as: 'reviewedBy',
187	        attributes: ['id', 'name', 'email']
188	      }, {
189	        model: User,
190	        as: 'createdUser',
191	        attributes: ['id', 'name', 'email']
192	      }]
193	    });
194	    
195	    if (!registration) {
196	      return res.status(404).json({ message: 'Registration not found' });
197	    }
198	    
199	    res.json(registration);
200	  } catch (error) {
201	    console.error('Error fetching registration:', error);
202	    res.status(500).json({ message: error.message });
203	  }
204	});
205	
206	module.exports = router;
207	
208
```

```text
32	        file.originalname.endsWith('.csv') || 
33	        file.originalname.endsWith('.xls') || 
34	        file.originalname.endsWith('.xlsx') || 
35	        file.originalname.endsWith('.json')) {
36	      cb(null, true);
37	    } else {

39	    }
40	  }

43	// Get paginated companies for user assignment (with Redis caching)
44	router.get('/for-assignment', auth, async (req, res) => {
45	  try {
46	    const { page = 1, limit = 50, search = '' } = req.query;
47	    const pageNum = parseInt(page);
48	    const limitNum = parseInt(limit);
49	    const offset = (pageNum - 1) * limitNum;
50	    
51	    // Create cache key
52	    const cacheKey = `companies:assignment:${pageNum}:${limitNum}:${search.toLowerCase().trim()}`;
53	    
54	    // Try to get from cache first
55	    if (redis) {
56	      try {
57	        const cached = await redis.get(cacheKey);
58	        if (cached) {
59	          return res.json(JSON.parse(cached));
60	        }
61	      } catch (cacheError) {
62	        // If Redis fails, continue without cache
63	        console.warn('Redis cache read failed, continuing without cache:', cacheError.message);
64	      }
65	    }
66	    
67	    const whereConditions = {
68	      isActive: true
69	    };
70	    
71	    if (search) {
72	      const searchLower = search.toLowerCase().trim();
73	      const searchConditions = [
74	        { name: { [Op.iLike]: `%${searchLower}%` } },
75	        { code: { [Op.iLike]: `%${searchLower}%` } }
76	      ];
77	      
78	      // Try to parse as number for referenceNo search
79	      const searchNum = parseInt(search);
80	      if (!isNaN(searchNum)) {
81	        // Search by exact referenceNo match
82	        searchConditions.push({ referenceNo: searchNum });
83	        // Also search by referenceNo as text (in case it's stored as text in some cases)
84	        searchConditions.push(
85	          Sequelize.where(
86	            Sequelize.cast(Sequelize.col('referenceNo'), 'TEXT'),
87	            { [Op.iLike]: `%${searchLower}%` }
88	          )
89	        );
90	      } else {
91	        // If not a number, still try to search referenceNo as text
92	        searchConditions.push(
93	          Sequelize.where(
94	            Sequelize.cast(Sequelize.col('referenceNo'), 'TEXT'),
95	            { [Op.iLike]: `%${searchLower}%` }
96	          )
97	        );
98	      }
99	      
100	      whereConditions[Op.or] = searchConditions;
```
