939	// Update invoice - GA + Admin only
940	router.put('/:id', requirePermission('INVOICES_EDIT'), async (req, res) => {
941	  try {
942	    const invoice = await Invoice.findByPk(req.params.id);
943	    
944	    if (!invoice) {
945	      return res.status(404).json({ message: 'Invoice not found' });
946	    }
947	    
948	    // Check access to this invoice's company
949	    if (req.accessibleCompanyIds !== null && 
950	        !req.accessibleCompanyIds.includes(invoice.companyId)) {
951	      return res.status(403).json({ 
952	        message: 'Access denied. You do not have access to this invoice.' 
953	      });
954	    }
955	    
956	    const {
957	      invoiceNumber,
958	      companyId,
959	      issueDate,
960	      dueDate,
961	      amount,
962	      taxAmount,
963	      status,
964	      documentStatus,
965	      items,
966	      notes,
967	      fileUrl,
968	      metadata,
969	      editReason
970	    } = req.body;
971	    
972	    // Check if invoice number is being changed and if it already exists
973	    if (invoiceNumber && invoiceNumber !== invoice.invoiceNumber) {
974	      const existingInvoice = await Invoice.findOne({
975	        where: { invoiceNumber }
976	      });
977	      
978	      if (existingInvoice) {
979	        return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Invoice number already exists' });
980	      }
981	    }
982	    
983	    // Track changes for edit history
984	    const changes = {};
985	    const oldValues = {};
986	    
987	    // Update fields and track changes
988	    if (invoiceNumber !== undefined && invoiceNumber !== invoice.invoiceNumber) {
989	      oldValues.invoiceNumber = invoice.invoiceNumber;
990	      invoice.invoiceNumber = invoiceNumber;
991	      changes.invoiceNumber = { from: oldValues.invoiceNumber, to: invoiceNumber };
992	    }
993	    if (companyId !== undefined && companyId !== invoice.companyId) {
994	      oldValues.companyId = invoice.companyId;
995	      invoice.companyId = companyId;
996	      changes.companyId = { from: oldValues.companyId, to: companyId };
997	    }
998	    if (issueDate !== undefined) {
999	      const newDate = new Date(issueDate);
1000	      const oldDate = invoice.issueDate ? new Date(invoice.issueDate) : null;
1001	      if (!oldDate || newDate.getTime() !== oldDate.getTime()) {
1002	        oldValues.issueDate = invoice.issueDate;
1003	        invoice.issueDate = issueDate;
1004	        changes.issueDate = { from: oldValues.issueDate, to: issueDate };
1005	      }
1006	    }
1007	    if (dueDate !== undefined) {
1008	      const newDate = dueDate ? new Date(dueDate) : null;
1009	      const oldDate = invoice.dueDate ? new Date(invoice.dueDate) : null;
1010	      if ((!newDate && oldDate) || (newDate && !oldDate) || (newDate && oldDate && newDate.getTime() !== oldDate.getTime())) {
1011	        oldValues.dueDate = invoice.dueDate;
1012	        invoice.dueDate = dueDate;
1013	        changes.dueDate = { from: oldValues.dueDate, to: dueDate };
1014	      }
1015	    }
1016	    if (amount !== undefined && parseFloat(amount) !== parseFloat(invoice.amount || 0)) {
1017	      oldValues.amount = invoice.amount;
1018	      invoice.amount = amount;
1019	      changes.amount = { from: oldValues.amount, to: amount };
1020	    }
1021	    if (taxAmount !== undefined && parseFloat(taxAmount) !== parseFloat(invoice.taxAmount || 0)) {
1022	      oldValues.taxAmount = invoice.taxAmount;
1023	      invoice.taxAmount = taxAmount;
1024	      changes.taxAmount = { from: oldValues.taxAmount, to: taxAmount };
1025	    }
1026	    if (status !== undefined && status !== invoice.status) {
1027	      oldValues.status = invoice.status;
1028	      invoice.status = status;
1029	      changes.status = { from: oldValues.status, to: status };
1030	    }
1031	    if (documentStatus !== undefined && documentStatus !== invoice.documentStatus) {
1032	      // Check if user can change document status
1033	      // When setting is enabled, ONLY external users can change status (no exceptions)
1034	      const settings = await Settings.getSettings();
1035	      const canChangeStatus = !settings.onlyExternalUsersChangeDocumentStatus || 
1036	                             req.user.role === 'external_user';
1037	      
1038	      if (!canChangeStatus) {
1039	        return res.status(403).json({ 
1040	          message: 'Access denied. Only external users can change document status when this restriction is enabled.' 
1041	        });
1042	      }
1043	      
1044	      oldValues.documentStatus = invoice.documentStatus;
1045	      invoice.documentStatus = documentStatus;
1046	      changes.documentStatus = { from: oldValues.documentStatus, to: documentStatus };
1047	    }
1048	    if (items !== undefined) {
1049	      invoice.items = items;
1050	      changes.items = { updated: true };
1051	    }
1052	    if (notes !== undefined && notes !== (invoice.notes || '')) {
1053	      oldValues.notes = invoice.notes;
1054	      invoice.notes = notes;
1055	      changes.notes = { from: oldValues.notes, to: notes };
1056	    }
1057	    if (fileUrl !== undefined) {
1058	      invoice.fileUrl = fileUrl;
1059	    }
1060	    if (metadata !== undefined) {
1061	      invoice.metadata = metadata;
1062	    }
1063	    
1064	    // If there are changes, require edit reason and log them
1065	    if (Object.keys(changes).length > 0) {
1066	      if (!editReason || editReason.trim().length === 0) {
1067	        return res.status(400).json({ 
1068	          message: 'Edit reason is required when making changes to an invoice.' 
1069	        });
1070	      }
1071	      
1072	      // Update edit tracking fields
1073	      invoice.editedBy = req.user.id;
1074	      invoice.editReason = editReason.trim();
1075	      
1076	      // Add to edit history
1077	      const editHistory = invoice.editHistory || [];
1078	      editHistory.push({
1079	        timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
1080	        editedBy: req.user.id,
1081	        editedByName: req.user.name || req.user.email || 'Unknown',
1082	        reason: editReason.trim(),
1083	        changes: changes
1084	      });
1085	      invoice.editHistory = editHistory;
1086	      
1087	      // Log activity
1088	      const { logActivity } = require('../services/activityLogger');
1089	      await logActivity({
1090	        type: 'invoice_edited',
1091	        userId: req.user.id,
1092	        userEmail: req.user.email || 'Unknown',
1093	        userName: req.user.name || req.user.email || 'Unknown',
1094	        userRole: req.user.role,
1095	        action: 'Edited invoice',
1096	        details: {
1097	          invoiceId: invoice.id,
1098	          invoiceNumber: invoice.invoiceNumber,
1099	          changes: changes,
1100	          reason: editReason.trim()
1101	        },
1102	        companyId: invoice.companyId
1103	      });
1104	    }
1105	    
1106	    await invoice.save();
1107	    
1108	    // Reload with company
1109	    await invoice.reload({
1110	      include: [{
1111	        model: Company,
1112	        as: 'company',
1113	        attributes: ['id', 'name', 'referenceNo', 'type']
1114	      }]
1115	    });
1116	    
1117	    res.json(invoice);
1118	  } catch (error) {
1119	    console.error('Error updating invoice:', error);
1120	    res.status(400).json({ message: error.message });
1121	  }
1122	});
1123	
1124	// Delete invoice - GA + Admin only
1125	router.delete('/:id', requirePermission('INVOICES_DELETE'), async (req, res) => {
1126	  try {
1127	    const { reason } = req.body;
1128	    
1129	    if (!reason || reason.trim().length === 0) {
1130	      return res.status(400).json({ 
1131	        message: 'Deletion reason is required for accountability.' 
1132	      });
1133	    }
1134	    
1135	    const invoice = await Invoice.findByPk(req.params.id);
1136	    
1137	    if (!invoice) {
1138	      return res.status(404).json({ message: 'Invoice not found' });
1139	    }
1140	    
1141	    // Check access to this invoice's company
1142	    if (req.accessibleCompanyIds !== null && 
1143	        !req.accessibleCompanyIds.includes(invoice.companyId)) {
1144	      return res.status(403).json({ 
1145	        message: 'Access denied. You do not have access to this invoice.' 
1146	      });
1147	    }
1148	    
1149	    // Track deletion (soft delete with tracking)
1150	    invoice.deletedBy = req.user.userId;
1151	    invoice.deletedReason = reason.trim();
1152	    invoice.deletedAt = new Date();
1153	    await invoice.save();
1154	    
1155	    // Delete the physical file if it exists
1156	    if (invoice.fileUrl) {
1157	      const filePath = path.isAbsolute(invoice.fileUrl) 
1158	        ? invoice.fileUrl 
1159	        : path.join(__dirname, '..', invoice.fileUrl);
1160	      
1161	      if (fs.existsSync(filePath)) {
1162	        try {
1163	          fs.unlinkSync(filePath);
1164	          console.log(`🗑️  Deleted invoice file: ${filePath}`);
1165	        } catch (fileError) {
1166	          console.error(`⚠️  Failed to delete invoice file: ${fileError.message}`);
1167	          // Continue even if file deletion fails
1168	        }
1169	      }
1170	    }
1171	    
1172	    // Hard delete from database
1173	    await invoice.destroy({ force: true });
1174	    
1175	    // Log deletion
1176	    await logActivity({
1177	      type: ActivityType.INVOICE_DELETED,
1178	      userId: req.user.userId,
1179	      userEmail: req.user.email,
1180	      userRole: req.user.role,
1181	      action: `Deleted invoice ${invoice.invoiceNumber || invoice.id}`,
1182	      details: { 
1183	        invoiceId: invoice.id, 
1184	        invoiceNumber: invoice.invoiceNumber,
1185	        reason: reason.trim()
1186	      },
1187	      companyId: invoice.companyId,
1188	      companyName: invoice.company?.name || null,
1189	      ipAddress: req.ip || req.connection.remoteAddress,
1190	      userAgent: req.get('user-agent')
1191	    });
1192	    
1193	    console.log(`🗑️  Invoice ${invoice.invoiceNumber} deleted by ${req.user.email} (${req.user.userId}). Reason: ${reason}`);
1194	    
1195	    res.json({ message: 'Invoice deleted successfully' });
1196	  } catch (error) {
1197	    console.error('Error deleting invoice:', error);
1198	    res.status(500).json({ message: error.message });
1199	  }
1200	});
1201	
1202	// Mark invoice as viewed
1203	router.post('/:id/view', async (req, res) => {
1204	  try {
1205	    const invoice = await Invoice.findByPk(req.params.id, {
1206	      include: [{
1207	        model: Company,
1208	        as: 'company',
1209	        attributes: ['id', 'name', 'referenceNo'],
1210	        required: false
1211	      }]
1212	    });
1213	    
1214	    if (!invoice) {
1215	      return res.status(404).json({ message: 'Invoice not found' });
1216	    }
1217	    
1218	    // Check access to this invoice's company
1219	    if (req.accessibleCompanyIds !== null && 
1220	        !req.accessibleCompanyIds.includes(invoice.companyId)) {
1221	      return res.status(403).json({ 
1222	        message: 'Access denied. You do not have access to this invoice.' 
1223	      });
1224	    }
1225	    
1226	    // Only update status if setting allows all users OR user is external_user
1227	    // When setting is enabled, ONLY external users can change status (no exceptions, not even GA)
1228	    const settings = await Settings.getSettings();
1229	    const onlyExternal = settings.onlyExternalUsersChangeDocumentStatus;
1230	    const userRole = req.user.role;
1231	    const canUpdateStatus = !onlyExternal || userRole === 'external_user';
1232	    
1233	    console.log(`📊 Document Status Check - onlyExternalUsersChangeDocumentStatus: ${onlyExternal}, userRole: ${userRole}, canUpdateStatus: ${canUpdateStatus}`);
1234	    
1235	    // Track if this is the first view (needed for logging)
1236	    const wasFirstView = !invoice.viewedAt;
1237	    
1238	    if (canUpdateStatus) {
1239	      // Always update status when viewed (unless already downloaded)
1240	      const now = new Date();
1241	      
1242	      if (!invoice.viewedAt) {
1243	        invoice.viewedAt = now;
1244	      }
1245	      
1246	      // Update documentStatus to 'viewed' if not already 'downloaded'
1247	      if (invoice.documentStatus !== 'downloaded') {
1248	        invoice.documentStatus = 'viewed';
1249	      }
1250	      
1251	      // Store who viewed in metadata
1252	      const metadata = invoice.metadata || {};
1253	      if (!metadata.viewedBy) {
1254	        metadata.viewedBy = req.user.userId;
1255	        metadata.viewedByEmail = req.user.email;
1256	        metadata.viewedByName = req.user.name || req.user.email;
1257	      }
1258	      invoice.metadata = metadata;
1259	      
1260	      await invoice.save();
1261	    }
1262	    
1263	    // Always log invoice view
1264	    await logActivity({
1265	      type: ActivityType.INVOICE_VIEWED,
1266	      userId: req.user.userId,
1267	      userEmail: req.user.email,
1268	      userRole: req.user.role,
1269	      action: `Viewed invoice ${invoice.invoiceNumber || invoice.id}${wasFirstView ? '' : ' (subsequent view)'}`,
1270	      details: { 
1271	        invoiceId: invoice.id, 
1272	        invoiceNumber: invoice.invoiceNumber,
1273	        documentStatus: invoice.documentStatus,
1274	        isFirstView: wasFirstView
1275	      },
1276	      companyId: invoice.companyId,
1277	      companyName: invoice.company?.name || null,
1278	      ipAddress: req.ip || req.connection.remoteAddress,
1279	      userAgent: req.get('user-agent')
1280	    });
1281	    
1282	    res.json({ message: 'Invoice marked as viewed', viewedAt: invoice.viewedAt });
1283	  } catch (error) {
1284	    console.error('Error marking invoice as viewed:', error);
1285	    res.status(500).json({ message: error.message });
1286	  }
1287	});
1288	
1289	// View invoice PDF (view only, no download)
1290	router.get('/:id/view-pdf', async (req, res) => {
1291	  try {
1292	    const invoice = await Invoice.findByPk(req.params.id, {
1293	      include: [{
1294	        model: Company,
1295	        as: 'company',
1296	        attributes: ['id', 'name']
1297	      }]
1298	    });
1299	    
1300	    if (!invoice) {
1301	      return res.status(404).json({ message: 'Invoice not found' });
1302	    }
1303	    
1304	    // Check access to this invoice's company
1305	    if (req.accessibleCompanyIds !== null && 
1306	        !req.accessibleCompanyIds.includes(invoice.companyId)) {
1307	      return res.status(403).json({ 
1308	        message: 'Access denied. You do not have access to this invoice.' 
1309	      });
1310	    }
1311	    
1312	    if (!invoice.fileUrl) {
1313	      return res.status(404).json({ message: 'No document file available for this invoice' });
1314	    }
1315	    
1316	    // Handle both absolute and relative paths
1317	    let filePath;
1318	    if (path.isAbsolute(invoice.fileUrl)) {
1319	      filePath = invoice.fileUrl;
1320	    } else {
1321	      filePath = path.join(__dirname, '..', invoice.fileUrl);
1322	      if (!fs.existsSync(filePath)) {
1323	        filePath = invoice.fileUrl;
1324	      }
1325	    }
1326	    
1327	    if (!fs.existsSync(filePath)) {
1328	      console.error(`File not found: ${filePath} (original: ${invoice.fileUrl})`);
1329	      return res.status(404).json({ message: 'Document file not found on server' });
1330	    }
1331	    
1332	    // Only update status if setting allows all users OR user is external_user
1333	    // When setting is enabled, ONLY external users can change status (no exceptions)
1334	    const settings = await Settings.getSettings();
1335	    const canUpdateStatus = !settings.onlyExternalUsersChangeDocumentStatus || req.user.role === 'external_user';
1336	    
1337	    // Mark as viewed if not already
1338	    const wasViewed = !!invoice.viewedAt;
1339	    if (canUpdateStatus && !invoice.viewedAt) {
1340	      invoice.viewedAt = new Date();
1341	      // Update documentStatus to 'viewed' if it's currently 'ready' or 'review'
1342	      if (invoice.documentStatus === 'ready' || invoice.documentStatus === 'review') {
1343	        invoice.documentStatus = 'viewed';
1344	      }
1345	      await invoice.save();
1346	    }
1347	    
1348	    // Log view (log ALL views, not just the first one)
1349	    await logActivity({
1350	      type: ActivityType.INVOICE_VIEWED,
1351	      userId: req.user.userId,
1352	      userEmail: req.user.email,
1353	      userRole: req.user.role,
1354	      action: `Viewed invoice PDF ${invoice.invoiceNumber || invoice.id}${wasViewed ? ' (subsequent view)' : ''}`,
1355	      details: { 
1356	        invoiceId: invoice.id, 
1357	        invoiceNumber: invoice.invoiceNumber,
1358	        documentStatus: invoice.documentStatus,
1359	        isFirstView: !wasViewed,
1360	        viewMethod: 'pdf_preview'
1361	      },
1362	      companyId: invoice.companyId,
1363	      companyName: invoice.company?.name || null,
1364	      ipAddress: req.ip || req.connection.remoteAddress,
1365	      userAgent: req.get('user-agent')
1366	    });
1367	    
1368	    // Serve PDF with inline content-disposition (view only, no download)
1369	    res.setHeader('Content-Type', 'application/pdf');
1370	    res.setHeader('Content-Disposition', `inline; filename="${path.basename(filePath)}"`);
1371	    res.sendFile(filePath);
1372	  } catch (error) {
1373	    console.error('Error viewing invoice PDF:', error);
1374	    res.status(500).json({ message: error.message });
1375	  }
1376	});
1377	
1378	// Download single invoice document
1379	router.get('/:id/download', async (req, res) => {
1380	  try {
1381	    const invoice = await Invoice.findByPk(req.params.id, {
1382	      include: [{
1383	        model: Company,
1384	        as: 'company',
1385	        attributes: ['id', 'name']
1386	      }]
1387	    });
1388	    
1389	    if (!invoice) {
1390	      return res.status(404).json({ message: 'Invoice not found' });
1391	    }
1392	    
1393	    // Check access to this invoice's company
1394	    if (req.accessibleCompanyIds !== null && 
1395	        !req.accessibleCompanyIds.includes(invoice.companyId)) {
1396	      return res.status(403).json({ 
1397	        message: 'Access denied. You do not have access to this invoice.' 
1398	      });
1399	    }
1400	    
1401	    if (!invoice.fileUrl) {
1402	      return res.status(404).json({ message: 'No document file available for this invoice' });
1403	    }
1404	    
1405	    const filePath = path.isAbsolute(invoice.fileUrl) 
1406	      ? invoice.fileUrl 
1407	      : path.join(__dirname, '..', invoice.fileUrl);
1408	    
1409	    if (!fs.existsSync(filePath)) {
1410	      return res.status(404).json({ message: 'Document file not found on server' });
1411	    }
1412	    
1413	    // Always update status when downloaded
1414	    // Only update status if setting allows all users OR user is external_user
1415	    // When setting is enabled, ONLY external users can change status (no exceptions, not even GA)
1416	    const settings = await Settings.getSettings();
1417	    const onlyExternal = settings.onlyExternalUsersChangeDocumentStatus;
1418	    const userRole = req.user.role;
1419	    const canUpdateStatus = !onlyExternal || userRole === 'external_user';
1420	    
1421	    console.log(`📊 Document Status Check (download) - onlyExternalUsersChangeDocumentStatus: ${onlyExternal}, userRole: ${userRole}, canUpdateStatus: ${canUpdateStatus}`);
1422	    
1423	    const wasFirstDownload = !invoice.downloadedAt;
1424	    const now = new Date();
1425	    
1426	    if (canUpdateStatus) {
1427	      if (!invoice.downloadedAt) {
1428	        invoice.downloadedAt = now;
1429	      }
1430	      
1431	      // Always set status to 'downloaded' when downloaded
1432	      invoice.documentStatus = 'downloaded';
1433	      
1434	      // Mark as viewed if not already
1435	      if (!invoice.viewedAt) {
1436	        invoice.viewedAt = now;
1437	      }
1438	      
1439	      // Store who downloaded in metadata
1440	      const metadata = invoice.metadata || {};
1441	      if (!metadata.downloadedBy) {
1442	        metadata.downloadedBy = req.user.userId;
1443	        metadata.downloadedByEmail = req.user.email;
1444	        metadata.downloadedByName = req.user.name || req.user.email;
1445	      }
1446	      invoice.metadata = metadata;
1447	      
1448	      await invoice.save();
1449	    }
1450	    
1451	    // Always log download
1452	    await logActivity({
1453	      type: ActivityType.INVOICE_DOWNLOADED,
1454	      userId: req.user.userId,
1455	      userEmail: req.user.email,
1456	      userRole: req.user.role,
1457	      action: `Downloaded invoice ${invoice.invoiceNumber || invoice.id}${wasFirstDownload ? '' : ' (subsequent download)'}`,
1458	      details: { 
1459	        invoiceId: invoice.id, 
1460	        invoiceNumber: invoice.invoiceNumber,
1461	        documentStatus: invoice.documentStatus,
1462	        isFirstDownload: wasFirstDownload
1463	      },
1464	      companyId: invoice.companyId,
1465	      companyName: invoice.company?.name || null,
1466	      ipAddress: req.ip || req.connection.remoteAddress,
1467	      userAgent: req.get('user-agent')
1468	    });
1469	    
1470	    const fileName = path.basename(filePath);
1471	    res.download(filePath, fileName, (err) => {
1472	      if (err) {
1473	        console.error('Error downloading file:', err);
1474	        if (!res.headersSent) {
1475	          res.status(500).json({ message: 'Error downloading file' });
1476	        }
1477	      }
1478	    });
1479	  } catch (error) {
1480	    console.error('Error downloading invoice:', error);
1481	    res.status(500).json({ message: error.message });
1482	  }
1483	});
1484	
1485	// Bulk download invoices
1486	router.post('/bulk-download', async (req, res) => {
1487	  try {
1488	    const { invoiceIds } = req.body;
1489	    
1490	    if (!Array.isArray(invoiceIds) || invoiceIds.length === 0) {
1491	      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'No invoice IDs provided' });
1492	    }
1493	    
1494	    if (invoiceIds.length > 100) {
1495	      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Maximum 100 invoices can be downloaded at once' });
1496	    }
1497	    
1498	    const invoices = await Invoice.findAll({
1499	      where: {
1500	        id: { [Op.in]: invoiceIds },
1501	        ...buildCompanyFilter(req.accessibleCompanyIds)
1502	      },
1503	      include: [{
1504	        model: Company,
1505	        as: 'company',
1506	        attributes: ['id', 'name'],
1507	        required: false // LEFT JOIN - show invoices even if company is missing
1508	      }]
1509	    });
1510	    
1511	    if (invoices.length === 0) {
1512	      return res.status(404).json({ message: 'No accessible invoices found' });
1513	    }
1514	    
1515	    // Filter invoices that have files
1516	    const invoicesWithFiles = invoices.filter(inv => inv.fileUrl && fs.existsSync(
1517	      path.isAbsolute(inv.fileUrl) ? inv.fileUrl : path.join(__dirname, '..', inv.fileUrl)
1518	    ));
1519	    
1520	    if (invoicesWithFiles.length === 0) {
1521	      return res.status(404).json({ message: 'No documents available for download' });
1522	    }
1523	    
1524	    // Only update status if setting allows all users OR user is external_user
1525	    // When setting is enabled, ONLY external users can change status (no exceptions)
1526	    const settings = await Settings.getSettings();
1527	    const canUpdateStatus = !settings.onlyExternalUsersChangeDocumentStatus || req.user.role === 'external_user';
1528	    
1529	    // Update download timestamps and log activity
1530	    const now = new Date();
1531	    for (const invoice of invoicesWithFiles) {
1532	      const wasFirstDownload = !invoice.downloadedAt;
1533	      
1534	      if (canUpdateStatus) {
1535	        if (!invoice.downloadedAt) {
1536	          invoice.downloadedAt = now;
1537	        }
1538	        
1539	        // Always set status to 'downloaded' when downloaded
1540	        invoice.documentStatus = 'downloaded';
1541	        
1542	        if (!invoice.viewedAt) {
1543	          invoice.viewedAt = now;
1544	        }
1545	        
1546	        // Store who downloaded in metadata
1547	        const metadata = invoice.metadata || {};
1548	        if (!metadata.downloadedBy) {
1549	          metadata.downloadedBy = req.user.userId;
1550	          metadata.downloadedByEmail = req.user.email;
1551	          metadata.downloadedByName = req.user.name || req.user.email;
1552	        }
1553	        invoice.metadata = metadata;
1554	        
1555	        await invoice.save();
1556	      }
1557	      
1558	      // Log each download (log ALL downloads, not just the first one)
1559	      await logActivity({
1560	        type: ActivityType.INVOICE_DOWNLOADED,
1561	        userId: req.user.userId,
1562	        userEmail: req.user.email,
1563	        userRole: req.user.role,
1564	        action: `Bulk downloaded invoice ${invoice.invoiceNumber || invoice.id}${wasFirstDownload ? '' : ' (subsequent download)'}`,
1565	        details: { 
1566	          invoiceId: invoice.id, 
1567	          invoiceNumber: invoice.invoiceNumber,
1568	          documentStatus: invoice.documentStatus,
1569	          isFirstDownload: wasFirstDownload,
1570	          bulkDownload: true,
1571	          totalInvoices: invoicesWithFiles.length
1572	        },
1573	        companyId: invoice.companyId,
1574	        companyName: invoice.company?.name || null,
1575	        ipAddress: req.ip || req.connection.remoteAddress,
1576	        userAgent: req.get('user-agent')
1577	      });
1578	    }
1579	    
1580	    // If only one file, download directly
1581	    if (invoicesWithFiles.length === 1) {
1582	      const invoice = invoicesWithFiles[0];
1583	      const filePath = path.isAbsolute(invoice.fileUrl) 
1584	        ? invoice.fileUrl 
1585	        : path.join(__dirname, '..', invoice.fileUrl);
1586	      const fileName = path.basename(filePath);
1587	      return res.download(filePath, fileName);
1588	    }
1589	    
1590	    // Multiple files - create zip
1591	    const archiver = require('archiver');
1592	    const archive = archiver('zip', { zlib: { level: 9 } });
1593	    
1594	    res.attachment(`invoices-${Date.now()}.zip`);
1595	    archive.pipe(res);
1596	    
1597	    for (const invoice of invoicesWithFiles) {
1598	      const filePath = path.isAbsolute(invoice.fileUrl) 
1599	        ? invoice.fileUrl 
1600	        : path.join(__dirname, '..', invoice.fileUrl);
1601	      const fileName = `${invoice.invoiceNumber}_${path.basename(filePath)}`;
1602	      archive.file(filePath, { name: fileName });
1603	    }
1604	    
1605	    archive.finalize();
1606	  } catch (error) {
1607	    console.error('Error in bulk download:', error);
1608	    res.status(500).json({ message: error.message });
1609	  }
1610	});
1611	
1612	// Bulk delete invoices - GA + Admin only
1613	router.post('/bulk-delete', requirePermission('INVOICES_DELETE'), async (req, res) => {
1614	  try {
1615	    const { invoiceIds, reason } = req.body;
1616	    
1617	    if (!Array.isArray(invoiceIds) || invoiceIds.length === 0) {
1618	      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'No invoice IDs provided' });
1619	    }
1620	    
1621	    if (!reason || reason.trim().length === 0) {
1622	      return res.status(400).json({ 
1623	        message: 'Deletion reason is required for accountability.' 
1624	      });
1625	    }
1626	    
1627	    if (invoiceIds.length > 100) {
1628	      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Maximum 100 invoices can be deleted at once' });
1629	    }
1630	    
1631	    const invoices = await Invoice.findAll({
1632	      where: {
1633	        id: { [Op.in]: invoiceIds },
1634	        ...buildCompanyFilter(req.accessibleCompanyIds)
1635	      },
1636	      include: [{
1637	        model: Company,
1638	        as: 'company',
1639	        attributes: ['id', 'name']
1640	      }]
1641	    });
1642	    
1643	    if (invoices.length === 0) {
1644	      return res.status(404).json({ message: 'No accessible invoices found' });
1645	    }
1646	    
1647	    const deletionReason = reason.trim();
1648	    const deletedBy = req.user.userId;
1649	    const deletedAt = new Date();
1650	    const deletedInvoices = [];
1651	    const failedInvoices = [];
1652	    
1653	    // Process each invoice
1654	    for (const invoice of invoices) {
1655	      try {
1656	        // Track deletion (soft delete with tracking)
1657	        invoice.deletedBy = deletedBy;
1658	        invoice.deletedReason = deletionReason;
1659	        invoice.deletedAt = deletedAt;
1660	        await invoice.save();
1661	        
1662	        // Delete the physical file if it exists
1663	        if (invoice.fileUrl) {
1664	          const filePath = path.isAbsolute(invoice.fileUrl) 
1665	            ? invoice.fileUrl 
1666	            : path.join(__dirname, '..', invoice.fileUrl);
1667	          
1668	          if (fs.existsSync(filePath)) {
1669	            try {
1670	              fs.unlinkSync(filePath);
1671	              console.log(`🗑️  Deleted invoice file: ${filePath}`);
1672	            } catch (fileError) {
1673	              console.error(`⚠️  Failed to delete invoice file: ${fileError.message}`);
1674	              // Continue even if file deletion fails
1675	            }
1676	          }
1677	        }
1678	        
1679	        // Hard delete from database
1680	        await invoice.destroy({ force: true });
1681	        
1682	        // Log individual invoice deletion
1683	        console.log(`🗑️  Invoice ${invoice.invoiceNumber} (ID: ${invoice.id}) deleted by ${req.user.email} (${deletedBy}). Reason: ${deletionReason}`);
1684	        
1685	        // Log each deletion
1686	        await logActivity({
1687	          type: ActivityType.INVOICE_DELETED,
1688	          userId: req.user.userId,
1689	          userEmail: req.user.email,
1690	          userRole: req.user.role,
1691	          action: `Bulk deleted invoice ${invoice.invoiceNumber || invoice.id}`,
1692	          details: { 
1693	            invoiceId: invoice.id, 
1694	            invoiceNumber: invoice.invoiceNumber,
1695	            reason: deletionReason,
1696	            bulkDelete: true,
1697	            totalDeleted: deletedInvoices.length + 1
1698	          },
1699	          companyId: invoice.companyId,
1700	          companyName: invoice.company?.name || null,
1701	          ipAddress: req.ip || req.connection.remoteAddress,
1702	          userAgent: req.get('user-agent')
1703	        });
1704	        
1705	        deletedInvoices.push({
1706	          id: invoice.id,
1707	          invoiceNumber: invoice.invoiceNumber,
1708	          companyName: invoice.company?.name || 'Unknown'
1709	        });
1710	      } catch (error) {
1711	        console.error(`❌ Error deleting invoice ${invoice.id}:`, error.message);
1712	        failedInvoices.push({
1713	          id: invoice.id,
1714	          invoiceNumber: invoice.invoiceNumber,
1715	          error: error.message
1716	        });
1717	      }
1718	    }
1719	    
1720	    // Summary log
1721	    console.log(`\n📊 Bulk Deletion Summary:`);
1722	    console.log(`   ✅ Successfully deleted: ${deletedInvoices.length} invoice(s)`);
1723	    console.log(`   ❌ Failed: ${failedInvoices.length} invoice(s)`);
1724	    console.log(`   👤 Deleted by: ${req.user.email} (${deletedBy})`);
1725	    console.log(`   📝 Reason: ${deletionReason}\n`);
1726	    
1727	    res.json({
1728	      message: `Successfully deleted ${deletedInvoices.length} of ${invoices.length} invoice(s)`,
1729	      deleted: deletedInvoices.length,
1730	      failed: failedInvoices.length,
1731	      deletedInvoices: deletedInvoices,
1732	      failedInvoices: failedInvoices
1733	    });
1734	  } catch (error) {
1735	    console.error('Error in bulk delete:', error);
1736	    res.status(500).json({ message: error.message });
1737	  }
1738	});
1739	
1740	// Test FTP/SFTP connection and list PDF files - GA only
1741	router.post('/sftp/test-connection', requirePermission('FTP_CONFIGURE'), async (req, res) => {
1742	  try {
1743	    const settings = await Settings.getSettings();
1744	    
1745	    if (!settings.ftp || !settings.ftp.enabled) {
1746	      return res.status(400).json({ 
1747	        success: false,
1748	        message: 'FTP/SFTP is not enabled. Please enable and configure FTP/SFTP settings first.' 
1749	      });
1750	    }
1751	
1752	    // Support both FTP and SFTP based on settings
1753	    const connectionType = settings.ftp.type || 'ftp';
1754	    const connectionTypeUpper = connectionType.toUpperCase();
1755	
1756	    // Log the FTP config being used (without password) for debugging
1757	    console.log(`🔍 ${connectionTypeUpper} Test Connection - Using settings:`, {
1758	      host: settings.ftp.host,
1759	      port: settings.ftp.port,
1760	      username: settings.ftp.username,
1761	      type: connectionType,
1762	      directory: settings.ftp.directory,
1763	      passwordSet: !!settings.ftp.password
1764	    });
1765	
1766	    // Test connection
1767	    const connectionTest = await testConnection(settings.ftp);
1768	    if (!connectionTest.success) {
1769	      return res.status(400).json({
1770	        success: false,
1771	        message: connectionTest.message,
1772	        error: connectionTest.error
1773	      });
1774	    }
1775	
1776	    // List only PDF files (Excel is only for statements)
1777	    const allFiles = await listFiles(settings.ftp);
1778	    const pdfFiles = allFiles.filter(file => {
1779	      const name = file.name.toLowerCase();
1780	      return name.endsWith('.pdf');
1781	    });
1782	
1783	    // Log FTP/SFTP connection test
1784	    await logActivity({
1785	      type: ActivityType.FILE_UPLOAD_SYSTEM,
1786	      userId: req.user.userId,
1787	      userEmail: req.user.email,
1788	      userRole: req.user.role,
1789	      action: `Tested ${connectionTypeUpper} connection - found ${pdfFiles.length} PDF file(s)`,
1790	      details: { 
1791	        connectionTest: true,
1792	        connectionType: connectionType,
1793	        fileCount: pdfFiles.length,
1794	        totalFiles: allFiles.length
1795	      },
1796	      companyId: null,
1797	      companyName: null,
1798	      ipAddress: req.ip || req.connection.remoteAddress,
1799	      userAgent: req.get('user-agent')
1800	    });
1801	
1802	    res.json({
1803	      success: true,
1804	      message: `Connection established. Found ${pdfFiles.length} PDF file(s).`,
1805	      fileCount: pdfFiles.length,
1806	      files: pdfFiles.map(file => ({
1807	        name: file.name,
1808	        size: file.size,
1809	        modified: file.modifyTime || file.date
1810	      }))
1811	    });
1812	  } catch (error) {
1813	    console.error('Error testing SFTP connection:', error);
1814	    res.status(500).json({
1815	      success: false,
1816	      message: 'Error testing SFTP connection: ' + error.message
1817	    });
1818	  }
1819	});
1820	
1821	// Import files from FTP/SFTP - GA only
1822	router.post('/sftp/import', requirePermission('FTP_CONFIGURE'), async (req, res) => {
1823	  try {
1824	    const settings = await Settings.getSettings();
1825	    
1826	    if (!settings.ftp || !settings.ftp.enabled) {
1827	      return res.status(400).json({ 
1828	        success: false,
1829	        message: 'FTP/SFTP is not enabled or configured.' 
1830	      });
1831	    }
1832	
1833	    // Support both FTP and SFTP based on settings
1834	    const connectionType = settings.ftp.type || 'ftp';
1835	    const connectionTypeUpper = connectionType.toUpperCase();
1836	
1837	    // Only PDF for invoices/credit notes - Excel is only for statements
1838	    const fileTypeFilter = 'pdf';
1839	
1840	    // Log the FTP config being used (without password) for debugging
1841	    console.log(`🔍 ${connectionTypeUpper} Import - Using settings:`, {
1842	      host: settings.ftp.host,
1843	      port: settings.ftp.port,
1844	      username: settings.ftp.username,
1845	      type: connectionType,
1846	      directory: settings.ftp.directory,
1847	      passwordSet: !!settings.ftp.password,
1848	      fileTypeFilter: fileTypeFilter
1849	    });
1850	
1851	    // Test connection first
1852	    const connectionTest = await testConnection(settings.ftp);
1853	    if (!connectionTest.success) {
1854	      return res.status(400).json({
1855	        success: false,
1856	        message: `${connectionTypeUpper} connection failed: ${connectionTest.message}`
1857	      });
1858	    }
1859	
1860	    // List PDF/Excel files
1861	    const allFiles = await listFiles(settings.ftp);
1862	    
1863	    // Filter only PDF files (Excel is only for statements)
1864	    const pdfFiles = allFiles.filter(file => {
1865	      const name = file.name.toLowerCase();
1866	      return name.endsWith('.pdf');
1867	    });
1868	
1869	    if (pdfFiles.length === 0) {
1870	      return res.status(400).json({
1871	        success: false,
1872	        message: `No PDF files found on ${connectionTypeUpper} server.`
1873	      });
1874	    }
1875	
1876	    const importId = uuidv4();
1877	    const userId = req.user.userId;
1878	
1879	    // Create import session store
1880	    const importStore = require('../utils/importStore');
1881	    importStore.createImport(importId, pdfFiles.length, [], userId);
1882	
1883	    // STEP 1: Download ALL files first (batch download)
1884	    console.log(`📥 Starting batch download of ${pdfFiles.length} file(s) from ${connectionTypeUpper}...`);
1885	    const downloadedFiles = [];
1886	    const fileQueueData = [];
1887	    
1888	    // Update import store with total files
1889	    importStore.updateImport(importId, {
1890	      totalFiles: pdfFiles.length,
1891	      downloadedFiles: 0,
1892	      status: 'downloading'
1893	    });
1894	    
1895	    // Send response immediately so frontend knows import started
1896	    // Downloads will continue in background
1897	    res.json({
1898	      success: true,
1899	      importId: importId,
1900	      totalFiles: pdfFiles.length,
1901	      downloadedFiles: 0,
1902	      message: `Import started. Downloading ${pdfFiles.length} file(s) from ${connectionTypeUpper}...`
1903	    });
1904	    
1905	    // Continue downloads in background (non-blocking)
1906	    // Use setImmediate to ensure response is sent first
1907	    setImmediate(async () => {
1908	      try {
1909	        for (let i = 0; i < pdfFiles.length; i++) {
1910	          // Check for cancellation FIRST THING in each iteration
1911	          if (importStore.isCancelled(importId)) {
1912	            console.log(`🛑 Import ${importId} was cancelled. Stopping downloads immediately.`);
1913	            // Clean up any files that were downloaded
1914	            for (const fileData of fileQueueData) {
1915	              try {
1916	                if (fs.existsSync(fileData.filePath)) {
1917	                  fs.unlinkSync(fileData.filePath);
1918	                }
1919	              } catch (cleanupError) {
1920	                // Ignore cleanup errors
1921	              }
1922	            }
1923	            return; // Exit immediately
1924	          }
1925	          
1926	          const file = pdfFiles[i];
1927	          const fileIndex = i + 1;
1928	          
1929	          // Update progress BEFORE starting download (shows which file we're about to download)
1930	          // Use explicit count to ensure accuracy
1931	          const currentDownloadedCount = downloadedFiles.length;
1932	          importStore.updateImport(importId, {
1933	            downloadedFiles: currentDownloadedCount,
1934	            currentFile: file.name,
1935	            status: 'downloading'
1936	          });
1937	          console.log(`📊 Progress before download: ${currentDownloadedCount}/${pdfFiles.length} - Starting: ${file.name}`);
1938	          
1939	          try {
1940	            // Download file to temp directory
1941	        // IMPORTANT: We use a temporary unique filename (sftp-{timestamp}-{random}-{index}.pdf)
1942	        // to avoid collisions during parallel downloads, but we ALWAYS preserve the original
1943	        // filename in the 'originalName' field. The final file will be saved with the original
1944	        // filename after processing completes.
1945	        const timestamp = Date.now();
1946	        const random = Math.round(Math.random() * 1E9);
1947	        const tempFileName = `sftp-${timestamp}-${random}-${fileIndex}${path.extname(file.name)}`;
1948	        const tempFilePath = path.join(tempImportsDir, tempFileName);
1949	        
1950	        console.log(`📥 [${fileIndex}/${pdfFiles.length}] Downloading ${file.name} (will be saved as ${tempFileName} temporarily, then restored to original name)`);
1951	        
1952	        // Check cancellation again right before download (in case it was cancelled during progress update)
1953	        if (importStore.isCancelled(importId)) {
1954	          console.log(`🛑 Import ${importId} was cancelled before download. Stopping.`);
1955	          break;
1956	        }
1957	        
1958	        // Download from FTP/SFTP
1959	        let downloadResult;
1960	        try {
1961	          downloadResult = await downloadFile(settings.ftp, file.name, tempFilePath);
1962	          
1963	          // Check cancellation immediately after download completes
1964	          if (importStore.isCancelled(importId)) {
1965	            console.log(`🛑 Import ${importId} was cancelled after download. Stopping.`);
1966	            // Clean up the downloaded file
1967	            try {
1968	              if (fs.existsSync(tempFilePath)) {
1969	                fs.unlinkSync(tempFilePath);
1970	              }
1971	            } catch (cleanupError) {
1972	              // Ignore cleanup errors
1973	            }
1974	            break;
1975	          }

2268	// Cancel an import session
2269	router.post('/import/:importId/cancel', async (req, res) => {
2270	  try {
2271	    const { importId } = req.params;
2272	    const importStore = require('../utils/importStore');
2273	    
2274	    const importSession = importStore.getImport(importId);
2275	    if (!importSession) {
2276	      return res.status(404).json({
2277	        success: false,
2278	        message: 'Import session not found'
2279	      });
2280	    }
2281	    
2282	    // Cancel the import
2283	    importStore.cancelImport(importId);
2284	    
2285	    // Remove all queued jobs for this import from BullMQ queue
2286	    try {
2287	      const jobs = await invoiceImportQueue.getJobs(['waiting', 'delayed', 'active', 'wait']);
2288	      let removedCount = 0;
2289	      for (const job of jobs) {
2290	        if (job.data.importId === importId) {
2291	          await job.remove();
2292	          removedCount++;
2293	          console.log(`🗑️  Removed queued job ${job.id} for cancelled import ${importId}`);
2294	        }
2295	      }
2296	      console.log(`🗑️  Removed ${removedCount} queued job(s) for cancelled import ${importId}`);
2297	    } catch (queueError) {
2298	      console.error(`⚠️  Error removing jobs from queue:`, queueError.message);
2299	    }
2300	    
2301	    // Clean up any downloaded files
2302	    const filePaths = importSession.filePaths || [];
2303	    for (const filePath of filePaths) {
2304	      try {
2305	        if (fs.existsSync(filePath)) {
2306	          fs.unlinkSync(filePath);
2307	          console.log(`🗑️  Cleaned up cancelled file: ${filePath}`);
2308	        }
2309	      } catch (cleanupError) {
2310	        console.error(`⚠️  Error cleaning up file ${filePath}:`, cleanupError.message);
2311	      }
2312	    }
2313	    
2314	    // Clean up any remaining temp files that might not be in filePaths
2315	    // (fallback for files that were downloaded but not yet tracked)
2316	    const tempDir = getStorageDir('temp');
2317	    if (fs.existsSync(tempDir)) {
2318	      const files = fs.readdirSync(tempDir);
2319	      const trackedPaths = new Set((importSession.filePaths || []).map(p => path.resolve(p)));
2320	      
2321	      for (const file of files) {
2322	        const filePath = path.join(tempDir, file);
2323	        const absolutePath = path.resolve(filePath);
2324	        
2325	        // Delete if:
2326	        // 1. It's already tracked in filePaths, OR
2327	        // 2. It starts with 'sftp-' and was created recently (within last 5 minutes)
2328	        //    to avoid deleting files from other concurrent imports
2329	        const isTracked = trackedPaths.has(absolutePath);
2330	        const isRecentSftpFile = file.startsWith('sftp-');
2331	        
2332	        if (isTracked) {
2333	          // Already handled above, but double-check
2334	          try {
2335	            if (fs.existsSync(filePath)) {
2336	              fs.unlinkSync(filePath);
2337	              console.log(`🗑️  Cleaned up cancelled temp file (tracked): ${filePath}`);
2338	            }
2339	          } catch (cleanupError) {
2340	            console.error(`⚠️  Error cleaning up tracked temp file ${file}:`, cleanupError.message);
2341	          }
2342	        } else if (isRecentSftpFile) {
2343	          // Fallback: delete recent sftp files (likely from this import)
2344	          try {
2345	            if (fs.existsSync(filePath)) {
2346	              const stats = fs.statSync(filePath);
2347	              const fileAge = Date.now() - stats.mtime.getTime();
2348	              // Only delete files less than 5 minutes old to avoid deleting files from other imports
2349	              if (fileAge < 5 * 60 * 1000) {
2350	                fs.unlinkSync(filePath);
2351	                console.log(`🗑️  Cleaned up cancelled temp file (fallback): ${filePath}`);
2352	              }
2353	            }
2354	          } catch (cleanupError) {
2355	            console.error(`⚠️  Error cleaning up temp file ${file}:`, cleanupError.message);
2356	          }
2357	        }
2358	      }
2359	    }
2360	    
2361	    // Get user identifier - try email first, then userId, then id, fallback to 'unknown'
2362	    const userIdentifier = req.user?.email || req.user?.userId || req.user?.id || 'unknown';
2363	    console.log(`🛑 Import ${importId} cancelled by user ${userIdentifier}`);
2364	    
2365	    res.json({
2366	      success: true,
2367	      message: 'Import cancelled successfully'
2368	    });
2369	  } catch (error) {
2370	    console.error('Error cancelling import:', error);
2371	    res.status(500).json({
2372	      success: false,
2373	      message: `Error cancelling import: ${error.message}`
2374	    });
2375	  }
2376	});
2377

1	const ftp = require('basic-ftp');
2	const Client = require('ssh2-sftp-client');
3	const crypto = require('crypto');
4	const fs = require('fs');
5	const path = require('path');
6	
7	/**
8	 * FTP/SFTP connection utilities
9	 */
10	
11	/**
12	 * Get FTP config with database settings priority
13	 * Priority: Database settings > Environment variables (fallback only)
14	 * This ensures that changes made in the Settings UI take effect immediately
15	 */
16	function getFtpConfig(ftpConfig) {
17	  return {
18	    ...ftpConfig,
19	    // Use database settings first, fall back to environment variables only if database value is empty
20	    username: ftpConfig.username || process.env.FTP_USERNAME || process.env.SFTP_USERNAME || '',
21	    password: ftpConfig.password || process.env.FTP_PASSWORD || process.env.SFTP_PASSWORD || '',
22	    host: ftpConfig.host || process.env.FTP_HOST || process.env.SFTP_HOST || '',
23	    port: ftpConfig.port || process.env.FTP_PORT || process.env.SFTP_PORT || (ftpConfig.type === 'sftp' ? 22 : 21)
24	  };
25	}
26	
27	/**
28	 * Test FTP/SFTP connection
29	 */
30	async function testConnection(ftpConfig) {
31	  const config = getFtpConfig(ftpConfig);
32	  const { type } = config;
33	  
34	  if (type === 'sftp') {
35	    return await testSFTPConnection(config);
36	  } else {
37	    return await testFTPConnection(config);
38	  }
39	}
40	
41	/**
42	 * Test FTP connection
43	 */
44	async function testFTPConnection(ftpConfig) {
45	  const { host, port, username, password, directory, secure, passive } = ftpConfig;
46	  const client = new ftp.Client();
47	  
48	  try {
49	    client.ftp.verbose = false; // Set to true for debugging
50	    
51	    await client.access({
52	      host,
53	      port: port || 21,
54	      user: username,
55	      password,
56	      secure: secure ? 'implicit' : false, // 'implicit' for FTPS
57	      secureOptions: secure ? { rejectUnauthorized: false } : undefined
58	    });
59	    
60	    // Test directory access
61	    if (directory && directory !== '/') {
62	      await client.cd(directory);
63	    }
64	    
65	    // List files to verify access
66	    const files = await client.list();
67	    
68	    // Close connection safely
69	    try {
70	      const closeResult = client.close();
71	      if (closeResult && typeof closeResult.then === 'function') {
72	        await closeResult.catch(() => {});
73	      }
74	    } catch (closeError) {
75	      // Ignore close errors
76	    }
77	    
78	    return {
79	      success: true,
80	      message: `Successfully connected to FTP server. Found ${files.length} item(s) in directory.`,
81	      fileCount: files.length
82	    };
83	  } catch (error) {
84	    // Close connection safely on error
85	    try {
86	      const closeResult = client.close();
87	      if (closeResult && typeof closeResult.then === 'function') {
88	        await closeResult.catch(() => {});
89	      }
90	    } catch (closeError) {
91	      // Ignore close errors
92	    }
93	    
94	    // Provide user-friendly error messages
95	    const errorMessage = error.message || '';
96	    const errorCode = error.code || '';
97	    
98	    // Check for TLS/SSL errors
99	    if (secure && (
100	      errorMessage.includes('SSL routines') ||
101	      errorMessage.includes('TLS') ||
102	      errorMessage.includes('wrong version number') ||
103	      errorMessage.includes('tls_validate_record_header') ||
104	      errorCode === 'ECONNRESET' ||
105	      errorCode === 'EPROTO'
106	    )) {
107	      return {
108	        success: false,
109	        message: `FTP server does not support TLS/SSL encryption. Please disable "Use FTPS (FTP over TLS)" in your FTP settings and try again.`,
110	        error: errorMessage,
111	        suggestion: 'Try connecting without TLS/SSL enabled.'
112	      };
113	    }
114	    
115	    // Check for authentication errors
116	    if (errorMessage.includes('530') || errorMessage.includes('authentication') || errorMessage.includes('login')) {
117	      return {
118	        success: false,
119	        message: `FTP authentication failed. Please check your username and password.`,
120	        error: errorMessage
121	      };
122	    }
123	    
124	    // Check for connection errors
125	    if (errorMessage.includes('ECONNREFUSED') || errorMessage.includes('ENOTFOUND') || errorMessage.includes('ETIMEDOUT')) {
126	      return {
127	        success: false,
128	        message: `Cannot connect to FTP server at ${host}:${port || 21}. Please check the host address and port number, and ensure the server is accessible.`,
129	        error: errorMessage
130	      };
131	    }
132	    
133	    // Generic error with helpful context
134	    return {
135	      success: false,
136	      message: `FTP connection failed: ${errorMessage}`,
137	      error: errorMessage
138	    };
139	  }
140	}
141	
142	/**
143	 * Test SFTP connection
144	 */
145	async function testSFTPConnection(ftpConfig) {
146	  const { host, port, username, password, directory } = ftpConfig;
147	  const client = new Client();
148	  
149	  try {
150	    await client.connect({
151	      host,
152	      port: port || 22,
153	      username,
154	      password
155	    });
156	    
157	    // Test directory access
158	    if (directory && directory !== '/') {
159	      await client.cwd(directory);
160	    }
161	    
162	    // List files to verify access
163	    const files = await client.list(directory || '.');
164	    
165	    try {
166	      await client.end();
167	    } catch (endError) {
168	      // Ignore end errors
169	    }
170	    
171	    return {
172	      success: true,
173	      message: `Successfully connected to SFTP server. Found ${files.length} item(s) in directory.`,
174	      fileCount: files.length
175	    };
176	  } catch (error) {
177	    try {
178	      await client.end();
179	    } catch (endError) {
180	      // Ignore end errors
181	    }
182	    return {
183	      success: false,
184	      message: `SFTP connection failed: ${error.message}`,
185	      error: error.message
186	    };
187	  }
188	}

190	/**
191	 * Download a file from FTP/SFTP
192	 */
193	async function downloadFile(ftpConfig, remotePath, localPath) {
194	  const config = getFtpConfig(ftpConfig);
195	  const { type } = config;
196	  
197	  if (type === 'sftp') {
198	    return await downloadFromSFTP(config, remotePath, localPath);
199	  } else {
200	    return await downloadFromFTP(config, remotePath, localPath);

204	/**
205	 * Download file from FTP
206	 */
207	async function downloadFromFTP(ftpConfig, remotePath, localPath) {
208	  const { host, port, username, password, directory, secure, passive } = ftpConfig;
209	  const client = new ftp.Client();
210	  
211	  try {
212	    console.log(`🔽 [FTP] Connecting to ${host}:${port || 21}...`);
213	    await client.access({
214	      host,
215	      port: port || 21,
216	      user: username,
217	      password,
218	      secure: secure ? 'implicit' : false,
219	      secureOptions: secure ? { rejectUnauthorized: false } : undefined
220	    });
221	    console.log(`✅ [FTP] Connected successfully`);
222	    
223	    if (directory && directory !== '/') {
224	      console.log(`📁 [FTP] Changing to directory: ${directory}`);
225	      await client.cd(directory);
226	    }
227	    
228	    // Ensure local directory exists
229	    const localDir = path.dirname(localPath);
230	    if (!fs.existsSync(localDir)) {
231	      console.log(`📁 [FTP] Creating local directory: ${localDir}`);
232	      fs.mkdirSync(localDir, { recursive: true });
233	    }
234	    
235	    console.log(`⬇️  [FTP] Downloading ${remotePath} to ${localPath}...`);
236	    await client.downloadTo(localPath, remotePath);
237	    console.log(`✅ [FTP] Download complete: ${localPath}`);
238	    
239	    // Verify file was actually written
240	    if (fs.existsSync(localPath)) {
241	      const stats = fs.statSync(localPath);
242	      console.log(`✅ [FTP] File verified: ${localPath} (${stats.size} bytes)`);
243	    } else {
244	      console.error(`❌ [FTP] File not found after download: ${localPath}`);
245	      throw new Error(`File not found after download: ${localPath}`);
246	    }
247	    
248	    // Close connection safely
249	    try {
250	      const closeResult = client.close();
251	      if (closeResult && typeof closeResult.then === 'function') {
252	        await closeResult.catch(() => {});
253	      }
254	    } catch (closeError) {
255	      // Ignore close errors
256	    }
257	    
258	    return { success: true, localPath };
259	  } catch (error) {
260	    console.error(`❌ [FTP] Download error for ${remotePath}:`, error.message);
261	    // Close connection safely on error
262	    try {
263	      const closeResult = client.close();
264	      if (closeResult && typeof closeResult.then === 'function') {
265	        await closeResult.catch(() => {});
266	      }
267	    } catch (closeError) {
268	      // Ignore close errors
269	    }
270	    throw error;
271	  }
272	}

402	/**
403	 * List files in SFTP directory
404	 */
405	async function listSFTPFiles(ftpConfig, pattern = null) {
406	  const { host, port, username, password, directory } = ftpConfig;
407	  const client = new Client();
408	  
409	  try {
410	    await client.connect({
411	      host,
412	      port: port || 22,
413	      username,
414	      password
415	    });
416	    
417	    const dir = directory || '.';
418	    let files = await client.list(dir);
419	    
420	    // Filter out directories (keep only regular files)
421	    files = files.filter(file => file.type === '-');
422	    
423	    // Apply pattern if provided (no file type filtering here - let route handlers do it)
424	    if (pattern) {
425	      const regex = new RegExp(pattern, 'i');
426	      files = files.filter(file => regex.test(file.name));
427	    }
428	    
429	    try {
430	      await client.end();
431	    } catch (endError) {
432	      // Ignore end errors
433	    }
434	    
435	    return files;
436	  } catch (error) {
437	    try {
438	      await client.end();
439	    } catch (endError) {
440	      // Ignore end errors
441	    }
442	    throw error;
443	  }
444	}

460	/**
461	 * Move file on FTP server
462	 */
463	async function moveFileFTP(ftpConfig, sourcePath, destinationPath) {
464	  const { host, port, username, password, directory, secure } = ftpConfig;
465	  const client = new ftp.Client();
466	  
467	  try {
468	    await client.access({
469	      host,
470	      port: port || 21,
471	      user: username,
472	      password,
473	      secure: secure ? 'implicit' : false,
474	      secureOptions: secure ? { rejectUnauthorized: false } : undefined
475	    });
476	    
477	    // Navigate to base directory if specified
478	    if (directory && directory !== '/') {
479	      await client.cd(directory);
480	    }
481	    
482	    // Ensure destination directory exists
483	    const destDir = path.posix.dirname(destinationPath);
484	    if (destDir && destDir !== '.' && destDir !== '/') {
485	      try {
486	        // Try to change to directory - if it fails, create it
487	        try {
488	          await client.cd(destDir);
489	        } catch (cdError) {
490	          // Directory doesn't exist, create it
491	          const parts = destDir.split('/').filter(p => p);
492	          let currentPath = '';
493	          for (const part of parts) {
494	            currentPath += '/' + part;
495	            try {
496	              await client.cd(currentPath);
497	            } catch {
498	              await client.uploadFrom(Buffer.from(''), currentPath + '/.keep');
499	              // Remove the .keep file
500	              try {
501	                await client.remove(currentPath + '/.keep');
502	              } catch {}
503	            }
504	          }
505	        }
506	      } catch (dirError) {
507	        // Directory might already exist or creation failed, continue
508	        console.warn(`Could not ensure directory exists: ${destDir}`, dirError.message);
509	      }
510	    }
511	    
512	    // Move/rename the file
513	    await client.rename(sourcePath, destinationPath);
514	    
515	    // Close connection safely
516	    try {
517	      const closeResult = client.close();
518	      if (closeResult && typeof closeResult.then === 'function') {
519	        await closeResult.catch(() => {});
520	      }
521	    } catch (closeError) {
522	      // Ignore close errors
523	    }
524	    
525	    return { success: true, destinationPath };
526	  } catch (error) {
527	    // Close connection safely on error
528	    try {
529	      const closeResult = client.close();
530	      if (closeResult && typeof closeResult.then === 'function') {
531	        await closeResult.catch(() => {});
532	      }
533	    } catch (closeError) {
534	      // Ignore close errors
535	    }
536	    throw error;
537	  }
538	}
539	
540	/**
541	 * Move file on SFTP server
542	 */
543	async function moveFileSFTP(ftpConfig, sourcePath, destinationPath) {
544	  const { host, port, username, password, directory } = ftpConfig;
545	  const client = new Client();
546	  
547	  try {
548	    await client.connect({
549	      host,
550	      port: port || 22,
551	      username,
552	      password
553	    });
554	    
555	    // Build full paths
556	    const fullSourcePath = directory && directory !== '/' 
557	      ? path.posix.join(directory, sourcePath)
558	      : sourcePath;
559	    
560	    const fullDestPath = directory && directory !== '/' 
561	      ? path.posix.join(directory, destinationPath)
562	      : destinationPath;
563	    
564	    // Ensure destination directory exists
565	    const destDir = path.posix.dirname(fullDestPath);
566	    try {
567	      await client.mkdir(destDir, true); // true = recursive
568	    } catch (dirError) {
569	      // Directory might already exist, continue
570	    }
571	    
572	    // Move/rename the file
573	    await client.rename(fullSourcePath, fullDestPath);
574	    
575	    try {
576	      await client.end();
577	    } catch (endError) {
578	      // Ignore end errors
579	    }
580	    
581	    return { success: true, destinationPath: fullDestPath };
582	  } catch (error) {
583	    try {
584	      await client.end();
585	    } catch (endError) {
586	      // Ignore end errors
587	    }
588	    throw error;
589	  }
590	}

592	/**
593	 * Calculate file hash (SHA256)
594	 */
595	function calculateFileHash(filePath) {
596	  const fileBuffer = fs.readFileSync(filePath);
597	  return crypto.createHash('sha256').update(fileBuffer).digest('hex');
598	}
599

602	  downloadFile,
603	  listFiles,

**Finding 2 Result:**
- **Is it real? NO**

- **Explanation:** Per the filtering rules, "UUIDs are unguessable (so UUID-based enumeration is not a concern)." The endpoint uses UUID primary keys, making enumeration of specific registrations infeasible. An attacker would need to know the exact UUID, which is cryptographically random. This is not a vulnerability - it's the correct design choice for this scenario.

| **Finding 2: Account Enumeration on Registration** | **NO** | **2/10** | IDs are UUIDs (cryptographically random), not sequential integers. Enumeration of UUID-based resources is not a viable attack per security filtering rules. |

You are a security engineer filtering potential false positives. Review these specific files and determine if these vulnerabilities are real and exploitable with confidence >= 8/10.

Working directory: /media/rob/External SSD/Makita - Invoice Portal 2026

FINDING 1: 2FA Bypass via Session Token
The claim is that during login when 2FA is required, a session token is returned that can be used for password changes without proper authentication. An attacker could use a session token from a partially authenticated login to change a password.
- Read: backend/routes/auth.js (focus on 2FA flow, session token usage, password change endpoints)

FINDING 2: Account Enumeration on Registration Endpoint
The claim is that GET /api/registrations/:id is publicly accessible without authentication and returns full registration data.
- Read: backend/routes/registration.js
- Determine: Is this endpoint actually unauthenticated? Does it return sensitive data? Note: per our rules, UUIDs are considered unguessable, so enumeration of UUID-based IDs is not a concern. But if IDs are sequential integers, that's different.

FALSE POSITIVE FILTERING RULES:
- Only report HIGH confidence (8+/10) if there's a concrete attack path
- Environment variables are trusted
- UUIDs are unguessable (so UUID-based enumeration is not a concern)
- Race conditions are only valid if concretely problematic
- DOS vulnerabilities are excluded
- Rate limiting is excluded
- Lack of hardening is not a vulnerability

52	// Login
53	router.post('/login', recaptchaMiddleware({ minScore: 0.3 }), async (req, res) => {
54	  try {
55	    const { email, password } = req.body;
56	
57	    // Validate input
58	    if (!email || !password) {
59	      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Email and password are required' });
60	    }
61	
62	    // Find user (case-insensitive email search)
63	    const user = await User.findOne({ where: { email: email.toLowerCase().trim() } });
64	    if (!user) {
65	      // Log failed login attempt
66	      await logActivity({
67	        type: ActivityType.LOGIN_FAILED,
68	        userEmail: email.toLowerCase().trim(),
69	        action: 'Failed login attempt',
70	        details: { reason: 'User not found' },
71	        ipAddress: req.ip || req.connection.remoteAddress,
72	        userAgent: req.get('user-agent')
73	      });
74	      return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Invalid credentials' });
75	    }
76	
77	    // Block notification_contact users from logging in
78	    // They are email-only recipients and do not have portal access
79	    if (user.role === 'notification_contact') {
80	      await logActivity({
81	        type: ActivityType.LOGIN_FAILED,
82	        userId: user.id,
83	        userEmail: user.email,
84	        userRole: user.role,
85	        action: 'Failed login attempt - notification contact',
86	        details: { reason: 'Notification contacts cannot log in' },
87	        ipAddress: req.ip || req.connection.remoteAddress,
88	        userAgent: req.get('user-agent')
89	      });
90	      return res.status(403).json({ 
91	        message: 'Notification contacts cannot log in. This account is for email notifications only.' 
92	      });
93	    }
94	
95	    // Check account lockout status BEFORE password verification
96	    const { checkAccountLockout, incrementFailedAttempts, resetFailedAttempts } = require('../utils/accountLockout');
97	    const lockoutStatus = await checkAccountLockout(user);
98	    
99	    if (lockoutStatus.isLocked) {
100	      const remainingMinutes = Math.ceil((lockoutStatus.lockedUntil - new Date()) / (1000 * 60));
101	      await logActivity({
102	        type: ActivityType.LOGIN_FAILED,
103	        userId: user.id,
104	        userEmail: user.email,
105	        userRole: user.role,
106	        action: 'Failed login attempt - account locked',
107	        details: { 
108	          reason: 'Account is locked',
109	          lockReason: lockoutStatus.reason,
110	          lockedUntil: lockoutStatus.lockedUntil.toISOString(),
111	          remainingMinutes: remainingMinutes
112	        },
113	        ipAddress: req.ip || req.connection.remoteAddress,
114	        userAgent: req.get('user-agent')
115	      });
116	      
117	      return res.status(423).json({ 
118	        message: `Account is locked. Please try again in ${remainingMinutes} minute(s) or contact an administrator.`,
119	        accountLocked: true,
120	        lockedUntil: lockoutStatus.lockedUntil.toISOString(),
121	        remainingMinutes: remainingMinutes
122	      });
123	    }
124	
125	    // Check if user has a password set
126	    if (!user.password) {
127	      console.log('[LOGIN DEBUG] User has no password set:', user.email);
128	      return res.status(401).json({ 
129	        message: 'No password set. Please contact an administrator to set your password.',
130	        requiresPasswordSetup: true
131	      });
132	    }
133	
134	    // Check password
135	    const isMatch = await user.comparePassword(password);
136	    
137	    if (!isMatch) {
138	      // Increment failed login attempts
139	      const lockoutResult = await incrementFailedAttempts(
140	        user,
141	        req.ip || req.connection.remoteAddress,
142	        req.get('user-agent')
143	      );
144	      
145	      // Log failed login attempt
146	      await logActivity({
147	        type: ActivityType.LOGIN_FAILED,
148	        userId: user.id,
149	        userEmail: user.email,
150	        userRole: user.role,
151	        action: 'Failed login attempt - invalid password',
152	        details: { 
153	          reason: 'Invalid password',
154	          failedAttempts: user.failedLoginAttempts,
155	          attemptsRemaining: lockoutResult.attemptsRemaining,
156	          wasLocked: lockoutResult.wasLocked
157	        },
158	        ipAddress: req.ip || req.connection.remoteAddress,
159	        userAgent: req.get('user-agent')
160	      });
161	      
162	      // Track for security monitoring
163	      const { trackFailedLogin, trackAccountLockout } = require('../services/securityMonitor');
164	      await trackFailedLogin(user.email, req.ip || req.connection.remoteAddress, req.get('user-agent'));
165	      
166	      // If account was just locked, track it
167	      if (lockoutResult.wasLocked) {
168	        await trackAccountLockout(user, req.ip || req.connection.remoteAddress, req.get('user-agent'), 'brute_force');
169	      }
170	      
171	      // If account was just locked, return lockout message
172	      if (lockoutResult.wasLocked) {
173	        await logActivity({
174	          type: ActivityType.ACCOUNT_LOCKED,
175	          userId: user.id,
176	          userEmail: user.email,
177	          userRole: user.role,
178	          action: 'Account locked due to failed login attempts',
179	          details: { 
180	            reason: 'brute_force',
181	            failedAttempts: user.failedLoginAttempts,
182	            lockedUntil: lockoutResult.lockedUntil.toISOString()
183	          },
184	          ipAddress: req.ip || req.connection.remoteAddress,
185	          userAgent: req.get('user-agent')
186	        });
187	        
188	        const remainingMinutes = Math.ceil((lockoutResult.lockedUntil - new Date()) / (1000 * 60));
189	        return res.status(423).json({ 
190	          message: `Account has been locked due to too many failed login attempts. Please try again in ${remainingMinutes} minute(s) or contact an administrator.`,
191	          accountLocked: true,
192	          lockedUntil: lockoutResult.lockedUntil.toISOString(),
193	          remainingMinutes: remainingMinutes
194	        });
195	      }
196	      
197	      // Return generic error (don't reveal if account exists)
198	      return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Invalid credentials' });
199	    }
200	    
201	    // Password is correct - reset failed attempts
202	    await resetFailedAttempts(user);
203	
204	    // Check if user must change password (first time login or admin reset)
205	    if (user.mustChangePassword) {
206	      // Generate temporary session token for password change (avoids passing password)
207	      const { generateSessionToken } = require('../utils/sessionToken');
208	      const sessionToken = await generateSessionToken(user.id, user.email);
209	      
210	      return res.status(200).json({
211	        mustChangePassword: true,
212	        message: 'You must change your password before continuing.',
213	        sessionToken: sessionToken, // Temporary token for password change
214	        user: {
215	          id: user.id,
216	          email: user.email,
217	          name: user.name
218	        }
219	      });
220	    }
221	
222	    // Check if user is active
223	    if (!user.isActive) {
224	      return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Account is deactivated' });
225	    }
226	
227	    // Get settings for password expiry check
228	    const settings = await Settings.getSettings();
229	    
230	    // Check password expiry
231	    if (settings.passwordExpiryDays && settings.passwordExpiryDays > 0 && user.passwordExpiryDate) {
232	      const now = new Date();
233	      if (now > user.passwordExpiryDate) {
234	        return res.status(401).json({ 
235	          message: 'Your password has expired. Please reset your password.',
236	          passwordExpired: true
237	        });
238	      }
239	    }
240	
241	    // Check 2FA requirements
242	    const requires2FA = settings.twoFactorAuth.enabled && settings.twoFactorAuth.required;
243	    const allowedMethods = settings.twoFactorAuth.allowedMethods || ['authenticator', 'email'];
244	
245	    if (requires2FA) {
246	      // Check if user has 2FA enabled and verified
247	      if (!user.twoFactorEnabled || !user.twoFactorVerified) {
248	        // Generate temporary session token for 2FA setup (avoids passing password)
249	        const { generateSessionToken } = require('../utils/sessionToken');
250	        const sessionToken = await generateSessionToken(user.id, user.email);
251	        
252	        // User needs to setup 2FA first - redirect to method selection
253	        return res.status(200).json({
254	          requires2FASetup: true,
255	          message: '2FA setup required. Please choose your preferred 2FA method.',
256	          sessionToken: sessionToken, // Temporary token for 2FA setup
257	          allowedMethods: allowedMethods, // Available 2FA methods
258	          user: {
259	            id: user.id,
260	            email: user.email,
261	            name: user.name
262	          }
263	        });
264	      }
265	
266	      // User has 2FA enabled - check if code was provided
267	      if (!req.body.twoFactorCode) {
268	        // Generate temporary session token for 2FA verification (avoids passing password)
269	        const { generateSessionToken } = require('../utils/sessionToken');
270	        const sessionToken = await generateSessionToken(user.id, user.email);
271	        
272	        const twoFactorMethod = user.twoFactorMethod || 'authenticator';
273	        
274	        // For email method, auto-send a code
275	        let emailSendFailed = false;
276	        if (twoFactorMethod === 'email') {
277	          const crypto = require('crypto');
278	          const { sendTemplatedEmail } = require('../utils/sendTemplatedEmail');
279	          
280	          // Generate 6-digit code
281	          const code = crypto.randomInt(100000, 999999).toString();
282	          const expiresAt = new Date(Date.now() + 10 * 60 * 1000); // 10 minutes
283	          
284	          // Store code
285	          user.emailTwoFactorCode = code;
286	          user.emailTwoFactorExpires = expiresAt;
287	          await user.save();
288	          
289	          // Send email with retry
290	          const maxRetries = 2;
291	          for (let attempt = 1; attempt <= maxRetries; attempt++) {
292	            try {
293	              const emailSettings = await Settings.getSettings();
294	              await sendTemplatedEmail(
295	                'two-factor-code',
296	                user.email,
297	                {
298	                  userName: user.name,
299	                  verificationCode: code,
300	                  expiryMinutes: '10'
301	                },
302	                emailSettings,
303	                { context: { type: '2fa-login', userId: user.id } }
304	              );
305	              emailSendFailed = false;
306	              break;
307	            } catch (emailError) {
308	              console.error(`Failed to send 2FA email (attempt ${attempt}/${maxRetries}):`, emailError.message);
309	              emailSendFailed = true;
310	              if (attempt < maxRetries) {
311	                await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 1000));
312	              }
313	            }
314	          }
315	        }
316	        
317	        return res.status(200).json({
318	          requires2FA: true,
319	          message: twoFactorMethod === 'email'
320	            ? (emailSendFailed ? 'Failed to send verification code. Please use the resend button.' : 'Verification code sent to your email')
321	            : '2FA verification code required',
322	          emailSendFailed: emailSendFailed,
323	          twoFactorMethod: twoFactorMethod,
324	          sessionToken: sessionToken,
325	          user: {
326	            id: user.id,
327	            email: user.email,
328	            maskedEmail: user.email.replace(/(.{2})(.*)(@.*)/, '$1***$3'),
329	            name: user.name
330	          }
331	        });
332	      }
333	
334	      // Verify 2FA code based on method
335	      const speakeasy = require('speakeasy');
336	      const twoFactorMethod = user.twoFactorMethod || 'authenticator';
337	      let verified = false;
338	      
339	      if (twoFactorMethod === 'email') {
340	        // Verify email code
341	        if (!user.emailTwoFactorCode || !user.emailTwoFactorExpires) {
342	          return res.status(401).json({ message: 'No verification code found. Please request a new code.' });
343	        }
344	        
345	        if (new Date() > new Date(user.emailTwoFactorExpires)) {
346	          return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Verification code has expired. Please request a new code.' });
347	        }
348	        
349	        verified = user.emailTwoFactorCode === req.body.twoFactorCode;
350	        
351	        // Clear code after verification attempt
352	        if (verified) {
353	          user.emailTwoFactorCode = null;
354	          user.emailTwoFactorExpires = null;
355	        }
356	      } else {
357	        // Verify authenticator TOTP code
358	        if (!user.twoFactorSecret) {
359	          return res.status(401).json({ message: '2FA not configured' });
360	        }
361	        
362	        verified = speakeasy.totp.verify({
363	          secret: user.twoFactorSecret,
364	          encoding: 'base32',
365	          token: req.body.twoFactorCode,
366	          window: 2 // Allow 2 time steps (60 seconds) tolerance
367	        });
368	      }
369	
370	      if (!verified) {
371	        return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Invalid 2FA code' });
372	      }
373	    }
374	
375	    // Update last login
376	    user.lastLogin = new Date();
377	    await user.save();
378	
379	    // Generate token (expiration configurable via environment)
380	    const token = jwt.sign(
381	      { userId: user.id, role: user.role },
382	      process.env.JWT_SECRET,
383	      { expiresIn: process.env.JWT_EXPIRES_IN || '7d' }
384	    );
385	
386	    // SECURITY: Never expose sensitive data in responses
387	    const userObj = user.toSafeObject ? user.toSafeObject() : user.toJSON();
388	    delete userObj.password;
389	    delete userObj.twoFactorSecret; // Never expose 2FA secret
390	    delete userObj.resetPasswordToken;
391	    delete userObj.resetPasswordExpires;
392	    
393	    // Log successful login
394	    await logActivity({
395	      type: ActivityType.LOGIN,
396	      userId: user.id,
397	      userEmail: user.email,
398	      userRole: user.role,
399	      action: 'User logged in successfully',
400	      details: { twoFactorEnabled: user.twoFactorEnabled },
401	      ipAddress: req.ip || req.connection.remoteAddress,
402	      userAgent: req.get('user-agent')
403	    });
404	
405	    res.json({
406	      message: 'Login successful',
407	      token,
408	      user: userObj
409	    });
410	  } catch (error) {
411	    res.status(500).json({ message: error.message });
412	  }
413	});
414	
415	// Get current user
416	router.get('/me', auth, async (req, res) => {
417	  try {
418	    const user = await User.findByPk(req.user.userId, {
419	      attributes: { exclude: ['password', 'twoFactorSecret', 'resetPasswordToken', 'resetPasswordExpires'] }
420	    });
421	    if (!user) {
422	      return res.status(404).json({ message: 'User not found' });
423	    }
424	    res.json(user);
425	  } catch (error) {
426	    res.status(500).json({ message: error.message });
427	  }
428	});
