2268	// Cancel an import session
2269	router.post('/import/:importId/cancel', async (req, res) => {
2270	  try {
2271	    const { importId } = req.params;
2272	    const importStore = require('../utils/importStore');
2273	    
2274	    const importSession = importStore.getImport(importId);
2275	    if (!importSession) {
2276	      return res.status(404).json({
2277	        success: false,
2278	        message: 'Import session not found'
2279	      });
2280	    }
2281	    
2282	    // Cancel the import
2283	    importStore.cancelImport(importId);
2284	    
2285	    // Remove all queued jobs for this import from BullMQ queue
2286	    try {
2287	      const jobs = await invoiceImportQueue.getJobs(['waiting', 'delayed', 'active', 'wait']);
2288	      let removedCount = 0;
2289	      for (const job of jobs) {
2290	        if (job.data.importId === importId) {
2291	          await job.remove();
2292	          removedCount++;
2293	          console.log(`🗑️  Removed queued job ${job.id} for cancelled import ${importId}`);
2294	        }
2295	      }
2296	      console.log(`🗑️  Removed ${removedCount} queued job(s) for cancelled import ${importId}`);
2297	    } catch (queueError) {
2298	      console.error(`⚠️  Error removing jobs from queue:`, queueError.message);
2299	    }
2300	    
2301	    // Clean up any downloaded files
2302	    const filePaths = importSession.filePaths || [];
2303	    for (const filePath of filePaths) {
2304	      try {
2305	        if (fs.existsSync(filePath)) {
2306	          fs.unlinkSync(filePath);
2307	          console.log(`🗑️  Cleaned up cancelled file: ${filePath}`);
2308	        }
2309	      } catch (cleanupError) {
2310	        console.error(`⚠️  Error cleaning up file ${filePath}:`, cleanupError.message);
2311	      }
2312	    }
2313	    
2314	    // Clean up any remaining temp files that might not be in filePaths
2315	    // (fallback for files that were downloaded but not yet tracked)
2316	    const tempDir = getStorageDir('temp');
2317	    if (fs.existsSync(tempDir)) {
2318	      const files = fs.readdirSync(tempDir);
2319	      const trackedPaths = new Set((importSession.filePaths || []).map(p => path.resolve(p)));
2320	      
2321	      for (const file of files) {
2322	        const filePath = path.join(tempDir, file);
2323	        const absolutePath = path.resolve(filePath);
2324	        
2325	        // Delete if:
2326	        // 1. It's already tracked in filePaths, OR
2327	        // 2. It starts with 'sftp-' and was created recently (within last 5 minutes)
2328	        //    to avoid deleting files from other concurrent imports
2329	        const isTracked = trackedPaths.has(absolutePath);
2330	        const isRecentSftpFile = file.startsWith('sftp-');
2331	        
2332	        if (isTracked) {
2333	          // Already handled above, but double-check
2334	          try {
2335	            if (fs.existsSync(filePath)) {
2336	              fs.unlinkSync(filePath);
2337	              console.log(`🗑️  Cleaned up cancelled temp file (tracked): ${filePath}`);
2338	            }
2339	          } catch (cleanupError) {
2340	            console.error(`⚠️  Error cleaning up tracked temp file ${file}:`, cleanupError.message);
2341	          }
2342	        } else if (isRecentSftpFile) {
2343	          // Fallback: delete recent sftp files (likely from this import)
2344	          try {
2345	            if (fs.existsSync(filePath)) {
2346	              const stats = fs.statSync(filePath);
2347	              const fileAge = Date.now() - stats.mtime.getTime();
2348	              // Only delete files less than 5 minutes old to avoid deleting files from other imports
2349	              if (fileAge < 5 * 60 * 1000) {
2350	                fs.unlinkSync(filePath);
2351	                console.log(`🗑️  Cleaned up cancelled temp file (fallback): ${filePath}`);
2352	              }
2353	            }
2354	          } catch (cleanupError) {
2355	            console.error(`⚠️  Error cleaning up temp file ${file}:`, cleanupError.message);
2356	          }
2357	        }
2358	      }
2359	    }
2360	    
2361	    // Get user identifier - try email first, then userId, then id, fallback to 'unknown'
2362	    const userIdentifier = req.user?.email || req.user?.userId || req.user?.id || 'unknown';
2363	    console.log(`🛑 Import ${importId} cancelled by user ${userIdentifier}`);
2364	    
2365	    res.json({
2366	      success: true,
2367	      message: 'Import cancelled successfully'
2368	    });
2369	  } catch (error) {
2370	    console.error('Error cancelling import:', error);
2371	    res.status(500).json({
2372	      success: false,
2373	      message: `Error cancelling import: ${error.message}`
2374	    });
2375	  }
2376	});
2377	
2378	module.exports = router;
2379

1	const express = require('express');
2	const jwt = require('jsonwebtoken');
3	const crypto = require('crypto');

10	
11	// Register
12	router.post('/register', async (req, res) => {
13	  try {
14	    const { name, email, password } = req.body;
15	
16	    // Check if user exists
17	    const existingUser = await User.findOne({ where: { email: email.toLowerCase().trim() } });
18	    if (existingUser) {
19	      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'User already exists' });
20	    }
21	
22	    // Create user — role is always external_user on self-registration
23	    const user = await User.create({
24	      name,
25	      email: email.toLowerCase().trim(),
26	      password,
27	      role: 'external_user'
28	    });
29	
30	    // Generate token (expiration configurable via environment)
31	    const token = jwt.sign(
32	      { userId: user.id, role: user.role },
33	      process.env.JWT_SECRET,
34	      { expiresIn: process.env.JWT_EXPIRES_IN || '7d' }
35	    );
36	
37	    res.status(201).json({
38	      message: 'User created successfully',
39	      token,
40	      user: {
41	        id: user.id,
42	        name: user.name,
43	        email: user.email,
44	        role: user.role
45	      }
46	    });
47	  } catch (error) {
48	    res.status(500).json({ message: error.message });
49	  }
50	});
51	
52	// Login
53	router.post('/login', recaptchaMiddleware({ minScore: 0.3 }), async (req, res) => {
54	  try {
55	    const { email, password } = req.body;
56	
57	    // Validate input
58	    if (!email || !password) {
59	      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Email and password are required' });
60	    }
61	
62	    // Find user (case-insensitive email search)
63	    const user = await User.findOne({ where: { email: email.toLowerCase().trim() } });
64	    if (!user) {
65	      // Log failed login attempt
66	      await logActivity({
67	        type: ActivityType.LOGIN_FAILED,
68	        userEmail: email.toLowerCase().trim(),
69	        action: 'Failed login attempt',
70	        details: { reason: 'User not found' },
71	        ipAddress: req.ip || req.connection.remoteAddress,
72	        userAgent: req.get('user-agent')
73	      });
74	      return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Invalid credentials' });
75	    }
76	
77	    // Block notification_contact users from logging in
78	    // They are email-only recipients and do not have portal access
79	    if (user.role === 'notification_contact') {
80	      await logActivity({
81	        type: ActivityType.LOGIN_FAILED,
82	        userId: user.id,
83	        userEmail: user.email,
84	        userRole: user.role,
85	        action: 'Failed login attempt - notification contact',
86	        details: { reason: 'Notification contacts cannot log in' },
87	        ipAddress: req.ip || req.connection.remoteAddress,
88	        userAgent: req.get('user-agent')
89	      });
90	      return res.status(403).json({ 
91	        message: 'Notification contacts cannot log in. This account is for email notifications only.' 
92	      });
93	    }
94	
95	    // Check account lockout status BEFORE password verification
96	    const { checkAccountLockout, incrementFailedAttempts, resetFailedAttempts } = require('../utils/accountLockout');
97	    const lockoutStatus = await checkAccountLockout(user);
98	    
99	    if (lockoutStatus.isLocked) {
100	      const remainingMinutes = Math.ceil((lockoutStatus.lockedUntil - new Date()) / (1000 * 60));
101	      await logActivity({
102	        type: ActivityType.LOGIN_FAILED,
103	        userId: user.id,
104	        userEmail: user.email,
105	        userRole: user.role,

111	          remainingMinutes: remainingMinutes
112	        },
113	        ipAddress: req.ip || req.connection.remoteAddress,
114	        userAgent: req.get('user-agent')
115	      });
116	      
117	      return res.status(423).json({ 
118	        message: `Account is locked. Please try again in ${remainingMinutes} minute(s) or contact an administrator.`,
119	        accountLocked: true,
120	        lockedUntil: lockoutStatus.lockedUntil.toISOString(),
121	        remainingMinutes: remainingMinutes
122	      });
123	    }
124	
125	    // Check if user has a password set
126	    if (!user.password) {
127	      console.log('[LOGIN DEBUG] User has no password set:', user.email);
128	      return res.status(401).json({ 
129	        message: 'No password set. Please contact an administrator to set your password.',
130	        requiresPasswordSetup: true
131	      });
132	    }
133	
134	    // Check password
135	    const isMatch = await user.comparePassword(password);
136	    
137	    if (!isMatch) {
138	      // Increment failed login attempts

141	        req.ip || req.connection.remoteAddress,
142	        req.get('user-agent')
143	      );

145	      // Log failed login attempt

150	        userRole: user.role,

180	            reason: 'brute_force',
181	            failedAttempts: user.failedLoginAttempts,
182	            lockedUntil: lockoutResult.lockedUntil.toISOString()
183	          },
184	          ipAddress: req.ip || req.connection.remoteAddress,
185	          userAgent: req.get('user-agent')
186	        });
187	        
188	        const remainingMinutes = Math.ceil((lockoutResult.lockedUntil - new Date()) / (1000 * 60));
189	        return res.status(423).json({ 
190	          message: `Account has been locked due to too many failed login attempts. Please try again in ${remainingMinutes} minute(s) or contact an administrator.`,
191	          accountLocked: true,
192	          lockedUntil: lockoutResult.lockedUntil.toISOString(),
193	          remainingMinutes: remainingMinutes
194	        });
195	      }
196	      
197	      // Return generic error (don't reveal if account exists)
198	      return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Invalid credentials' });
199	    }
200	    
201	    // Password is correct - reset failed attempts
202	    await resetFailedAttempts(user);
203	
204	    // Check if user must change password (first time login or admin reset)
205	    if (user.mustChangePassword) {
206	      // Generate temporary session token for password change (avoids passing password)
207	      const { generateSessionToken } = require('../utils/sessionToken');
208	      const sessionToken = await generateSessionToken(user.id, user.email);
209	      
210	      return res.status(200).json({
211	        mustChangePassword: true,
212	        message: 'You must change your password before continuing.',
213	        sessionToken: sessionToken, // Temporary token for password change
214	        user: {
215	          id: user.id,
216	          email: user.email,
217	          name: user.name
218	        }
219	      });
220	    }
221	
222	    // Check if user is active
223	    if (!user.isActive) {
224	      return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Account is deactivated' });
225	    }
226	
227	    // Get settings for password expiry check
228	    const settings = await Settings.getSettings();
229	    
230	    // Check password expiry
231	    if (settings.passwordExpiryDays && settings.passwordExpiryDays > 0 && user.passwordExpiryDate) {
232	      const now = new Date();
233	      if (now > user.passwordExpiryDate) {
234	        return res.status(401).json({ 
235	          message: 'Your password has expired. Please reset your password.',
236	          passwordExpired: true
237	        });
238	      }
239	    }
240	
241	    // Check 2FA requirements
242	    const requires2FA = settings.twoFactorAuth.enabled && settings.twoFactorAuth.required;
243	    const allowedMethods = settings.twoFactorAuth.allowedMethods || ['authenticator', 'email'];
244	
245	    if (requires2FA) {
246	      // Check if user has 2FA enabled and verified
247	      if (!user.twoFactorEnabled || !user.twoFactorVerified) {
248	        // Generate temporary session token for 2FA setup (avoids passing password)
249	        const { generateSessionToken } = require('../utils/sessionToken');
250	        const sessionToken = await generateSessionToken(user.id, user.email);
251	        
252	        // User needs to setup 2FA first - redirect to method selection
253	        return res.status(200).json({
254	          requires2FASetup: true,
255	          message: '2FA setup required. Please choose your preferred 2FA method.',
256	          sessionToken: sessionToken, // Temporary token for 2FA setup
257	          allowedMethods: allowedMethods, // Available 2FA methods
258	          user: {
259	            id: user.id,
260	            email: user.email,
261	            name: user.name
262	          }
263	        });
264	      }
265	
266	      // User has 2FA enabled - check if code was provided
267	      if (!req.body.twoFactorCode) {
268	        // Generate temporary session token for 2FA verification (avoids passing password)
269	        const { generateSessionToken } = require('../utils/sessionToken');
270	        const sessionToken = await generateSessionToken(user.id, user.email);
271	        
272	        const twoFactorMethod = user.twoFactorMethod || 'authenticator';
273	        
274	        // For email method, auto-send a code
275	        let emailSendFailed = false;
276	        if (twoFactorMethod === 'email') {
277	          const crypto = require('crypto');
278	          const { sendTemplatedEmail } = require('../utils/sendTemplatedEmail');
279	          
280	          // Generate 6-digit code
281	          const code = crypto.randomInt(100000, 999999).toString();
282	          const expiresAt = new Date(Date.now() + 10 * 60 * 1000); // 10 minutes
283	          
284	          // Store code
285	          user.emailTwoFactorCode = code;
286	          user.emailTwoFactorExpires = expiresAt;
287	          await user.save();
288	          
289	          // Send email with retry
290	          const maxRetries = 2;
291	          for (let attempt = 1; attempt <= maxRetries; attempt++) {
292	            try {
293	              const emailSettings = await Settings.getSettings();
294	              await sendTemplatedEmail(
295	                'two-factor-code',
296	                user.email,
297	                {
298	                  userName: user.name,
299	                  verificationCode: code,
300	                  expiryMinutes: '10'
301	                },
302	                emailSettings,
303	                { context: { type: '2fa-login', userId: user.id } }
304	              );
305	              emailSendFailed = false;
306	              break;
307	            } catch (emailError) {
308	              console.error(`Failed to send 2FA email (attempt ${attempt}/${maxRetries}):`, emailError.message);
309	              emailSendFailed = true;
310	              if (attempt < maxRetries) {
311	                await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 1000));
312	              }
313	            }
314	          }
315	        }
316	        
317	        return res.status(200).json({
318	          requires2FA: true,
319	          message: twoFactorMethod === 'email'
320	            ? (emailSendFailed ? 'Failed to send verification code. Please use the resend button.' : 'Verification code sent to your email')
321	            : '2FA verification code required',
322	          emailSendFailed: emailSendFailed,
323	          twoFactorMethod: twoFactorMethod,
324	          sessionToken: sessionToken,
325	          user: {
326	            id: user.id,
327	            email: user.email,
328	            maskedEmail: user.email.replace(/(.{2})(.*)(@.*)/, '$1***$3'),
329	            name: user.name
330	          }
331	        });
332	      }
333	
334	      // Verify 2FA code based on method
335	      const speakeasy = require('speakeasy');
336	      const twoFactorMethod = user.twoFactorMethod || 'authenticator';
337	      let verified = false;
338	      
339	      if (twoFactorMethod === 'email') {
340	        // Verify email code
341	        if (!user.emailTwoFactorCode || !user.emailTwoFactorExpires) {
342	          return res.status(401).json({ message: 'No verification code found. Please request a new code.' });
343	        }
344	        
345	        if (new Date() > new Date(user.emailTwoFactorExpires)) {
346	          return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Verification code has expired. Please request a new code.' });
347	        }
348	        
349	        verified = user.emailTwoFactorCode === req.body.twoFactorCode;
350	        
351	        // Clear code after verification attempt
352	        if (verified) {
353	          user.emailTwoFactorCode = null;
354	          user.emailTwoFactorExpires = null;
355	        }
356	      } else {
357	        // Verify authenticator TOTP code
358	        if (!user.twoFactorSecret) {
359	          return res.status(401).json({ message: '2FA not configured' });
360	        }
361	        
362	        verified = speakeasy.totp.verify({
363	          secret: user.twoFactorSecret,
364	          encoding: 'base32',
365	          token: req.body.twoFactorCode,
366	          window: 2 // Allow 2 time steps (60 seconds) tolerance
367	        });
368	      }
369	
370	      if (!verified) {
371	        return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Invalid 2FA code' });
372	      }
373	    }
374	
375	    // Update last login
376	    user.lastLogin = new Date();
377	    await user.save();
378	
379	    // Generate token (expiration configurable via environment)
380	    const token = jwt.sign(
381	      { userId: user.id, role: user.role },
382	      process.env.JWT_SECRET,
383	      { expiresIn: process.env.JWT_EXPIRES_IN || '7d' }
384	    );
385	
386	    // SECURITY: Never expose sensitive data in responses
387	    const userObj = user.toSafeObject ? user.toSafeObject() : user.toJSON();
388	    delete userObj.password;
389	    delete userObj.twoFactorSecret; // Never expose 2FA secret
390	    delete userObj.resetPasswordToken;
391	    delete userObj.resetPasswordExpires;
392	    
393	    // Log successful login
394	    await logActivity({
395	      type: ActivityType.LOGIN,
396	      userId: user.id,
397	      userEmail: user.email,
398	      userRole: user.role,
399	      action: 'User logged in successfully',
400	      details: { twoFactorEnabled: user.twoFactorEnabled },
401	      ipAddress: req.ip || req.connection.remoteAddress,
402	      userAgent: req.get('user-agent')
403	    });
404	
405	    res.json({
406	      message: 'Login successful',
407	      token,
408	      user: userObj
409	    });
410	  } catch (error) {
411	    res.status(500).json({ message: error.message });
412	  }
413	});

430	// Forgot password - Request password reset
431	router.post('/forgot-password', recaptchaMiddleware({ minScore: 0.3 }), async (req, res) => {
432	  try {
433	    const { email } = req.body;
434	
435	    if (!email) {
436	      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Email is required' });
437	    }
438	
439	    // Find user
440	    const user = await User.findOne({ where: { email: email.toLowerCase().trim() } });
441	    
442	    // Always return success to prevent email enumeration
443	    // But only send email if user exists
444	    if (user && user.isActive) {
445	      // Generate reset token
446	      const resetToken = crypto.randomBytes(32).toString('hex');
447	      const resetTokenHash = crypto.createHash('sha256').update(resetToken).digest('hex');
448	      
449	      // Set token and expiration (1 hour)
450	      user.resetPasswordToken = resetTokenHash;
451	      user.resetPasswordExpires = Date.now() + 3600000; // 1 hour
452	      await user.save();
453	
454	      // Get settings for email configuration
455	      const settings = await Settings.getSettings();
456	      
457	      // Send email if email provider is configured
458	      const { isEmailEnabled, sendEmail } = require('../utils/emailService');
459	      const { getResetPasswordUrl } = require('../utils/urlConfig');
460	      if (isEmailEnabled(settings)) {
461	        try {
462	          const resetUrl = getResetPasswordUrl(resetToken);
463	          
464	          // Use systemEmail if set, otherwise use provider's fromEmail
465	          const fromEmail = settings.systemEmail || 
466	            settings.emailProvider?.smtp?.fromEmail || 
467	            settings.emailProvider?.office365?.fromEmail ||
468	            settings.emailProvider?.resend?.fromEmail ||
469	            settings.emailProvider?.smtp2go?.fromEmail;
470	          
471	          const fromName = settings.companyName || 
472	            settings.emailProvider?.smtp?.fromName || 
473	            settings.emailProvider?.resend?.fromName ||
474	            settings.emailProvider?.smtp2go?.fromName ||
475	            'Makita EDI Portal';
476	          
477	          // Calculate expiry time for email (1 hour = 3600000ms)
478	          const expiryMs = user.resetPasswordExpires - Date.now();
479	          const expiryHours = Math.floor(expiryMs / (1000 * 60 * 60));
480	          const expiryMinutes = Math.floor((expiryMs % (1000 * 60 * 60)) / (1000 * 60));
481	          let expiryTime = '1 hour'; // Default
482	          if (expiryHours > 0) {
483	            expiryTime = expiryHours === 1 ? '1 hour' : `${expiryHours} hours`;
484	          } else if (expiryMinutes > 0) {
485	            expiryTime = expiryMinutes === 1 ? '1 minute' : `${expiryMinutes} minutes`;
486	          }
487	          
488	          // Try to use email template, fallback to hardcoded if template not found
489	          try {
490	            const { sendTemplatedEmail } = require('../utils/sendTemplatedEmail');
491	            await sendTemplatedEmail(
492	              'password-reset',
493	              user.email,
494	              {
495	                userName: user.name,
496	                userEmail: user.email,
497	                resetUrl: resetUrl,
498	                expiryTime: expiryTime
499	              },
500	              settings
501	            );
502	          } catch (templateError) {
503	            console.warn('Email template not found, using Tabler template:', templateError.message);
504	            // Fallback to Tabler template
505	            const { renderTemplate } = require('../utils/tablerEmailRenderer');
506	            
507	            const html = renderTemplate('password-reset', {
508	              userName: user.name,
509	              resetUrl,
510	              expiryTime: '1 hour'
511	            }, settings);
512	            
513	            await sendEmail({
514	              to: user.email,
515	              subject: 'Password Reset Request',
516	              html,
517	              text: `
518	Password Reset Request
519	
520	Hello ${user.name},
521	
522	You requested to reset your password. Click the link below to reset it:
523	
524	${resetUrl}
525	
526	This link will expire in 1 hour.
527	
528	If you didn't request this, please ignore this email.
529	
530	Best regards,
531	${fromName}
532	              `.trim()
533	            }, settings);
534	          }
535	        } catch (emailError) {
536	          console.error('Error sending password reset email:', emailError);
537	          // Still return success to user, but log the error
538	        }
539	      } else {
540	        console.warn('[AUTH] Password reset requested but SMTP is not configured — email not sent for:', user.email);
541	      }
542	    }
543	
544	    // Always return success message
545	    res.json({ 
546	      message: 'If an account with that email exists, a password reset link has been sent.' 
547	    });
548	  } catch (error) {
549	    console.error('Forgot password error:', error);
550	    res.status(500).json({ message: 'An error occurred. Please try again later.' });
551	  }
552	});

556	  return res.status(400).json({

561	// Validate reset token
562	router.get('/validate-reset-token', async (req, res) => {
563	  try {
564	    const { token } = req.query;
565	
566	    if (!token) {
567	      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Token is required' });
568	    }
569	
570	    // Hash the token
571	    const resetTokenHash = crypto.createHash('sha256').update(token).digest('hex');
572	
573	    // Find user with valid token
574	    const user = await User.findOne({
575	      where: {
576	        resetPasswordToken: resetTokenHash,
577	        resetPasswordExpires: { [Op.gt]: Date.now() }
578	      }
579	    });
580	
581	    if (!user) {
582	      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Invalid or expired reset token' });
583	    }
584	
585	    res.json({ message: 'Token is valid' });
586	  } catch (error) {
587	    res.status(500).json({ message: error.message });
588	  }
589	});
590	
591	// Reset password
592	router.post('/reset-password', async (req, res) => {
593	  try {
594	    const { token, password } = req.body;
595	
596	    if (!token || !password) {
597	      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Token and password are required' });
598	    }
599	
600	    // Validate password strength
601	    if (password.length < 8) {
602	      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Password must be at least 8 characters long' });
603	    }
604	    if (!/[A-Z]/.test(password)) {
605	      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Password must contain at least one uppercase letter' });
606	    }
607	    if (!/[a-z]/.test(password)) {
608	      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Password must contain at least one lowercase letter' });
609	    }
610	    if (!/[0-9]/.test(password)) {
611	      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Password must contain at least one number' });
612	    }
613	
614	    // Hash the token
615	    const resetTokenHash = crypto.createHash('sha256').update(token).digest('hex');
616	
617	    // Find user with valid token
618	    const user = await User.findOne({
619	      where: {
620	        resetPasswordToken: resetTokenHash,
621	        resetPasswordExpires: { [Op.gt]: Date.now() }
622	      }
623	    });
624	
625	    if (!user) {
626	      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Invalid or expired reset token' });
627	    }
628	
629	    // Update password and clear reset token
630	    user.password = password;
631	    user.resetPasswordToken = null;
632	    user.resetPasswordExpires = null;
633	    user.mustChangePassword = false; // Clear the flag since user is setting their own password
634	    await user.save();
635	
636	    // Log password reset
637	    await logActivity({
638	      type: ActivityType.PASSWORD_RESET,
639	      userId: user.id,
640	      userEmail: user.email,
641	      userRole: user.role,
642	      action: 'Password reset completed',
643	      ipAddress: req.ip || req.connection.remoteAddress,
644	      userAgent: req.get('user-agent')
645	    });
646	
647	    res.json({ message: 'Password reset successfully' });
648	  } catch (error) {
649	    res.status(500).json({ message: error.message });
650	  }
651	});
652	
653	// Change password (for first-time login or forced password change)
654	router.post('/change-password', async (req, res) => {
655	  try {
656	    const { sessionToken, currentPassword, newPassword } = req.body;
657	
658	    if (!sessionToken || !newPassword) {
659	      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Session token and new password are required' });
660	    }
661	
662	    // Validate password strength
663	    if (newPassword.length < 8) {
664	      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Password must be at least 8 characters long' });
665	    }
666	    if (!/[A-Z]/.test(newPassword)) {
667	      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Password must contain at least one uppercase letter' });
668	    }
669	    if (!/[a-z]/.test(newPassword)) {
670	      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Password must contain at least one lowercase letter' });
671	    }
672	    if (!/[0-9]/.test(newPassword)) {
673	      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Password must contain at least one number' });
674	    }
675	
676	    // Verify session token
677	    const { verifySessionToken } = require('../utils/sessionToken');
678	    const tokenData = await verifySessionToken(sessionToken, true);
679	    
680	    if (!tokenData || !tokenData.userId) {
681	      return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Invalid or expired session token' });
682	    }
683	
684	    // Find user
685	    const user = await User.findByPk(tokenData.userId);
686	    if (!user) {
687	      return res.status(404).json({ message: 'User not found' });
688	    }
689	
690	    // If current password is provided, verify it (for users changing their own password)
691	    // If mustChangePassword is true, we allow changing without current password (first-time login)
692	    if (currentPassword) {
693	      if (!user.password) {
694	        return res.status(400).json({ message: 'No password set. Cannot verify current password.' });
695	      }
696	      const isMatch = await user.comparePassword(currentPassword);
697	      if (!isMatch) {
698	        return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Current password is incorrect' });
699	      }
700	    } else if (!user.mustChangePassword) {
701	      // If mustChangePassword is false, current password is required
702	      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Current password is required' });
703	    }
704	
705	    // Update password
706	    user.password = newPassword;
707	    user.mustChangePassword = false; // Clear the flag after password change
708	    await user.save();
709	
710	    // Send password changed email notification
711	    try {
712	      const Settings = require('../models/Settings');
713	      const settings = await Settings.getSettings();
714	      
715	      const { isEmailEnabled } = require('../utils/emailService');
716	      if (isEmailEnabled(settings)) {
717	        const { sendTemplatedEmail } = require('../utils/sendTemplatedEmail');
718	        await sendTemplatedEmail(
719	          'password-changed',
720	          user.email,
721	          {
722	            userName: user.name || user.email,
723	            userEmail: user.email
724	          },
725	          settings
726	        );
727	      }
728	    } catch (emailError) {
729	      // Don't fail the password change if email fails
730	      console.warn('Failed to send password changed email:', emailError.message);
731	    }
732	
733	    res.json({ message: 'Password changed successfully' });
734	  } catch (error) {
735	    res.status(500).json({ message: error.message });
736	  }
737	});
738	
739	module.exports = router;
740	
741

1	const express = require('express');
2	const { File, Customer, Company, User, Invoice, CreditNote, Statement, sequelize } = require('../models');
3	const { Op } = require('sequelize');
4	const globalAdmin = require('../middleware/globalAdmin');
5	const auth = require('../middleware/auth');
6	const crypto = require('crypto');
7	const path = require('path');
8	const fs = require('fs');
9	const router = express.Router();
10	
11	/**
12	 * Get all files with filtering and pagination
13	 */
14	router.get('/', globalAdmin, async (req, res) => {
15	  try {
16	    const {
17	      page = 1,
18	      limit = 50,
19	      status,
20	      fileType,
21	      processingMethod,
22	      dateFrom,
23	      dateTo,
24	      search,
25	      customerId,
26	      assigned
27	    } = req.query;
28	
29	    const where = {
30	      deletedAt: null // Only show non-deleted files by default
31	    };
32	    const include = [];
33	
34	    // Status filter
35	    if (status && status !== 'all') {
36	      where.status = status;
37	    }
38	
39	    // File type filter
40	    if (fileType && fileType !== 'all') {
41	      where.fileType = fileType;
42	    }
43	
44	    // Processing method filter
45	    if (processingMethod && processingMethod !== 'all') {
46	      where.processingMethod = processingMethod;
47	    }
48	
49	    // Date range filter
50	    if (dateFrom || dateTo) {
51	      where.uploadedAt = {};
52	      if (dateFrom) {
53	        where.uploadedAt[Op.gte] = new Date(dateFrom);
54	      }
55	      if (dateTo) {
56	        where.uploadedAt[Op.lte] = new Date(dateTo + 'T23:59:59.999Z');
57	      }
58	    }
59	
60	    // Search filter (filename, invoice number, account number)
61	    if (search) {
62	      const searchConditions = [
63	        { fileName: { [Op.iLike]: `%${search}%` } }
64	      ];
65	      
66	      // Search in JSONB parsedData field
67	      searchConditions.push(
68	        sequelize.where(
69	          sequelize.cast(sequelize.col('File.parsedData'), 'text'),
70	          { [Op.iLike]: `%${search}%` }
71	        )
72	      );
73	      
74	      where[Op.or] = searchConditions;
75	    }
76	
77	    // Customer filter
78	    if (customerId && customerId !== 'all') {
79	      where.customerId = customerId;
80	    }
81	
82	    // Assigned filter (has customerId vs null)
83	    if (assigned === 'true') {
84	      where.customerId = { [Op.ne]: null };
85	    } else if (assigned === 'false') {
86	      where.customerId = null;
87	    }
88	
89	    // Include associations
90	    include.push(
91	      {
92	        model: Customer,
93	        as: 'customer',
94	        attributes: ['id', 'name', 'accountNumber'],
95	        required: false
96	      },
97	      {
98	        model: User,
99	        as: 'uploadedBy',
100	        attributes: ['id', 'name', 'email'],
101	        required: false
102	      }
103	    );
104	
105	    // Get total count for pagination (without include for performance)
106	    const total = await File.count({ where });
107	
108	    // Get files with pagination
109	    const offset = (parseInt(page) - 1) * parseInt(limit);
110	    const files = await File.findAll({
111	      where,
112	      include,
113	      order: [['uploadedAt', 'DESC']],
114	      limit: parseInt(limit),
115	      offset: offset
116	    });
117	
118	    res.json({
119	      files: files.map(file => file.toJSON()),
120	      pagination: {
121	        page: parseInt(page),
122	        limit: parseInt(limit),
123	        total,
124	        pages: Math.ceil(total / parseInt(limit))
125	      }
126	    });
127	  } catch (error) {
128	    console.error('Error fetching files:', error);
129	    res.status(500).json({
130	      success: false,
131	      message: 'Error fetching files: ' + error.message
132	    });
133	  }
134	});
135	
136	/**
137	 * Get single file with full details
138	 */
139	router.get('/:id', globalAdmin, async (req, res) => {
140	  try {
141	    const { id } = req.params;
142	
143	    // Validate UUID
144	    const uuidRegex = /^[0-9a-f]{8}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{12}$/i;
145	    if (!uuidRegex.test(id)) {
146	      return res.status(400).json({
147	        success: false,
148	        message: 'Invalid file ID format'
149	      });
150	    }
151	
152	    const file = await File.findByPk(id, {
153	      include: [
154	        {
155	          model: Customer,
156	          as: 'customer',
157	          required: false
158	        },
159	        {
160	          model: User,
161	          as: 'uploadedBy',
162	          attributes: ['id', 'name', 'email'],
163	          required: false
164	        }
165	      ]
166	    });
167	
168	    if (!file) {
169	      return res.status(404).json({
170	        success: false,
171	        message: 'File not found'
172	      });
173	    }
174	
175	    res.json(file.toJSON());
176	  } catch (error) {
177	    console.error('Error fetching file:', error);
178	    res.status(500).json({
179	      success: false,
180	      message: 'Error fetching file: ' + error.message
181	    });
182	  }
183	});

185	/**
186	 * Bulk delete files
187	 */
188	router.delete('/bulk', globalAdmin, async (req, res) => {
189	  try {
190	    const { fileIds } = req.body;
191	
192	    if (!fileIds || !Array.isArray(fileIds) || fileIds.length === 0) {
193	      return res.status(400).json({
194	        success: false,
195	        message: 'File IDs array is required'
196	      });
197	    }
198	
199	    // Validate UUIDs
200	    const uuidRegex = /^[0-9a-f]{8}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{12}$/i;
201	    const validIds = fileIds.filter(id => uuidRegex.test(id));
202	
203	    if (validIds.length === 0) {
204	      return res.status(400).json({
205	        success: false,
206	        message: 'No valid file IDs provided'
207	      });
208	    }
209	
210	    // Delete files (soft delete by setting deletedAt)
211	    const deletedCount = await File.update(
212	      { deletedAt: new Date() },
213	      {
214	        where: {
215	          id: { [Op.in]: validIds },
216	          deletedAt: null
217	        }
218	      }
219	    );
220	
221	    res.json({
222	      success: true,
223	      message: `Successfully deleted ${deletedCount[0]} file(s)`,
224	      deletedCount: deletedCount[0]
225	    });
226	  } catch (error) {
227	    console.error('Error bulk deleting files:', error);
228	    res.status(500).json({
229	      success: false,
230	      message: 'Error deleting files: ' + error.message
231	    });
232	  }
233	});

236	 * Delete single file

238	router.delete('/:id', globalAdmin, async (req, res) => {

241	
242	    // Validate UUID

313	/**
314	 * Get statistics for parsing log

316	router.get('/stats/summary', globalAdmin, async (req, res) => {
317	  try {
318	    const [stats] = await sequelize.query(`
319	      SELECT 
320	        COUNT(*)::int as total,
321	        COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'parsed')::int as parsed,
322	        COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'failed')::int as failed,
323	        COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'processing')::int as processing,
324	        COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'pending')::int as pending,
325	        COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'duplicate')::int as duplicate,
326	        COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE "processingMethod" = 'vision')::int as vision,
327	        COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE "processingMethod" = 'documentai')::int as documentai,
328	        COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE "customerId" IS NOT NULL)::int as assigned
329	      FROM files
330	      WHERE "deletedAt" IS NULL
331	    `);
332	
333	    res.json({
334	      success: true,
335	      stats: stats[0] || {}
336	    });
337	  } catch (error) {
338	    console.error('Error fetching stats:', error);
339	    res.status(500).json({
340	      success: false,
341	      message: 'Error fetching stats: ' + error.message
342	    });

346	/**
347	 * Test Import - Create a file with mock parsed data to test import logic
348	 * This bypasses FTP and parsing, allowing you to test the matching and document creation logic
349	 * 
350	 * Example request body:
351	 * {
352	 *   "fileName": "test-invoice-123.pdf",
353	 *   "fileType": "invoice",
354	 *   "accountNumber": "12345",  // Will try to match to company
355	 *   "invoiceNumber": "INV-001",
356	 *   "amount": 1500.00,
357	 *   "date": "2025-12-09"
358	 * }
359	 */
360	router.post('/test-import', auth, globalAdmin, async (req, res) => {
361	  try {
362	    const rawFileName = req.body.fileName;
363	    const sanitizedFileName = rawFileName ? path.basename(rawFileName) : null;
364	    if (sanitizedFileName && !/^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$/.test(sanitizedFileName)) {
365	      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Invalid fileName: only alphanumeric characters, dots, hyphens, and underscores are allowed' });
366	    }
367	    const fileName = sanitizedFileName || `test-invoice-${Date.now()}.pdf`;
368	    const {
369	      fileType = 'invoice', // 'invoice', 'credit_note', 'statement'
370	      parsedData = {},
371	      accountNumber = null, // Will try to match to company
372	      invoiceNumber = null,
373	      amount = null,
374	      date = null,
375	      companyId = null // Optional: directly assign to a company
376	    } = req.body;
377	
378	    // Build parsed data from provided fields or use provided parsedData
379	    const mockParsedData = {
380	      invoiceNumber: invoiceNumber || parsedData.invoiceNumber || `INV-${Date.now()}`,
381	      accountNumber: accountNumber || parsedData.accountNumber || null,
382	      amount: amount !== null ? parseFloat(amount) : (parsedData.amount || 100.00),
383	      date: date ? new Date(date).toISOString() : (parsedData.date || new Date().toISOString()),
384	      documentType: fileType,
385	      ...parsedData // Allow overriding with full parsedData object
386	    };
387	
388	    // Create a dummy file path (file doesn't need to exist for testing)
389	    const { ensureStorageDirs, getStorageDir, getFilePath } = require('../config/storage');
390	    ensureStorageDirs();
391	    const dummyFilePath = getFilePath('invoices', fileName);
392	
393	    // Create a dummy file (empty file for testing)
394	    if (!fs.existsSync(dummyFilePath)) {
395	      fs.writeFileSync(dummyFilePath, Buffer.from('TEST FILE - Created for import testing'));
396	    }
397	
398	    const fileStats = fs.statSync(dummyFilePath);
399	    const fileHash = crypto.createHash('sha256').update(fs.readFileSync(dummyFilePath)).digest('hex');
400	
401	    // Try to match company by account number if provided
402	    let matchedCompanyId = companyId;
403	    if (!matchedCompanyId && mockParsedData.accountNumber) {
404	      const company = await Company.findOne({
405	        where: {
406	          referenceNo: mockParsedData.accountNumber.toString()
407	        }
408	      });
409	      
410	      if (company) {
411	        matchedCompanyId = company.id;
412	        console.log(`✅ Matched company: ${company.name} (${company.referenceNo})`);
413	      } else {
414	        console.log(`⚠️  No company found with account number: ${mockParsedData.accountNumber}`);
415	      }
416	    }
417	
418	    // Determine status based on whether company was matched
419	    let status = 'parsed';
420	    let failureReason = null;
421	    
422	    if (!matchedCompanyId && mockParsedData.accountNumber) {
423	      status = 'unallocated';
424	      failureReason = 'unallocated';
425	    } else if (!matchedCompanyId && !mockParsedData.accountNumber) {
426	      status = 'unallocated';
427	      failureReason = 'unallocated';
428	    }
429	
430	    // Create file record (File model uses customerId, but we'll store Company ID there)
431	    const file = await File.create({
432	      fileName,
433	      fileHash,
434	      filePath: dummyFilePath,
435	      fileSize: fileStats.size,
436	      fileType: fileType,
437	      status: status,
438	      failureReason: failureReason,
439	      parsedData: mockParsedData,
440	      processingMethod: 'manual', // Using 'manual' since 'test' is not in the enum
441	      customerId: matchedCompanyId, // File model uses customerId field (legacy, but works with Company)
442	      uploadedById: req.user.userId,
443	      metadata: {
444	        isTestImport: true,
445	        testData: {
446	          accountNumber: mockParsedData.accountNumber,
447	          invoiceNumber: mockParsedData.invoiceNumber,
448	          amount: mockParsedData.amount,
449	          date: mockParsedData.date
450	        }
451	      }
452	    });
453	
454	    // If company was matched, try to create the appropriate document
455	    let document = null;
456	    if (matchedCompanyId && status === 'parsed') {
457	      try {
458	        const issueDate = new Date(mockParsedData.date);
459	        
460	        if (fileType === 'invoice') {
461	          document = await Invoice.create({
462	            companyId: matchedCompanyId,
463	            invoiceNumber: mockParsedData.invoiceNumber,
464	            issueDate: issueDate,
465	            amount: mockParsedData.amount || 0,
466	            status: 'draft', // Valid status: 'draft', 'sent', 'paid', 'overdue', 'cancelled'
467	            metadata: {
468	              source: 'test_import',
469	              fileId: file.id,
470	              parsedData: mockParsedData
471	            }
472	          });
473	        } else if (fileType === 'credit_note') {
474	          document = await CreditNote.create({
475	            companyId: matchedCompanyId,
476	            creditNoteNumber: mockParsedData.invoiceNumber || mockParsedData.creditNumber || `CN-${Date.now()}`,
477	            issueDate: issueDate,
478	            amount: mockParsedData.amount || 0,
479	            status: 'draft', // Valid status: 'draft', 'sent', 'applied', 'cancelled'
480	            metadata: {
481	              source: 'test_import',
482	              fileId: file.id,
483	              parsedData: mockParsedData
484	            }
485	          });
486	        } else if (fileType === 'statement') {
487	          // Statement requires periodStart, periodEnd, openingBalance, closingBalance, etc.
488	          const periodStart = issueDate;
489	          const periodEnd = new Date(issueDate);
490	          periodEnd.setMonth(periodEnd.getMonth() + 1); // Default to 1 month period
491	          
492	          document = await Statement.create({
493	            companyId: matchedCompanyId,
494	            statementNumber: mockParsedData.invoiceNumber || mockParsedData.statementNumber || `STMT-${Date.now()}`,
495	            periodStart: periodStart,
496	            periodEnd: periodEnd,
497	            openingBalance: 0,
498	            closingBalance: mockParsedData.amount || 0,
499	            totalDebits: mockParsedData.amount || 0,
500	            totalCredits: 0,
501	            status: 'draft', // Valid status: 'draft', 'sent', 'acknowledged', 'disputed'
502	            metadata: {
503	              source: 'test_import',
504	              fileId: file.id,
505	              parsedData: mockParsedData
506	            }
507	          });
508	        }
509	        
510	        if (document) {
511	          console.log(`✅ Created ${fileType}: ${document.id}`);
512	        }
513	      } catch (docError) {
514	        console.error(`⚠️  Failed to create ${fileType} document:`, docError.message);
515	        console.error(`   Error details:`, docError.errors || docError);
516	        // Don't fail the import if document creation fails
517	      }
518	    }
519	
520	    res.json({
521	      success: true,
522	      message: `Test import completed: ${status === 'parsed' ? 'Successfully matched and processed' : 'Unallocated - no matching company found'}`,
523	      file: {
524	        id: file.id,
525	        fileName: file.fileName,
526	        status: file.status,
527	        failureReason: file.failureReason
528	      },
529	      company: matchedCompanyId ? {
530	        id: matchedCompanyId,
531	        matched: true
532	      } : {
533	        matched: false,
534	        reason: mockParsedData.accountNumber ? `No company found with account number: ${mockParsedData.accountNumber}` : 'No account number provided'
535	      },
536	      document: document ? {
537	        id: document.id,
538	        type: fileType
539	      } : null,
540	      parsedData: mockParsedData
541	    });
542	  } catch (error) {
543	    console.error('Test import error:', error);
544	    res.status(500).json({
545	      success: false,
546	      message: 'Error creating test import: ' + error.message
547	    });
548	  }
549	});
550	
551	module.exports = router;
552	
553
```

1	const { sequelize } = require('../config/database');
2	const { Sequelize } = require('sequelize');
3	
4	// Import models
5	const User = require('./User')(sequelize, Sequelize.DataTypes);
6	const Report = require('./Report')(sequelize, Sequelize.DataTypes);
7	const Settings = require('./Settings')(sequelize, Sequelize.DataTypes);
8	const Customer = require('./Customer')(sequelize, Sequelize.DataTypes);
9	const File = require('./File')(sequelize, Sequelize.DataTypes);
10	const Template = require('./SupplierTemplate')(sequelize, Sequelize.DataTypes);
11	const Company = require('./Company')(sequelize, Sequelize.DataTypes);
12	const UserCompany = require('./UserCompany')(sequelize, Sequelize.DataTypes);
13	const Invoice = require('./Invoice')(sequelize, Sequelize.DataTypes);
14	const CreditNote = require('./CreditNote')(sequelize, Sequelize.DataTypes);
15	const Statement = require('./Statement')(sequelize, Sequelize.DataTypes);
16	const EmailTemplate = require('./EmailTemplate')(sequelize, Sequelize.DataTypes);
17	const DocumentQuery = require('./DocumentQuery')(sequelize, Sequelize.DataTypes);
18	const PendingRegistration = require('./PendingRegistration')(sequelize, Sequelize.DataTypes);
19	const ImportTransaction = require('./ImportTransaction')(sequelize, Sequelize.DataTypes);
20	const ColumnConfiguration = require('./ColumnConfiguration')(sequelize, Sequelize.DataTypes);
21	const EmailLog = require('./EmailLog')(sequelize, Sequelize.DataTypes);
22	const Supplier = require('./Supplier')(sequelize);
23	const SupplierTemplate = require('./SupplierTemplateSupplier')(sequelize);
24	const SupplierDocument = require('./SupplierDocument')(sequelize);
25	const SupplierFile = require('./SupplierFile')(sequelize);
26	
27	// Define associations
28	// User associations
29	User.hasMany(User, { foreignKey: 'addedById', as: 'addedUsers' });
30	User.belongsTo(User, { foreignKey: 'addedById', as: 'addedByUser' });
31	
32	User.hasMany(Report, { foreignKey: 'createdById', as: 'reports' });
33	Report.belongsTo(User, { foreignKey: 'createdById', as: 'createdBy' });
34	
35	// Customer associations (Parent/Child hierarchy)
36	Customer.hasMany(Customer, { 
37	  foreignKey: 'parentId', 
38	  as: 'children',
39	  onDelete: 'SET NULL'
40	});
41	Customer.belongsTo(Customer, { 
42	  foreignKey: 'parentId', 
43	  as: 'parent',
44	  onDelete: 'SET NULL'
45	});
46	
47	// Customer can have multiple reports
48	Customer.hasMany(Report, { foreignKey: 'customerId', as: 'reports' });
49	Report.belongsTo(Customer, { foreignKey: 'customerId', as: 'customer' });
50	
51	// Customer can have multiple files
52	Customer.hasMany(File, { foreignKey: 'customerId', as: 'files' });
53	File.belongsTo(Customer, { foreignKey: 'customerId', as: 'customer' });
54	
55	// User can have multiple files (uploaded by)
56	User.hasMany(File, { foreignKey: 'uploadedById', as: 'uploadedFiles' });
57	File.belongsTo(User, { foreignKey: 'uploadedById', as: 'uploadedBy' });
58	
59	// User can have multiple files (manually edited by)
60	User.hasMany(File, { foreignKey: 'manuallyEditedById', as: 'manuallyEditedFiles' });
61	File.belongsTo(User, { foreignKey: 'manuallyEditedById', as: 'manuallyEditedBy' });
62	
63	// Company associations
64	User.hasMany(Company, { foreignKey: 'createdById', as: 'createdCompanies' });
65	Company.belongsTo(User, { foreignKey: 'createdById', as: 'createdBy' });
66	
67	// Company hierarchy (Parent/Child)
68	Company.hasMany(Company, { 
69	  foreignKey: 'parentId', 
70	  as: 'children',
71	  onDelete: 'SET NULL'
72	});
73	Company.belongsTo(Company, { 
74	  foreignKey: 'parentId', 
75	  as: 'parent',
76	  onDelete: 'SET NULL'
77	});
78	
79	// Primary Contact association (Company has one primary contact user for notifications)
80	Company.belongsTo(User, { foreignKey: 'primaryContactId', as: 'primaryContact' });
81	User.hasMany(Company, { foreignKey: 'primaryContactId', as: 'primaryContactFor' });
82	
83	// User-Company many-to-many association
84	User.belongsToMany(Company, {
85	  through: UserCompany,
86	  foreignKey: 'userId',
87	  otherKey: 'companyId',
88	  as: 'companies'
89	});
90	
91	Company.belongsToMany(User, {
92	  through: UserCompany,
93	  foreignKey: 'companyId',
94	  otherKey: 'userId',
95	  as: 'users'
96	});
97	
98	// Document associations (Invoices, Credit Notes, Statements)
99	Company.hasMany(Invoice, { foreignKey: 'companyId', as: 'invoices' });
100	Invoice.belongsTo(Company, { foreignKey: 'companyId', as: 'company' });
101	
102	Company.hasMany(CreditNote, { foreignKey: 'companyId', as: 'creditNotes' });
103	CreditNote.belongsTo(Company, { foreignKey: 'companyId', as: 'company' });
104	
105	Company.hasMany(Statement, { foreignKey: 'companyId', as: 'statements' });
106	Statement.belongsTo(Company, { foreignKey: 'companyId', as: 'company' });
107	
108	// Credit Note can be related to an Invoice
109	Invoice.hasMany(CreditNote, { foreignKey: 'invoiceId', as: 'creditNotes' });
110	CreditNote.belongsTo(Invoice, { foreignKey: 'invoiceId', as: 'invoice' });
111	
112	// User associations for documents
113	User.hasMany(Invoice, { foreignKey: 'createdById', as: 'createdInvoices' });
114	Invoice.belongsTo(User, { foreignKey: 'createdById', as: 'createdBy' });
115	
116	User.hasMany(CreditNote, { foreignKey: 'createdById', as: 'createdCreditNotes' });
117	CreditNote.belongsTo(User, { foreignKey: 'createdById', as: 'createdBy' });
118	
119	User.hasMany(Statement, { foreignKey: 'createdById', as: 'createdStatements' });
120	Statement.belongsTo(User, { foreignKey: 'createdById', as: 'createdBy' });
121	
122	// Document Query associations
123	Company.hasMany(DocumentQuery, { foreignKey: 'companyId', as: 'queries' });
124	DocumentQuery.belongsTo(Company, { foreignKey: 'companyId', as: 'company' });
125	
126	User.hasMany(DocumentQuery, { foreignKey: 'lastMessageBy', as: 'queries' });
127	DocumentQuery.belongsTo(User, { foreignKey: 'lastMessageBy', as: 'lastMessageByUser' });
128	
129	// Pending Registration associations
130	User.hasMany(PendingRegistration, { foreignKey: 'reviewedById', as: 'reviewedRegistrations' });
131	PendingRegistration.belongsTo(User, { foreignKey: 'reviewedById', as: 'reviewedBy' });
132	
133	User.hasOne(PendingRegistration, { foreignKey: 'createdUserId', as: 'createdFromRegistration' });
134	PendingRegistration.belongsTo(User, { foreignKey: 'createdUserId', as: 'createdUser' });
135	
136	// Import Transaction associations
137	User.hasMany(ImportTransaction, { foreignKey: 'userId', as: 'importTransactions' });
138	ImportTransaction.belongsTo(User, { foreignKey: 'userId', as: 'user' });
139	
140	// Supplier associations
141	User.hasMany(Supplier, { foreignKey: 'createdById', as: 'createdSuppliers' });
142	Supplier.belongsTo(User, { foreignKey: 'createdById', as: 'createdBy' });
143

Now let me analyze these files carefully for real security vulnerabilities.

## SECURITY ANALYSIS REPORT

Based on my thorough analysis of the requested files, I found **ONE HIGH-CONFIDENCE SECURITY VULNERABILITY**:

---

### FINDING #1: PATH TRAVERSAL VIA FTP FOLDER CONFIGURATION

**File:** `backend/jobs/fileImport.js`

**Location:** Lines 160-162

**Vulnerable Code:**
```javascript
// Line 160 - ftpFolder comes from job.data.ftpFolder (user-controlled)
const relativeSourcePath = ftpFolder ? `${ftpFolder}/${fileName}` : fileName;

// Line 162 - This path is then passed to moveFile() without sanitization
const dateFolder = new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0];
const duplicatePath = `${folderStructure.processed}/Duplicates/${dateFolder}/${fileName}`;
```

**Attack Vector:**
- The `ftpFolder` value originates from `ftpScanner.js` at line 72 and 134, which comes from user configuration in `settings.ftp.folders[]`
- An attacker with Settings modification privileges could inject path traversal sequences like `../../` into `ftpConfig.folders[].path`
- When constructing `folderPath` (line 53-64 in ftpScanner.js), no validation prevents `../` sequences
- This leads to `relativeSourcePath` like `../../sensitive/files/invoice.pdf`
- The file operations then manipulate paths outside the intended FTP folder structure

**Impact:**
- **Access to unauthorized files:** Attacker can read/move files outside intended FTP directory
- **Directory traversal:** Can target parent directories like `../../../etc/` 
- **File manipulation:** Can move/copy files to unintended locations
- **Data breach:** Access to sensitive documents stored in sibling/parent directories

**Confidence:** 9/10 (High confidence - path concatenation without validation is clear vulnerability, though requires Settings-level access)

---

### ANALYSIS OF OTHER FILES

**ftpScanner.js** (Lines 52-64): Path construction with user-supplied `folder.path` lacks validation. No checks for `../`, `..\\`, or absolute paths. However, this primarily creates FTP paths which are still vulnerable.

**localFolderScanner.js**: Safe - uses `path.resolve()` and `path.join()` properly, with hardcoded base paths from config/storage.

**supplierDocumentProcessor.js**: Safe - paths are constructed using `path.join()` with validated components. The `fileName` is from file system operations, not user input.

**pdfExtractor.js** (Lines 14-28): Safe - only uses `fs.readFileSync()` with pre-validated file paths. No command injection via PDF processing. The `pdf-parse` library handles PDF parsing safely without shell commands.

**parsing.js**: This file was returned as `pdfExtractor.js` content. No eval(), Function(), or command injection vulnerabilities detected.

---

### RECOMMENDATION

**Immediate Action Required:**
1. Add path validation in `ftpScanner.js` line 53: Reject any `folder.path` containing `../`, `..\\`, or starting with `/`
2. Implement whitelist validation for FTP folder paths in Settings
3. Use `path.normalize()` and verify normalized path stays within base directory

**Example Fix Pattern:**
```javascript
const folderPath = folder.path;
const normalized = path.normalize(folderPath);
if (normalized.startsWith('..') || path.isAbsolute(normalized)) {
  throw new Error(`Invalid folder path: ${folderPath}`);
}
```
```

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-04-30 08:19:53Z)**_

Perfect. I now have all the information I need. Let me provide my assessment for each finding:

## SECURITY FINDINGS ASSESSMENT

**FINDING 1: JWT Role Manipulation - Authorization Bypass**

**Real vulnerability? NO**  
**Confidence score: 2/10**

**Explanation:**  
The JWT is properly signed and verified using `jwt.verify(token, process.env.JWT_SECRET)` in auth.js (line 11), permissions.js (lines 25, 60, 93, 122, 151), globalAdmin.js (line 12), and roleCheck.js (lines 15, 42). The implementation uses HMAC signing (default jsonwebtoken behavior with a secret), which is cryptographically sound. An attacker cannot modify JWT claims without the secret. The code properly:
- Verifies the signature on every request (no validation bypassed)
- Stores the role in the JWT payload and validates it against a centralized permissions map (permissions.js)
- Enforces permission checks in middleware before allowing access

The role is only used in permission lookups against the hardcoded PERMISSIONS object. There's no path to privilege escalation through JWT manipulation without possession of the JWT_SECRET (which is a trusted environment variable). This is a design non-issue - it's correct.

---

**FINDING 2: Document Access Control via JWT Caching**

**Real vulnerability? NO**  
**Confidence score: 1/10**

**Explanation:**  
This is a FALSE POSITIVE. The company IDs are NOT cached in the JWT. Let me trace the flow:

1. `documentAccess.js` checkDocumentAccess middleware (line 16) runs on each request
2. It explicitly queries the database on every request:
   - Line 35: `User.findByPk(user.userId, { include: [{ model: Company, ... }] })` - fetches the user with fresh company assignments from the database
   - Line 53: Checks `userWithCompanies.allCompanies` fresh from DB
   - Lines 72-73: Calls `getAccessibleCompanyIds(assignedCompanyIds)` which queries the Company model in companyHierarchy.js
3. The result (accessible company IDs) is stored in `req.accessibleCompanyIds` - this is request-scoped, not cached in the JWT
4. Every new request repeats this fetch, so if a user's company assignments change, the change is reflected immediately on the next request

No JWT caching of company access. The implementation is correct.

---

**FINDING 3: Insecure CSP Headers**

**Real vulnerability? NO (but note the architecture context)**  
**Confidence score: 3/10**

**Explanation:**  
In server.js (lines 307-328), the CSP configuration does include:
- `scriptSrc: ["'self'", "'unsafe-inline'", "'unsafe-eval'"]` (line 311)
- `styleSrc: ["'self'", "'unsafe-inline'", ...]` (line 312)

However, this is NOT an exploitable vulnerability for these reasons:

1. **React SPA architecture justification**: The comment on line 311 explicitly states "Allow inline scripts for React" - this is a known, intentional trade-off for React SPAs. React's build system and dev server often require inline scripts to function properly.

2. **No XSS injection path identified**: CSP with unsafe-inline only becomes dangerous if there's a reflected XSS vulnerability that allows injecting arbitrary scripts. The application would need:
   - User input reflected in HTML without sanitization
   - An XSS injection point
   
   Even with `unsafe-inline`, a properly structured CSP still blocks:
   - External script loading (not in scriptSrc)
   - Object embeds (`objectSrc: ["'none'"]`)
   - Frame embeds restricted to `frameSrc: ["'self'"]`

3. **This is a hardening concern, not an exploitable vulnerability**: Per your false positive rules, "lack of hardening measures should not be reported." The CSP is intentionally relaxed for a legitimate React architecture reason, not broken. A proper fix would require moving to a Content Security Policy compatible with React without unsafe-inline (e.g., using nonces or CSP hash-src), which is a future improvement, not a security bug.

---

## SUMMARY

| Finding | Real? | Confidence | Reason |
|---------|-------|-----------|---------|
| JWT Role Manipulation | NO | 2/10 | JWTs properly signed/verified with secret; no modification possible |
| Document Access JWT Caching | NO | 1/10 | Company IDs fetched fresh from DB on every request, not cached in JWT |
| Insecure CSP Headers | NO | 3/10 | unsafe-inline is intentional for React SPA; no XSS vector identified; hardening concern, not exploitable vulnerability |

**Recommendation:** All three findings are false positives. None meet the 8/10 confidence threshold for exploitation.

 `./backend/jobs/ftpScanner.js`

```text
1	const { listFiles } = require('../utils/ftp');
2	const { File, Settings } = require('../models');
3	const { fileImportQueue } = require('../config/queue');
4	const { Op } = require('sequelize');
5	
6	/**
7	 * Scheduled job to scan FTP folders and queue files for import
8	 * This should be run periodically (e.g., every 5-15 minutes)
9	 */
10	
11	async function scanFTPFolders() {
12	  try {
13	    console.log('🔍 Starting FTP folder scan...');
14	    
15	    const settings = await Settings.getSettings();
16	    
17	    if (!settings.ftp || !settings.ftp.enabled) {
18	      console.log('ℹ️  FTP import is not enabled');
19	      return { scanned: 0, queued: 0 };
20	    }
21	    
22	    const ftpConfig = settings.ftp;
23	    let totalScanned = 0;
24	    let totalQueued = 0;
25	    
26	    // Single folder mode - scan base directory only
27	    if (ftpConfig.singleFolderMode) {
28	      console.log(`📁 Scanning single folder: ${ftpConfig.directory}`);
29	      
30	      const files = await listFiles(ftpConfig);
31	      totalScanned += files.length;
32	      
33	      for (const file of files) {
34	        const queued = await queueFileIfNew(file, ftpConfig, ftpConfig.directory, ftpConfig.singleFolderFileType || 'invoice');
35	        if (queued) totalQueued++;
36	      }
37	    } else {
38	      // Multi-folder mode - scan each configured folder
39	      const folders = ftpConfig.folders || [];
40	      const folderStructure = ftpConfig.folderStructure || {
41	        unprocessed: '/Unprocessed',
42	        processed: '/Processed',
43	        failed: '/Failed'
44	      };
45	      
46	      for (const folder of folders) {
47	        if (!folder.enabled) {
48	          console.log(`⏭️  Skipping disabled folder: ${folder.path}`);
49	          continue;
50	        }
51	        
52	        // Use folder path as-is (should be relative to base directory)
53	        const folderPath = folder.path;
54	        const fileType = folder.fileType || 'auto'; // 'auto' means detect from filename
55	        
56	        console.log(`📁 Scanning folder: ${folderPath} (type: ${fileType === 'auto' ? 'auto-detect' : fileType})`);
57	        
58	        try {
59	          // Create folder-specific FTP config
60	          const folderConfig = {
61	            ...ftpConfig,
62	            directory: ftpConfig.directory === '/' 
63	              ? folderPath 
64	              : `${ftpConfig.directory}${folderPath}`
65	          };
66	          
67	          const files = await listFiles(folderConfig);
68	          totalScanned += files.length;
69	          
70	          for (const file of files) {
71	            // Always use 'auto' for file type - will be detected from PDF content
72	            const queued = await queueFileIfNew(file, folderConfig, folderPath, 'auto');
73	            if (queued) totalQueued++;
74	          }
75	        } catch (error) {
76	          console.error(`❌ Error scanning folder ${folderPath}:`, error.message);
77	        }
78	      }
79	    }
80	    
81	    console.log(`✅ FTP scan completed: ${totalScanned} files scanned, ${totalQueued} new files queued`);
82	    
83	    return {
84	      scanned: totalScanned,
85	      queued: totalQueued
86	    };
87	  } catch (error) {
88	    console.error('❌ FTP scan error:', error.message);
89	    throw error;
90	  }
91	}
92	
93	/**
94	 * Queue a file for import if it hasn't been processed before
95	 */
96	async function queueFileIfNew(file, ftpConfig, folderPath, fileType) {
97	  try {
98	    // Check if file already exists in queue (by filename and folder)
99	    // BullMQ uses getJobs with state names including 'wait'
100	    const existingJobs = await fileImportQueue.getJobs(['waiting', 'wait', 'active', 'delayed']);
101	    const alreadyQueued = existingJobs.some(job => 
102	      job.data.fileName === file.name && 
103	      job.data.ftpFolder === folderPath
104	    );
105	    
106	    if (alreadyQueued) {
107	      console.log(`⏭️  File already queued: ${file.name}`);
108	      return false;
109	    }
110	    
111	    // Check if file was recently processed (within last hour)
112	    // This prevents re-queuing files that are currently being processed
113	    const recentFile = await File.findOne({
114	      where: {
115	        fileName: file.name,
116	        ftpFolder: folderPath,
117	        uploadedAt: {
118	          [Op.gte]: new Date(Date.now() - 60 * 60 * 1000) // Last hour
119	        }
120	      }
121	    });
122	    
123	    if (recentFile) {
124	      console.log(`⏭️  File recently processed: ${file.name}`);
125	      return false;
126	    }
127	    
128	    // Queue the file (BullMQ format: name, data, options)
129	    await fileImportQueue.add('file-import', {
130	      fileName: file.name,
131	      remotePath: file.name,
132	      ftpConfig,
133	      fileType,
134	      ftpFolder: folderPath
135	    }, {
136	      jobId: `ftp-import-${Date.now()}-${file.name}`,
137	      removeOnComplete: true,
138	      removeOnFail: false
139	    });
140	    
141	    console.log(`✅ Queued file: ${file.name} (${fileType})`);
142	    return true;
143	  } catch (error) {
144	    console.error(`❌ Error queueing file ${file.name}:`, error.message);
145	    return false;
146	  }
147	}
148	
149	module.exports = {
150	  scanFTPFolders
151	};
152	
153
```
</details></tool-use>
