198	              extractedCode: extractedSupplierCode,

200	            }];
201	            await supplierFile.save();

223	    
224	    await job.updateProgress(30);
225	    
226	    // Check for duplicate
227	    const existingDocument = await SupplierDocument.findOne({

233

343	        missingFields: validation.missing,

348

360	    const documentDate = parsedData.documentDate || parsedData.document_date || parsedData.invoiceDate || parsedData.invoice_date || parsedData.date;
361	    const dueDate = parsedData.dueDate || parsedData.due_date || null;
362	    const amount = parseFloat(parsedData.totalAmount || parsedData.total_amount || parsedData.amount || parsedData.total || 0);
363	    const taxAmount = parseFloat(parsedData.taxAmount || parsedData.tax_amount || parsedData.tax || 0);
364	    const vatAmount = parseFloat(parsedData.vatAmount || parsedData.vat_amount || parsedData.vat || 0);
365	    const items = parsedData.items || [];
366	    
367	    // Validate document date

369	    if (documentDate) {
370	      docDate = new Date(documentDate);
371	      if (isNaN(docDate.getTime())) {

373	      }
374	    } else {

376	    }

378	    await job.updateProgress(70);
379

391	      if (existingDoc) {

394	        ensureDir(path.dirname(failedPath));

396	        fs.unlinkSync(filePath);
397	        
398	        const dupInvoiceResult = {

402	          duplicateDocumentId: existingDoc.id,

408	        if (jobImportId) {
409	          await supplierImportStore.addResult(jobImportId, dupInvoiceResult);
410	        }
411	        
412	        return dupInvoiceResult;
413	      }
414	    }
415	    
416	    // Move file to processed storage: /mnt/data/processed/supplier-documents/{supplierId}/{YYYY}/{MM}/{DD}/
417	    const year = docDate.getFullYear().toString();
418	    const month = String(docDate.getMonth() + 1).padStart(2, '0');
419	    const day = String(docDate.getDate()).padStart(2, '0');
420	    const datedFolder = path.join(PROCESSED_SUPPLIER_DOCUMENTS, resolvedSupplierId.toString(), year, month, day);
421	    ensureDir(datedFolder);
422	    const finalFileName = `${Date.now()}-${fileName}`;
423	    const finalPath = path.join(datedFolder, finalFileName);
424	    
425	    fs.copyFileSync(filePath, finalPath);
426	    fs.unlinkSync(filePath); // Remove temp file
427	    
428	    await job.updateProgress(80);
429	    
430	    // Create or update supplier document
431	    let document = null;
432	    if (documentId) {
433	      // Re-parsing existing document
434	      document = await SupplierDocument.findByPk(documentId);
435	      if (!document) {
436	        throw new Error(`Document not found: ${documentId}`);
437	      }
438	      
439	      // Update fields
440	      document.invoiceNumber = invoiceNumber;
441	      document.poNumber = poNumber;
442	      document.documentDate = docDate;
443	      document.dueDate = dueDate ? new Date(dueDate) : null;
444	      document.amount = amount;
445	      document.taxAmount = taxAmount;
446	      document.vatAmount = vatAmount;
447	      document.extractedFields = parsedData;
448	      document.items = items;
449	      document.fileUrl = finalPath;
450	      document.templateId = template.id;
451	      document.parsedAt = new Date();
452	      document.parsedBy = userId;
453	      document.confidence = 100; // TODO: Calculate confidence
454	      document.status = 'ready';
455	      
456	      await document.save();
457	    } else {
458	      // Create new document
459	      document = await SupplierDocument.create({
460	        supplierId: resolvedSupplierId,
461	        templateId: template.id,
462	        documentType,
463	        invoiceNumber,
464	        poNumber,
465	        documentDate: docDate,
466	        dueDate: dueDate ? new Date(dueDate) : null,
467	        amount,
468	        taxAmount,
469	        vatAmount,
470	        extractedFields: parsedData,
471	        items,

474	        fileHash: calculatedHash,
475	        status: 'ready',
476	        documentStatus: 'ready',
477	        parsedAt: new Date(),
478	        parsedBy: userId,
479	        confidence: 100, // TODO: Calculate confidence

482	    }

489	        supplierFile.supplierDocumentId = document.id;
490	        supplierFile.fileUrl = finalPath;

496	    
497	    // Log activity
498	    await logActivity({
499	      type: ActivityType.SUPPLIER_DOCUMENT_CREATED,
500	      userId: userId,

534

541	        fs.unlinkSync(filePath);

551	        if (supplierFile) {
552	          supplierFile.status = 'failed';
553	          supplierFile.processingErrors = [{ message: error.message }];
554	          await supplierFile.save();

570	        });

573	      }
574	    }

576	    throw error;
577	  }
578	}
579	
580	/**
581	 * Extract supplier identifiers (code and name) from raw document text
582	 * Used for auto-detection when no supplierId is provided
583	 * @param {string} text - Raw text extracted from document
584	 * @returns {Object} Object with supplierCode and supplierName if found
585	 */
586	function extractSupplierIdentifiersFromText(text) {
587	  const result = {
588	    supplierCode: null,
589	    supplierName: null
590	  };
591	  
592	  if (!text || typeof text !== 'string') {
593	    return result;
594	  }
595	  
596	  // Common patterns for supplier/account codes
597	  const codePatterns = [
598	    // Account Number patterns
599	    /account\s*(?:number|no\.?|#|code)?[:\s]*([A-Z0-9][\w\-]{2,20})/i,
600	    /a\/c\s*(?:no\.?|#)?[:\s]*([A-Z0-9][\w\-]{2,20})/i,
601	    /customer\s*(?:number|no\.?|#|code|ref)?[:\s]*([A-Z0-9][\w\-]{2,20})/i,
602	    /cust\.?\s*(?:no\.?|#|code)?[:\s]*([A-Z0-9][\w\-]{2,20})/i,
603	    /our\s*ref(?:erence)?[:\s]*([A-Z0-9][\w\-]{2,20})/i,
604	    /your\s*ref(?:erence)?[:\s]*([A-Z0-9][\w\-]{2,20})/i,
605	    /supplier\s*(?:code|ref|number|no\.?|#)?[:\s]*([A-Z0-9][\w\-]{2,20})/i
606	  ];
607	  
608	  // Try each pattern to find supplier code
609	  for (const pattern of codePatterns) {
610	    const match = text.match(pattern);
611	    if (match && match[1]) {
612	      const code = match[1].trim();
613	      // Validate: should be alphanumeric, not too short or too long
614	      if (code.length >= 3 && code.length <= 20 && !/^(no|yes|na|n\/a|the|and|for)$/i.test(code)) {
615	        result.supplierCode = code;
616	        break;
617	      }
618	    }
619	  }
620	  
621	  // Common patterns for supplier/company names
622	  // Look for "From:", "Supplier:", company name at top of document, etc.
623	  const namePatterns = [
624	    /(?:from|supplier|vendor|billed\s*by|invoiced\s*by)[:\s]+([A-Z][A-Za-z0-9\s\-&',\.]+(?:Ltd|Limited|Inc|Corp|PLC|LLP|LLC)?)/i,
625	    /^([A-Z][A-Z\s\-&'\.]{5,50}(?:Ltd|Limited|Inc|Corp|PLC|LLP|LLC)?)/m
626	  ];
627	  
628	  // Try each pattern to find supplier name
629	  for (const pattern of namePatterns) {
630	    const match = text.match(pattern);
631	    if (match && match[1]) {
632	      const name = match[1].trim();
633	      // Validate: should be reasonable length and not common words
634	      if (name.length >= 3 && name.length <= 100) {
635	        // Clean up the name - remove trailing punctuation
636	        result.supplierName = name.replace(/[,\.\s]+$/, '').trim();
637	        break;
638	      }
639	    }
640	  }
641	  
642	  return result;
643	}
644	
645	module.exports = {
646	  processSupplierDocumentImport,
647	  extractSupplierIdentifiersFromText // Export for testing
648	};
649
```

1	const documentai = require('@google-cloud/documentai');
2	const fs = require('fs');

4	/**
5	 * Test Google Document AI connection
6	 */
7	async function testDocumentAIConnection(documentAIConfig = {}) {
8	  let client = null;
9	  
10	  try {
11	    const projectId = documentAIConfig.projectId || process.env.GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT_ID;
12	    const location = documentAIConfig.location || process.env.GOOGLE_CLOUD_LOCATION || 'us';
13	    const processorId = documentAIConfig.processorId || process.env.GOOGLE_DOCUMENT_AI_PROCESSOR_ID;
14	    
15	    if (!projectId || !processorId) {
16	      return {
17	        success: false,
18	        message: 'Document AI requires both Project ID and Processor ID. Please configure these in Parsing Provider settings.',
19	        error: 'Missing configuration'
20	      };
21	    }
22	    
23	    // Initialize client with credentials
24	    // Priority: 1) JSON from env var, 2) JSON from database, 3) File path from env var, 4) File path from settings
25	    const credentialsJson = process.env.GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS_JSON || 
26	                           documentAIConfig.credentialsJson;
27	    const credentialsPath = process.env.GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS || 
28	                           documentAIConfig.credentialsPath;
29	    
30	    if (credentialsJson) {
31	      // Use JSON from environment variable or database
32	      try {
33	        const credentials = typeof credentialsJson === 'string' 
34	          ? JSON.parse(credentialsJson) 
35	          : credentialsJson;
36	        client = new documentai.DocumentProcessorServiceClient({
37	          credentials: credentials
38	        });
39	      } catch (parseError) {
40	        return {
41	          success: false,
42	          message: `Invalid JSON in credentials: ${parseError.message}`,
43	          error: 'Invalid JSON'
44	        };
45	      }
46	    } else if (credentialsPath) {
47	      if (!fs.existsSync(credentialsPath)) {
48	        return {
49	          success: false,
50	          message: `Credentials file not found: ${credentialsPath}`,
51	          error: 'File not found'
52	        };
53	      }
54	      
55	      client = new documentai.DocumentProcessorServiceClient({
56	        keyFilename: credentialsPath
57	      });
58	    } else {
59	      client = new documentai.DocumentProcessorServiceClient();
60	    }
61	    
62	    // Test by getting processor information
63	    const processorName = `projects/${projectId}/locations/${location}/processors/${processorId}`;
64	    
65	    try {
66	      const [processor] = await client.getProcessor({
67	        name: processorName
68	      });
69	      
70	      return {
71	        success: true,
72	        message: 'Successfully connected to Google Document AI',
73	        details: {
74	          projectId,
75	          location,
76	          processorId,
77	          processorName: processor.displayName || 'N/A',
78	          processorType: processor.type || 'N/A',
79	          credentialsPath: credentialsPath ? 'Set' : 'Using default'
80	        }
81	      };
82	    } catch (processorError) {
83	      if (processorError.message.includes('NOT_FOUND')) {
84	        return {
85	          success: false,
86	          message: `Processor not found: ${processorId}. Please check that the Processor ID is correct and exists in your project.`,
87	          error: processorError.message,
88	          suggestion: 'Create a processor in Google Cloud Console → Document AI → Processors'
89	        };
90	      }
91	      throw processorError;
92	    }
93	  } catch (error) {
94	    const errorMessage = error.message || '';
95	    
96	    if (errorMessage.includes('ENOENT') || errorMessage.includes('not found')) {
97	      return {
98	        success: false,
99	        message: 'Credentials file not found. Please check your GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS environment variable.',
100	        error: errorMessage
101	      };
102	    }
103	    
104	    if (errorMessage.includes('PERMISSION_DENIED') || errorMessage.includes('403')) {
105	      return {
106	        success: false,
107	        message: 'Permission denied. Please check that your service account has the "Document AI API User" role enabled.',
108	        error: errorMessage,
109	        suggestion: 'Enable the Document AI API in Google Cloud Console and grant the service account the necessary permissions.'
110	      };
111	    }
112	    
113	    if (errorMessage.includes('UNAUTHENTICATED') || errorMessage.includes('401')) {
114	      return {
115	        success: false,
116	        message: 'Authentication failed. Please check that your service account JSON file is valid.',
117	        error: errorMessage
118	      };
119	    }
120	    
121	    if (errorMessage.includes('API not enabled')) {
122	      return {
123	        success: false,
124	        message: 'Document AI API is not enabled for your project. Please enable it in Google Cloud Console.',
125	        error: errorMessage,
126	        suggestion: 'Go to Google Cloud Console → APIs & Services → Enable APIs → Search for "Document AI API" and enable it.'
127	      };
128	    }
129	    
130	    return {
131	      success: false,
132	      message: `Document AI connection failed: ${errorMessage}`,
133	      error: errorMessage
134	    };
135	  }
136	}
137	
138	module.exports = {
139	  testDocumentAIConnection
140	};
141
```

1	/**
2	 * Local PDF text extraction using pdf-parse
3	 * No cloud costs - extracts embedded text directly from PDFs
4	 */
5	
6	const pdfParse = require('pdf-parse');
7	const fs = require('fs');
8	
9	/**
10	 * Extract text from PDF file
11	 * @param {string} filePath - Path to PDF file
12	 * @returns {Promise<Object>} - Extracted text and metadata
13	 */
14	async function extractTextFromPDF(filePath) {
15	  try {
16	    const dataBuffer = fs.readFileSync(filePath);
17	    const data = await pdfParse(dataBuffer);
18	    
19	    return {
20	      text: data.text,
21	      numPages: data.numpages,
22	      info: data.info,
23	      metadata: data.metadata,
24	      version: data.version
25	    };
26	  } catch (error) {
27	    throw new Error(`Failed to extract text from PDF: ${error.message}`);
28	  }
29	}
30	
31	/**
32	 * Extract text and basic invoice fields using simple patterns
33	 * @param {string} filePath - Path to PDF file
34	 * @returns {Promise<Object>} - Parsed invoice data
35	 */
36	async function extractInvoiceData(filePath) {
37	  const extracted = await extractTextFromPDF(filePath);
38	  const text = extracted.text;
39	  
40	  // Basic field extraction using common patterns
41	  // These will be replaced by supplier-specific templates
42	  const invoiceNumber = extractInvoiceNumber(text);
43	  const date = extractDate(text);
44	  const amount = extractAmount(text);
45	  const accountNumber = extractAccountNumber(text);
46	  const customerName = extractCustomerName(text);
47	  const customerPO = extractCustomerPO(text);
48	  const goodsAmount = extractGoodsAmount(text);
49	  const vatAmount = extractVatAmount(text);
50	  
51	  return {
52	    fullText: text,
53	    invoiceNumber,
54	    date,
55	    amount,
56	    accountNumber,
57	    customerName,
58	    customerPO,
59	    goodsAmount,
60	    vatAmount,
61	    numPages: extracted.numPages,
62	    metadata: extracted.metadata,
63	    rawText: text
64	  };
65	}
66	
67	/**
68	 * Extract invoice number using common patterns
69	 * Improved patterns to handle various formats including "Invoice No.5942501" and "Invoice No. 5942480"
70	 */
71	function extractInvoiceNumber(text) {
72	  const patterns = [
73	    // "Invoice No. 5942480" or "Invoice No.5942501" or "Invoice No: 5942501" or "Invoice No 5942501"
74	    // This pattern specifically handles the space after "No." and captures the number
75	    /invoice\s+no\.?\s+:?\s*(\d{4,}[A-Z0-9\-_]*)/i,
76	    // "Invoice #5942501" or "Invoice #: 5942501"
77	    /invoice\s*#\s*:?\s*([A-Z0-9\-_]+)/i,
78	    // "Invoice Number: 5942501" or "Invoice Number 5942501"
79	    /invoice\s+number\s*:?\s*([A-Z0-9\-_]+)/i,
80	    // "INV-5942501" or "INV 5942501"
81	    /(?:^|\s)(INV[-\s]?[A-Z0-9\-_]+)/i,
82	    // "OC1_INV_05942483" format
83	    /(?:^|\s)([A-Z]{2,}[-\s_]?INV[-\s_]?\d+[A-Z0-9\-_]*)/i,
84	    // Generic: "Invoice 5942501"
85	    /invoice\s+([A-Z0-9\-_]+)/i,
86	    // Fallback: long numeric sequences that might be invoice numbers (7+ digits)
87	    /(?:^|\s)(\d{7,}[A-Z0-9\-_]*)/i
88	  ];
89	  
90	  for (const pattern of patterns) {
91	    const match = text.match(pattern);
92	    if (match && match[1]) {
93	      const invoiceNum = match[1].trim();
94	      // Validate it looks like an invoice number
95	      // Must be at least 4 characters (to avoid matching "No" or short numbers)
96	      if (invoiceNum.length >= 4 && invoiceNum.length <= 50) {
97	        // Don't match if it's just "No" or common words
98	        if (!/^(no|yes|na|n\/a)$/i.test(invoiceNum)) {
99	          return invoiceNum;
100	        }
101	      }
102	    }
103	  }
104	  
105	  return null;
106	}
107	
108	/**
109	 * Extract date using common patterns
110	 */
111	function extractDate(text) {
112	  // Common date patterns
113	  const patterns = [
114	    /(\d{1,2}[\/\-]\d{1,2}[\/\-]\d{2,4})/, // DD/MM/YYYY or DD-MM-YYYY
115	    /(\d{4}[\/\-]\d{1,2}[\/\-]\d{1,2})/, // YYYY/MM/DD or YYYY-MM-DD
116	    /(\d{1,2}\s+(?:Jan|Feb|Mar|Apr|May|Jun|Jul|Aug|Sep|Oct|Nov|Dec)[a-z]*\s+\d{2,4})/i, // DD MMM YYYY
117	    /((?:Jan|Feb|Mar|Apr|May|Jun|Jul|Aug|Sep|Oct|Nov|Dec)[a-z]*\s+\d{1,2},?\s+\d{2,4})/i // MMM DD, YYYY
118	  ];
119	  
120	  for (const pattern of patterns) {
121	    const match = text.match(pattern);
122	    if (match && match[1]) {
123	      return match[1].trim();
124	    }
125	  }
126	  
127	  return null;
128	}
129	
130	/**
131	 * Extract amount/total using common patterns
132	 * Handles formats like "Invoice Total£0.00" or "Total: £0.00"
133	 */
134	function extractAmount(text) {
135	  // Look for currency symbols and amounts
136	  const patterns = [
137	    // "Invoice Total£0.00" or "Invoice Total: £0.00" - most specific
138	    /invoice\s+total\s*:?\s*[£$€]?\s*([\d,]+\.?\d{2})/i,
139	    // "Total: £0.00" or "Total £0.00"
140	    /(?:^|\s)total\s*:?\s*[£$€]?\s*([\d,]+\.?\d{2})/i,
141	    // "Amount Due: £0.00"
142	    /(?:amount\s+due|balance\s+due|grand\s+total)\s*:?\s*[£$€]?\s*([\d,]+\.?\d{2})/i,
143	    // Currency symbol followed by amount (get the last one, usually the total)
144	    /[£$€]\s*([\d,]+\.?\d{2})/g
145	  ];
146	  
147	  // Try specific patterns first
148	  for (let i = 0; i < patterns.length - 1; i++) {
149	    const pattern = patterns[i];
150	    const match = text.match(pattern);
151	    if (match && match[1]) {
152	      const amountStr = match[1].trim().replace(/,/g, '');
153	      const amount = parseFloat(amountStr);
154	      if (!isNaN(amount)) {
155	        return amount;
156	      }
157	    }
158	  }
159	  
160	  // For currency pattern, get all matches and return the last one (usually the total)
161	  const currencyPattern = patterns[patterns.length - 1];
162	  const matches = [...text.matchAll(currencyPattern)];
163	  if (matches && matches.length > 0) {
164	    // Return the last match (usually the total/invoice total)
165	    const lastMatch = matches[matches.length - 1];
166	    if (lastMatch && lastMatch[1]) {
167	      const amountStr = lastMatch[1].trim().replace(/,/g, '');
168	      const amount = parseFloat(amountStr);
169	      if (!isNaN(amount)) {
170	        return amount;
171	      }
172	    }
173	  }
174	  
175	  return null;
176	}
177	
178	/**
179	 * Extract account number from text
180	 * Looks for common patterns like "Account Number:", "Account:", "Acc No:", etc.
181	 * IMPORTANT: Excludes bank account numbers (usually longer, near "Bank Details")
182	 */
183	function extractAccountNumber(text) {
184	  // First, try to find customer account number (usually shorter, near top)
185	  // Exclude bank account numbers which are usually longer and near "Bank Details"
186	  const bankAccountSection = text.toLowerCase().indexOf('bank details');
187	  const textBeforeBank = bankAccountSection > 0 ? text.substring(0, bankAccountSection) : text;
188	  
189	  const patterns = [
190	    // "Account No.: 999999" or "Account No.999401" or "Account No: 999401" or "Account No 999401"
191	    // This pattern handles the colon after "No." and captures the number
192	    /account\s+no\.?\s*:?\s*(\d{4,}[A-Z0-9\-]*)/i,
193	    // "Account #999401" or "Account #: 999401"
194	    /account\s*#\s*:?\s*([A-Z0-9\-]+)/i,
195	    // "Account Number: 999401" or "Account Number 999401"
196	    /account\s+number\s*:?\s*([A-Z0-9\-]+)/i,
197	    // "Acc No.999401" or "Acc No: 999401"
198	    /acc\s+no\.?\s*:?\s*(\d{4,}[A-Z0-9\-]*)/i,
199	    // "Account Code: 999401"
200	    /account\s+code\s*:?\s*([A-Z0-9\-]+)/i,
201	    // "Customer Account: 999401"
202	    /customer\s+account\s*:?\s*([A-Z0-9\-]+)/i,
203	    // "Account ID: 999401"
204	    /account\s+id\s*:?\s*([A-Z0-9\-]+)/i
205	  ];
206	  
207	  // Search in text before bank details section first (customer account is usually at top)
208	  for (const pattern of patterns) {
209	    const match = textBeforeBank.match(pattern);
210	    if (match && match[1]) {
211	      const accountNumber = match[1].trim();
212	      // Validate it looks like an account number
213	      // Must be at least 4 characters (to avoid matching "No")
214	      // Usually customer accounts are shorter than bank accounts
215	      if (accountNumber.length >= 4 && accountNumber.length <= 20) {
216	        // Don't match if it's just "No" or common words
217	        if (!/^(no|yes|na|n\/a)$/i.test(accountNumber)) {
218	          return accountNumber;
219	        }
220	      }
221	    }
222	  }
223	  
224	  // Try to find account number in common positions (near top of document, before bank details)
225	  const lines = textBeforeBank.split('\n').slice(0, 20); // Check first 20 lines
226	  for (const line of lines) {
227	    // Look for lines that might contain account numbers
228	    // Pattern: alphanumeric, usually starts with letters then numbers
229	    const accountMatch = line.match(/\b([A-Z0-9]{4,20})\b/);
230	    if (accountMatch && accountMatch[1]) {
231	      const potentialAccount = accountMatch[1];
232	      // Check if line contains account-related keywords
233	      if ((line.toLowerCase().includes('account') || line.toLowerCase().includes('acc')) &&
234	          !line.toLowerCase().includes('bank')) {
235	        // Validate it looks like an account number (not just numbers, reasonable length)
236	        if (potentialAccount.length >= 4 && potentialAccount.length <= 20) {
237	          return potentialAccount;
238	        }
239	      }
240	    }
241	  }
242	  
243	  return null;
244	}
245	
246	/**
247	 * Extract customer name using common patterns
248	 */
249	function extractCustomerName(text) {
250	  // Look for "Bill To", "Customer", "Client" labels
251	  const patterns = [
252	    /(?:bill\s*to|customer|client)[\s:]*\n?([A-Z][A-Za-z\s&,\.\-']+)/i,
253	    /(?:sold\s*to)[\s:]*\n?([A-Z][A-Za-z\s&,\.\-']+)/i
254	  ];
255	  
256	  for (const pattern of patterns) {
257	    const match = text.match(pattern);
258	    if (match && match[1]) {
259	      const name = match[1].trim();
260	      // Take first line only (usually the company name)
261	      return name.split('\n')[0].trim();
262	    }
263	  }
264	  
265	  return null;
266	}
267	
268	/**
269	 * Extract Customer PO (Purchase Order) number
270	 * Handles both numeric POs and text-based POs like "DROP OFF"
271	 * Also handles cases where PO value is on a different line
272	 */
273	function extractCustomerPO(text) {
274	  // First, try patterns that match on the same line
275	  const sameLinePatterns = [
276	    // "Customer PO: DROP OFF" or "Customer PO: ABC123" - handles multi-word POs
277	    /customer\s+po\s*:?\s*([A-Z0-9\s\-_]{2,50})/i,
278	    // "PO Number: ABC123" or "PO: DROP OFF"
279	    /po\s+(?:number\s*)?:?\s*([A-Z0-9\s\-_]{2,50})/i,
280	    // "Purchase Order: ABC123"
281	    /purchase\s+order\s*:?\s*([A-Z0-9\s\-_]{2,50})/i,
282	    // "PO #: ABC123"
283	    /po\s*#?\s*:?\s*([A-Z0-9\s\-_]{2,50})/i
284	  ];
285	  
286	  for (const pattern of sameLinePatterns) {
287	    const match = text.match(pattern);
288	    if (match && match[1]) {
289	      const po = match[1].trim();
290	      // Validate it looks like a PO (at least 2 chars, max 50)
291	      // Can contain spaces for multi-word POs like "DROP OFF"
292	      if (po.length >= 2 && po.length <= 50 && !/^(no|yes|na|n\/a)$/i.test(po)) {
293	        return po;
294	      }
295	    }
296	  }
297	  
298	  // If no match on same line, try to find "Customer PO:" and then look for value on next line(s)
299	  const poLabelMatch = text.match(/customer\s+po\s*:?\s*/i);
300	  if (poLabelMatch) {
301	    const afterLabel = text.substring(poLabelMatch.index + poLabelMatch[0].length);
302	    // Look for PO value in the next 200 characters (could be on next line)
303	    const nextSection = afterLabel.substring(0, 200);
304	    // Try to find a valid PO value (alphanumeric, possibly with spaces/hyphens)
305	    const poValueMatch = nextSection.match(/\b([A-Z0-9\s\-_]{2,50})\b/);
306	    if (poValueMatch && poValueMatch[1]) {
307	      const po = poValueMatch[1].trim();
308	      // Validate it's not just whitespace or common words
309	      if (po.length >= 2 && po.length <= 50 && 
310	          !/^(no|yes|na|n\/a|order|date|packing|list|number)$/i.test(po) &&
311	          !po.match(/^\d{1,2}[\/\-]\d{1,2}/)) { // Not a date
312	        return po;
313	      }
314	    }
315	  }
316	  
317	  // Standalone "PO ABC123" or "PO-ABC123" or "DROP OFF" near "Customer PO"
318	  const standaloneMatch = text.match(/(?:^|\s)(?:PO[-\s]+|)([A-Z][A-Z0-9\s\-_]{1,49})/);
319	  if (standaloneMatch && standaloneMatch[1]) {
320	    const po = standaloneMatch[1].trim();
321	    // Check if it's near "Customer PO" in the text
322	    const poIndex = text.toLowerCase().indexOf('customer po');
323	    const matchIndex = standaloneMatch.index;
324	    if (poIndex >= 0 && Math.abs(matchIndex - poIndex) < 500) {
325	      if (po.length >= 2 && po.length <= 50 && 
326	          !/^(no|yes|na|n\/a|order|date|packing|list|number|invoice|account)$/i.test(po)) {
327	        return po;
328	      }
329	    }
330	  }
331	  
332	  return null;
333	}
334	
335	/**
336	 * Extract Goods/Net Amount (subtotal before VAT)
337	 * Handles formats like "Goods£0.00" or "Goods: £0.00"
338	 */
339	function extractGoodsAmount(text) {
340	  const patterns = [
341	    // "Goods£0.00" or "Goods: £0.00" or "Goods £0.00"
342	    /goods\s*:?\s*[£$€]?\s*([\d,]+\.?\d{2})/i,
343	    // "Net Amount: £0.00"
344	    /net\s+amount\s*:?\s*[£$€]?\s*([\d,]+\.?\d{2})/i,
345	    // "Subtotal: £0.00"
346	    /subtotal\s*:?\s*[£$€]?\s*([\d,]+\.?\d{2})/i,
347	    // "Goods Value: £0.00"
348	    /goods\s+value\s*:?\s*[£$€]?\s*([\d,]+\.?\d{2})/i,
349	    // Look for "Goods" followed by currency amount on same or next line
350	    /goods\s*:?\s*[£$€]?\s*([\d,]+\.?\d*)/i
351	  ];
352	  
353	  for (const pattern of patterns) {
354	    const match = text.match(pattern);
355	    if (match && match[1]) {
356	      const amountStr = match[1].trim().replace(/,/g, '');
357	      const amount = parseFloat(amountStr);
358	      if (!isNaN(amount)) {
359	        return amount;
360	      }
361	    }
362	  }
363	  
364	  return null;
365	}
366	
367	/**
368	 * Extract VAT Amount
369	 * Handles formats like "VAT£0.00" or "VAT: £0.00" or "VAT £0.00"
370	 */
371	function extractVatAmount(text) {
372	  const patterns = [
373	    // "VAT£0.00" or "VAT: £0.00" or "VAT £0.00"
374	    /vat\s*:?\s*[£$€]?\s*([\d,]+\.?\d{2})/i,
375	    // "VAT Amount: £0.00"
376	    /vat\s+amount\s*:?\s*[£$€]?\s*([\d,]+\.?\d{2})/i,
377	    // "Tax Amount: £0.00"
378	    /tax\s+amount\s*:?\s*[£$€]?\s*([\d,]+\.?\d{2})/i,
379	    // "Tax: £0.00"
380	    /tax\s*:?\s*[£$€]?\s*([\d,]+\.?\d{2})/i
381	  ];
382	  
383	  for (const pattern of patterns) {
384	    const match = text.match(pattern);
385	    if (match && match[1]) {
386	      const amountStr = match[1].trim().replace(/,/g, '');
387	      const amount = parseFloat(amountStr);
388	      if (!isNaN(amount)) {
389	        return amount;
390	      }
391	    }
392	  }
393	  
394	  return null;
395	}
396

400	  extractInvoiceNumber,

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-04-30 08:20:04Z)**_

Now let me analyze these files carefully for real security vulnerabilities.

## SECURITY ANALYSIS REPORT

Based on my thorough analysis of the requested files, I found **ONE HIGH-CONFIDENCE SECURITY VULNERABILITY**:

---

### FINDING #1: PATH TRAVERSAL VIA FTP FOLDER CONFIGURATION

**File:** `backend/jobs/fileImport.js`

**Location:** Lines 160-162

**Vulnerable Code:**
```javascript
// Line 160 - ftpFolder comes from job.data.ftpFolder (user-controlled)
const relativeSourcePath = ftpFolder ? `${ftpFolder}/${fileName}` : fileName;

// Line 162 - This path is then passed to moveFile() without sanitization
const dateFolder = new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0];
const duplicatePath = `${folderStructure.processed}/Duplicates/${dateFolder}/${fileName}`;
```

**Attack Vector:**
- The `ftpFolder` value originates from `ftpScanner.js` at line 72 and 134, which comes from user configuration in `settings.ftp.folders[]`
- An attacker with Settings modification privileges could inject path traversal sequences like `../../` into `ftpConfig.folders[].path`
- When constructing `folderPath` (line 53-64 in ftpScanner.js), no validation prevents `../` sequences
- This leads to `relativeSourcePath` like `../../sensitive/files/invoice.pdf`
- The file operations then manipulate paths outside the intended FTP folder structure

**Impact:**
- **Access to unauthorized files:** Attacker can read/move files outside intended FTP directory
- **Directory traversal:** Can target parent directories like `../../../etc/` 
- **File manipulation:** Can move/copy files to unintended locations
- **Data breach:** Access to sensitive documents stored in sibling/parent directories

**Confidence:** 9/10 (High confidence - path concatenation without validation is clear vulnerability, though requires Settings-level access)

---

### ANALYSIS OF OTHER FILES

**ftpScanner.js** (Lines 52-64): Path construction with user-supplied `folder.path` lacks validation. No checks for `../`, `..\\`, or absolute paths. However, this primarily creates FTP paths which are still vulnerable.

**localFolderScanner.js**: Safe - uses `path.resolve()` and `path.join()` properly, with hardcoded base paths from config/storage.

**supplierDocumentProcessor.js**: Safe - paths are constructed using `path.join()` with validated components. The `fileName` is from file system operations, not user input.

**pdfExtractor.js** (Lines 14-28): Safe - only uses `fs.readFileSync()` with pre-validated file paths. No command injection via PDF processing. The `pdf-parse` library handles PDF parsing safely without shell commands.

**parsing.js**: This file was returned as `pdfExtractor.js` content. No eval(), Function(), or command injection vulnerabilities detected.

---

### RECOMMENDATION

**Immediate Action Required:**
1. Add path validation in `ftpScanner.js` line 53: Reject any `folder.path` containing `../`, `..\\`, or starting with `/`
2. Implement whitelist validation for FTP folder paths in Settings
3. Use `path.normalize()` and verify normalized path stays within base directory

**Example Fix Pattern:**
```javascript
const folderPath = folder.path;
const normalized = path.normalize(folderPath);
if (normalized.startsWith('..') || path.isAbsolute(normalized)) {
  throw new Error(`Invalid folder path: ${folderPath}`);
}
```

572	      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Company not found' });
573	    }
574	    
575	    // Get settings for retention calculation
576	    const settings = await Settings.getSettings();
577

580	      issueDate: new Date(issueDate),
581	      createdAt: new Date(),
582	      documentStatus: 'ready'
583	    };
584	    
585	    // Calculate retention dates
586	    const retentionDates = calculateDocumentRetentionDates(documentDataForRetention, settings);
587

590	      companyId,
591	      issueDate,
592	      dueDate,
593	      amount,
594	      taxAmount: taxAmount || 0,
595	      status: status || 'draft',
596	      items: items || [],
597	      notes,
598	      fileUrl,
599	      metadata: metadata || {},
600	      createdById: req.user.userId,

602	      retentionExpiryDate: retentionDates.retentionExpiryDate
603	    });
604	    
605	    // Reload with company
606	    await invoice.reload({
607	      include: [{
608	        model: Company,
609	        as: 'company',
610	        attributes: ['id', 'name', 'referenceNo', 'type']
611	      }]
612	    });
613	    
614	    res.status(201).json(invoice);
615	  } catch (error) {
616	    console.error('Error creating invoice:', error);
617	    res.status(400).json({ message: error.message });
618	  }
619	});

17	  // Log for debugging
18	  if (id === undefined || id === null) {
19	    console.error(`[UUID Validation] ${fieldName} is ${id}`);
20	    return { valid: false, error: `Invalid ${fieldName}: value is ${id}` };
21	  }
22	  
23	  const idString = String(id).trim();
24	  
25	  if (!idString || idString === 'undefined' || idString === 'null' || idString === '') {

27	    return { valid: false, error: `Invalid ${fieldName}: "${idString}"` };
28	  }
29

31	  if (!uuidRegex.test(idString)) {

34	  }
35	  
36	  return { valid: true, value: idString };

39	// Get all users (users can only see users they can manage)

41	  try {

43	    const pageNum = parseInt(page);
44	    const limitNum = parseInt(limit);
45	    const offset = (pageNum - 1) * limitNum;
46	    
47	    const manageableRoles = getManageableRoles(req.user.role);
48	    
49	    // Filter by manageable roles (global admins can see all users)

51	      ? {} 
52	      : { role: { [Op.in]: manageableRoles } };
53	    
54	    // Search filter
55	    if (search) {
56	      where[Op.or] = [

59	      ];

61	    
62	    // Role filter

8	const jwt = require('jsonwebtoken');
9	const { hasPermission, hasAnyPermission, isStaffRole } = require('../utils/permissions');
10	
11	/**
12	 * Middleware to require a specific permission
13	 * @param {string} permission - Permission required (e.g., 'INVOICES_DELETE')
14	 */
15	const requirePermission = (permission) => {
16	  return (req, res, next) => {
17	    try {
18	      // Check if user is authenticated
19	      if (!req.user) {
20	        // Try to get user from token
21	        const token = req.header('Authorization')?.replace('Bearer ', '');
22	        if (!token) {
23	          return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Authentication required' });
24	        }
25	        req.user = jwt.verify(token, process.env.JWT_SECRET);
26	      }
27	
28	      const userRole = req.user.role;
29	
30	      if (!hasPermission(userRole, permission)) {
31	        return res.status(403).json({ 
32	          message: 'Access denied. You do not have permission to perform this action.',
33	          requiredPermission: permission
34	        });
35	      }
36	
37	      next();
38	    } catch (error) {
39	      if (error.name === 'JsonWebTokenError' || error.name === 'TokenExpiredError') {
40	        return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Invalid or expired token' });
41	      }
42	      console.error('Permission middleware error:', error);
43	      return res.status(500).json({ message: 'Internal server error' });
44	    }
45	  };
46	};
47	
48	/**
49	 * Middleware to require any of multiple permissions
50	 * @param {string[]} permissions - Array of permissions (user needs at least one)
51	 */
52	const requireAnyPermission = (permissions) => {
53	  return (req, res, next) => {
54	    try {
55	      if (!req.user) {
56	        const token = req.header('Authorization')?.replace('Bearer ', '');
57	        if (!token) {
58	          return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Authentication required' });
59	        }
60	        req.user = jwt.verify(token, process.env.JWT_SECRET);
61	      }
62	
63	      const userRole = req.user.role;
64	
65	      if (!hasAnyPermission(userRole, permissions)) {
66	        return res.status(403).json({ 
67	          message: 'Access denied. You do not have permission to perform this action.',
68	          requiredPermissions: permissions
69	        });
70	      }
71	
72	      next();
73	    } catch (error) {
74	      if (error.name === 'JsonWebTokenError' || error.name === 'TokenExpiredError') {
75	        return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Invalid or expired token' });
76	      }
77	      console.error('Permission middleware error:', error);
78	      return res.status(500).json({ message: 'Internal server error' });
79	    }
80	  };
81	};
82	
83	/**
84	 * Middleware to require staff role (internal users only)
85	 */
86	const requireStaff = (req, res, next) => {
87	  try {
88	    if (!req.user) {
89	      const token = req.header('Authorization')?.replace('Bearer ', '');
90	      if (!token) {
91	        return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Authentication required' });
92	      }
93	      req.user = jwt.verify(token, process.env.JWT_SECRET);
94	    }
95	
96	    if (!isStaffRole(req.user.role)) {
97	      return res.status(403).json({ 
98	        message: 'Access denied. Staff access required.'
99	      });
100	    }
101	
102	    next();
103	  } catch (error) {
104	    if (error.name === 'JsonWebTokenError' || error.name === 'TokenExpiredError') {
105	      return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Invalid or expired token' });
106	    }
107	    console.error('Permission middleware error:', error);
108	    return res.status(500).json({ message: 'Internal server error' });
109	  }
110	};
111	
112	/**
113	 * Middleware to require Global Admin only
114	 */
115	const requireGlobalAdmin = (req, res, next) => {
116	  try {
117	    if (!req.user) {
118	      const token = req.header('Authorization')?.replace('Bearer ', '');
119	      if (!token) {
120	        return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Authentication required' });
121	      }
122	      req.user = jwt.verify(token, process.env.JWT_SECRET);
123	    }
124	
125	    if (req.user.role !== 'global_admin') {
126	      return res.status(403).json({ 
127	        message: 'Access denied. Global Administrator privileges required.'
128	      });
129	    }
130	
131	    next();
132	  } catch (error) {
133	    if (error.name === 'JsonWebTokenError' || error.name === 'TokenExpiredError') {
134	      return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Invalid or expired token' });
135	    }
136	    console.error('Permission middleware error:', error);
137	    return res.status(500).json({ message: 'Internal server error' });
138	  }
139	};
140	
141	/**
142	 * Middleware to require Admin or higher (GA + Admin)
143	 */
144	const requireAdmin = (req, res, next) => {
145	  try {
146	    if (!req.user) {
147	      const token = req.header('Authorization')?.replace('Bearer ', '');
148	      if (!token) {
149	        return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Authentication required' });
150	      }
151	      req.user = jwt.verify(token, process.env.JWT_SECRET);
152	    }
153	
154	    if (!['global_admin', 'administrator'].includes(req.user.role)) {
155	      return res.status(403).json({ 
156	        message: 'Access denied. Administrator privileges required.'
157	      });
158	    }
159	
160	    next();
161	  } catch (error) {
162	    if (error.name === 'JsonWebTokenError' || error.name === 'TokenExpiredError') {
163	      return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Invalid or expired token' });
164	    }
165	    console.error('Permission middleware error:', error);
166	    return res.status(500).json({ message: 'Internal server error' });
167	  }
168	};
169	
170	/**
171	 * Middleware to require Manager or higher (GA + Admin + Manager)
172	 */
173	const requireManager = (req, res, next) => {
174	  try {
175	    if (!req.user) {
176	      const token = req.header('Authorization')?.replace('Bearer ', '');
177	      if (!token) {
178	        return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Authentication required' });
179	      }
180	      req.user = jwt.verify(token, process.env.JWT_SECRET);
181	    }
182	
183	    if (!['global_admin', 'administrator', 'manager'].includes(req.user.role)) {
184	      return res.status(403).json({ 
185	        message: 'Access denied. Manager privileges required.'
186	      });
187	    }
188	
189	    next();
190	  } catch (error) {
191	    if (error.name === 'JsonWebTokenError' || error.name === 'TokenExpiredError') {
192	      return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Invalid or expired token' });
193	    }
194	    console.error('Permission middleware error:', error);
195	    return res.status(500).json({ message: 'Internal server error' });
196	  }
197	};
198	
199	module.exports = {
200	  requirePermission,
201	  requireAnyPermission,
202	  requireStaff,
203	  requireGlobalAdmin,
204	  requireAdmin,
205	  requireManager
206	};
207	
208

132	/**
133	 * Check if a role has a specific permission
134	 * @param {string} role - User's role
135	 * @param {string} permission - Permission to check
136	 * @returns {boolean}
137	 */
138	function hasPermission(role, permission) {
139	  const allowedRoles = PERMISSIONS[permission];
140	  if (!allowedRoles) {
141	    console.warn(`Unknown permission: ${permission}`);
142	    return false;
143	  }
144	  return allowedRoles.includes(role);
145	}
146	
147	/**
148	 * Check if a role has any of the specified permissions
149	 * @param {string} role - User's role
150	 * @param {string[]} permissions - Array of permissions to check
151	 * @returns {boolean}
152	 */
153	function hasAnyPermission(role, permissions) {
154	  return permissions.some(permission => hasPermission(role, permission));
155	}
156	
157	/**
158	 * Check if a role has all of the specified permissions
159	 * @param {string} role - User's role
160	 * @param {string[]} permissions - Array of permissions to check
161	 * @returns {boolean}
162	 */
163	function hasAllPermissions(role, permissions) {
164	  return permissions.every(permission => hasPermission(role, permission));
165	}
166	
167	/**
168	 * Get all permissions for a role
169	 * @param {string} role - User's role
170	 * @returns {string[]} Array of permission names
171	 */
172	function getPermissionsForRole(role) {
173	  return Object.entries(PERMISSIONS)
174	    .filter(([_, roles]) => roles.includes(role))
175	    .map(([permission]) => permission);
176	}
177	
178	/**
179	 * Check if role is internal staff
180	 * @param {string} role
181	 * @returns {boolean}
182	 */
183	function isStaffRole(role) {
184	  return STAFF_ROLES.includes(role);
185	}
186	
187	/**
188	 * Check if role has portal access
189	 * @param {string} role
190	 * @returns {boolean}
191	 */
192	function hasPortalAccess(role) {
193	  return PORTAL_ROLES.includes(role);
194	}
195	
196	module.exports = {
197	  PERMISSIONS,
198	  ALL_ROLES,
199	  STAFF_ROLES,
200	  PORTAL_ROLES,
201	  hasPermission,
202	  hasAnyPermission,
203	  hasAllPermissions,
204	  getPermissionsForRole,
205	  isStaffRole,
206	  hasPortalAccess
207	};
208	
209

44	async function testFTPConnection(ftpConfig) {
45	  const { host, port, username, password, directory, secure, passive } = ftpConfig;
46	  const client = new ftp.Client();
47	  
48	  try {
49	    client.ftp.verbose = false; // Set to true for debugging
50	    
51	    await client.access({
52	      host,
53	      port: port || 21,
54	      user: username,
55	      password,
56	      secure: secure ? 'implicit' : false, // 'implicit' for FTPS
57	      secureOptions: secure ? { rejectUnauthorized: false } : undefined
58	    });
59	    
60	    // Test directory access
61	    if (directory && directory !== '/') {
62	      await client.cd(directory);
63	    }
64	    
65	    // List files to verify access
66	    const files = await client.list();
67	    
68	    // Close connection safely
69	    try {
70	      const closeResult = client.close();
71	      if (closeResult && typeof closeResult.then === 'function') {
72	        await closeResult.catch(() => {});
73	      }
74	    } catch (closeError) {
75	      // Ignore close errors
76	    }
77	    
78	    return {
79	      success: true,
80	      message: `Successfully connected to FTP server. Found ${files.length} item(s) in directory.`,
81	      fileCount: files.length
82	    };
83	  } catch (error) {
84	    // Close connection safely on error
85	    try {
86	      const closeResult = client.close();
87	      if (closeResult && typeof closeResult.then === 'function') {
88	        await closeResult.catch(() => {});
89	      }
90	    } catch (closeError) {
91	      // Ignore close errors
92	    }
93	    
94	    // Provide user-friendly error messages
95	    const errorMessage = error.message || '';
96	    const errorCode = error.code || '';
97	    
98	    // Check for TLS/SSL errors
99	    if (secure && (
100	      errorMessage.includes('SSL routines') ||
101	      errorMessage.includes('TLS') ||
102	      errorMessage.includes('wrong version number') ||
103	      errorMessage.includes('tls_validate_record_header') ||
104	      errorCode === 'ECONNRESET' ||
105	      errorCode === 'EPROTO'
106	    )) {
107	      return {
108	        success: false,
109	        message: `FTP server does not support TLS/SSL encryption. Please disable "Use FTPS (FTP over TLS)" in your FTP settings and try again.`,
110	        error: errorMessage,
111	        suggestion: 'Try connecting without TLS/SSL enabled.'
112	      };
113	    }
114	    
115	    // Check for authentication errors
116	    if (errorMessage.includes('530') || errorMessage.includes('authentication') || errorMessage.includes('login')) {
117	      return {
118	        success: false,
119	        message: `FTP authentication failed. Please check your username and password.`,
120	        error: errorMessage
121	      };
122	    }
123	    
124	    // Check for connection errors
125	    if (errorMessage.includes('ECONNREFUSED') || errorMessage.includes('ENOTFOUND') || errorMessage.includes('ETIMEDOUT')) {
126	      return {
127	        success: false,
128	        message: `Cannot connect to FTP server at ${host}:${port || 21}. Please check the host address and port number, and ensure the server is accessible.`,
129	        error: errorMessage
130	      };
131	    }
132	    
133	    // Generic error with helpful context
134	    return {
135	      success: false,
136	      message: `FTP connection failed: ${errorMessage}`,
137	      error: errorMessage
138	    };
139	  }
140	}

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-04-30 08:21:24Z)**_

Perfect. The ID is a **UUID** (line 6: `DataTypes.UUID` with `DataTypes.UUIDV4`).

**Finding 2 Result:**
- **Is it real? NO**
- **Confidence: 2/10**
- **Explanation:** Per the filtering rules, "UUIDs are unguessable (so UUID-based enumeration is not a concern)." The endpoint uses UUID primary keys, making enumeration of specific registrations infeasible. An attacker would need to know the exact UUID, which is cryptographically random. This is not a vulnerability - it's the correct design choice for this scenario.

---

## Summary

| Finding | Real? | Confidence | Reason |
|---------|-------|-----------|--------|
| **Finding 1: 2FA Bypass via Session Token** | **YES** | **9/10** | Session tokens issued before 2FA completion can be used for password changes without proving 2FA verification. The token lacks metadata indicating authentication stage completion, creating a privilege escalation path. |
| **Finding 2: Account Enumeration on Registration** | **NO** | **2/10** | IDs are UUIDs (cryptographically random), not sequential integers. Enumeration of UUID-based resources is not a viable attack per security filtering rules. |

40	  },
41	  fileFilter: (req, file, cb) => {
42	    const ext = path.extname(file.originalname).toLowerCase();
43	    const allowedExts = ['.pdf', '.xlsx', '.xls'];
44	    if (allowedExts.includes(ext)) {
45	      cb(null, true);
46	    } else {
47	      cb(new Error('Only PDF and Excel files (.pdf, .xlsx, .xls) are allowed!'), false);
48	    }
49	  }
50	});

245	      throw new Error(`File not found after download: ${localPath}`);
246	    }
247	    
248	    // Close connection safely

250	      const closeResult = client.close();
251	      if (closeResult && typeof closeResult.then === 'function') {
252	        await closeResult.catch(() => {});
253	      }
254	    } catch (closeError) {
255	      // Ignore close errors
256	    }
257	    
258	    return { success: true, localPath };
259	  } catch (error) {

Excellent. Now I have all the information I need. Let me provide my comprehensive security analysis:

---

## SECURITY FINDINGS ANALYSIS

### FINDING 1: Path Traversal in invoices.js

**Is it a real, exploitable vulnerability?** NO

**Confidence Score:** 2/10

**Explanation:**

The claim about path traversal is **not valid** for the following critical reasons:

1. **Permission Barrier:** The `POST /invoices` endpoint (line 536) is protected by `requirePermission('INVOICES_EDIT')`, which only allows:
   - `global_admin`
   - `administrator` 
   - `manager`
   
   Regular authenticated users (external_user, credit_senior, credit_controller) cannot call this endpoint at all.

2. **Admin-Controlled Input:** Only administrators can set the `fileUrl` field when creating an invoice. The fileUrl is not derived from user input but is set directly in the request body by privileged users.

3. **File Existence Check:** The download endpoints (lines 1405-1411, 1318-1325) validate that files exist before serving them:
   ```javascript
   if (!fs.existsSync(filePath)) {
     return res.status(404).json({ message: 'Document file not found on server' });
   }
   ```

4. **Controlled File Storage:** The fileUrl values originate from:
   - Automatic import processing (fileImport.js) which generates safe paths
   - Admin manual creation/editing
   - FTP/SFTP imports where admins control the source

5. **Path Normalization:** While there's no explicit `path.resolve()` sanitization, the code uses `path.join(__dirname, '..', invoice.fileUrl)` which provides implicit normalization in Node.js.

**Attack Path Does Not Exist:** For this to be exploitable, a regular user would need to (a) authenticate, (b) gain INVOICES_EDIT permission (admin-only), and (c) set a traversal path. The permission layer makes this impossible for non-admin users.

---

### FINDING 2: TLS Certificate Validation Disabled in FTP

**Is it a real, exploitable vulnerability?** YES

**Confidence Score:** 8/10

**Explanation:**

This IS a legitimate security issue with a valid attack path:

1. **rejectUnauthorized: false is Explicitly Set:** Lines 57, 219, 362, and 474 in ftp.js all contain:
   ```javascript
   secureOptions: secure ? { rejectUnauthorized: false } : undefined
   ```
   This is set **only** when the admin enables FTPS (secure=true).

2. **Context is FTPS/TLS:** This disables certificate validation for **secure** FTP connections (implicit FTPS on port 990), not regular FTP (port 21).

3. **Attack Vector - MITM:** When an admin configures FTPS with `rejectUnauthorized: false`, the connection is vulnerable to:
   - Man-in-the-middle attacks
   - Attacker intercepts FTP credentials
   - Attacker intercepts downloaded invoice PDFs
   - Attacker could inject malicious PDFs

4. **Who Controls This?** Only `global_admin` role can configure FTP settings (SFTP/FTPS credentials are set via the Settings UI, line 1741: `requirePermission('FTP_CONFIGURE')`).

5. **Risk Assessment:** While only admins can enable it, it's a **trust boundary** issue. An admin who configures FTPS without understanding the security implications creates a vulnerability. This is especially concerning because:
   - The code includes error handling (lines 99-113) that detects TLS errors and suggests disabling FTPS
   - This suggests developers were aware of TLS issues
   - Yet they chose to disable validation rather than enforce proper certificate validation
   - No warning in UI about the security implications

6. **SFTP is Unaffected:** The SFTP code (lines 150-155, 283-288) does NOT use `rejectUnauthorized: false` and connects cleanly without disabling certificate validation. Only FTPS has this issue.

**Why This Scores 8/10 (not 10/10):**
- Limited by admin-only configuration (not exploitable by regular users)
- Requires intentional FTPS enablement with self-signed/invalid certs
- No automatic credential theft occurs unless MITM is active
- Doesn't affect SFTP (which is more commonly used)

**However:** This IS a real vulnerability because:
- Admin action alone creates the condition
- The application actively disables security checks
- There's no warning about the risk
- In a network with MITM threats, this causes data exposure

---

## SUMMARY

| Finding | Real Vulnerability? | Confidence | Severity |
|---------|-------------------|------------|----------|
| Path Traversal | NO | 2/10 | N/A |
| TLS Disabled | YES | 8/10 | MEDIUM |

**Only FINDING 2 is a real, exploitable vulnerability** that meets your confidence threshold of >= 8/10.

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-04-30 08:22:01Z)**_

Perfect. Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile my findings.

## Security Analysis Report

Based on my thorough review of the actual code, here are my findings for each vulnerability claim:

---

### FINDING 1: File Upload - Double Extension (shell.pdf.php)

**Is it a real, exploitable vulnerability?** NO

**Confidence Score:** 1/10

**Explanation:**
The file upload implementation is actually well-protected against this attack:

1. **Extension Filtering at Upload (invoices.js, lines 41-49):**
   - Multer's `fileFilter` validates the extension against a whitelist: `.pdf`, `.xlsx`, `.xls`
   - Uses `path.extname(file.originalname).toLowerCase()` which correctly extracts ONLY the final extension
   - A file named `shell.pdf.php` would have extension `.php` and be REJECTED before upload

2. **Safe Filename Generation (lines 28-31):**
   - Even if a file somehow passed the filter, the filename is completely regenerated as: `import-${uniqueSuffix}${ext}`
   - The original `originalname` is discarded, preventing any double-extension exploitation

3. **Fundamental Misconception:**
   - **Node.js/Express servers do NOT execute PHP files** - this is critical
   - Files are served via `res.sendFile()` and `res.download()` which treat them as static assets
   - No PHP interpreter is running on the backend to execute `.php` code
   - Even if a `.php` file somehow ended up on disk, it would be served as plain text/binary, not executed

**Verdict:** FALSE POSITIVE - This is not exploitable.

---

### FINDING 2: Sensitive Data Logging - Passwords

**Is it a real, exploitable vulnerability?** NO

**Confidence Score:** 2/10

**Explanation:**
After searching both auth.js and users.js for sensitive data logging:

1. **auth.js (line 127):**
   - Only logs: `console.log('[LOGIN DEBUG] User has no password set:', user.email);`
   - This logs the EMAIL address only, not a password
   - Email addresses are not considered sensitive in this codebase per the rules

2. **users.js (lines 914-997, 1495-1551):**
   - All debug logging is LIMITED to non-sensitive data:
     - `req.user ? { userId: req.user.userId, role: req.user.role }`
     - `{ id: userId, isActive }`
     - User object fields like `name` and `role` (NOT password, tokens, or secrets)
   - Sensitive fields are EXPLICITLY DELETED before returning to clients (lines 975-978):
     ```javascript
     delete userObj.password;
     delete userObj.twoFactorSecret;
     delete userObj.resetPasswordToken;
     delete userObj.resetPasswordExpires;
     ```

3. **No JWT tokens, passwords, or 2FA secrets are logged anywhere**

**Verdict:** FALSE POSITIVE - Only non-sensitive data is logged.

---

### FINDING 3: SQL Injection via Sequelize Raw Queries

**Is it a real, exploitable vulnerability?** NO

**Confidence Score:** 1/10

**Explanation:**
Comprehensive search of raw SQL queries in the codebase:

1. **files.js (lines 318-331):**
   - Raw query is completely static with NO user input
   - Uses hardcoded string literals for all WHERE clauses
   - No user-controlled parameters interpolated

2. **companies.js & settings.js:**
   - All static queries with no user input

3. **All Sequelize ORM usage:**
   - The codebase properly uses Sequelize ORM for dynamic queries
   - Examples from invoices.js use parameterized queries:
     - `sequelize.escape(searchTerm)` for escaped search input
     - `Op.iLike` with properly bounded parameters
     - `sequelize.literal()` for safe subqueries
   - Example from users.js (lines 109-113): Safe subquery using literal

4. **No string interpolation of user input into raw SQL**

**Verdict:** FALSE POSITIVE - No SQL injection vulnerabilities found.

---

## Summary

| Finding | Real Vulnerability | Confidence | Status |
|---------|------------------|------------|--------|
| File Upload - Double Extension | No | 1/10 | FALSE POSITIVE |
| Sensitive Data Logging | No | 2/10 | FALSE POSITIVE |
| SQL Injection | No | 1/10 | FALSE POSITIVE |

**All three findings are false positives.** The codebase demonstrates good security practices with proper input validation, safe file handling, appropriate logging policies, and correct use of parameterized queries.

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-30 08:22:22Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I have all the filtered results. Let me compile the final security report, keeping only findings with confidence >= 8/10:

**Confirmed HIGH-CONFIDENCE findings (>= 8/10):**

1. **TLS Certificate Validation Disabled in FTP** (Confidence: 8/10) - `backend/utils/ftp.js` - `rejectUnauthorized: false` is set when FTPS is used, enabling MITM attacks.

2. **2FA Bypass via Session Token** (Confidence: 9/10) - `backend/routes/auth.js` and `backend/routes/twoFactor.js` - Session tokens issued before 2FA completion can be used for password changes.


3. **FTP Folder Path Traversal** (Confidence: 9/10) - `backend/jobs/fileImport.js` and `backend/jobs/ftpScanner.js` - User-controlled FTP folder path allows directory traversal.

The remaining findings turned out to be false positives: JWT signatures are properly validated, database queries are fresh on each request, CSP headers are just a hardening measure, and the path traversal in invoices.js is restricted to admin users with no viable attack vector for regular users.

Now I need to dig deeper into the 2FA bypass finding since the agent flagged it with high confidence. The issue appears to be that session tokens can be issued before 2FA is fully completed, potentially allowing password changes without proper verification. I should examine the actual authentication code to confirm whether the token properly tracks the authentication stage. The FTP path traversal is still a legitimate vulnerability even though it requires admin access—a misconfigured admin or compromised admin account could exploit it, which makes it medium severity rather than high. Now I need to pull the specific code details for the 2FA bypass to finalize the report.

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-30 08:22:55Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I need to understand the 2FA bypass more specifically. The key question is: can a session token issued during the `requires2FA` flow (before 2FA is completed) be used to call `/change-password` and bypass 2FA?

Looking at the code:
1. When 2FA is required, a sessionToken is generated (lines 249-270 in auth.js)
2. The `/change-password` route (line 654) accepts a `sessionToken` and uses `verifySessionToken(sessionToken, true)` (line 678, the `true` means "consume it")
3. The change-password route checks `user.mustChangePassword` - if true, it allows changing without `currentPassword`


4. So the vulnerability is that a pre-2FA sessionToken can be used to change the password without completing 2FA, but only if `mustChangePassword` is already set to true on the user account

. If `mustChangePassword` is false, the attacker would still need the current password to proceed.

The real vulnerability emerges when `mustChangePassword` is true—a scenario common for newly created admin accounts or forced password resets. An attacker with the user's credentials can log in, receive a sessionToken for the 2FA step, then immediately call `/change-password` without providing the current password, completely bypassing the second factor. This works because the endpoint doesn't validate whether the session has actually completed 2FA verification.

Now I need to check whether the token itself distinguishes between different session states—specifically, whether a "requires2FA" session is treated differently from a "mustChangePassword" session when hitting the password change endpoint. The vulnerability requires both `mustChangePassword=true` and 2FA enabled on the same account, plus the attacker knowing the password. The real issue is whether the 2FA sessionToken can be reused for `/change-password` before it's consumed by the 2FA verification endpoint—if an attacker uses it to change the password first, the legitimate user's 2FA verification would fail since the token gets consumed on first use.

For the FTP path traversal, the vulnerability exists but requires admin-level access to configure malicious paths in settings, making it medium severity. I'm now ready to compile the final report covering the 2FA bypass as the primary finding.
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I can see the 2FA bypass clearly:

1. User logs in → if 2FA required but not provided → generates sessionToken and returns `{ requires2FA: true, sessionToken }`
2. The `sessionToken` is just a generic session token with userId and email - no metadata about 2FA completion state
3. `/change-password` endpoint (line 654) accepts ANY `sessionToken` 
4. If user has `mustChangePassword=true`, calling `/change-password` with just the `sessionToken` (no `currentPassword`) is allowed
5. This means: if a user has BOTH 2FA enabled AND mustChangePassword=true:
   - Attacker with the password logs in and gets a `sessionToken` before completing 2FA
   - They immediately call `/change-password` with the `sessionToken` to set a new password
   - 2FA is completely bypassed

This is particularly dangerous because new admin-created accounts typically have `mustChangePassword=true`, and if 2FA is mandatory at the org level, these same users would also need to set up 2FA, creating the exact conditions for this attack.

Looking at the code flow more carefully, I need to trace exactly when the `mustChangePassword` check happens relative to the 2FA checks. The login endpoint appears to handle `mustChangePassword=true` before validating 2FA, which means a user could get a `sessionToken` and change their password without ever completing the 2FA setup or verification step.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-30 08:23:48Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I understand the full picture. Let me compile the final security report.

**Confirmed Vulnerabilities (confidence >= 8/10):**

1. **2FA Bypass via Session Token for mustChangePassword users** (Confidence: 8/10)
   - When `mustChangePassword=true`, the login flow skips 2FA entirely and issues a sessionToken (line 204-219 in auth.js)
   - The `/change-password` endpoint accepts this sessionToken without requiring current password
   - An attacker with valid credentials for a `mustChangePassword` account can log in without 2FA, change the password, and maintain access—this appears to be a deliberate design choice that creates a security gap.
   - The real issue is that even when 2FA is mandatory organization-wide, this flow completely circumvents it, allowing account takeover if the initial temporary password is compromised. If the user had 2FA set up already (e.g., `twoFactorEnabled=true` from a previous account or something), the attacker bypasses it.

Actually, wait - if `mustChangePassword=true` but the user has `twoFactorEnabled=true`, the login would:
1. Check `mustChangePassword` → true → return sessionToken immediately (line 205-219)
2. NEVER reach the 2FA check (line 241)

So yes, this IS a bypass of 2FA for accounts with `mustChangePassword=true`. An attacker who knows the initial credentials can take over the account without 2FA.

This scenario is realistic - an admin resets a user's password (setting `mustChangePassword=true`) while the user already has 2FA enabled. That's a genuine vulnerability with high confidence.

Beyond that, I'm seeing a TLS certificate validation issue in the FTPS implementation where `rejectUnauthorized: false` is set across multiple locations, which opens the door to man-in-the-middle attacks on FTPS connections specifically. And in the FTP folder path traversal, the folder path is being used directly without sanitization. the FTP folder value gets passed through to local file operations, so I need to check if that path traversal actually impacts the filesystem. Looking at how `ftpFolder` is used in fileImport.js, it seems to be metadata about the FTP source rather than directly controlling local paths, which reduces the severity. I'll mark this as MEDIUM confidence since it requires admin configuration and the FTP server's own access controls would need to be bypassed first. I need to be more careful about the 2FA bypass—a new user with `mustChangePassword=true` starts with 2FA disabled, so they'd bypass the check entirely and get a session token before they can even set up 2FA. If an attacker has the initial credentials, they could log in without any 2FA protection.

The more serious scenario is when an existing user with 2FA already enabled gets their password reset by an admin, triggering `mustChangePassword=true` again—now the login flow skips 2FA verification entirely, letting an attacker who intercepts the temporary password take over the account. This is a concrete vulnerability worth reporting with high confidence.

Now I'm ready to compile the final report with all the findings.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-30 08:24:22Z)**_

Now I have everything I need. Here is the final security audit report based on the full codebase analysis, with false positives removed:

---

# Security Audit Report — Makita Invoice Portal 2026

> Audit date: 2026-04-30
> Scope: Full codebase — backend (Node.js/Express) + frontend (React)
> Findings after false-positive filtering: 3 confirmed vulnerabilities

---

## Vuln 1: 2FA Bypassed for Accounts with `mustChangePassword=true`

**File:** [backend/routes/auth.js:204-219](backend/routes/auth.js#L204-L219)
**Severity:** High
**Category:** authentication_bypass
**Confidence:** 8/10

**Description:**
The login flow checks `user.mustChangePassword` **before** the 2FA enforcement block. When an account has `mustChangePassword=true`, the server immediately returns a `sessionToken` and exits — the 2FA check on lines 241–324 is never reached. This means any account in this state has its second factor silently waived.

Crucially, the `/change-password` endpoint (line 654) accepts this same sessionToken and, if `mustChangePassword=true`, allows a full password change with **no current password and no 2FA code** required:

```js
// auth.js:700-702
} else if (!user.mustChangePassword) {
  // If mustChangePassword is false, current password is required
  return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Current password is required' });
}
```

**Exploit Scenario:**
1. Admin resets an existing user's password and the system sets `mustChangePassword=true` (e.g. after a support request). The user already has 2FA enrolled.
2. Attacker intercepts or obtains the temporary password (e.g. via a weak reset email channel, phishing, or an internal leak).
3. Attacker `POST /api/auth/login` with the temporary credentials → receives `{ mustChangePassword: true, sessionToken: "..." }` — no 2FA challenge is issued.
4. Attacker immediately calls `POST /api/auth/change-password` with the `sessionToken` and their chosen `newPassword`.
5. Account is fully compromised; the legitimate user's 2FA provided zero protection.

**Recommendation:**
Move the `mustChangePassword` check to **after** the 2FA verification block, or issue the force-change-password sessionToken only once a 2FA challenge has been successfully completed for users who have `twoFactorEnabled=true`.

---

## Vuln 2: FTPS Connections Disable TLS Certificate Validation

**File:** [backend/utils/ftp.js:57](backend/utils/ftp.js#L57), [219](backend/utils/ftp.js#L219), [362](backend/utils/ftp.js#L362), [474](backend/utils/ftp.js#L474)
**Severity:** Medium
**Category:** certificate_validation_bypass
**Confidence:** 8/10

**Description:**
Every FTPS connection helper in the file (there are four) passes `rejectUnauthorized: false` in the TLS options whenever secure mode is active:

```js
secureOptions: secure ? { rejectUnauthorized: false } : undefined
```

This disables certificate chain and hostname verification for all FTPS (implicit TLS) connections. The SFTP code path does not share this problem.

**Exploit Scenario:**
1. The application is deployed in a network where a MITM position is achievable (e.g., cloud VPC with compromised routing, on-prem network with ARP spoofing, or a shared-network ISP).
2. An attacker intercepts the FTPS connection between the server and the configured FTP host, presenting a self-signed certificate.
3. Because `rejectUnauthorized: false` is set, the TLS handshake succeeds unconditionally.
4. The attacker reads all downloaded invoice PDFs in transit, captures the FTP credentials used for authentication, and can inject malformed PDFs into the import pipeline.
5. This is especially impactful because FTP credentials are long-lived and the downloaded content feeds directly into the parsing and invoice creation pipelines.

**Recommendation:**
Remove `rejectUnauthorized: false`. If the FTP server uses a self-signed certificate, add the CA certificate to the trust store instead:

```js
secureOptions: secure ? { ca: fs.readFileSync(process.env.FTP_CA_CERT) } : undefined
```

If a setting allows this to be toggled, it should be coupled with a visible UI warning.

---

## Vuln 3: FTP Folder Paths Accepted Without Traversal Validation

**File:** [backend/jobs/ftpScanner.js:52-64](backend/jobs/ftpScanner.js#L52-L64)
**Severity:** Medium
**Category:** path_traversal
**Confidence:** 8/10

**Description:**
When multi-folder FTP scanning is configured, each folder's `path` value is used verbatim with no sanitisation:

```js
// ftpScanner.js:52-64
const folderPath = folder.path; // taken directly from DB settings, no validation
...
const folderConfig = {
  directory: ftpConfig.directory
    ? folderPath
    : `${ftpConfig.directory}${folderPath}`   // string concat, no normalisation
};
```

This `folderPath` is then propagated as `ftpFolder` into the Bull job payload and later used in `fileImport.js:160`:

```js
const relativeSourcePath = ftpFolder ? `${ftpFolder}/${fileName}` : fileName;
```

A value like `../../payroll` would traverse outside the intended FTP root and cause the scanner to process files from unintended server-side directories, or write metadata records labelled with adversarial paths into the database.

**Exploit Scenario:**
1. An attacker gains the `global_admin` role (e.g., via a compromised admin account or privilege escalation).
2. They configure an FTP folder with `path: "../../sensitiveDir"`.
3. The scanner lists and queues files from a directory the application was never intended to touch on the FTP server.
4. Imported files are parsed and stored in the database, potentially ingesting documents from a shared FTP server that belong to other tenants or system areas.

**Recommendation:**
Validate each `folder.path` value on save (in the settings route) and on use in the scanner:

```js
const normalised = path.posix.normalize(folder.path);
if (normalised.startsWith('..') || path.posix.isAbsolute(normalised)) {
  throw new Error(`Unsafe FTP folder path rejected: ${folder.path}`);
}
```

---

## Summary

| # | Severity | Category | File | Line(s) | Confidence |
|---|----------|----------|------|---------|------------|
| 1 | **High** | Authentication Bypass | [routes/auth.js](backend/routes/auth.js) | 204–219, 654–707 | 8/10 |
| 2 | **Medium** | Cert Validation Bypass | [utils/ftp.js](backend/utils/ftp.js) | 57, 219, 362, 474 | 8/10 |
| 3 | **Medium** | Path Traversal | [jobs/ftpScanner.js](backend/jobs/ftpScanner.js) | 52–64 | 8/10 |

**Findings confirmed as false positives (not reported):** JWT role manipulation (tokens are properly HMAC-signed), document access caching (re-queried from DB per request), CSP unsafe-inline (hardening concern, no XSS injection point), path traversal in invoice download (admin-only write path), file upload double-extension (Node.js doesn't execute PHP, MIME validated), SQL injection in raw queries (all static, no user interpolation), PII in logs (only email addresses logged, not passwords or tokens), and registration UUID enumeration (UUIDs are unguessable).

6	/**
7	 * Scheduled job to scan FTP folders and queue files for import
8	 * This should be run periodically (e.g., every 5-15 minutes)
9	 */
10	
11	async function scanFTPFolders() {
12	  try {
13	    console.log('🔍 Starting FTP folder scan...');
14	    
15	    const settings = await Settings.getSettings();
16	    
17	    if (!settings.ftp || !settings.ftp.enabled) {
18	      console.log('ℹ️  FTP import is not enabled');
19	      return { scanned: 0, queued: 0 };
20	    }
21	    
22	    const ftpConfig = settings.ftp;
23	    let totalScanned = 0;
24	    let totalQueued = 0;
25	    
26	    // Single folder mode - scan base directory only
27	    if (ftpConfig.singleFolderMode) {
28	      console.log(`📁 Scanning single folder: ${ftpConfig.directory}`);
29	      
30	      const files = await listFiles(ftpConfig);
31	      totalScanned += files.length;
32	      
33	      for (const file of files) {
34	        const queued = await queueFileIfNew(file, ftpConfig, ftpConfig.directory, ftpConfig.singleFolderFileType || 'invoice');
35	        if (queued) totalQueued++;
36	      }
37	    } else {
38	      // Multi-folder mode - scan each configured folder
39	      const folders = ftpConfig.folders || [];
40	      const folderStructure = ftpConfig.folderStructure || {
41	        unprocessed: '/Unprocessed',
42	        processed: '/Processed',
43	        failed: '/Failed'
44	      };
45	      
46	      for (const folder of folders) {
47	        if (!folder.enabled) {
48	          console.log(`⏭️  Skipping disabled folder: ${folder.path}`);
49	          continue;
50	        }
51	        
52	        // Use folder path as-is (should be relative to base directory)
53	        const folderPath = folder.path;
54	        const fileType = folder.fileType || 'auto'; // 'auto' means detect from filename
55	        
56	        console.log(`📁 Scanning folder: ${folderPath} (type: ${fileType === 'auto' ? 'auto-detect' : fileType})`);
57	        
58	        try {
59	          // Create folder-specific FTP config
60	          const folderConfig = {
61	            ...ftpConfig,
62	            directory: ftpConfig.directory === '/' 
63	              ? folderPath 
64	              : `${ftpConfig.directory}${folderPath}`
65	          };
66	          
67	          const files = await listFiles(folderConfig);
68	          totalScanned += files.length;
69	          
70	          for (const file of files) {
71	            // Always use 'auto' for file type - will be detected from PDF content
72	            const queued = await queueFileIfNew(file, folderConfig, folderPath, 'auto');
73	            if (queued) totalQueued++;
74	          }
75	        } catch (error) {
76	          console.error(`❌ Error scanning folder ${folderPath}:`, error.message);
77	        }
78	      }
79	    }
80	    
81	    console.log(`✅ FTP scan completed: ${totalScanned} files scanned, ${totalQueued} new files queued`);
82	    
83	    return {
84	      scanned: totalScanned,
85	      queued: totalQueued
86	    };
87	  } catch (error) {
88	    console.error('❌ FTP scan error:', error.message);
89	    throw error;
90	  }
91	}

93	/**
94	 * Queue a file for import if it hasn't been processed before
95	 */
96	async function queueFileIfNew(file, ftpConfig, folderPath, fileType) {
97	  try {
98	    // Check if file already exists in queue (by filename and folder)
99	    // BullMQ uses getJobs with state names including 'wait'
100	    const existingJobs = await fileImportQueue.getJobs(['waiting', 'wait', 'active', 'delayed']);
101	    const alreadyQueued = existingJobs.some(job => 
102	      job.data.fileName === file.name && 
103	      job.data.ftpFolder === folderPath
104	    );
105	    
106	    if (alreadyQueued) {
107	      console.log(`⏭️  File already queued: ${file.name}`);
108	      return false;
109	    }
110	    
111	    // Check if file was recently processed (within last hour)
112	    // This prevents re-queuing files that are currently being processed
113	    const recentFile = await File.findOne({
114	      where: {
115	        fileName: file.name,
116	        ftpFolder: folderPath,
117	        uploadedAt: {
118	          [Op.gte]: new Date(Date.now() - 60 * 60 * 1000) // Last hour
119	        }
120	      }
121	    });
122	    
123	    if (recentFile) {
124	      console.log(`⏭️  File recently processed: ${file.name}`);
125	      return false;
126	    }
127	    
128	    // Queue the file (BullMQ format: name, data, options)
129	    await fileImportQueue.add('file-import', {
130	      fileName: file.name,
131	      remotePath: file.name,
132	      ftpConfig,
133	      fileType,
134	      ftpFolder: folderPath
135	    }, {
136	      jobId: `ftp-import-${Date.now()}-${file.name}`,
137	      removeOnComplete: true,
138	      removeOnFail: false
139	    });
140	    
141	    console.log(`✅ Queued file: ${file.name} (${fileType})`);
142	    return true;
143	  } catch (error) {
144	    console.error(`❌ Error queueing file ${file.name}:`, error.message);
145	    return false;
