```text
1	const nodemailer = require('nodemailer');
2	const axios = require('axios');
3	
4	/**
5	 * Unified Email Service
6	 * Supports multiple email providers: SMTP, Office 365 (Microsoft Graph), Resend, SMTP2Go
7	 * 
8	 * Features:
9	 * - Connection pooling for SMTP providers (reuses connections)
10	 * - Provider-specific transporter caching
11	 * - Automatic connection health checks and reconnection
12	 */
13	
14	// Transporter pool cache - stores transporters by provider config key
15	const transporterPool = new Map();
16	
17	// Configuration for connection pooling
18	const POOL_CONFIG = {
19	  maxConnections: parseInt(process.env.EMAIL_SMTP_POOL_SIZE) || 10,
20	  maxMessages: parseInt(process.env.EMAIL_SMTP_MAX_MESSAGES_PER_CONNECTION) || 100,
21	  pool: true,
22	  rateDelta: 1000, // 1 second between messages on same connection
23	  rateLimit: 14 // Max messages per rateDelta
24	};
25	
26	/**
27	 * Generate a unique key for transporter caching based on SMTP config
28	 * @param {Object} smtpConfig - SMTP configuration
29	 * @returns {string} Cache key
30	 */
31	function getTransporterKey(smtpConfig) {
32	  const host = smtpConfig.host || '';
33	  const port = smtpConfig.port || 587;
34	  const user = smtpConfig.auth?.user || smtpConfig.user || '';
35	  return `smtp:${host}:${port}:${user}`;
36	}
37	
38	/**
39	 * Get or create a pooled transporter for SMTP
40	 * @param {Object} smtpConfig - SMTP configuration
41	 * @returns {Object} Nodemailer transporter instance
42	 */
43	function getPooledTransporter(smtpConfig) {
44	  const key = getTransporterKey(smtpConfig);
45	  
46	  // Check if transporter exists in pool
47	  if (transporterPool.has(key)) {
48	    const cached = transporterPool.get(key);
49	    // Verify transporter is still valid
50	    if (cached.transporter && cached.transporter.verify) {
51	      return cached.transporter;
52	    }
53	    // Remove invalid transporter
54	    transporterPool.delete(key);
55	  }
56	  
57	  // Normalize localhost to 127.0.0.1 to avoid IPv6 resolution issues on Windows
58	  let host = smtpConfig.host;
59	  if (host.toLowerCase() === 'localhost') {
60	    host = '127.0.0.1';
61	  }
62	  
63	  // For MailHog and local SMTP servers, don't require auth if username/password are empty
64	  const authConfig = {};
65	  const username = smtpConfig.auth?.user || smtpConfig.user || '';
66	  const password = smtpConfig.auth?.password || smtpConfig.password || '';
67	  
68	  // Only add auth if username and password are provided
69	  if (username && password) {
70	    authConfig.auth = {
71	      user: username,
72	      pass: password
73	    };
74	  }
75	  
76	  // For localhost/MailHog, disable TLS certificate validation
77	  const isLocalhost = host === '127.0.0.1' || host === 'localhost';
78	  
79	  // Determine secure flag based on port (safety check)
80	  // Port 25 = unencrypted, Port 587 = STARTTLS (secure: false), Port 465 = SSL/TLS (secure: true)
81	  const port = smtpConfig.port || 587;
82	  let secure = smtpConfig.secure || false;
83	  if (port === 25) {
84	    // Port 25 is always unencrypted - force secure to false
85	    secure = false;
86	  } else if (port === 465) {
87	    // Port 465 requires SSL/TLS - force secure to true
88	    secure = true;
89	  }
90	  // Port 587 and others use STARTTLS (secure: false, but TLS is negotiated)
91	  
92	  // Create pooled transporter
93	  const transporter = nodemailer.createTransport({
94	    host: host,
95	    port: port,
96	    secure: secure,
97	    ...authConfig,
98	    tls: {
99	      rejectUnauthorized: isLocalhost ? false : (smtpConfig.rejectUnauthorized !== false)
100	    },
101	    connectionTimeout: 10000, // 10 second timeout
102	    greetingTimeout: 10000,
103	    socketTimeout: 10000,
104	    // Connection pooling configuration
105	    pool: POOL_CONFIG.pool,
106	    maxConnections: POOL_CONFIG.maxConnections,
107	    maxMessages: POOL_CONFIG.maxMessages,
108	    rateDelta: POOL_CONFIG.rateDelta,
109	    rateLimit: POOL_CONFIG.rateLimit
110	  });
111	  
112	  // Store in pool with metadata
113	  transporterPool.set(key, {
114	    transporter,
115	    config: smtpConfig,
116	    createdAt: Date.now(),
117	    lastUsed: Date.now(),
118	    messageCount: 0,
119	    connectionCount: 0
120	  });
121	  
122	  // Handle transporter errors and cleanup
123	  transporter.on('error', (error) => {
124	    console.error(`[EmailService] Transporter error for ${key}:`, error.message);
125	    // Remove from pool on error - will be recreated on next use
126	    transporterPool.delete(key);
127	  });
128	  
129	  // Track connection events
130	  transporter.on('token', (token) => {
131	    const cached = transporterPool.get(key);
132	    if (cached) {
133	      cached.connectionCount++;
134	    }
135	  });
136	  
137	  console.log(`[EmailService] Created pooled transporter for ${key} (pool: ${POOL_CONFIG.maxConnections} connections, ${POOL_CONFIG.maxMessages} messages/connection)`);
138	  
139	  return transporter;
140	}
141	
142	/**
143	 * Cleanup idle transporters from pool (called periodically)
144	 */
145	function cleanupTransporterPool() {
146	  const now = Date.now();
147	  const maxIdleTime = 5 * 60 * 1000; // 5 minutes
148	  
149	  for (const [key, cached] of transporterPool.entries()) {
150	    if (now - cached.lastUsed > maxIdleTime) {
151	      // Close transporter and remove from pool
152	      if (cached.transporter && cached.transporter.close) {
153	        cached.transporter.close();
154	      }
155	      console.log(`[EmailService] Cleaned up idle transporter: ${key} (${cached.messageCount || 0} messages sent)`);
156	      transporterPool.delete(key);
157	    }
158	  }
159	}
160	
161	/**
162	 * Get transporter pool statistics for monitoring
163	 * @returns {Object} Pool statistics
164	 */
165	function getTransporterPoolStats() {
166	  const stats = {
167	    totalTransporters: transporterPool.size,
168	    transporters: []
169	  };
170	  
171	  for (const [key, cached] of transporterPool.entries()) {
172	    stats.transporters.push({
173	      key,
174	      messageCount: cached.messageCount || 0,
175	      connectionCount: cached.connectionCount || 0,
176	      lastUsed: new Date(cached.lastUsed).toISOString(),
177	      age: Date.now() - cached.createdAt
178	    });
179	  }
180	  
181	  return stats;
182	}
183	
184	// Cleanup idle transporters every 5 minutes
185	setInterval(cleanupTransporterPool, 5 * 60 * 1000);
186	
187	/**
188	 * Get email provider configuration from settings or environment variables
189	 * Priority: Environment variables > Database settings (for credentials)
190	 * Database settings are still used for non-sensitive config (enabled flag, fromEmail, etc.)
191	 */
192	function getEmailProviderConfig(settings) {
193	  // Priority: Database provider setting first, then environment variables (for credentials)
194	  // Only use env EMAIL_PROVIDER if database doesn't have a provider configured
195	  const dbProvider = settings?.emailProvider?.provider;
196	  const envProvider = process.env.EMAIL_PROVIDER;
197	  
198	  // Use database provider if set, otherwise fall back to env or default to 'smtp'
199	  const provider = dbProvider || envProvider || 'smtp';
200	  
201	  // Debug logging to trace provider selection
202	  console.log('[EmailService] Provider selection:', {
203	    dbProvider: dbProvider || 'NOT SET',
204	    envProvider: envProvider || 'NOT SET',
205	    finalProvider: provider,
206	    hasEmailProvider: !!settings?.emailProvider,
207	    emailProviderKeys: settings?.emailProvider ? Object.keys(settings.emailProvider) : []
208	  });
209	  
210	  // If database has a provider configured, use it (respect database settings)
211	  // Only use env provider if database doesn't specify one
212	  if (provider === 'smtp') {
213	    // Use env vars for credentials, database for other config
214	    const dbConfig = settings?.emailProvider?.smtp || {};
215	    const port = parseInt(process.env.SMTP_PORT || dbConfig.port || '587');
216	    
217	    // Automatically set secure based on port: 465 = SSL/TLS, others = STARTTLS or unencrypted
218	    // Port 25 is unencrypted, port 587 uses STARTTLS (secure: false), port 465 uses SSL (secure: true)
219	    let secure = false;
220	    if (process.env.SMTP_SECURE === 'true') {
221	      secure = true;
222	    } else if (dbConfig.secure === true) {
223	      secure = true;
224	    } else if (port === 465) {
225	      secure = true; // Port 465 requires SSL/TLS
226	    } else {
227	      secure = false; // Port 25, 587, etc. use STARTTLS or are unencrypted
228	    }
229	    
230	    return {
231	      enabled: settings?.emailProvider?.enabled || (process.env.SMTP_HOST ? true : false),
232	      provider: 'smtp',
233	      smtp: {
234	        host: process.env.SMTP_HOST || dbConfig.host || '',
235	        port: port,
236	        secure: secure,
237	        auth: {
238	          user: process.env.SMTP_USER || dbConfig.auth?.user || '',
239	          password: process.env.SMTP_PASSWORD || dbConfig.auth?.password || '' // Env var takes priority
240	        },
241	        fromEmail: process.env.SMTP_FROM_EMAIL || dbConfig.fromEmail || '',
242	        fromName: process.env.SMTP_FROM_NAME || dbConfig.fromName || 'Makita EDI Portal',
243	        rejectUnauthorized: process.env.SMTP_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED !== 'false' && (dbConfig.rejectUnauthorized !== false)
244	      }
245	    };
246	  }
247	  
248	  if (provider === 'office365') {
249	    // Use env vars for credentials, database for other config
250	    const dbConfig = settings?.emailProvider?.office365 || {};
251	    
252	    // Only enable if explicitly enabled in database OR if env vars are set AND database doesn't disable it
253	    const hasEnvVars = process.env.OFFICE365_TENANT_ID && process.env.OFFICE365_CLIENT_ID && process.env.OFFICE365_CLIENT_SECRET;
254	    const enabled = settings?.emailProvider?.enabled === true || (hasEnvVars && settings?.emailProvider?.enabled !== false);
255	    
256	    return {
257	      enabled: enabled,
258	      provider: 'office365',
259	      office365: {
260	        tenantId: process.env.OFFICE365_TENANT_ID || dbConfig.tenantId || '',
261	        clientId: process.env.OFFICE365_CLIENT_ID || dbConfig.clientId || '',
262	        clientSecret: process.env.OFFICE365_CLIENT_SECRET || dbConfig.clientSecret || '', // Env var takes priority
263	        fromEmail: process.env.OFFICE365_FROM_EMAIL || dbConfig.fromEmail || '',
264	        sendAsUser: process.env.OFFICE365_SEND_AS_USER || dbConfig.sendAsUser || ''
265	      }
266	    };
267	  }
268	  
269	  if (provider === 'resend') {
270	    // Use env vars for credentials, database for other config
271	    const dbConfig = settings?.emailProvider?.resend || {};
272	    return {
273	      enabled: settings?.emailProvider?.enabled || (process.env.RESEND_API_KEY ? true : false),
274	      provider: 'resend',
275	      resend: {
276	        apiKey: process.env.RESEND_API_KEY || dbConfig.apiKey || '', // Env var takes priority
277	        fromEmail: process.env.RESEND_FROM_EMAIL || dbConfig.fromEmail || '',
278	        fromName: process.env.RESEND_FROM_NAME || dbConfig.fromName || 'Makita EDI Portal'
279	      }
280	    };
281	  }
282	  
283	  if (provider === 'smtp2go') {
284	    // Use env vars for credentials, database for other config
285	    const dbConfig = settings?.emailProvider?.smtp2go || {};
286	    return {
287	      enabled: settings?.emailProvider?.enabled || (process.env.SMTP2GO_API_KEY ? true : false),
288	      provider: 'smtp2go',
289	      smtp2go: {
290	        apiKey: process.env.SMTP2GO_API_KEY || dbConfig.apiKey || '', // Env var takes priority
291	        fromEmail: process.env.SMTP2GO_FROM_EMAIL || dbConfig.fromEmail || '',
292	        fromName: process.env.SMTP2GO_FROM_NAME || dbConfig.fromName || 'Makita EDI Portal'
293	      }
294	    };
295	  }
296	  
297	  if (provider === 'mailtrap') {
298	    // Use env vars for credentials, database for other config
299	    const dbConfig = settings?.emailProvider?.mailtrap || {};
300	    return {
301	      enabled: settings?.emailProvider?.enabled || (process.env.MAILTRAP_USER ? true : false),
302	      provider: 'mailtrap',
303	      mailtrap: {
304	        host: process.env.MAILTRAP_HOST || dbConfig.host || 'sandbox.smtp.mailtrap.io',
305	        port: parseInt(process.env.MAILTRAP_PORT || dbConfig.port || '2525'),
306	        secure: dbConfig.secure || false,
307	        auth: {
308	          user: process.env.MAILTRAP_USER || dbConfig.auth?.user || '',
309	          password: process.env.MAILTRAP_PASSWORD || dbConfig.auth?.password || ''
310	        },
311	        fromEmail: process.env.MAILTRAP_FROM_EMAIL || dbConfig.fromEmail || '',
312	        fromName: process.env.MAILTRAP_FROM_NAME || dbConfig.fromName || 'Makita EDI Portal',
313	        rejectUnauthorized: false
314	      }
315	    };
316	  }
317	  
318	  // Fallback to database if no env vars and database has config
319	  if (settings?.emailProvider?.enabled) {
320	    return settings.emailProvider;
321	  }
322	  
323	  return null;
324	}
325	
326	/**
327	 * Check if email is enabled
328	 * Mailtrap provider = test mode, always enabled regardless of global setting
329	 * @param {Object} settings - Settings object
330	 * @returns {boolean} - Whether email is enabled
331	 */
332	function isEmailEnabled(settings) {
333	  const provider = settings?.emailProvider?.provider;
334	  
335	  // Mailtrap = test mode, always enabled
336	  if (provider === 'mailtrap') {
337	    return true;
338	  }
339	  
340	  // Otherwise use the global enabled flag
341	  return settings?.emailProvider?.enabled === true || settings?.smtp?.enabled === true;
342	}
343	
344	/**
345	 * Send email using the configured provider
346	 * @param {Object} options - Email options
347	 * @param {string} options.to - Recipient email address
348	 * @param {string} options.subject - Email subject
349	 * @param {string} options.html - HTML email body
350	 * @param {string} options.text - Plain text email body (optional)
351	 * @param {Array} options.attachments - Array of attachment objects (optional)
352	 * @param {Object} settings - Settings object with email configuration
353	 * @returns {Promise<Object>} - Result object with success status and message
354	 */
355	async function sendEmail(options, settings) {
356	  let { to, cc, subject, html, text, attachments = [] } = options;
357	  
358	  // EMAIL TEST MODE: Redirect all emails to a single test address
359	  const testMode = settings?.emailProvider?.testMode;
360	  if (testMode?.enabled && testMode?.redirectEmail) {
361	    const originalRecipient = to;
362	    to = testMode.redirectEmail;
363	    subject = `[TEST -> ${originalRecipient}] ${subject}`;
364	    console.log(`[EmailService] TEST MODE: Redirecting from ${originalRecipient} to ${to}`);
365	  }
366	  
367	  // Get provider config from database or environment
368	  const providerConfig = getEmailProviderConfig(settings);
369	  
370	  // Use isEmailEnabled helper - Mailtrap bypasses the global enable flag
371	  if (!providerConfig || !isEmailEnabled(settings)) {
372	    const error = new Error('Email provider is not configured or enabled');
373	    throw error;
374	  }
375	
376	  const provider = providerConfig.provider || 'smtp';
377	  const startTime = Date.now();
378	  
379	  // Build the final options (may have modified to/subject from test mode)
380	  const finalOptions = { to, cc, subject, html, text, attachments };
381	
382	  try {
383	    let result;
384	    switch (provider) {
385	      case 'smtp':
386	        result = await sendViaSMTP(finalOptions, providerConfig.smtp);
387	        break;
388	      case 'office365':
389	        // Office 365 supports batch recipients (array of emails)
390	        if (Array.isArray(finalOptions.to) && finalOptions.to.length > 1) {
391	          result = await sendBatchViaOffice365(finalOptions, providerConfig.office365);
392	        } else {
393	          result = await sendViaOffice365(finalOptions, providerConfig.office365);
394	        }
395	        break;
396	      case 'resend':
397	        result = await sendViaResend(finalOptions, providerConfig.resend);
398	        break;
399	      case 'smtp2go':
400	        result = await sendViaSMTP2Go(finalOptions, providerConfig.smtp2go);
401	        break;
402	      case 'mailtrap':
403	        // Mailtrap uses standard SMTP, reuse sendViaSMTP
404	        result = await sendViaSMTP(finalOptions, providerConfig.mailtrap);
405	        break;
406	      default:
407	        throw new Error(`Unsupported email provider: ${provider}`);
408	    }
409	
410	    return result;
411	  } catch (error) {
412	    throw error;
413	  }
414	}
415	
416	/**
417	 * Send email via standard SMTP (with connection pooling)
418	 */
419	async function sendViaSMTP(options, smtpConfig) {
420	  if (!smtpConfig || !smtpConfig.host) {
421	    throw new Error('SMTP configuration is incomplete');
422	  }
423	
424	  // Get pooled transporter (reuses connections)
425	  const transporter = getPooledTransporter(smtpConfig);
426	  
427	  // Update last used time and message count
428	  const key = getTransporterKey(smtpConfig);
429	  if (transporterPool.has(key)) {
430	    const cached = transporterPool.get(key);
431	    cached.lastUsed = Date.now();
432	    cached.messageCount = (cached.messageCount || 0) + 1;
433	  }
434	
435	  const fromEmail = smtpConfig.fromEmail || smtpConfig.from;
436	  const fromName = smtpConfig.fromName || 'Invoice Portal';
437	
438	  const mailOptions = {
439	    from: `"${fromName}" <${fromEmail}>`,
440	    to: options.to,
441	    cc: options.cc, // CC recipients if provided
442	    subject: options.subject,
443	    html: options.html,
444	    text: options.text || options.html.replace(/<[^>]*>/g, ''), // Strip HTML for text version
445	    attachments: options.attachments || []
446	  };
447	
448	  const result = await transporter.sendMail(mailOptions);
449	  
450	  // Store from email in options for logging
451	  options.from = fromEmail;
452	  
453	  return {
454	    success: true,
455	    messageId: result.messageId,
456	    provider: 'smtp',
457	    response: result.response,
458	    fromEmail: fromEmail
459	  };
460	}
461	
462	/**
463	 * Send email via Office 365 (Microsoft Graph API)
464	 * Supports single recipient or batch recipients (up to 500 per message)
465	 * Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/outlook-mail-concept-overview
466	 * 
467	 * @param {Object} options - Email options
468	 * @param {string|string[]} options.to - Single recipient email or array of recipients (max 500)
469	 * @param {string} options.subject - Email subject
470	 * @param {string} options.html - HTML email body
471	 * @param {string} options.text - Plain text email body (optional)
472	 * @param {Array} options.attachments - Array of attachment objects (optional)
473	 * @param {Object} config - Office 365 configuration
474	 * @returns {Promise<Object>} Result object
475	 */
476	async function sendViaOffice365(options, config) {
477	  if (!config || !config.clientId || !config.clientSecret || !config.tenantId) {
478	    throw new Error('Office 365 configuration is incomplete');
479	  }
480	
481	  // Get access token
482	  const tokenEndpoint = `https://login.microsoftonline.com/${config.tenantId}/oauth2/v2.0/token`;
483	  
484	  const tokenResponse = await axios.post(tokenEndpoint, new URLSearchParams({
485	    client_id: config.clientId,
486	    client_secret: config.clientSecret,
487	    scope: 'https://graph.microsoft.com/.default',
488	    grant_type: 'client_credentials'
489	  }), {
490	    headers: {
491	      'Content-Type': 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded'
492	    }
493	  });
494	
495	  const accessToken = tokenResponse.data.access_token;
496	
497	  // Handle single recipient or array of recipients
498	  const recipients = Array.isArray(options.to) ? options.to : [options.to];
499	  const ccRecipients = options.cc ? (Array.isArray(options.cc) ? options.cc : [options.cc]) : [];
500	  
501	  // Office 365 limit: 500 recipients per message (TO + CC combined)
502	  const totalRecipients = recipients.length + ccRecipients.length;
503	  if (totalRecipients > 500) {
504	    throw new Error(`Office 365 supports maximum 500 recipients per message (TO + CC). Received ${totalRecipients} recipients.`);
505	  }
506	
507	  // Prepare message with multiple recipients
508	  const message = {
509	    message: {
510	      subject: options.subject,
511	      body: {
512	        contentType: 'HTML',
513	        content: options.html
514	      },
515	      toRecipients: recipients.map(email => ({
516	        emailAddress: {
517	          address: email
518	        }
519	      }))
520	    }
521	  };
522	  
523	  // Add CC recipients if provided
524	  if (ccRecipients.length > 0) {
525	    message.message.ccRecipients = ccRecipients.map(email => ({
526	      emailAddress: {
527	        address: email
528	      }
529	    }));
530	  }
531	
532	  // Add attachments if provided
533	  if (options.attachments && options.attachments.length > 0) {
534	    message.message.attachments = await Promise.all(
535	      options.attachments.map(async (att) => {
536	        const fs = require('fs');
537	        const path = require('path');
538	        
539	        let contentBytes;
540	        if (att.path) {
541	          contentBytes = fs.readFileSync(att.path).toString('base64');
542	        } else if (att.content) {
543	          // Check if already base64 encoded
544	          if (att.encoding === 'base64') {
545	            contentBytes = att.content;
546	          } else {
547	            contentBytes = Buffer.from(att.content).toString('base64');
548	          }
549	        } else {
550	          throw new Error('Attachment must have either path or content');
551	        }
552	
553	        return {
554	          '@odata.type': '#microsoft.graph.fileAttachment',
555	          name: att.filename || path.basename(att.path || 'attachment'),
556	          contentType: att.contentType || 'application/octet-stream',
557	          contentBytes: contentBytes
558	        };
559	      })
560	    );
561	  }
562	
563	  // Send email via Microsoft Graph API
564	  // Use sendAsUser if provided, otherwise use fromEmail
565	  const userEmail = config.sendAsUser || config.fromEmail;
566	  if (!userEmail) {
567	    throw new Error('Office 365 fromEmail or sendAsUser is required');
568	  }
569	
570	  const sendEndpoint = `https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/users/${userEmail}/sendMail`;
571	
572	  const response = await axios.post(sendEndpoint, message, {
573	    headers: {
574	      'Authorization': `Bearer ${accessToken}`,
575	      'Content-Type': 'application/json'
576	    }
577	  });
578	
579	  // Store from email in options for logging
580	  options.from = userEmail;
581	
582	  return {
583	    success: true,
584	    messageId: response.headers['x-request-id'] || 'unknown',
585	    provider: 'office365',
586	    response: response.data,
587	    fromEmail: userEmail,
588	    recipientCount: recipients.length
589	  };
590	}
591	
592	/**
593	 * Send batch emails via Office 365 (splits large batches into chunks of 500)
594	 * @param {Object} options - Email options
595	 * @param {string[]} options.to - Array of recipient emails
596	 * @param {string} options.subject - Email subject
597	 * @param {string} options.html - HTML email body
598	 * @param {string} options.text - Plain text email body (optional)
599	 * @param {Array} options.attachments - Array of attachment objects (optional)
600	 * @param {Object} config - Office 365 configuration
601	 * @returns {Promise<Object>} Result object with batch details
602	 */
603	async function sendBatchViaOffice365(options, config) {
604	  const recipients = Array.isArray(options.to) ? options.to : [options.to];
605	  const maxRecipientsPerMessage = 500;
606	  
607	  if (recipients.length === 0) {
608	    throw new Error('No recipients provided');
609	  }
610	  
611	  // If within limit, send as single message
612	  if (recipients.length <= maxRecipientsPerMessage) {
613	    return await sendViaOffice365(options, config);
614	  }
615	  
616	  // Split into batches of 500
617	  const batches = [];
618	  for (let i = 0; i < recipients.length; i += maxRecipientsPerMessage) {
619	    batches.push(recipients.slice(i, i + maxRecipientsPerMessage));
620	  }
621	  
622	  console.log(`[EmailService] Office 365 batch: ${recipients.length} recipients split into ${batches.length} messages`);
623	  
624	  // Send each batch
625	  const results = await Promise.all(
626	    batches.map((batch, index) => 
627	      sendViaOffice365({
628	        ...options,
629	        to: batch
630	      }, config)
631	    )
632	  );
633	  
634	  return {
635	    success: true,
636	    provider: 'office365',
637	    totalRecipients: recipients.length,
638	    messagesSent: batches.length,
639	    results: results
640	  };
641	}
642	
643	/**
644	 * Send email via Resend API
645	 * Reference: https://resend.com/docs/api-reference/introduction
646	 */
647	async function sendViaResend(options, config) {
648	  if (!config || !config.apiKey) {
649	    throw new Error('Resend configuration is incomplete');
650	  }
651	
652	  const payload = {
653	    from: config.fromEmail || 'onboarding@resend.dev',
654	    to: options.to,
655	    subject: options.subject,
656	    html: options.html,
657	    ...(options.text && { text: options.text })
658	  };
659	
660	  // Add attachments if provided
661	  if (options.attachments && options.attachments.length > 0) {
662	    payload.attachments = await Promise.all(
663	      options.attachments.map(async (att) => {
664	        const fs = require('fs');
665	        const path = require('path');
666	        
667	        let base64Content;
668	        if (att.path) {
669	          base64Content = fs.readFileSync(att.path).toString('base64');
670	        } else if (att.content) {
671	          // Check if already base64 encoded
672	          if (att.encoding === 'base64') {
673	            base64Content = att.content;
674	          } else {
675	            base64Content = Buffer.from(att.content).toString('base64');
676	          }
677	        } else {
678	          throw new Error('Attachment must have either path or content');
679	        }
680	
681	        return {
682	          filename: att.filename || path.basename(att.path || 'attachment'),
683	          content: base64Content,
684	          ...(att.contentType && { type: att.contentType })
685	        };
686	      })
687	    );
688	  }
689	
690	  const response = await axios.post('https://api.resend.com/emails', payload, {
691	    headers: {
692	      'Authorization': `Bearer ${config.apiKey}`,
693	      'Content-Type': 'application/json'
694	    }
695	  });
696	
697	  const fromEmail = config.fromEmail || 'onboarding@resend.dev';
698	  
699	  // Store from email in options for logging
700	  options.from = fromEmail;
701	
702	  return {
703	    success: true,
704	    messageId: response.data.id,
705	    provider: 'resend',
706	    response: response.data,
707	    fromEmail: fromEmail
708	  };
709	}
710	
711	/**
712	 * Send email via SMTP2Go API
713	 * Reference: https://developers.smtp2go.com/docs/getting-started
714	 */
715	async function sendViaSMTP2Go(options, config) {
716	  if (!config || !config.apiKey) {
717	    throw new Error('SMTP2Go configuration is incomplete');
718	  }
719	
720	  // Handle CC - SMTP2Go supports CC as array
721	  const toEmails = Array.isArray(options.to) ? options.to : [options.to];
722	  const ccEmails = options.cc ? (Array.isArray(options.cc) ? options.cc : [options.cc]) : [];
723	  
724	  const payload = {
725	    api_key: config.apiKey,
726	    to: toEmails,
727	    ...(ccEmails.length > 0 && { cc: ccEmails }),
728	    sender: config.fromEmail || 'noreply@smtp2go.com',
729	    subject: options.subject,
730	    html_body: options.html,
731	    ...(options.text && { text_body: options.text })
732	  };
733	
734	  // Add attachments if provided
735	  if (options.attachments && options.attachments.length > 0) {
736	    const fs = require('fs');
737	    const path = require('path');
738	    
739	    payload.attachments = await Promise.all(
740	      options.attachments.map(async (att) => {
741	        let base64Content;
742	        
743	        if (att.path) {
744	          // Read file from disk and encode to base64
745	          base64Content = fs.readFileSync(att.path).toString('base64');
746	        } else if (att.content) {
747	          // Content provided - check if already base64 encoded
748	          if (att.encoding === 'base64') {
749	            // Already base64 encoded, use as-is
750	            base64Content = att.content;
751	          } else {
752	            // Raw content, encode to base64
753	            base64Content = Buffer.from(att.content).toString('base64');
754	          }
755	        } else {
756	          throw new Error('Attachment must have either path or content');
757	        }
758	
759	        return {
760	          filename: att.filename || path.basename(att.path || 'attachment'),
761	          fileblob: base64Content,
762	          mimetype: att.contentType || 'application/octet-stream'
763	        };
764	      })
765	    );
766	  }
767	
768	  let response;
769	  try {
770	    response = await axios.post('https://api.smtp2go.com/v3/email/send', payload, {
771	      headers: {
772	        'Content-Type': 'application/json',
773	        'X-Smtp2go-Api-Key': config.apiKey
774	      }
775	    });
776	  } catch (axiosError) {
777	    // Log the full error response from SMTP2Go
778	    if (axiosError.response) {
779	      console.error('[SMTP2Go] API Error Response:', JSON.stringify(axiosError.response.data, null, 2));
780	      console.error('[SMTP2Go] Status:', axiosError.response.status);
781	      const errorMsg = axiosError.response.data?.data?.error || axiosError.response.data?.message || axiosError.message;
782	      throw new Error(`SMTP2Go API error (${axiosError.response.status}): ${errorMsg}`);
783	    }
784	    throw axiosError;
785	  }
786	
787	  if (response.data.data && response.data.data.error_code) {
788	    throw new Error(`SMTP2Go error: ${response.data.data.error}`);
789	  }
790	
791	  const fromEmail = config.fromEmail || 'noreply@smtp2go.com';
792	  
793	  // Store from email in options for logging
794	  options.from = fromEmail;
795	
796	  return {
797	    success: true,
798	    messageId: response.data.data?.email_id || 'unknown',
799	    provider: 'smtp2go',
800	    response: response.data,
801	    fromEmail: fromEmail
802	  };
803	}
804	
805	/**
806	 * Test email provider configuration
807	 */
808	async function testEmailProvider(settings, requestContext = {}) {
809	  // Get provider config from database or environment
810	  const providerConfig = getEmailProviderConfig(settings);
811	  
812	  if (!providerConfig || !providerConfig.enabled) {
813	    throw new Error('Email provider is not configured or enabled');
814	  }
815	
816	  const testEmail = providerConfig.testEmail || settings?.systemEmail || process.env.TEST_EMAIL;
817	  if (!testEmail) {
818	    throw new Error('Test email address is required. Set it in Settings or TEST_EMAIL environment variable.');
819	  }
820	
821	  try {
822	    await sendEmail({
823	      to: testEmail,
824	      subject: 'Test Email from Makita EDI Portal',
825	      html: '<p>This is a test email to verify your email provider configuration.</p><p>If you received this, your email settings are working correctly!</p>',
826	      text: 'This is a test email to verify your email provider configuration. If you received this, your email settings are working correctly!',
827	      isTestEmail: true,
828	      ...requestContext
829	    }, settings);
830	
831	    return {
832	      success: true,
833	      message: 'Test email sent successfully'
834	    };
835	  } catch (error) {
836	    return {
837	      success: false,
838	      message: error.message || 'Failed to send test email'
839	    };
840	  }
841	}
842	
843	module.exports = {
844	  sendEmail,
845	  testEmailProvider,
846	  sendViaSMTP,
847	  sendViaOffice365,
848	  sendBatchViaOffice365,
849	  sendViaResend,
850	  sendViaSMTP2Go,
851	  isEmailEnabled,
852	  getPooledTransporter,
853	  cleanupTransporterPool,
854	  getTransporterPoolStats
855	};
856	
857
```

<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./backend/utils/sendTemplatedEmail.js`

```text
1	/**
2	 * Send email using an HTML template file
3	 * Templates are loaded from backend/templates/emails/*.html
4	 * 
5	 * @param {string} templateName - Name of the template (e.g., 'welcome', 'password-reset')
6	 * @param {string} to - Recipient email address
7	 * @param {Object} data - Data object with variables for template
8	 * @param {Object} settings - Settings object with email configuration
9	 * @param {Object} requestContext - Optional request context for logging (ipAddress, userAgent, requestId, userId)
10	 * @returns {Promise<Object>} - Result from sendEmail
11	 */
12	const fs = require('fs');
13	const path = require('path');
14	const { sendEmail } = require('./emailService');
15	
16	// Template cache to avoid re-reading files in production
17	const templateCache = {};
18	
19	/**
20	 * Get the templates directory path
21	 */
22	function getTemplatesDir() {
23	  return path.join(__dirname, '../templates/emails');
24	}
25	
26	/**
27	 * Load a template file (with caching in production)
28	 */
29	function loadTemplate(templateName) {
30	  const cacheKey = templateName;
31	  
32	  if (templateCache[cacheKey] && process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production') {
33	    return templateCache[cacheKey];
34	  }
35	  
36	  const templatePath = path.join(getTemplatesDir(), `${templateName}.html`);
37	  
38	  if (!fs.existsSync(templatePath)) {
39	    throw new Error(`Email template not found: ${templateName}.html`);
40	  }
41	  
42	  const template = fs.readFileSync(templatePath, 'utf8');
43	  templateCache[cacheKey] = template;
44	  
45	  return template;
46	}
47	
48	/**
49	 * Extract subject from HTML title tag
50	 */
51	function extractSubject(html, data) {
52	  const titleMatch = html.match(/<title[^>]*>(.*?)<\/title>/i);
53	  if (titleMatch) {
54	    let subject = titleMatch[1];
55	    // Replace variables in subject
56	    subject = replaceVariables(subject, data);
57	    return subject;
58	  }
59	  return 'Notification';
60	}
61	
62	function escapeHtml(str) {
63	  return str
64	    .replace(/&/g, '&amp;')
65	    .replace(/</g, '&lt;')
66	    .replace(/>/g, '&gt;')
67	    .replace(/"/g, '&quot;')
68	    .replace(/'/g, '&#x27;');
69	}
70	
71	/**
72	 * Replace all occurrences of {{variable}} with values
73	 */
74	function replaceVariables(template, variables) {
75	  let result = template;
76	
77	  for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(variables)) {
78	    const regex = new RegExp(`\\{\\{${key}\\}\\}`, 'g');
79	    result = result.replace(regex, escapeHtml(String(value ?? '')));
80	  }
81	
82	  return result;
83	}
84	
85	/**
86	 * Process conditional sections in template
87	 * Supports: {{#if variable}}content{{/if}} and {{#unless variable}}content{{/unless}}
88	 */
89	function processConditionals(template, variables) {
90	  let result = template;
91	  
92	  // Match {{#if variable}}...{{/if}} - non-greedy
93	  const ifRegex = /\{\{#if\s+(\w+)\}\}([\s\S]*?)\{\{\/if\}\}/g;
94	  
95	  result = result.replace(ifRegex, (match, variable, content) => {
96	    const value = variables[variable];
97	    // Show content if value is truthy
98	    if (value && value !== 'false' && value !== '0') {
99	      return content;
100	    }
101	    return '';
102	  });
103	  
104	  // Handle {{#unless variable}}...{{/unless}}
105	  const unlessRegex = /\{\{#unless\s+(\w+)\}\}([\s\S]*?)\{\{\/unless\}\}/g;
106	  
107	  result = result.replace(unlessRegex, (match, variable, content) => {
108	    const value = variables[variable];
109	    // Show content if value is falsy
110	    if (!value || value === 'false' || value === '0') {
111	      return content;
112	    }
113	    return '';
114	  });
115	  
116	  // Handle {{#each items}}...{{/each}} for arrays
117	  const eachRegex = /\{\{#each\s+(\w+)\}\}([\s\S]*?)\{\{\/each\}\}/g;
118	  
119	  result = result.replace(eachRegex, (match, arrayName, itemTemplate) => {
120	    const items = variables[arrayName];
121	    if (!Array.isArray(items) || items.length === 0) {
122	      return '';
123	    }
124	    
125	    return items.map(item => {
126	      let itemHtml = itemTemplate;
127	      // Replace {{this.property}} with item.property
128	      itemHtml = itemHtml.replace(/\{\{this\.(\w+)\}\}/g, (m, prop) => {
129	        return item[prop] !== undefined ? String(item[prop]) : '';
130	      });
131	      return itemHtml;
132	    }).join('');
133	  });
134	  
135	  return result;
136	}
137	
138	/**
139	 * Generate a darker shade for button hover
140	 */
141	function darkenColor(hex, percent = 10) {
142	  hex = hex.replace('#', '');
143	  let r = parseInt(hex.substring(0, 2), 16);
144	  let g = parseInt(hex.substring(2, 4), 16);
145	  let b = parseInt(hex.substring(4, 6), 16);
146	  r = Math.max(0, Math.floor(r * (100 - percent) / 100));
147	  g = Math.max(0, Math.floor(g * (100 - percent) / 100));
148	  b = Math.max(0, Math.floor(b * (100 - percent) / 100));
149	  return `#${r.toString(16).padStart(2, '0')}${g.toString(16).padStart(2, '0')}${b.toString(16).padStart(2, '0')}`;
150	}
151	
152	const { getFrontendUrl, getBackendUrl, getLoginUrl } = require('./urlConfig');
153	
154	/**
155	 * Get branding variables from settings
156	 */
157	function getBrandingVariables(settings) {
158	  const primaryColor = settings?.primaryColor || '#066FD1';
159	  const primaryColorHover = darkenColor(primaryColor);
160	  const companyName = settings?.companyName || 'Makita EDI Portal';
161	  const supportEmail = settings?.supportEmail || 'support@example.com';
162	  const portalUrl = getFrontendUrl();
163	  const loginUrl = getLoginUrl();
164	  
165	  // Build logo URL
166	  // Logos are stored as paths like "/uploads/logo-xxx.png" and served directly at /uploads/
167	  let logoUrl = '';
168	  if (settings?.logoLight) {
169	    if (settings.logoLight.startsWith('http')) {
170	      logoUrl = settings.logoLight;
171	    } else {
172	      const backendUrl = getBackendUrl();
173	      // Logo path already includes /uploads/, so just append to backend URL
174	      logoUrl = `${backendUrl}${settings.logoLight.startsWith('/') ? '' : '/'}${settings.logoLight}`;
175	    }
176	  }
177	  
178	  return {
179	    primaryColor,
180	    primaryColorHover,
181	    companyName,
182	    supportEmail,
183	    portalUrl,
184	    loginUrl,
185	    logoUrl,
186	    currentYear: new Date().getFullYear().toString()
187	  };
188	}
189	
190	/**
191	 * Strip HTML tags for plain text version
192	 */
193	function stripHtml(html) {
194	  if (!html) return '';
195	  return html
196	    .replace(/<style[^>]*>[\s\S]*?<\/style>/gi, '')
197	    .replace(/<script[^>]*>[\s\S]*?<\/script>/gi, '')
198	    .replace(/<[^>]+>/g, '')
199	    .replace(/&nbsp;/g, ' ')
200	    .replace(/&amp;/g, '&')
201	    .replace(/&lt;/g, '<')
202	    .replace(/&gt;/g, '>')
203	    .replace(/&quot;/g, '"')
204	    .replace(/&#39;/g, "'")
205	    .replace(/\s+/g, ' ')
206	    .trim();
207	}
208	
209	/**
210	 * Render email template from HTML file
211	 */
212	function renderEmailTemplate(templateName, data, settings) {
213	  // Load template
214	  const template = loadTemplate(templateName);
215	  
216	  // Get branding variables
217	  const brandingVars = getBrandingVariables(settings);
218	  
219	  // Merge all variables (user data overrides branding)
220	  const allVariables = {
221	    ...brandingVars,
222	    ...data
223	  };
224	  
225	  // Extract subject from title tag before processing
226	  const subject = extractSubject(template, allVariables);
227	  
228	  // Replace variables
229	  let html = replaceVariables(template, allVariables);
230	  
231	  // Process conditionals
232	  html = processConditionals(html, allVariables);
233	  
234	  // Generate plain text version
235	  const text = stripHtml(html);
236	  
237	  return { subject, html, text };
238	}
239	
240	/**
241	 * Send email using template
242	 */
243	async function sendTemplatedEmail(templateName, to, data, settings, requestContext = {}) {
244	  try {
245	    // Render the template
246	    const rendered = renderEmailTemplate(templateName, data, settings);
247	    
248	    // Determine email category based on template name
249	    let category = 'system';
250	    if (['password-reset', 'password-changed', 'welcome', 'registration-request', 'registration-approved', 'registration-rejected'].includes(templateName)) {
251	      category = 'auth';
252	    } else if (['document-notification', 'document-summary', 'document-deleted', 'query-notification'].includes(templateName)) {
253	      category = 'document';
254	    } else if (['import-summary', 'retention-cleanup-summary'].includes(templateName)) {
255	      category = 'notification';
256	    }
257	    
258	    // Send the email with request context
259	    const result = await sendEmail({
260	      to,
261	      subject: rendered.subject,
262	      html: rendered.html,
263	      text: rendered.text,
264	      templateName,
265	      templateCategory: category,
266	      emailType: category,
267	      ...requestContext
268	    }, settings);
269	    
270	    return result;
271	  } catch (error) {
272	    console.error(`Error sending templated email (${templateName}):`, error);
273	    throw error;
274	  }
275	}
276	
277	module.exports = {
278	  sendTemplatedEmail,
279	  renderEmailTemplate,
280	  loadTemplate,
281	  getBrandingVariables
282	};
283
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./backend/utils/excelParser.js`

```text
1	/**
2	 * Excel Parser Utility
3	 * Extracts invoice data from Excel files using template cell mappings
4	 */
5	
6	const XLSX = require('xlsx');
7	const XLSX_CALC = require('xlsx-calc');
8	
9	/**
10	 * Convert column letter to number (A=1, B=2, ..., Z=26, AA=27, etc.)
11	 */
12	function columnToNumber(column) {
13	  let result = 0;
14	  for (let i = 0; i < column.length; i++) {
15	    result = result * 26 + (column.charCodeAt(i) - 'A'.charCodeAt(0) + 1);
16	  }
17	  return result;
18	}
19	
20	/**
21	 * Convert column number to letter (1=A, 2=B, ..., 26=Z, 27=AA, etc.)
22	 */
23	function numberToColumn(num) {
24	  let result = '';
25	  while (num > 0) {
26	    num--;
27	    result = String.fromCharCode(65 + (num % 26)) + result;
28	    num = Math.floor(num / 26);
29	  }
30	  return result;
31	}
32	
33	/**
34	 * Extract fields from Excel file using template cell mappings
35	 * @param {Buffer|string} excelFile - Excel file buffer or file path
36	 * @param {Object} template - Template with excelCells mappings
37	 * @returns {Promise<Object>} - Extracted fields
38	 */
39	async function extractFieldsFromExcel(excelFile, template) {
40	  if (!template.excelCells || Object.keys(template.excelCells).length === 0) {
41	    throw new Error('Template has no Excel cell mappings defined');
42	  }
43	
44	  let workbook;
45	  
46	  // Load workbook from buffer or file path
47	  if (Buffer.isBuffer(excelFile)) {
48	    workbook = XLSX.read(excelFile, { type: 'buffer' });
49	  } else if (typeof excelFile === 'string') {
50	    workbook = XLSX.readFile(excelFile);
51	  } else {
52	    throw new Error('Invalid Excel file format. Expected Buffer or file path.');
53	  }
54	  
55	  // Recalculate all formulas in the workbook using xlsx-calc
56	  // This populates cell.v with calculated values even if the file wasn't saved with them
57	  try {
58	    XLSX_CALC(workbook);
59	    console.log('✅ Formulas recalculated using xlsx-calc');
60	  } catch (calcError) {
61	    console.warn('⚠️  Error recalculating formulas with xlsx-calc:', calcError.message);
62	    // Continue anyway - some formulas might still work
63	  }
64	
65	  // Start with empty object - only add fields that are actually mapped in the template
66	  const extracted = {
67	    fullText: ''
68	  };
69	
70	  // Get sheet names
71	  const sheetNames = workbook.SheetNames;
72	  if (sheetNames.length === 0) {
73	    throw new Error('Excel file has no sheets');
74	  }
75	  
76	  // For multi-page files, always use the last sheet for parsing
77	  // This ensures we parse the most recent page when files have multiple pages
78	  const lastSheetIndex = sheetNames.length - 1;
79	  console.log(`📄 File has ${sheetNames.length} sheet(s). Using last sheet: "${sheetNames[lastSheetIndex]}"`);
80	
81	  // Extract all text for fullText
82	  let allText = [];
83	  sheetNames.forEach(sheetName => {
84	    const worksheet = workbook.Sheets[sheetName];
85	    const sheetData = XLSX.utils.sheet_to_json(worksheet, { header: 1, defval: '' });
86	    sheetData.forEach(row => {
87	      allText.push(row.join(' '));
88	    });
89	  });
90	  extracted.fullText = allText.join('\n');
91	
92	  // Extract fields using cell mappings
93	  for (const [fieldName, cellMapping] of Object.entries(template.excelCells)) {
94	    if (!cellMapping || !cellMapping.column || !cellMapping.row) {
95	      console.warn(`⚠️  Skipping ${fieldName}: missing column or row`);
96	      continue;
97	    }
98	
99	    // Always use the last sheet for parsing (for multi-page files)
100	    // The sheet index in the mapping is stored but we override to use last sheet
101	    const sheetIndex = lastSheetIndex;
102	    const sheetName = sheetNames[sheetIndex];
103	    const worksheet = workbook.Sheets[sheetName];
104	    
105	    console.log(`   Using sheet: "${sheetName}" (index ${sheetIndex}) for field ${fieldName}`);
106	
107	    // Convert column letter to number (1-based)
108	    const colNum = columnToNumber(cellMapping.column.toUpperCase());
109	    const rowNum = parseInt(cellMapping.row);
110	
111	    // Check if it's a range (has endColumn and endRow)
112	    const isRange = cellMapping.endColumn && cellMapping.endRow;
113	    let value = '';
114	
115	    if (isRange) {
116	      // Extract range of cells
117	      const endColNum = columnToNumber(cellMapping.endColumn.toUpperCase());
118	      const endRowNum = parseInt(cellMapping.endRow);
119	      
120	      const values = [];
121	      for (let R = rowNum - 1; R < endRowNum; R++) {
122	        const rowValues = [];
123	        for (let C = colNum - 1; C < endColNum; C++) {
124	          const cellAddress = XLSX.utils.encode_cell({ r: R, c: C });
125	          const cell = worksheet[cellAddress];
126	          if (cell) {
127	            rowValues.push(cell.w || cell.v || '');
128	          }
129	        }
130	        if (rowValues.length > 0) {
131	          values.push(rowValues.join('\t')); // Tab-separated for rows
132	        }
133	      }
134	      value = values.join('\n'); // Newline-separated for multiple rows
135	      console.log(`   Extracted ${fieldName} from range ${cellMapping.column}${cellMapping.row}:${cellMapping.endColumn}${cellMapping.endRow}`);
136	    } else {
137	      // Single cell
138	      const cellAddress = XLSX.utils.encode_cell({ r: rowNum - 1, c: colNum - 1 });
139	      const cell = worksheet[cellAddress];
140	
141	      if (!cell) {
142	        console.warn(`⚠️  Cell ${cellMapping.column}${cellMapping.row} not found in sheet "${sheetName}"`);
143	        continue;
144	      }
145	
146	      // Get cell value - handle formulas and different cell types
147	      // For formulas: cell.f contains the formula, cell.v contains the calculated value
148	      // If cell.v is undefined, the formula result wasn't calculated (file might need recalculation)
149	      
150	      // Handle formulas - xlsx-calc should have already recalculated them
151	      // After xlsx-calc, cell.v should contain the calculated value
152	      if (cell.f) {
153	        console.log(`   📐 Cell ${cellMapping.column}${cellMapping.row} contains formula: ${cell.f}`);
154	        console.log(`   📐 Cell type: ${cell.t}, cell.v: ${JSON.stringify(cell.v)}, cell.w: ${JSON.stringify(cell.w)}`);
155	        
156	        // After xlsx-calc, cell.v should have the calculated value
157	        // Check if cell.v has a meaningful value (not undefined, null, or empty string)
158	        if (cell.v !== undefined && cell.v !== null && cell.v !== '') {
159	          // Has calculated value from xlsx-calc - use it
160	          value = cell.v.toString();
161	          console.log(`   ✅ Using calculated value from xlsx-calc: "${value}"`);
162	        } else if (cell.w && cell.w.trim() !== '') {
163	          // Fallback to formatted display if calculated value is missing
164	          console.log(`   📐 No calculated value, trying cell.w (formatted): "${cell.w}"`);
165	          
166	          // Try to extract numeric value from formatted string (e.g., "£0.00", "0.00", "£1,234.56")
167	          const cleaned = cell.w.replace(/[£$€,\s]/g, '');
168	          const numMatch = cleaned.match(/[\d.]+/);
169	          
170	          if (numMatch && !isNaN(parseFloat(numMatch[0]))) {
171	            value = parseFloat(numMatch[0]).toString();
172	            console.log(`   ✅ Extracted numeric value from formatted string "${cell.w}": "${value}"`);
173	          } else {
174	            value = cell.w;
175	            console.log(`   ⚠️  Could not extract number from "${cell.w}", using as-is`);
176	          }
177	        } else {
178	          // Formula couldn't be calculated - try to extract from fullText as last resort
179	          // This is useful when formulas use unsupported functions like INDIRECT
180	          console.warn(`   ⚠️  Formula in ${cellMapping.column}${cellMapping.row} could not be calculated. Formula: ${cell.f}`);
181	          console.log(`   🔍 Attempting to extract value from fullText as fallback...`);
182	          
183	          // The pattern in the text is: "VAT % Goods VAT Invoice Total" followed by VAT% (e.g., "20"),
184	          // then registration numbers, then three numbers (can be integers or decimals): "27.6 5.52 33.12" or "0 0 0"
185	          // Where: Goods=first number, VAT=second number, Invoice Total=third number
186	          const fieldNameLower = fieldName.toLowerCase();
187	          
188	          // Look for the sequence of three numbers after "Invoice Total" and registration numbers
189	          // Pattern: After "Invoice Total" comes VAT%, then registration info, then three numbers (integers or decimals)
190	          // More specifically: look for three consecutive numbers near the end before "Note"
191	          // Pattern: ([\d,]+(?:\.\d+)?) matches integers like "0" or decimals like "27.6"
192	          const threeNumbersMatch = extracted.fullText.match(/(?:Invoice\s+Total|NPWD\d+)\s+([\d,]+(?:\.\d+)?)\s+([\d,]+(?:\.\d+)?)\s+([\d,]+(?:\.\d+)?)(?:\s+Note|\s*$)/i);
193	          
194	          if (threeNumbersMatch) {
195	            const goodsValue = threeNumbersMatch[1].replace(/,/g, '');
196	            const vatValue = threeNumbersMatch[2].replace(/,/g, '');
197	            const totalValue = threeNumbersMatch[3].replace(/,/g, '');
198	            
199	            console.log(`   📊 Found three values in sequence: Goods=${goodsValue}, VAT=${vatValue}, Total=${totalValue}`);
200	            
201	            if (fieldNameLower.includes('invoice_total') || fieldNameLower.includes('total')) {
202	              value = totalValue;
203	              console.log(`   ✅ Extracted Invoice Total from fullText: "${value}"`);
204	            } else if (fieldNameLower.includes('vat_amount') || (fieldNameLower.includes('vat') && !fieldNameLower.includes('rate'))) {
205	              value = vatValue;
206	              console.log(`   ✅ Extracted VAT Amount from fullText: "${value}"`);
207	            } else if (fieldNameLower.includes('goods') || fieldNameLower.includes('net')) {
208	              value = goodsValue;
209	              console.log(`   ✅ Extracted Goods Amount from fullText: "${value}"`);
210	            }
211	            } else {
212	              // Fallback: try to find individual patterns, but be more careful to avoid false matches
213	              // Updated to handle integers (like "0") as well as decimals
214	              if (fieldNameLower.includes('invoice_total') || fieldNameLower.includes('total')) {
215	                // Look for "Invoice Total" followed by a number, but avoid matching VAT%
216	                // The actual total should be after registration numbers
217	                const totalMatch = extracted.fullText.match(/Invoice\s+Total[\s\w\/]+\s+[\d]+\s+[\w\/\s]+\s+[\d,]+(?:\.\d+)?\s+[\d,]+(?:\.\d+)?\s+([\d,]+(?:\.\d+)?)/i);
218	                if (totalMatch && totalMatch[1]) {
219	                  value = totalMatch[1].replace(/,/g, '');
220	                  console.log(`   ✅ Extracted Invoice Total from fullText (fallback): "${value}"`);
221	                }
222	              } else if (fieldNameLower.includes('vat_amount') || (fieldNameLower.includes('vat') && !fieldNameLower.includes('rate'))) {
223	                // Look for VAT amount - should be the middle of three numbers after registration
224	                const vatMatch = extracted.fullText.match(/Invoice\s+Total[\s\w\/]+\s+[\d]+\s+[\w\/\s]+\s+[\d,]+(?:\.\d+)?\s+([\d,]+(?:\.\d+)?)\s+[\d,]+(?:\.\d+)?/i);
225	                if (vatMatch && vatMatch[1]) {
226	                  value = vatMatch[1].replace(/,/g, '');
227	                  console.log(`   ✅ Extracted VAT Amount from fullText (fallback): "${value}"`);
228	                }
229	              } else if (fieldNameLower.includes('goods') || fieldNameLower.includes('net')) {
230	                // Look for Goods - should be the first of three numbers after registration
231	                const goodsMatch = extracted.fullText.match(/Invoice\s+Total[\s\w\/]+\s+[\d]+\s+[\w\/\s]+\s+([\d,]+(?:\.\d+)?)\s+[\d,]+(?:\.\d+)?\s+[\d,]+(?:\.\d+)?/i);
232	                if (goodsMatch && goodsMatch[1]) {
233	                  value = goodsMatch[1].replace(/,/g, '');
234	                  console.log(`   ✅ Extracted Goods Amount from fullText (fallback): "${value}"`);
235	                }
236	              }
237	            }
238	          
239	          if (!value || value === '') {
240	            value = '';
241	            console.warn(`   ⚠️  Could not extract value for ${fieldName} from formula or fullText`);
242	          }
243	        }
244	      } else if (cell.t === 'n') {
245	        // Regular number (not a formula)
246	        value = cell.v !== undefined && cell.v !== null ? cell.v.toString() : '';
247	      } else if (cell.t === 'd') {
248	        // Date
249	        const dateValue = XLSX.SSF.parse_date_code(cell.v);
250	        if (dateValue) {
251	          // Format as YYYY-MM-DD
252	          value = `${dateValue.y}-${String(dateValue.m).padStart(2, '0')}-${String(dateValue.d).padStart(2, '0')}`;
253	        } else {
254	          value = cell.v !== undefined && cell.v !== null ? cell.v.toString() : '';
255	        }
256	      } else if (cell.t === 'b') {
257	        // Boolean
258	        value = cell.v ? 'true' : 'false';
259	      } else if (cell.t === 'e') {
260	        // Error
261	        value = cell.w || cell.v || '';
262	        console.warn(`   ⚠️  Cell ${cellMapping.column}${cellMapping.row} contains an error: ${value}`);
263	      } else {
264	        // String or other (not a formula, not a number, not a date, not boolean, not error)
265	        // Regular string cell
266	        value = cell.w || (cell.v !== undefined && cell.v !== null ? cell.v.toString() : '');
267	      }
268	
269	      console.log(`   Extracted ${fieldName} from ${cellMapping.column}${cellMapping.row}: "${value}"`);
270	    }
271	
272	    // Apply transformations if defined
273	    if (template.transformations && template.transformations[fieldName]) {
274	      const originalValue = value;
275	      value = applyTransformations(value, template.transformations[fieldName]);
276	      console.log(`   After transformations: "${value}" (from "${originalValue}")`);
277	    }
278	
279	    // Store with the original prefixed field name for consistency
280	    // All fields (default and custom) use their template-prefixed names
281	    extracted[fieldName] = value;
282	    console.log(`   ✅ Stored as ${fieldName}: "${value}"`);
283	  }
284	
285	  // Extract custom fields if template has customFields defined
286	  if (template.customFields && typeof template.customFields === 'object' && Object.keys(template.customFields).length > 0) {
287	    console.log(`📋 Extracting ${Object.keys(template.customFields).length} custom field(s) from Excel...`);
288	    
289	    const templateCode = template.code || '';
290	    
291	    for (const [customFieldName, customFieldConfig] of Object.entries(template.customFields)) {
292	      // Check if this custom field has excelCells mapping
293	      const prefixedFieldName = templateCode ? `${templateCode}_${customFieldName}` : customFieldName;
294	      const cellMapping = template.excelCells?.[prefixedFieldName] || template.excelCells?.[customFieldName];
295	      
296	      if (cellMapping && cellMapping.column && cellMapping.row) {
297	        const sheetIndex = sheetNames.length - 1; // Use last sheet
298	        const sheetName = sheetNames[sheetIndex];
299	        const worksheet = workbook.Sheets[sheetName];
300	        
301	        const colNum = columnToNumber(cellMapping.column.toUpperCase());
302	        const rowNum = parseInt(cellMapping.row);
303	        
304	        const isRange = cellMapping.endColumn && cellMapping.endRow;
305	        let value = '';
306	        
307	        if (isRange) {
308	          const endColNum = columnToNumber(cellMapping.endColumn.toUpperCase());
309	          const endRowNum = parseInt(cellMapping.endRow);
310	          
311	          const values = [];
312	          for (let R = rowNum - 1; R < endRowNum; R++) {
313	            const rowValues = [];
314	            for (let C = colNum - 1; C < endColNum; C++) {
315	              const cellAddress = XLSX.utils.encode_cell({ r: R, c: C });
316	              const cell = worksheet[cellAddress];
317	              if (cell) {
318	                rowValues.push(cell.w || cell.v || '');
319	              }
320	            }
321	            if (rowValues.length > 0) {
322	              values.push(rowValues.join('\t'));
323	            }
324	          }
325	          value = values.join('\n');
326	        } else {
327	          const cellAddress = XLSX.utils.encode_cell({ r: rowNum - 1, c: colNum - 1 });
328	          const cell = worksheet[cellAddress];
329	          
330	          if (cell) {
331	            if (cell.f) {
332	              // Formula - use calculated value
333	              value = cell.v !== undefined && cell.v !== null ? cell.v.toString() : (cell.w || '');
334	            } else if (cell.t === 'n') {
335	              value = cell.v !== undefined && cell.v !== null ? cell.v.toString() : '';
336	            } else if (cell.t === 'd') {
337	              const dateValue = XLSX.SSF.parse_date_code(cell.v);
338	              if (dateValue) {
339	                value = `${dateValue.y}-${String(dateValue.m).padStart(2, '0')}-${String(dateValue.d).padStart(2, '0')}`;
340	              } else {
341	                value = cell.v !== undefined && cell.v !== null ? cell.v.toString() : '';
342	              }
343	            } else {
344	              value = cell.w || (cell.v !== undefined && cell.v !== null ? cell.v.toString() : '');
345	            }
346	          }
347	        }
348	        
349	        // Format value based on dataType
350	        if (customFieldConfig.dataType === 'currency' || customFieldConfig.dataType === 'number') {
351	          // Clean amount value (remove currency symbols, commas, etc.)
352	          value = value.replace(/[£$€¥₹,\s]/g, '').replace(/[^\d.-]/g, '');
353	        }
354	        
355	        // Store custom field with its name (not prefixed)
356	        extracted[customFieldName] = value;
357	        console.log(`   ✅ Stored custom field "${customFieldName}" = "${value}"`);
358	      }
359	    }
360	  }
361	
362	  // Don't add documentType if we already have a prefixed version
363	  // Only determine document type if not explicitly set via template mapping
364	  const hasDocumentTypeField = Object.keys(extracted).some(key => 
365	    key.includes('document_type') || key.includes('documentType')
366	  );
367	  
368	  if (!hasDocumentTypeField) {
369	    // Only set documentType if no template field mapped it
370	    const docTypeUpper = extracted.fullText.toUpperCase();
371	    if (docTypeUpper.includes('CREDIT') || docTypeUpper.includes('CN')) {
372	      extracted.documentType = 'credit_note';
373	    } else if (docTypeUpper.includes('STATEMENT')) {
374	      extracted.documentType = 'statement';
375	    } else {
376	      extracted.documentType = 'invoice';
377	    }
378	  }
379	
380	  return extracted;
381	}
382	
383	/**
384	 * Map field name from template to extracted object
385	 * Handles both prefixed (templateCode_fieldName) and unprefixed field names
386	 */
387	function mapFieldName(fieldName) {
388	  // Known field names that might be prefixed with template code
389	  // Order matters: longer/more specific names first
390	  const knownFields = [
391	    'invoice_total',
392	    'invoice_number',
393	    'account_number',
394	    'account_no',
395	    'document_type',
396	    'vat_amount',
397	    'customer_name',
398	    'customer_po',
399	    'goods_amount',
400	    'date',
401	    'amount',
402	    'total',
403	    'customerPO',
404	    'goodsAmount',
405	    'vatAmount'
406	  ];
407	  
408	  // Extract actual field name by removing template code prefix
409	  // Format: {templateCode}_{fieldName} or just {fieldName}
410	  let actualFieldName = fieldName;
411	  
412	  // First, try to extract the base field name by removing any template code prefix
413	  // Template code prefix pattern: {templateCode}_{fieldName}
414	  // We'll look for known fields after the last underscore
415	  const lastUnderscoreIndex = fieldName.lastIndexOf('_');
416	  if (lastUnderscoreIndex > 0) {
417	    const possibleBaseName = fieldName.substring(lastUnderscoreIndex + 1);
418	    // Check if this matches a known field
419	    if (knownFields.includes(possibleBaseName)) {
420	      actualFieldName = possibleBaseName;
421	    } else {
422	      // Try matching the full suffix after first underscore (for multi-part field names)
423	      // e.g., "rtewast_invoice_total" -> "invoice_total"
424	      for (const knownField of knownFields) {
425	        if (fieldName.endsWith('_' + knownField)) {
426	          actualFieldName = knownField;
427	          break;
428	        }
429	      }
430	    }
431	  }
432	  
433	  // If no prefix found, check if it's already a known field
434	  if (actualFieldName === fieldName && !knownFields.includes(fieldName)) {
435	    // Field doesn't match known patterns - might be a custom field
436	    // Return as-is (will keep the prefix if present)
437	    return fieldName;
438	  }
439	  
440	  const mapping = {
441	    'document_type': 'documentType',
442	    'invoice_number': 'invoiceNumber',
443	    'account_number': 'accountNumber',
444	    'account_no': 'accountNumber', // Also handle account_no
445	    'customer_name': 'customerName',
446	    'date': 'date',
447	    'amount': 'amount',
448	    'total': 'amount',
449	    'invoice_total': 'amount', // Map invoice_total to amount
450	    'customer_po': 'customerPO',
451	    'customerPO': 'customerPO',
452	    'goods_amount': 'goodsAmount',
453	    'goodsAmount': 'goodsAmount',
454	    'vat_amount': 'vatAmount',
455	    'vatAmount': 'vatAmount'
456	  };
457	  
458	  return mapping[actualFieldName] || actualFieldName;
459	}
460	
461	/**
462	 * Apply transformations to extracted value
463	 */
464	function applyTransformations(value, transformations) {
465	  let result = value;
466	
467	  if (transformations.remove) {
468	    for (const char of transformations.remove) {
469	      result = result.replace(new RegExp(char.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, '\\$&'), 'g'), '');
470	    }
471	  }
472	
473	  if (transformations.trim) {
474	    result = result.trim();
475	  }
476	
477	  if (transformations.uppercase) {
478	    result = result.toUpperCase();
479	  }
480	
481	  if (transformations.lowercase) {
482	    result = result.toLowerCase();
483	  }
484	
485	  if (transformations.parseFloat) {
486	    result = parseFloat(result.replace(/[^\d.-]/g, ''));
487	  }
488	
489	  if (transformations.parseInt) {
490	    result = parseInt(result.replace(/[^\d-]/g, ''), 10);
491	  }
492	
493	  return result;
494	}
495	
496	/**
497	 * Get Excel file preview data (for template builder)
498	 * @param {Buffer|string} excelFile - Excel file buffer or file path
499	 * @returns {Promise<Object>} - Sheet data and metadata
500	 */
501	async function getExcelPreview(excelFile) {
502	  let workbook;
503	  
504	  if (Buffer.isBuffer(excelFile)) {
505	    workbook = XLSX.read(excelFile, { type: 'buffer' });
506	  } else if (typeof excelFile === 'string') {
507	    workbook = XLSX.readFile(excelFile);
508	  } else {
509	    throw new Error('Invalid Excel file format. Expected Buffer or file path.');
510	  }
511	
512	  const sheets = {};
513	  workbook.SheetNames.forEach(sheetName => {
514	    const worksheet = workbook.Sheets[sheetName];
515	    // Get raw data with cell addresses
516	    const range = XLSX.utils.decode_range(worksheet['!ref'] || 'A1');
517	    const sheetData = [];
518	    
519	    for (let R = range.s.r; R <= range.e.r; R++) {
520	      const row = [];
521	      for (let C = range.s.c; C <= range.e.c; C++) {
522	        const cellAddress = XLSX.utils.encode_cell({ r: R, c: C });
523	        const cell = worksheet[cellAddress];
524	        row.push(cell ? (cell.w || cell.v) : '');
525	      }
526	      sheetData.push(row);
527	    }
528	    
529	    sheets[sheetName] = sheetData;
530	  });
531	
532	  return {
533	    sheets,
534	    sheetNames: workbook.SheetNames
535	  };
536	}
537	
538	module.exports = {
539	  extractFieldsFromExcel,
540	  getExcelPreview,
541	  columnToNumber,
542	  numberToColumn
543	};
544	
545
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./backend/routes/reports.js`

```text
1	const express = require('express');
2	const { Report, Sequelize } = require('../models');
3	const { Op } = Sequelize;
4	const auth = require('../middleware/auth');
5	const { requirePermission } = require('../middleware/permissions');
6	const router = express.Router();
7	
8	// All routes require GA only
9	router.use(auth);
10	router.use(requirePermission('REPORTS_VIEW'));
11	
12	// Get all reports
13	router.get('/', async (req, res) => {
14	  try {
15	    const { User } = require('../models');
16	    const reports = await Report.findAll({
17	      include: [{
18	        model: User,
19	        as: 'createdBy',
20	        attributes: ['id', 'name', 'email']
21	      }],
22	      order: [['createdAt', 'DESC']]
23	    });
24	    res.json(reports);
25	  } catch (error) {
26	    res.status(500).json({ message: error.message });
27	  }
28	});
29	
30	// Get single report
31	router.get('/:id', async (req, res) => {
32	  try {
33	    const { User } = require('../models');
34	    const report = await Report.findByPk(req.params.id, {
35	      include: [{
36	        model: User,
37	        as: 'createdBy',
38	        attributes: ['id', 'name', 'email']
39	      }]
40	    });
41	    
42	    if (!report) {
43	      return res.status(404).json({ message: 'Report not found' });
44	    }
45	    
46	    res.json(report);
47	  } catch (error) {
48	    res.status(500).json({ message: error.message });
49	  }
50	});
51	
52	// Create report
53	router.post('/', async (req, res) => {
54	  try {
55	    const report = await Report.create({
56	      ...req.body,
57	      createdById: req.user.userId
58	    });
59	    res.status(201).json(report);
60	  } catch (error) {
61	    res.status(400).json({ message: error.message });
62	  }
63	});
64	
65	// Update report
66	router.put('/:id', async (req, res) => {
67	  try {
68	    const report = await Report.findByPk(req.params.id);
69	    
70	    if (!report) {
71	      return res.status(404).json({ message: 'Report not found' });
72	    }
73	
74	    // Check if user is the creator or admin
75	    if (report.createdById !== req.user.userId && req.user.role !== 'global_admin' && req.user.role !== 'administrator') {
76	      return res.status(403).json({ message: 'Not authorized' });
77	    }
78	
79	    await report.update(req.body);
80	    res.json(report);
81	  } catch (error) {
82	    res.status(400).json({ message: error.message });
83	  }
84	});
85	
86	// Delete report
87	router.delete('/:id', async (req, res) => {
88	  try {
89	    const report = await Report.findByPk(req.params.id);
90	    
91	    if (!report) {
92	      return res.status(404).json({ message: 'Report not found' });
93	    }
94	
95	    // Check if user is the creator or admin
96	    if (report.createdById !== req.user.userId && req.user.role !== 'global_admin' && req.user.role !== 'administrator') {
97	      return res.status(403).json({ message: 'Not authorized' });
98	    }
99	
100	    await report.destroy();
101	    res.json({ message: 'Report deleted successfully' });
102	  } catch (error) {
103	    res.status(500).json({ message: error.message });
104	  }
105	});
106	
107	module.exports = router;
108	
109
```

 `./backend/utils/tablerEmailRenderer.js`

```text
1	/**
2	 * Tabler Email Template Renderer
3	 * 
4	 * Renders Tabler email templates with dynamic variable substitution
5	 * Uses the portal's branding settings (primaryColor, logo, companyName)
6	 */
7	
8	const fs = require('fs');
9	const path = require('path');
10	
11	// Template cache to avoid re-reading files
12	const templateCache = {};
13	
14	/**
15	 * Get the templates directory path
16	 */
17	function getTemplatesDir() {
18	  return path.join(__dirname, '../templates/emails');
19	}
20	
21	/**
22	 * Load a template file (with caching)
23	 */
24	function loadTemplate(templateName) {
25	  const cacheKey = templateName;
26	  
27	  if (templateCache[cacheKey] && process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production') {
28	    return templateCache[cacheKey];
29	  }
30	  
31	  const templatePath = path.join(getTemplatesDir(), `${templateName}.html`);
32	  
33	  if (!fs.existsSync(templatePath)) {
34	    throw new Error(`Email template not found: ${templateName}`);
35	  }
36	  
37	  const template = fs.readFileSync(templatePath, 'utf8');
38	  templateCache[cacheKey] = template;
39	  
40	  return template;
41	}
42	
43	/**
44	 * Replace all occurrences of {{variable}} with values
45	 */
46	function replaceVariables(template, variables) {
47	  let result = template;
48	
49	  for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(variables)) {
50	    const regex = new RegExp(`\\{\\{${key}\\}\\}`, 'g');
51	    result = result.replace(regex, escapeHtml(String(value ?? '')));
52	  }
53	
54	  return result;
55	}
56	
57	/**
58	 * Generate a darker shade for button hover
59	 */
60	function darkenColor(hex, percent = 10) {
61	  // Remove # if present
62	  hex = hex.replace('#', '');
63	  
64	  // Parse RGB
65	  let r = parseInt(hex.substring(0, 2), 16);
66	  let g = parseInt(hex.substring(2, 4), 16);
67	  let b = parseInt(hex.substring(4, 6), 16);
68	  
69	  // Darken
70	  r = Math.max(0, Math.floor(r * (100 - percent) / 100));
71	  g = Math.max(0, Math.floor(g * (100 - percent) / 100));
72	  b = Math.max(0, Math.floor(b * (100 - percent) / 100));
73	  
74	  // Convert back to hex
75	  return `#${r.toString(16).padStart(2, '0')}${g.toString(16).padStart(2, '0')}${b.toString(16).padStart(2, '0')}`;
76	}
77	
78	const { getFrontendUrl, getBackendUrl } = require('./urlConfig');
79	
80	/**
81	 * Get branding variables from settings
82	 */
83	function getBrandingVariables(settings) {
84	  const primaryColor = settings?.primaryColor || '#066FD1';
85	  const primaryColorHover = darkenColor(primaryColor);
86	  const companyName = settings?.companyName || 'Invoice Portal';
87	  const supportEmail = settings?.supportEmail || 'support@example.com';
88	  const portalUrl = getFrontendUrl();
89	  
90	  // Build logo URL
91	  // Logos are stored as paths like "/uploads/logo-xxx.png" and served directly at /uploads/
92	  let logoUrl = '';
93	  if (settings?.logoLight) {
94	    // Check if it's already a full URL
95	    if (settings.logoLight.startsWith('http')) {
96	      logoUrl = settings.logoLight;
97	    } else {
98	      // Construct URL from backend
99	      // Logo path already includes /uploads/, so just append to backend URL
100	      const backendUrl = getBackendUrl();
101	      logoUrl = `${backendUrl}${settings.logoLight.startsWith('/') ? '' : '/'}${settings.logoLight}`;
102	    }
103	  }
104	  
105	  return {
106	    primaryColor,
107	    primaryColorHover,
108	    companyName,
109	    supportEmail,
110	    portalUrl,
111	    logoUrl,
112	    currentYear: new Date().getFullYear().toString()
113	  };
114	}
115	
116	/**
117	 * Render an email template with variables
118	 * 
119	 * @param {string} templateName - Name of the template (without .html)
120	 * @param {object} variables - Variables to substitute
121	 * @param {object} settings - Portal settings (for branding)
122	 * @returns {string} Rendered HTML
123	 */
124	function renderTemplate(templateName, variables = {}, settings = {}) {
125	  const template = loadTemplate(templateName);
126	  
127	  // Get branding variables
128	  const brandingVars = getBrandingVariables(settings);
129	  
130	  // Merge all variables (user variables override branding)
131	  const allVariables = {
132	    ...brandingVars,
133	    ...variables
134	  };
135	  
136	  // Replace variables
137	  let html = replaceVariables(template, allVariables);
138	  
139	  // Handle conditional sections: {{#if variable}}content{{/if}}
140	  html = processConditionals(html, allVariables);
141	  
142	  return html;
143	}
144	
145	/**
146	 * Process conditional sections in template
147	 * Supports: {{#if variable}}content{{/if}}
148	 */
149	function processConditionals(template, variables) {
150	  let result = template;
151	  
152	  // Match {{#if variable}}...{{/if}} - non-greedy
153	  const ifRegex = /\{\{#if\s+(\w+)\}\}([\s\S]*?)\{\{\/if\}\}/g;
154	  
155	  result = result.replace(ifRegex, (match, variable, content) => {
156	    const value = variables[variable];
157	    // Show content if value is truthy
158	    if (value && value !== 'false' && value !== '0') {
159	      return content;
160	    }
161	    return '';
162	  });
163	  
164	  // Handle {{#unless variable}}...{{/unless}}
165	  const unlessRegex = /\{\{#unless\s+(\w+)\}\}([\s\S]*?)\{\{\/unless\}\}/g;
166	  
167	  result = result.replace(unlessRegex, (match, variable, content) => {
168	    const value = variables[variable];
169	    // Show content if value is falsy
170	    if (!value || value === 'false' || value === '0') {
171	      return content;
172	    }
173	    return '';
174	  });
175	  
176	  return result;
177	}
178	
179	/**
180	 * Format a date for display in emails
181	 */
182	function formatDate(date) {
183	  if (!date) return '';
184	  const d = new Date(date);
185	  return d.toLocaleDateString('en-GB', {
186	    day: '2-digit',
187	    month: 'short',
188	    year: 'numeric'
189	  });
190	}
191	
192	/**
193	 * Format currency for display
194	 */
195	function formatCurrency(amount, currency = 'GBP') {
196	  if (amount === null || amount === undefined) return '';
197	  const num = parseFloat(amount);
198	  if (isNaN(num)) return '';
199	  
200	  return new Intl.NumberFormat('en-GB', {
201	    style: 'currency',
202	    currency: currency
203	  }).format(num);
204	}
205	
206	/**
207	 * Escape HTML entities
208	 */
209	function escapeHtml(text) {
210	  if (!text) return '';
211	  const map = {
212	    '&': '&amp;',
213	    '<': '&lt;',
214	    '>': '&gt;',
215	    '"': '&quot;',
216	    "'": '&#039;'
217	  };
218	  return String(text).replace(/[&<>"']/g, m => map[m]);
219	}
220	
221	/**
222	 * Generate a VML-compatible button (works in Outlook)
223	 */
224	function generateButton(text, url, settings, options = {}) {
225	  const primaryColor = settings?.primaryColor || '#066FD1';
226	  const width = options.width || 200;
227	  const fullWidth = options.fullWidth || false;
228	  
229	  const widthStyle = fullWidth ? 'width: 100%;' : `width: ${width}px;`;
230	  
231	  return `
232	    <table role="presentation" cellspacing="0" cellpadding="0" border="0" style="margin: 0 auto;">
233	      <tr>
234	        <td style="background-color: ${primaryColor};" align="center">
235	          <!--[if mso]>
236	          <v:rect xmlns:v="urn:schemas-microsoft-com:vml" xmlns:w="urn:schemas-microsoft-com:office:word"
237	            href="${url}" style="height:44px;v-text-anchor:middle;${widthStyle}"
238	            strokecolor="${primaryColor}" fillcolor="${primaryColor}">
239	            <w:anchorlock/>
240	            <center style="color:#ffffff;font-family:Arial,sans-serif;font-size:14px;font-weight:500;">
241	              ${escapeHtml(text)}
242	            </center>
243	          </v:rect>
244	          <![endif]-->
245	          <!--[if !mso]><!-->
246	          <a href="${url}" style="background-color: ${primaryColor}; color: #ffffff;
247	            display: inline-block; font-family: Inter, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500;
248	            line-height: 44px; text-align: center; text-decoration: none; ${widthStyle}
249	            -webkit-text-size-adjust: none; mso-hide: all;">
250	            ${escapeHtml(text)}
251	          </a>
252	          <!--<![endif]-->
253	        </td>
254	      </tr>
255	    </table>
256	  `;
257	}
258	
259	module.exports = {
260	  renderTemplate,
261	  loadTemplate,
262	  getBrandingVariables,
263	  formatDate,
264	  formatCurrency,
265	  escapeHtml,
266	  generateButton,
267	  darkenColor
268	};
269	
270
```
</details></tool-use>
