<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./backend/utils/sessionToken.js`

```text
1	const crypto = require('crypto');
2	const jwt = require('jsonwebtoken');
3	const { redis } = require('../config/redis');
4	
5	// Use Redis for session tokens (supports distributed systems)
6	// Falls back to in-memory Map if Redis is unavailable
7	const useRedis = process.env.REDIS_HOST || process.env.REDIS_URL;
8	const sessionTokens = new Map(); // Fallback for when Redis is unavailable
9	
10	/**
11	 * Generate a temporary session token for 2FA setup
12	 * This allows us to avoid passing passwords around
13	 */
14	const generateSessionToken = async (userId, email) => {
15	  const token = crypto.randomBytes(32).toString('hex');
16	  const expiresAt = Date.now() + 10 * 60 * 1000; // 10 minutes
17	  const sessionData = {
18	    userId,
19	    email,
20	    expiresAt,
21	    createdAt: Date.now()
22	  };
23	  
24	  if (useRedis) {
25	    try {
26	      // Store in Redis with expiration
27	      const ttl = Math.ceil((expiresAt - Date.now()) / 1000); // Convert to seconds
28	      await redis.setex(`session:${token}`, ttl, JSON.stringify(sessionData));
29	      return token;
30	    } catch (error) {
31	      console.error('Redis error in generateSessionToken, falling back to memory:', error.message);
32	      // Fall through to in-memory storage
33	    }
34	  }
35	  
36	  // Fallback to in-memory storage
37	  sessionTokens.set(token, sessionData);
38	  cleanupExpiredTokens();
39	  
40	  return token;
41	};
42	
43	/**
44	 * Verify a session token (without consuming it)
45	 * Returns user data if valid, null if invalid/expired
46	 */
47	const verifySessionToken = async (token, consume = false) => {
48	  let session = null;
49	  
50	  if (useRedis) {
51	    try {
52	      const sessionData = await redis.get(`session:${token}`);
53	      if (sessionData) {
54	        session = JSON.parse(sessionData);
55	        
56	        // Check expiration
57	        if (Date.now() > session.expiresAt) {
58	          await redis.del(`session:${token}`);
59	          return null;
60	        }
61	        
62	        // Consume token if requested
63	        if (consume) {
64	          await redis.del(`session:${token}`);
65	        }
66	        
67	        return {
68	          userId: session.userId,
69	          email: session.email
70	        };
71	      }
72	      return null;
73	    } catch (error) {
74	      console.error('Redis error in verifySessionToken, falling back to memory:', error.message);
75	      // Fall through to in-memory check
76	    }
77	  }
78	  
79	  // Fallback to in-memory storage
80	  session = sessionTokens.get(token);
81	  
82	  if (!session) {
83	    return null;
84	  }
85	  
86	  if (Date.now() > session.expiresAt) {
87	    sessionTokens.delete(token);
88	    return null;
89	  }
90	  
91	  // Only consume token if requested (for one-time use scenarios)
92	  if (consume) {
93	    sessionTokens.delete(token);
94	  }
95	  
96	  return {
97	    userId: session.userId,
98	    email: session.email
99	  };
100	};
101	
102	/**
103	 * Clean up expired tokens
104	 */
105	const cleanupExpiredTokens = () => {
106	  const now = Date.now();
107	  for (const [token, session] of sessionTokens.entries()) {
108	    if (now > session.expiresAt) {
109	      sessionTokens.delete(token);
110	    }
111	  }
112	};
113	
114	// Clean up expired tokens every 5 minutes
115	setInterval(cleanupExpiredTokens, 5 * 60 * 1000);
116	
117	module.exports = {
118	  generateSessionToken,
119	  verifySessionToken
120	};
121	
122
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./backend/jobs/localFolderScanner.js`

```text
1	/**
2	 * Local Folder Scanner Job
3	 * Scans the FTP upload folder for new PDF files and queues them for import.
4	 * 
5	 * Processing Flow:
6	 * 1. Scan FTP_UPLOAD_PATH for new files
7	 * 2. Calculate hash for each file
8	 * 3. Check if hash exists in database (duplicate detection)
9	 * 4. If duplicate: move to unprocessed/duplicates/
10	 * 5. If new: queue for processing, file stays in upload folder until processed
11	 * 6. Invoice import job moves file to processed/ or unprocessed/failed/ after processing
12	 */
13	
14	const fs = require('fs');
15	const path = require('path');
16	const { File, Settings, User } = require('../models');
17	const { invoiceImportQueue, emailQueue } = require('../config/queue');
18	const { Op } = require('sequelize');
19	const crypto = require('crypto');
20	const importLogger = require('../services/importLogger');
21	const { isEmailEnabled } = require('../utils/emailService');
22	
23	// Import storage configuration
24	const {
25	  FTP_UPLOAD_PATH,
26	  UNPROCESSED_DUPLICATES,
27	  UNPROCESSED_FAILED,
28	  ensureDir,
29	  getUnprocessedFilePath
30	} = require('../config/storage');
31	
32	// Supported file extensions
33	const SUPPORTED_EXTENSIONS = ['.pdf', '.xlsx', '.xls'];
34	
35	/**
36	 * Calculate file hash (SHA-256) for duplicate detection
37	 * @param {string} filePath - Path to the file
38	 * @returns {Promise<string>} SHA-256 hash of the file
39	 */
40	function calculateFileHash(filePath) {
41	  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
42	    const hash = crypto.createHash('sha256');
43	    const stream = fs.createReadStream(filePath);
44	    
45	    stream.on('data', (data) => hash.update(data));
46	    stream.on('end', () => resolve(hash.digest('hex')));
47	    stream.on('error', reject);
48	  });
49	}
50	
51	/**
52	 * Move file to duplicates folder
53	 * @param {string} filePath - Current file path
54	 * @param {string} fileName - File name
55	 * @returns {string} New file path
56	 */
57	function moveToDuplicates(filePath, fileName) {
58	  const destPath = getUnprocessedFilePath('duplicate', fileName);
59	  
60	  // Handle duplicate filenames by adding timestamp
61	  let finalPath = destPath;
62	  if (fs.existsSync(destPath)) {
63	    const ext = path.extname(fileName);
64	    const base = path.basename(fileName, ext);
65	    const dir = path.dirname(destPath);
66	    finalPath = path.join(dir, `${base}-${Date.now()}${ext}`);
67	  }
68	  
69	  fs.renameSync(filePath, finalPath);
70	  console.log(`📋 Moved duplicate to: ${finalPath}`);
71	  return finalPath;
72	}
73	
74	/**
75	 * Move file to failed folder with error log
76	 * @param {string} filePath - Current file path
77	 * @param {string} fileName - File name
78	 * @param {string} error - Error message
79	 * @returns {string} New file path
80	 */
81	function moveToFailed(filePath, fileName, error) {
82	  const destPath = getUnprocessedFilePath('failed', fileName);
83	  
84	  // Handle duplicate filenames
85	  let finalPath = destPath;
86	  if (fs.existsSync(destPath)) {
87	    const ext = path.extname(fileName);
88	    const base = path.basename(fileName, ext);
89	    const dir = path.dirname(destPath);
90	    finalPath = path.join(dir, `${base}-${Date.now()}${ext}`);
91	  }
92	  
93	  fs.renameSync(filePath, finalPath);
94	  
95	  // Write error log alongside the file
96	  const errorLogPath = finalPath + '.error.txt';
97	  fs.writeFileSync(errorLogPath, `Failed at: ${new Date().toISOString()}\nError: ${error}\n`);
98	  
99	  console.log(`❌ Moved to failed: ${finalPath}`);
100	  return finalPath;
101	}
102	
103	/**
104	 * Check if file hash exists in database (duplicate detection)
105	 * @param {string} fileHash - SHA-256 hash of the file
106	 * @returns {Promise<boolean>} True if duplicate exists
107	 */
108	async function isDuplicateHash(fileHash) {
109	  const existingFile = await File.findOne({
110	    where: {
111	      fileHash,
112	      deletedAt: null
113	    }
114	  });
115	  return !!existingFile;
116	}
117	
118	/**
119	 * Scan the FTP upload folder and queue files for import
120	 * @returns {Promise<Object>} Scan results
121	 */
122	async function scanLocalFolder() {
123	  const results = {
124	    scanned: 0,
125	    queued: 0,
126	    skipped: 0,
127	    duplicates: 0,
128	    errors: [],
129	    files: []
130	  };
131	  
132	  // Start logging the run
133	  const runContext = await importLogger.startRun();
134	  
135	  try {
136	    // Resolve to absolute path so we always scan the same path regardless of process cwd
137	    const uploadDir = path.resolve(FTP_UPLOAD_PATH);
138	    await importLogger.log.info(`Scanning FTP upload folder: ${uploadDir}`);
139	    
140	    // Check if upload directory exists
141	    if (!fs.existsSync(uploadDir)) {
142	      console.log(`⚠️  FTP upload folder does not exist: ${uploadDir}`);
143	      console.log('   Creating folder...');
144	      ensureDir(uploadDir);
145	      await importLogger.log.warn(`Created missing FTP upload folder: ${uploadDir}`);
146	      return results;
147	    }
148	    
149	    // Ensure unprocessed directories exist
150	    ensureDir(UNPROCESSED_DUPLICATES);
151	    ensureDir(UNPROCESSED_FAILED);
152	    
153	    console.log(`🔍 DEBUG: Reading directory: ${uploadDir}`);
154	    console.log(`🔍 DEBUG: Directory exists: ${fs.existsSync(uploadDir)}`);
155	    
156	    let files = [];
157	    try {
158	      files = fs.readdirSync(uploadDir);
159	    } catch (readErr) {
160	      console.error(`❌ Cannot read FTP upload directory: ${readErr.code || readErr.message}`);
161	      console.error(`   Path: ${uploadDir}`);
162	      if (readErr.code === 'EACCES') {
163	        console.error('   💡 Process user may not have read permission. Run PM2 as the same user that can ls this folder (e.g. rob).');
164	      }
165	      await importLogger.log.warn(`Failed to read directory: ${readErr.message}`);
166	      await importLogger.endRun(runContext, results);
167	      return results;
168	    }
169	    console.log(`🔍 DEBUG: Total files/dirs in folder: ${files.length}`);
170	    if (files.length > 0) {
171	      console.log(`🔍 DEBUG: First 5 files: ${files.slice(0, 5).join(', ')}`);
172	    }
173	
174	    // Filter for supported file types
175	    const supportedFiles = files.filter(file => {
176	      const ext = path.extname(file).toLowerCase();
177	      const isSupported = SUPPORTED_EXTENSIONS.includes(ext);
178	      if (!isSupported && files.length <= 10) {
179	        console.log(`🔍 DEBUG: File "${file}" has extension "${ext}" - not supported`);
180	      }
181	      return isSupported;
182	    });
183	    
184	    console.log(`📁 Found ${supportedFiles.length} supported file(s) in FTP upload folder`);
185	    results.scanned = supportedFiles.length;
186	    
187	    if (supportedFiles.length === 0) {
188	      console.log('ℹ️  No files to process');
189	      await importLogger.log.info('No files to process');
190	      await importLogger.endRun(runContext, results);
191	      return results;
192	    }
193	    
194	    // Get system user for import attribution
195	    const systemUser = await User.findOne({
196	      where: { role: 'global_admin' },
197	      order: [['createdAt', 'ASC']]
198	    });
199	    
200	    // Generate batch import ID for all files in this scan
201	    const batchImportId = `ftp-batch-${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).substr(2, 9)}`;
202	    
203	    // Get existing queued jobs to check for already-queued files
204	    const existingJobs = await invoiceImportQueue.getJobs(['waiting', 'wait', 'active', 'delayed']);
205	    const queuedFileNames = new Set(existingJobs.map(job => job.data?.fileName).filter(Boolean));
206	    const queuedHashes = new Set(existingJobs.map(job => job.data?.fileHash).filter(Boolean));
207	    
208	    // FIRST PASS: Determine which files will be queued (without actually queuing)
209	    // This fixes the race condition where jobs complete before batch is registered
210	    const filesToQueue = [];
211	    
212	    for (const fileName of supportedFiles) {
213	      const filePath = path.join(uploadDir, fileName);
214	      
215	      try {
216	        const stats = fs.statSync(filePath);
217	        
218	        // Skip if file is too new (still being uploaded) - wait 30 seconds
219	        const fileAge = Date.now() - stats.mtimeMs;
220	        if (fileAge < 30000) {
221	          console.log(`[Scan] File too new, waiting: ${fileName} (${Math.round(fileAge/1000)}s old)`);
222	          results.skipped++;
223	          continue;
224	        }
225	        
226	        // Skip if already in queue by filename
227	        if (queuedFileNames.has(fileName)) {
228	          console.log(`[Scan] File already queued (by name): ${fileName}`);
229	          results.skipped++;
230	          continue;
231	        }
232	        
233	        // Calculate hash for duplicate detection
234	        const fileHash = await calculateFileHash(filePath);
235	        
236	        // Skip if hash already in queue
237	        if (queuedHashes.has(fileHash)) {
238	          console.log(`[Scan] File already queued (by hash): ${fileName}`);
239	          results.skipped++;
240	          continue;
241	        }
242	        
243	        // Check for duplicate in database (hash-based)
244	        const isDuplicate = await isDuplicateHash(fileHash);
245	        
246	        if (isDuplicate) {
247	          console.log(`[Scan] Duplicate detected (hash match): ${fileName}`);
248	          await importLogger.log.info(`Duplicate file detected: ${fileName}`);
249	          
250	          // Move to duplicates folder immediately
251	          moveToDuplicates(filePath, fileName);
252	          
253	          results.duplicates++;
254	          continue;
255	        }
256	        
257	        // Check for recently processed file with same name (within last hour)
258	        const recentFile = await File.findOne({
259	          where: {
260	            fileName,
261	            uploadedAt: {
262	              [Op.gte]: new Date(Date.now() - 60 * 60 * 1000)
263	            }
264	          }
265	        });
266	        
267	        if (recentFile) {
268	          console.log(`[Scan] File recently processed: ${fileName}`);
269	          results.skipped++;
270	          continue;
271	        }
272	        
273	        // This file will be queued - add to list
274	        filesToQueue.push({
275	          filePath,
276	          fileName,
277	          fileHash,
278	          fileSize: stats.size
279	        });
280	        
281	      } catch (fileError) {
282	        console.error(`[Scan] Error processing file ${fileName}:`, fileError.message);
283	        await importLogger.log.error(`Error: ${fileName} - ${fileError.message}`);
284	        
285	        results.errors.push({
286	          fileName,
287	          error: fileError.message
288	        });
289	        
290	        // Move failed file to unprocessed/failed
291	        try {
292	          moveToFailed(filePath, fileName, fileError.message);
293	        } catch (moveError) {
294	          console.error(`   Could not move failed file: ${moveError.message}`);
295	        }
296	      }
297	    }
298	    
299	    // REGISTER BATCH BEFORE QUEUING JOBS (critical for notification tracking)
300	    // This ensures the batch exists in Redis before any job can complete
301	    if (filesToQueue.length > 0) {
302	      try {
303	        const { registerBatch } = require('../services/batchNotificationService');
304	        await registerBatch(batchImportId, filesToQueue.length, {
305	          userId: systemUser?.id || null,
306	          userEmail: systemUser?.email || 'system',
307	          source: 'ftp-scan'
308	        });
309	        console.log(`[Batch ${batchImportId}] Registered batch with ${filesToQueue.length} jobs for notification tracking`);
310	      } catch (batchError) {
311	        console.warn('Failed to register batch:', batchError.message);
312	      }
313	    }
314	    
315	    // SECOND PASS: Actually queue the files
316	    for (const file of filesToQueue) {
317	      try {
318	        await invoiceImportQueue.add('invoice-import', {
319	          filePath: file.filePath,
320	          fileName: file.fileName,
321	          originalName: file.fileName,
322	          importId: batchImportId, // Use batch ID for notification tracking
323	          userId: systemUser?.id || null,
324	          source: 'ftp-upload',
325	          fileHash: file.fileHash,
326	          documentType: 'auto', // Auto-detect from template matching
327	          priority: 0 // Normal priority (manual uploads get priority 1)
328	        }, {
329	          jobId: `ftp-import-${Date.now()}-${file.fileHash.substring(0, 8)}`,
330	          priority: 0, // Normal priority
331	          attempts: 3,
332	          backoff: {
333	            type: 'exponential',
334	            delay: 2000
335	          },
336	          removeOnComplete: true,
337	          removeOnFail: false
338	        });
339	        
340	        console.log(`[Scan] Queued file: ${file.fileName} (batch: ${batchImportId})`);
341	        await importLogger.log.info(`Queued: ${file.fileName}`);
342	        
343	        results.queued++;
344	        results.files.push({
345	          fileName: file.fileName,
346	          fileSize: file.fileSize,
347	          fileHash: file.fileHash,
348	          importId: batchImportId
349	        });
350	        
351	      } catch (queueError) {
352	        console.error(`[Scan] Error queuing file ${file.fileName}:`, queueError.message);
353	        results.errors.push({
354	          fileName: file.fileName,
355	          error: queueError.message
356	        });
357	      }
358	    }
359	    
360	    // Log completion
361	    await importLogger.log.info(`Scan complete: ${results.scanned} scanned, ${results.queued} queued, ${results.duplicates} duplicates, ${results.errors.length} errors`);
362	    await importLogger.endRun(runContext, results);
363	    
364	    // Update settings with last run info
365	    try {
366	      const settings = await Settings.getSettingsForUpdate();
367	      const currentSettings = settings.importSettings || {};
368	      settings.importSettings = {
369	        ...currentSettings,
370	        lastRun: new Date().toISOString(),
371	        lastRunDuration: Date.now() - runContext.startTime,
372	        lastRunStats: {
373	          scanned: results.scanned,
374	          queued: results.queued,
375	          duplicates: results.duplicates,
376	          skipped: results.skipped,
377	          errors: results.errors.length
378	        }
379	      };
380	      await settings.save();
381	      await Settings.invalidateCache();
382	    } catch (settingsError) {
383	      console.error('Failed to update settings with run info:', settingsError.message);
384	    }
385	    
386	    return results;
387	    
388	  } catch (error) {
389	    await importLogger.log.error(`Folder scan failed: ${error.message}`);
390	    results.errors.push({
391	      fileName: null,
392	      error: error.message
393	    });
394	    
395	    // Still end the run to record the failure
396	    await importLogger.endRun(runContext, results);
397	    throw error;
398	  }
399	}
400	
401	/**
402	 * Process the local folder scan job (called by queue worker)
403	 * @param {Object} job - BullMQ job object
404	 */
405	async function processLocalFolderScan(job) {
406	  console.log(`📋 Processing FTP folder scan job: ${job.id}`);
407	  
408	  const results = await scanLocalFolder();
409	  
410	  // Send summary email if there were any files processed or errors
411	  if (results.queued > 0 || results.errors.length > 0) {
412	    try {
413	      await sendScanSummaryEmail(results);
414	    } catch (emailError) {
415	      console.error('⚠️  Failed to send scan summary email:', emailError.message);
416	    }
417	  }
418	  
419	  return results;
420	}
421	
422	/**
423	 * Send scan summary email to global admins
424	 */
425	async function sendScanSummaryEmail(results) {
426	  const { Settings, User } = require('../models');
427	  const { wrapEmailContent } = require('../utils/emailTheme');
428	  const settings = await Settings.getSettings();
429	  
430	  // Check if email is enabled (Mailtrap = test mode, always enabled)
431	  if (!isEmailEnabled(settings)) {
432	    console.log('[Scan] Email not enabled, skipping scan summary email');
433	    return;
434	  }
435	  
436	  // Get global admin emails
437	  const admins = await User.findAll({
438	    where: { 
439	      role: 'global_admin',
440	      isActive: true
441	    },
442	    attributes: ['email', 'name']
443	  });
444	  
445	  if (admins.length === 0) {
446	    console.log('[Scan] No active global admins to notify');
447	    return;
448	  }
449	  
450	  const portalName = settings.portalName || settings.siteTitle || 'Makita Invoice Portal';
451	  const primaryColor = settings.primaryColor || '#066fd1';
452	  const hasErrors = results.errors.length > 0;
453	  
454	  // Build email content (will be wrapped with branding)
455	  const subject = hasErrors 
456	    ? `FTP Import Scan Complete (${results.errors.length} errors) - ${portalName}`
457	    : `FTP Import Scan Complete - ${portalName}`;
458	  
459	  let emailContent = `
460	    <h2 style="color: ${hasErrors ? '#dc3545' : primaryColor}; margin-bottom: 20px;">FTP Import Scan Results</h2>
461	    <p>The scheduled FTP folder scan has completed.</p>
462	    
463	    <table style="width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse; margin: 20px 0;">
464	      <tr style="background: #f8f9fa;">
465	        <td style="padding: 12px; border: 1px solid #e0e0e0; font-weight: 600;">Files Scanned</td>
466	        <td style="padding: 12px; border: 1px solid #e0e0e0;">${results.scanned}</td>
467	      </tr>
468	      <tr>
469	        <td style="padding: 12px; border: 1px solid #e0e0e0; font-weight: 600;">Queued for Import</td>
470	        <td style="padding: 12px; border: 1px solid #e0e0e0; color: #198754; font-weight: 600;">${results.queued}</td>
471	      </tr>
472	      <tr style="background: #f8f9fa;">
473	        <td style="padding: 12px; border: 1px solid #e0e0e0; font-weight: 600;">Duplicates (Moved)</td>
474	        <td style="padding: 12px; border: 1px solid #e0e0e0;">${results.duplicates}</td>
475	      </tr>
476	      <tr>
477	        <td style="padding: 12px; border: 1px solid #e0e0e0; font-weight: 600;">Skipped</td>
478	        <td style="padding: 12px; border: 1px solid #e0e0e0;">${results.skipped}</td>
479	      </tr>
480	      <tr style="background: #f8f9fa;">
481	        <td style="padding: 12px; border: 1px solid #e0e0e0; font-weight: 600;">Errors</td>
482	        <td style="padding: 12px; border: 1px solid #e0e0e0; color: ${hasErrors ? '#dc3545' : 'inherit'}; font-weight: ${hasErrors ? '600' : 'normal'};">${results.errors.length}</td>
483	      </tr>
484	    </table>
485	  `;
486	  
487	  // Add queued files list (limit to first 20)
488	  if (results.files.length > 0) {
489	    const displayFiles = results.files.slice(0, 20);
490	    emailContent += `
491	      <h3 style="color: ${primaryColor}; margin-top: 24px;">Files Queued for Import:</h3>
492	      <ul style="padding-left: 20px; margin: 12px 0;">
493	        ${displayFiles.map(f => `<li style="margin-bottom: 4px;">${f.fileName} (${(f.fileSize / 1024).toFixed(1)} KB)</li>`).join('')}
494	        ${results.files.length > 20 ? `<li style="font-style: italic; color: #667085;">...and ${results.files.length - 20} more</li>` : ''}
495	      </ul>
496	    `;
497	  }
498	  
499	  // Add errors if any
500	  if (results.errors.length > 0) {
501	    emailContent += `
502	      <div style="background: #f8d7da; border: 1px solid #d63939; border-radius: 4px; padding: 12px; margin-top: 20px;">
503	        <h3 style="color: #721c24; margin: 0 0 12px 0;">Errors:</h3>
504	        <ul style="padding-left: 20px; color: #721c24; margin: 0;">
505	          ${results.errors.map(e => `<li><strong>${e.fileName || 'General'}</strong>: ${e.error}</li>`).join('')}
506	        </ul>
507	      </div>
508	    `;
509	  }
510	  
511	  emailContent += `
512	    <p style="color: #667085; font-size: 12px; margin-top: 24px;">
513	      Scan completed: ${new Date().toLocaleString()}
514	    </p>
515	  `;
516	  
517	  // Wrap with portal branding
518	  const html = wrapEmailContent(emailContent, settings);
519	  
520	  // Queue email for each admin
521	  for (const admin of admins) {
522	    await emailQueue.add('email', {
523	      to: admin.email,
524	      subject,
525	      html,
526	      text: `FTP Import Scan Results\n\nScanned: ${results.scanned}\nQueued: ${results.queued}\nDuplicates: ${results.duplicates}\nSkipped: ${results.skipped}\nErrors: ${results.errors.length}`,
527	      metadata: {
528	        type: 'ftp-scan-summary',
529	        userId: null,
530	        userEmail: 'system'
531	      }
532	    });
533	    
534	    console.log(`[Scan] Queued scan summary email to: ${admin.email}`);
535	  }
536	}
537	
538	// Export functions and paths
539	module.exports = {
540	  scanLocalFolder,
541	  processLocalFolderScan,
542	  moveToDuplicates,
543	  moveToFailed,
544	  calculateFileHash,
545	  isDuplicateHash,
546	  // Re-export paths for convenience
547	  FTP_UPLOAD_PATH,
548	  UNPROCESSED_DUPLICATES,
549	  UNPROCESSED_FAILED
550	};
551

16	/**
17	 * Initialize Google Cloud clients based on settings
18	 */
19	async function initGoogleClients(settings = null) {
20	  // Get settings if not provided
21	  if (!settings) {
22	    const { Settings } = require('../models');
23	    settings = await Settings.getSettings();
24	  }
25	  
26	  const parsingConfig = settings.parsingProvider || {};
27	  
28	  // Initialize Document AI if enabled
29	  if (parsingConfig.documentai?.enabled === true) {
30	    if (!documentAIClient) {
31	      // Priority: 1) JSON from env var, 2) JSON from database, 3) File path from env var, 4) File path from settings
32	      const credentialsJson = process.env.GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS_JSON || 
33	                             parsingConfig.documentai?.credentialsJson;
34	      const credentialsPath = process.env.GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS || 
35	                             parsingConfig.documentai?.credentialsPath;
36	      
37	      try {
38	        const documentai = require('@google-cloud/documentai');
39	        
40	        if (credentialsJson) {
41	          // Use JSON from environment variable or database
42	          const credentials = typeof credentialsJson === 'string' 
43	            ? JSON.parse(credentialsJson) 
44	            : credentialsJson;
45	          documentAIClient = new documentai.DocumentProcessorServiceClient({
46	            credentials: credentials
47	          });
48	          const source = process.env.GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS_JSON 
49	            ? 'environment variable' 
50	            : 'database';
51	          console.log(`✅ Google Document AI initialized (using JSON from ${source})`);
52	        } else if (credentialsPath) {
53	          // Use file path
54	          documentAIClient = new documentai.DocumentProcessorServiceClient({
55	            keyFilename: credentialsPath
56	          });
57	          console.log('✅ Google Document AI initialized (using credentials file)');
58	        } else {
59	          // Try default credentials
60	          documentAIClient = new documentai.DocumentProcessorServiceClient();
61	          console.log('✅ Google Document AI initialized (using default credentials)');
62	        }
63	      } catch (error) {
64	        console.warn('⚠️  Google Document AI not available:', error.message);
65	        if (!credentialsJson && !credentialsPath) {
66	          console.warn('⚠️  Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS_JSON or GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS environment variable.');
67	        }
68	      }
69	    }
70	  }
71	}

73	/**
74	 * Process file import job
75	 */
76	async function processFileImport(job) {
77	  const { fileName, remotePath, ftpConfig } = job.data;
78	  
79	  console.log(`📥 Processing file import: ${fileName}`);
80	  
81	  try {
82	    // Get settings for file storage path
83	    const settings = await Settings.getSettings();
84	    const parsingConfig = settings.parsingProvider || {};
85	    const { ensureStorageDirs, getStorageDir } = require('../config/storage');
86	    
87	    // Ensure storage directories exist
88	    ensureStorageDirs();
89	    const uploadsDir = getStorageDir('invoices');
90	    
91	    // Generate unique local filename
92	    const fileExt = path.extname(fileName);
93	    const uniqueFileName = `${crypto.randomUUID()}${fileExt}`;
94	    const localPath = path.join(uploadsDir, uniqueFileName);
95	    
96	    // Download file from FTP
97	    console.log(`⬇️  Downloading ${fileName} from FTP...`);
98	    await downloadFile(ftpConfig, remotePath || fileName, localPath);
99	    
100	    // Calculate file hash to check for duplicates
101	    console.log(`🔍 Calculating file hash...`);
102	    const fileHash = calculateFileHash(localPath);
103	    const fileStats = fs.statSync(localPath);
104	    
105	    // File type will be detected from PDF content during parsing
106	    // Default to 'unknown' until parsed
107	    let fileType = 'unknown';
108	    const ftpFolder = job.data.ftpFolder || null;
109	    
110	    // Get retention settings for duplicate detection
111	    const retentionDays = settings.fileRetentionDays;
112	    
113	    // Check if file already exists (duplicate)
114	    // Allow re-upload if file was deleted more than retentionDays ago
115	    const { Op } = require('sequelize');
116	    let existingFile = await File.findOne({
117	      where: {
118	        fileHash: fileHash,
119	        deletedAt: null // Only check non-deleted files
120	      }
121	    });
122	    
123	    // If not found, check if there's a deleted file that can be re-uploaded
124	    if (!existingFile && retentionDays !== null) {
125	      const cutoffDate = new Date();
126	      cutoffDate.setDate(cutoffDate.getDate() - retentionDays);
127	      
128	      // Find deleted file that's beyond retention period (can be re-uploaded)
129	      const deletedFile = await File.findOne({
130	        where: {
131	          fileHash: fileHash,
132	          deletedAt: {
133	            [Op.lt]: cutoffDate // Deleted more than retentionDays ago
134	          }
135	        },
136	        order: [['deletedAt', 'DESC']] // Get most recently deleted
137	      });
138	      
139	      if (deletedFile) {
140	        console.log(`ℹ️  File was previously deleted (${deletedFile.id}), allowing re-upload`);
141	        // Allow re-upload - don't treat as duplicate
142	        existingFile = null;
143	      }
144	    }
145	    
146	    if (existingFile) {
147	      console.log(`⚠️  Duplicate file detected: ${fileName} (hash: ${fileHash.substring(0, 8)}...)`);
148	      
149	      // Delete downloaded file (duplicate)
150	      fs.unlinkSync(localPath);
151	      
152	      // Get folder structure for moving duplicate
153	      const folderStructure = settings.ftp?.folderStructure || {
154	        unprocessed: '/Unprocessed',
155	        processed: '/Processed',
156	        failed: '/Failed'
157	      };
158	      
159	      // Move duplicate file to a Duplicates subfolder in Processed
160	      const relativeSourcePath = ftpFolder ? `${ftpFolder}/${fileName}` : fileName;
161	      const dateFolder = new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0];
162	      const duplicatePath = `${folderStructure.processed}/Duplicates/${dateFolder}/${fileName}`;
163	      
164	      try {
165	        await moveFile(ftpConfig, relativeSourcePath, duplicatePath);
166	        console.log(`✅ Moved duplicate file to: ${duplicatePath}`);
167	      } catch (moveError) {
168	        console.error(`⚠️  Failed to move duplicate file: ${moveError.message}`);
169	      }
170	      
171	      // Create file record with duplicate status
172	      const file = await File.create({
173	        fileName,
174	        fileHash,
175	        filePath: localPath, // Will be cleaned up
176	        fileSize: fileStats.size,
177	        status: 'duplicate',
178	        fileType,
179	        ftpFolder,
180	        metadata: {
181	          originalFileId: existingFile.id,
182	          ftpSource: remotePath || fileName,
183	          jobId: job.id,
184	          duplicateOf: existingFile.id,
185	          ftpDuplicatePath: duplicatePath,
186	          movedAt: new Date().toISOString()
187	        }
188	      });
189	      
190	      return {
191	        success: true,
192	        status: 'duplicate',
193	        fileId: file.id,
194	        message: `File ${fileName} is a duplicate of existing file ${existingFile.id}`,
195	        movedTo: duplicatePath
196	      };
197	    }
198	    
199	    // Find system user (or use null for FTP imports)
200	    const systemUser = await User.findOne({ where: { email: 'system@localhost' } }).catch(() => null);
201	    
202	    // Create file record
203	    const file = await File.create({
204	      fileName,
205	      fileHash,
206	      filePath: localPath,
207	      fileSize: fileStats.size,
208	      status: 'processing',
209	      fileType,
210	      ftpFolder,
211	      uploadedById: systemUser?.id || null,
212	      metadata: {
213	        ftpSource: remotePath || fileName,
214	        jobId: job.id,
215	        originalFileName: fileName
216	      }
217	    });
218	    
219	    // Update job progress
220	    await job.updateProgress(25);
221	    
222	    // Check if file is Excel (reuse fileExt from above, just convert to lowercase)
223	    const fileExtLower = fileExt.toLowerCase();
224	    const isExcel = ['.xlsx', '.xls'].includes(fileExtLower);
225	    
226	    // Handle Excel files
227	    if (isExcel) {
228	      console.log(`📊 Processing Excel file: ${fileName}`);
229	      const { extractFieldsFromExcel } = require('../utils/excelParser');
230	      
231	      // ALWAYS use the default template for Excel files
232	      const template = await Template.findDefaultTemplate('excel', null);
233	      
234	      if (!template) {
235	        throw new Error('No default Excel template found. Please create and set a default Excel template first.');
236	      }
237	      
238	      console.log(`   Using DEFAULT Excel template: ${template.name} (ID: ${template.id}, isDefault: ${template.isDefault})`);
239	      
240	      // Extract data using template
241	      const extractedData = await extractFieldsFromExcel(localPath, template);
242	      
243	      // Determine file type from extracted data
244	      fileType = extractedData.documentType || 'invoice';
245	      
246	      // Update file record with extracted data
247	      await file.update({
248	        status: 'processed',
249	        fileType,
250	        invoiceNumber: extractedData.invoiceNumber,
251	        date: extractedData.date,
252	        amount: extractedData.amount,
253	        accountNumber: extractedData.accountNumber,
254	        customerName: extractedData.customerName,
255	        metadata: {
256	          ...file.metadata,
257	          extractedData,
258	          template: template.name,
259	          templateId: template.id,
260	          processingMethod: 'excel_template'
261	        }
262	      });
263	      
264	      console.log(`✅ Excel file processed successfully`);
265	      console.log(`   Invoice Number: ${extractedData.invoiceNumber || 'N/A'}`);
266	      console.log(`   Account Number: ${extractedData.accountNumber || 'N/A'}`);
267	      console.log(`   Amount: ${extractedData.amount || 'N/A'}`);
268	      
269	      return {
270	        success: true,
271	        status: 'processed',
272	        fileId: file.id,
273	        extractedData
274	      };
275	    }
276	    
277	    // Use local PDF extraction with coordinate-based templates
278	    console.log(`🔍 Attempting to parse ${fileName} with local PDF extraction...`);
279	    console.log(`   File path: ${localPath}`);
280	    console.log(`   File size: ${fileStats.size} bytes`);
281	    let parsedData = null;
282	    let processingMethod = 'local_coordinates';
283	    let supplierTemplate = null;
284	    
285	    try {
286	      // Read PDF buffer
287	      const pdfBuffer = fs.readFileSync(localPath);
288	      
289	      // Try to detect document type from content first
290	      const { extractTextFromPDF } = require('../utils/pdfExtractor');
291	      const extractedText = await extractTextFromPDF(localPath);
292	      console.log(`✅ Extracted ${extractedText.text.length} characters of text locally`);
293	      
294	      // Detect document type from content
295	      const detectedType = detectDocumentTypeFromContent(extractedText.text);
296	      console.log(`📄 Detected document type: ${detectedType}`);
297	      
298	      // ALWAYS use the default template for the detected document type
299	      // CRITICAL: Never use a template of a different type - this causes field mismatches!
300	      let template = await Template.findDefaultTemplate('pdf', detectedType);
301	      
302	      if (template) {
303	        // Validate that template type matches detected type
304	        if (template.templateType !== detectedType) {
305	          console.error(`❌ TEMPLATE TYPE MISMATCH! Detected: ${detectedType}, Template: ${template.templateType}`);
306	          console.error(`   This will cause field extraction errors! Rejecting this template.`);
307	          template = null; // Reject mismatched template
308	        } else {
309	          console.log(`✅ Using DEFAULT template: ${template.name} (${template.templateType}, ID: ${template.id}, isDefault: ${template.isDefault})`);
310	        }
311	      }
312	      
313	      // If no matching template found, try to find any template of the correct type (even if not default)
314	      if (!template && detectedType) {
315	        template = await Template.findTemplateByFileType('pdf', detectedType);
316	        if (template) {
317	          console.log(`⚠️  Using non-default template for ${detectedType}: ${template.name} (ID: ${template.id}, templateType: ${template.templateType})`);
318	        }
319	      }
320	      
321	      if (template) {
322	        // Use coordinate-based extraction
323	        parsedData = await Template.extractFieldsFromCoordinates(pdfBuffer, template);
324	        processingMethod = `local_coordinates_${template.code}`;
325	        console.log(`✅ Extracted fields using coordinate template`);
326	        
327	        // Validate template type matches (double-check)
328	        if (template.templateType !== detectedType) {
329	          console.error(`❌ CRITICAL MISMATCH: Detected document type: ${detectedType}, but using template type: ${template.templateType}`);
330	          if (!parsedData.metadata) parsedData.metadata = {};
331	          parsedData.metadata.templateTypeMismatch = {
332	            detected: detectedType,
333	            templateUsed: template.templateType,
334	            templateName: template.name,
335	            warning: `Document detected as ${detectedType} but parsed with ${template.templateType} template`
336	          };
337	        } else {
338	          console.log(`✅ Template type validation passed: Detected ${detectedType}, Template ${template.templateType}`);
339	        }
340	      } else {
341	        console.error(`❌ CRITICAL: No PDF template found for detected type: ${detectedType}`);
342	        console.error(`   This document cannot be processed correctly. Please create a default template for ${detectedType}.`);
343	        // Fallback to basic extraction (no template)
344	        parsedData = await extractInvoiceData(localPath);
345	        processingMethod = 'local_basic';
346	      }
347	      
348	      if (parsedData && parsedData.fullText) {
349	        const textLength = parsedData.fullText.length;
350	        console.log(`✅ Successfully parsed locally`);
351	        console.log(`   Extracted ${textLength} characters of text`);
352	        console.log(`   Invoice #: ${parsedData.invoiceNumber || 'Not found'}`);
353	        console.log(`   Account #: ${parsedData.accountNumber || 'Not found'}`);
354	        console.log(`   Date: ${parsedData.date || 'Not found'}`);
355	        console.log(`   Amount: ${parsedData.amount || 'Not found'}`);
356	        
357	        // Use detected type or parsed document type
358	        if (parsedData.documentType) {
359	          fileType = parsedData.documentType;
360	        } else {
361	          fileType = detectedType;
362	        }
363	        console.log(`📄 Document type: ${fileType}`);
364	      } else {
365	        console.log(`⚠️  Local extraction returned no data`);
366	      }
367	    } catch (localError) {
368	      console.error(`❌ Local PDF extraction failed: ${localError.message}`);
369	      console.error(`   Error stack:`, localError.stack);
370	      // No fallback - local extraction is primary method
371	      parsedData = null;
372	    }
373	    
374	    // Update file with parsing results
375	    await job.updateProgress(75);
376	    
377	    // Get folder structure from settings
378	    const folderStructure = settings.ftp?.folderStructure || {
379	      unprocessed: '/Unprocessed',
380	      processed: '/Processed',
381	      failed: '/Failed'
382	    };
383	    
384	    // Determine destination folder based on status
385	    let destinationPath = null;
386	    
387	    if (parsedData) {
388	      // Match company by account number (referenceNo)
389	      // IMPORTANT: Documents are assigned to companies using account number/company number (referenceNo)
390	      // This is a CORE feature requirement
391	      let matchedCompanyId = null;
392	      let document = null;
393	      
394	      if (parsedData.accountNumber) {
395	        // Try to find company by referenceNo (account number)
396	        const company = await Company.findOne({
397	          where: {
398	            referenceNo: parsedData.accountNumber.toString().trim()
399	          }
400	        });
401	        
402	        if (company) {
403	          matchedCompanyId = company.id;
404	          console.log(`✅ Matched company: ${company.name} (Account: ${company.referenceNo})`);
405	          
406	          // Create invoice/credit note/statement based on document type
407	          // Only create for INVOICE or CREDIT_NOTE document types
408	          if (fileType === 'invoice' || parsedData.documentType?.toLowerCase() === 'invoice') {
409	            try {
410	              const issueDate = parsedData.date ? new Date(parsedData.date) : new Date();
411	              
412	              document = await Invoice.create({
413	                companyId: matchedCompanyId,
414	                invoiceNumber: parsedData.invoiceNumber || `INV-${Date.now()}-${file.id.substring(0, 8)}`,
415	                issueDate: issueDate,
416	                amount: parsedData.amount || 0,
417	                taxAmount: parsedData.vatAmount || 0,
418	                status: 'draft',
419	                fileUrl: file.filePath,
420	                metadata: {
421	                  source: 'ftp_import',
422	                  fileId: file.id,
423	                  fileName: fileName,
424	                  parsedData: parsedData,
425	                  processingMethod: processingMethod
426	                }
427	              });
428	              
429	              console.log(`✅ Created invoice: ${document.invoiceNumber} for company: ${company.name}`);
430	            } catch (invoiceError) {
431	              console.error(`⚠️  Failed to create invoice:`, invoiceError.message);
432	              // Continue processing even if invoice creation fails
433	            }
434	          } else if (fileType === 'credit_note' || parsedData.documentType?.toLowerCase() === 'credit_note') {
435	            try {
436	              const issueDate = parsedData.date ? new Date(parsedData.date) : new Date();
437	              
438	              document = await CreditNote.create({
439	                companyId: matchedCompanyId,
440	                creditNoteNumber: parsedData.invoiceNumber || parsedData.creditNumber || `CN-${Date.now()}-${file.id.substring(0, 8)}`,
441	                issueDate: issueDate,
442	                amount: parsedData.amount || 0,
443	                status: 'draft',
444	                fileUrl: file.filePath, // Will be updated after file is moved
445	                metadata: {
446	                  source: 'ftp_import',
447	                  fileId: file.id,
448	                  fileName: fileName,
449	                  parsedData: parsedData,
450	                  processingMethod: processingMethod
451	                }
452	              });
453	              
454	              console.log(`✅ Created credit note: ${document.creditNoteNumber} for company: ${company.name}`);
455	            } catch (creditNoteError) {
456	              console.error(`⚠️  Failed to create credit note:`, creditNoteError.message);
457	            }
458	          }
459	        } else {
460	          console.log(`⚠️  No company found with account number: ${parsedData.accountNumber}`);
461	          console.log(`   File will be marked as unallocated`);
462	        }
463	      } else {
464	        console.log(`⚠️  No account number found in parsed data`);
465	        console.log(`   File will be marked as unallocated`);
466	      }
467	      
468	      // TEST MODE: If enabled and no company matched, assign to test company
469	      // This ensures ALL documents trigger email notifications during testing
470	      let isTestModeAllocation = false;
471	      const testMode = settings?.emailProvider?.testMode;
472	      const isTestModeEnabled = testMode?.enabled && testMode?.redirectEmail;
473	      const testCompanyId = settings?.testModeDefaultCompanyId;
474	      
475	      if (!matchedCompanyId && isTestModeEnabled && testCompanyId) {
476	        console.log(`🧪 TEST MODE: Assigning unallocated document to test company ${testCompanyId}`);
477	        matchedCompanyId = testCompanyId;
478	        isTestModeAllocation = true;
479	        
480	        // Now create the document record for this test-allocated file
481	        const testCompany = await Company.findByPk(testCompanyId);
482	        if (testCompany) {
483	          console.log(`🧪 TEST MODE: Using test company: ${testCompany.name}`);
484	          
485	          if (fileType === 'invoice' || parsedData.documentType?.toLowerCase() === 'invoice') {
486	            try {
487	              const issueDate = parsedData.date ? new Date(parsedData.date) : new Date();
488	              
489	              document = await Invoice.create({
490	                companyId: matchedCompanyId,
491	                invoiceNumber: parsedData.invoiceNumber || `INV-${Date.now()}-${file.id.substring(0, 8)}`,
492	                issueDate: issueDate,
493	                amount: parsedData.amount || 0,
494	                taxAmount: parsedData.vatAmount || 0,
495	                status: 'draft',
496	                fileUrl: file.filePath,
497	                metadata: {
498	                  source: 'ftp_import',
499	                  fileId: file.id,
500	                  fileName: fileName,
501	                  parsedData: parsedData,
502	                  processingMethod: processingMethod,
503	                  testModeAllocation: true,
504	                  originalAccountNumber: parsedData.accountNumber || null
505	                }
506	              });
507	              
508	              console.log(`🧪 TEST MODE: Created invoice: ${document.invoiceNumber} (test-allocated)`);
509	            } catch (invoiceError) {
510	              console.error(`⚠️  Failed to create test-allocated invoice:`, invoiceError.message);
511	            }
512	          } else if (fileType === 'credit_note' || parsedData.documentType?.toLowerCase() === 'credit_note') {
513	            try {
514	              const issueDate = parsedData.date ? new Date(parsedData.date) : new Date();
515	              
516	              document = await CreditNote.create({
517	                companyId: matchedCompanyId,
518	                creditNoteNumber: parsedData.creditNumber || parsedData.invoiceNumber || `CN-${Date.now()}-${file.id.substring(0, 8)}`,
519	                issueDate: issueDate,
520	                amount: parsedData.amount || 0,
521	                status: 'draft',
522	                fileUrl: file.filePath,
523	                metadata: {
524	                  source: 'ftp_import',
525	                  fileId: file.id,
526	                  fileName: fileName,
527	                  parsedData: parsedData,
528	                  processingMethod: processingMethod,
529	                  testModeAllocation: true,
530	                  originalAccountNumber: parsedData.accountNumber || null
531	                }
532	              });
533	              
534	              console.log(`🧪 TEST MODE: Created credit note: ${document.creditNoteNumber} (test-allocated)`);
535	            } catch (creditNoteError) {
536	              console.error(`⚠️  Failed to create test-allocated credit note:`, creditNoteError.message);
537	            }
538	          } else {
539	            // Default to invoice if type is unclear
540	            try {
541	              const issueDate = parsedData.date ? new Date(parsedData.date) : new Date();
542	              
543	              document = await Invoice.create({
544	                companyId: matchedCompanyId,
545	                invoiceNumber: parsedData.invoiceNumber || `INV-${Date.now()}-${file.id.substring(0, 8)}`,
546	                issueDate: issueDate,
547	                amount: parsedData.amount || 0,
548	                taxAmount: parsedData.vatAmount || 0,
549	                status: 'draft',
550	                fileUrl: file.filePath,
551	                metadata: {
552	                  source: 'ftp_import',
553	                  fileId: file.id,
554	                  fileName: fileName,
555	                  parsedData: parsedData,
556	                  processingMethod: processingMethod,
557	                  testModeAllocation: true,
558	                  originalAccountNumber: parsedData.accountNumber || null
559	                }
560	              });
561	              
562	              console.log(`🧪 TEST MODE: Created invoice (default type): ${document.invoiceNumber} (test-allocated)`);
563	            } catch (invoiceError) {
564	              console.error(`⚠️  Failed to create test-allocated invoice:`, invoiceError.message);
565	            }
566	          }
567	        } else {
568	          console.error(`🧪 TEST MODE ERROR: Test company ${testCompanyId} not found!`);
569	        }
570	      }
571	      
572	      // Determine status based on whether company was matched
573	      let finalStatus = 'parsed';
574	      let failureReason = null;
575	      
576	      if (!matchedCompanyId) {
577	        finalStatus = 'unallocated';
578	        failureReason = 'no_company_match';
579	      } else if (isTestModeAllocation) {
580	        // Mark as parsed but with test allocation flag
581	        finalStatus = 'parsed';
582	      }
583	      
584	      await file.update({
585	        status: finalStatus,
586	        processingMethod,
587	        parsedData,
588	        processedAt: new Date(),
589	        failureReason: failureReason,
590	        customerId: matchedCompanyId // File model uses customerId field (legacy, but works with Company)
591	      });
592	      
593	      // Move file to Processed folder
594	      const relativeSourcePath = ftpFolder ? `${ftpFolder}/${fileName}` : fileName;
595	      
596	      // Create destination path with date folder for organization
597	      const dateFolder = new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0]; // YYYY-MM-DD
598	      destinationPath = `${folderStructure.processed}/${dateFolder}/${fileName}`;
599	      
600	      // Move file on FTP server
601	      try {
602	        await moveFile(ftpConfig, relativeSourcePath, destinationPath);
603	        console.log(`✅ Moved file to Processed: ${destinationPath}`);
604	        
605	        // Update file metadata with new location
606	        await file.update({
607	          metadata: {
608	            ...file.metadata,
609	            ftpProcessedPath: destinationPath,
610	            movedAt: new Date().toISOString()
611	          }
612	        });
613	        
614	        // CRITICAL: Update the document's fileUrl with the new location
615	        // so email attachments can find the file
616	        if (document) {
617	          await document.update({ fileUrl: destinationPath });
618	          console.log(`✅ Updated document fileUrl to: ${destinationPath}`);
619	        }
620	      } catch (moveError) {
621	        console.error(`⚠️  Failed to move file to Processed folder: ${moveError.message}`);
622	        // Don't fail the job if move fails - file is still processed
623	      }
624	      
625	      await job.updateProgress(100);
626	      
627	      return {
628	        success: true,
629	        status: finalStatus,
630	        fileId: file.id,
631	        processingMethod,
632	        message: matchedCompanyId 
633	          ? `File ${fileName} successfully parsed and matched to company` 
634	          : `File ${fileName} successfully parsed but no company match found`,
635	        movedTo: destinationPath,
636	        companyId: matchedCompanyId,
637	        documentId: document?.id,
638	        documentType: document ? (fileType === 'invoice' || parsedData.documentType?.toLowerCase() === 'invoice' ? 'invoice' : 'credit_note') : null
639	      };
640	    } else {
641	      // Parsing failed
642	      await file.update({
643	        status: 'failed',
644	        errorMessage: 'Local PDF extraction failed - no template found or extraction error'
645	      });
646	      
647	      // Move file to Failed folder
648	      const relativeSourcePath = ftpFolder ? `${ftpFolder}/${fileName}` : fileName;
649	      
650	      // Create destination path with date folder
651	      const dateFolder = new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0];
652	      destinationPath = `${folderStructure.failed}/${dateFolder}/${fileName}`;
653	      
654	      // Move file on FTP server
655	      try {
656	        await moveFile(ftpConfig, relativeSourcePath, destinationPath);
657	        console.log(`⚠️  Moved failed file to Failed folder: ${destinationPath}`);
658	        
659	        // Update file metadata
660	        await file.update({
661	          metadata: {
662	            ...file.metadata,
663	            ftpFailedPath: destinationPath,
664	            movedAt: new Date().toISOString()
665	          }
666	        });
667	      } catch (moveError) {
668	        console.error(`⚠️  Failed to move file to Failed folder: ${moveError.message}`);
669	      }
670	      
671	      return {
672	        success: false,
673	        status: 'failed',
674	        fileId: file.id,
675	        message: `File ${fileName} could not be parsed automatically`,
676	        movedTo: destinationPath
677	      };
678	    }
679	    
680	  } catch (error) {
681	    console.error(`❌ Error processing file ${fileName}:`, error.message);
682	    
683	    // Try to create file record with error status
684	    try {
685	      const file = await File.create({
686	        fileName,
687	        fileHash: 'error', // Placeholder
688	        filePath: '',
689	        fileSize: 0,
690	        status: 'failed',
691	        errorMessage: error.message,
692	        metadata: {
693	          ftpSource: remotePath || fileName,
694	          jobId: job.id
695	        }
696	      });
697	      
698	      return {
699	        success: false,
700	        status: 'failed',
701	        fileId: file.id,
702	        error: error.message
703	      };
704	    } catch (createError) {
705	      return {
706	        success: false,
707	        status: 'error',
708	        error: error.message
709	      };
710	    }
711	  }
712	}
713	
714	/**
715	 * Parse PDF with Google Document AI
716	 */
717	async function parseWithDocumentAI(filePath) {
718	  if (!documentAIClient) {
719	    const { Settings } = require('../models');
720	    const settings = await Settings.getSettings();
721	    await initGoogleClients(settings);
722	    if (!documentAIClient) {
723	      throw new Error('Google Document AI not configured. Please enable it in Parsing Provider settings.');
724	    }
725	  }
726	  
727	  // Get settings to retrieve Document AI configuration
728	  const { Settings } = require('../models');
729	  const settings = await Settings.getSettings();
730	  const parsingConfig = settings.parsingProvider || {};
731	  
732	  const projectId = parsingConfig.documentai?.projectId || process.env.GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT_ID;
733	  const location = parsingConfig.documentai?.location || process.env.GOOGLE_CLOUD_LOCATION || 'us';
734	  const processorId = parsingConfig.documentai?.processorId || process.env.GOOGLE_DOCUMENT_AI_PROCESSOR_ID;
735	  
736	  if (!projectId || !processorId) {
737	    throw new Error('Google Document AI not fully configured. Please set projectId and processorId in Parsing Provider settings.');
738	  }
739	  
740	  const name = `projects/${projectId}/locations/${location}/processors/${processorId}`;
741	  
742	  // Read file
743	  const fileContent = fs.readFileSync(filePath);
744	  
745	  const request = {
746	    name,
747	    rawDocument: {
748	      content: fileContent.toString('base64'),
749	      mimeType: 'application/pdf'
750	    }
751	  };
752	  
753	  const [result] = await documentAIClient.processDocument(request);
754	  const document = result.document;
755	  
756	  // Extract structured data from Document AI
757	  // Extract structured data from Document AI
758	  // Document AI provides entities which are more reliable than regex
759	  const parsedData = {
760	    fullText: document.text,
761	    invoiceNumber: extractInvoiceNumber(document.text),
762	    date: extractDate(document.text),
763	    amount: extractAmount(document.text),
764	    customerName: extractCustomerName(document.text),
765	    accountNumber: extractAccountNumber(document.text),
766	    // Additional fields
767	    customerPO: extractCustomerPO(document.text),
768	    vatAmount: extractVATAmount(document.text),
769	    goodsAmount: extractGoodsAmount(document.text),
770	    entities: document.entities || [],
771	    rawText: document.text
772	  };
773	  
774	  // Try to extract from Document AI entities if available (more reliable)
775	  if (document.entities && Array.isArray(document.entities)) {
776	    for (const entity of document.entities) {
777	      const type = entity.type?.toLowerCase() || '';
778	      const value = entity.mentionText || entity.normalizedValue?.textValue || '';
779	      
780	      if (value) {
781	        // Map Document AI entity types to our fields
782	        if (type.includes('invoice_number') || type.includes('invoice_id')) {
783	          parsedData.invoiceNumber = value;
784	        } else if (type.includes('date') || type.includes('invoice_date')) {
785	          parsedData.date = value;
786	        } else if (type.includes('total') || type.includes('amount')) {
787	          const amount = parseFloat(value.replace(/[^0-9.]/g, ''));
788	          if (!isNaN(amount)) {
789	            parsedData.amount = amount;
790	          }
791	        } else if (type.includes('account') || type.includes('account_number')) {
792	          parsedData.accountNumber = value;
793	        } else if (type.includes('customer') || type.includes('customer_name')) {
794	          parsedData.customerName = value;
795	        }
796	      }
797	    }
798	  }
799	  
800	  return parsedData;
801	}
802	
803	/**
804	 * Extract invoice number from text
805	 * Improved patterns to handle various formats including "Invoice No.5942501"
806	 */
807	function extractInvoiceNumber(text) {
808	  const patterns = [
809	    // "Invoice No. 5942480" or "Invoice No.5942501" or "Invoice No: 5942501" or "Invoice No 5942501"
810	    // This pattern specifically handles the space after "No." and captures the number
811	    /invoice\s+no\.?\s+:?\s*(\d{4,}[A-Z0-9\-_]*)/i,
812	    // "Invoice #5942501" or "Invoice #: 5942501"
813	    /invoice\s*#\s*:?\s*([A-Z0-9\-_]+)/i,
814	    // "Invoice Number: 5942501" or "Invoice Number 5942501"
815	    /invoice\s+number\s*:?\s*([A-Z0-9\-_]+)/i,
816	    // "INV-5942501" or "INV 5942501"
817	    /(?:^|\s)(INV[-\s]?[A-Z0-9\-_]+)/i,
818	    // "OC1_INV_05942483" format
819	    /(?:^|\s)([A-Z]{2,}[-\s_]?INV[-\s_]?\d+[A-Z0-9\-_]*)/i,
820	    // Generic: "Invoice 5942501"
821	    /invoice\s+([A-Z0-9\-_]+)/i,
822	    // Fallback: long numeric sequences that might be invoice numbers (7+ digits)
823	    /(?:^|\s)(\d{7,}[A-Z0-9\-_]*)/i
824	  ];
825	  
826	  for (const pattern of patterns) {
827	    const match = text.match(pattern);
828	    if (match && match[1]) {
829	      const invoiceNum = match[1].trim();
830	      // Validate it looks like an invoice number
831	      // Must be at least 4 characters (to avoid matching "No" or short numbers)
832	      if (invoiceNum.length >= 4 && invoiceNum.length <= 50) {
833	        // Don't match if it's just "No" or common words
834	        if (!/^(no|yes|na|n\/a)$/i.test(invoiceNum)) {
835	          return invoiceNum;
836	        }
837	      }
838	    }
839	  }
840	  
841	  return null;
842	}
843	
844	/**
845	 * Extract date from text
846	 * Handles various date formats including UK format (DD/MM/YYYY)
847	 */
848	function extractDate(text) {
849	  const patterns = [
850	    // UK format: DD/MM/YYYY or DD-MM-YYYY (most common for invoices)
851	    /(?:date|invoice\s+date|issue\s+date|dated?)\s*:?\s*(\d{1,2}[\/\-]\d{1,2}[\/\-]\d{2,4})/i,
852	    // US format: MM/DD/YYYY or YYYY-MM-DD
853	    /(\d{4}[\/\-]\d{1,2}[\/\-]\d{1,2})/,
854	    // UK format without label
855	    /(\d{1,2}[\/\-]\d{1,2}[\/\-]\d{2,4})/,
856	    // Written format
857	    /(january|february|march|april|may|june|july|august|september|october|november|december)\s+\d{1,2},?\s+\d{4}/i,
858	    // Short month format
859	    /(\d{1,2}\s+(?:jan|feb|mar|apr|may|jun|jul|aug|sep|oct|nov|dec)[a-z]*\s+\d{2,4})/i
860	  ];
861	  
862	  for (const pattern of patterns) {
863	    const match = text.match(pattern);
864	    if (match && match[1]) {
865	      const dateStr = match[1].trim();
866	      // Validate it looks like a date
867	      if (dateStr.length >= 6 && dateStr.length <= 20) {
868	        return dateStr;
869	      }
870	    }
871	  }
872	  
873	  return null;
874	}
875	
876	/**
877	 * Extract amount/total from text
878	 * Handles UK currency (£) and various formats
879	 */
880	function extractAmount(text) {
881	  const patterns = [
882	    // "Invoice Total£0.00" or "Invoice Total: £0.00" - most specific
883	    /invoice\s+total\s*:?\s*[£$€]?\s*([\d,]+\.?\d{2})/i,
884	    // "Total: £0.00" or "Total £0.00"
885	    /(?:^|\s)total\s*:?\s*[£$€]?\s*([\d,]+\.?\d{2})/i,
886	    // "Amount Due: £0.00"
887	    /(?:amount\s+due|balance\s+due|grand\s+total)\s*:?\s*[£$€]?\s*([\d,]+\.?\d{2})/i,
888	    // Currency symbol followed by amount (get the last one, usually the total)
889	    /[£$€]\s*([\d,]+\.?\d{2})/g
890	  ];
891	  
892	  // Try specific patterns first
893	  for (let i = 0; i < patterns.length - 1; i++) {
894	    const pattern = patterns[i];
895	    const match = text.match(pattern);
896	    if (match && match[1]) {
897	      const amountStr = match[1].trim().replace(/,/g, '');
898	      const amount = parseFloat(amountStr);
899	      if (!isNaN(amount)) {
900	        return amount;
901	      }
902	    }
903	  }
904	  
905	  // For currency pattern, get all matches and return the last one (usually the total)
906	  const currencyPattern = patterns[patterns.length - 1];
907	  const matches = [...text.matchAll(currencyPattern)];
908	  if (matches && matches.length > 0) {
909	    // Return the last match (usually the total/invoice total)
910	    const lastMatch = matches[matches.length - 1];
911	    if (lastMatch && lastMatch[1]) {
912	      const amountStr = lastMatch[1].trim().replace(/,/g, '');
913	      const amount = parseFloat(amountStr);
914	      if (!isNaN(amount)) {
915	        return amount;
916	      }
917	    }
918	  }
919	  
920	  return null;
921	}
922	
923	/**
924	 * Extract customer name from text
925	 * Looks for "Bill To", "Customer", "Sold To" labels
926	 */
927	function extractCustomerName(text) {
928	  const patterns = [
929	    /(?:bill\s+to|customer|client|sold\s+to)[\s:]*\n?\s*([A-Z][A-Za-z\s&,\.\-']{2,50})/i,
930	    /(?:bill\s+to|customer|client)[\s:]*\n?\s*([A-Z][A-Za-z\s&,\.\-']{2,50})/i
931	  ];
932	  
933	  for (const pattern of patterns) {
934	    const match = text.match(pattern);
935	    if (match && match[1]) {
936	      const name = match[1].trim();
937	      // Take first line only (usually the company name)
938	      return name.split('\n')[0].trim();
939	    }
940	  }
941	  
942	  // Fallback: look for company-like names in first 10 lines
943	  const lines = text.split('\n').slice(0, 10);
944	  for (const line of lines) {
945	    const trimmed = line.trim();
946	    // Look for lines that look like company names (not dates, numbers, or too short)
947	    if (trimmed.length > 3 && trimmed.length < 50 && 
948	        !trimmed.match(/^\d/) && 
949	        !trimmed.match(/^\d{1,2}[\/\-]\d{1,2}/) && // Not a date
950	        !trimmed.match(/^[£$€]/)) { // Not a currency amount
951	      return trimmed;
952	    }
953	  }
954	  
955	  return null;
956	}
957	
958	/**
959	 * Extract Customer PO (Purchase Order) from text
960	 * Handles both numeric POs and text-based POs like "DROP OFF"
961	 * Also handles cases where PO value is on a different line
962	 */
963	function extractCustomerPO(text) {
964	  // First, try patterns that match on the same line
965	  const sameLinePatterns = [
966	    // "Customer PO: DROP OFF" or "Customer PO: ABC123" - handles multi-word POs
967	    /customer\s+po\s*:?\s*([A-Z0-9\s\-_]{2,50})/i,
968	    // "PO Number: ABC123" or "PO: DROP OFF"
969	    /po\s+(?:number\s*)?:?\s*([A-Z0-9\s\-_]{2,50})/i,
970	    // "Purchase Order: ABC123"
971	    /purchase\s+order\s*:?\s*([A-Z0-9\s\-_]{2,50})/i,
972	    // "PO #: ABC123"
973	    /po\s*#?\s*:?\s*([A-Z0-9\s\-_]{2,50})/i
974	  ];
975	  
976	  for (const pattern of sameLinePatterns) {
977	    const match = text.match(pattern);
978	    if (match && match[1]) {
979	      const po = match[1].trim();
980	      // Validate it looks like a PO (at least 2 chars, max 50)
981	      // Can contain spaces for multi-word POs like "DROP OFF"
982	      if (po.length >= 2 && po.length <= 50 && !/^(no|yes|na|n\/a)$/i.test(po)) {
983	        return po;
984	      }
985	    }
986	  }
987	  
988	  // If no match on same line, try to find "Customer PO:" and then look for value on next line(s)
989	  const poLabelMatch = text.match(/customer\s+po\s*:?\s*/i);
990	  if (poLabelMatch) {
991	    const afterLabel = text.substring(poLabelMatch.index + poLabelMatch[0].length);
992	    // Look for PO value in the next 200 characters (could be on next line)
993	    const nextSection = afterLabel.substring(0, 200);
994	    // Try to find a valid PO value (alphanumeric, possibly with spaces/hyphens)
995	    const poValueMatch = nextSection.match(/\b([A-Z0-9\s\-_]{2,50})\b/);
996	    if (poValueMatch && poValueMatch[1]) {
997	      const po = poValueMatch[1].trim();
998	      // Validate it's not just whitespace or common words
999	      if (po.length >= 2 && po.length <= 50 && 
1000	          !/^(no|yes|na|n\/a|order|date|packing|list|number)$/i.test(po) &&
1001	          !po.match(/^\d{1,2}[\/\-]\d{1,2}/)) { // Not a date
1002	        return po;
1003	      }
1004	    }
1005	  }
1006	  
1007	  // Standalone "PO ABC123" or "PO-ABC123" or "DROP OFF" near "Customer PO"
1008	  const standaloneMatch = text.match(/(?:^|\s)(?:PO[-\s]+|)([A-Z][A-Z0-9\s\-_]{1,49})/);
1009	  if (standaloneMatch && standaloneMatch[1]) {
1010	    const po = standaloneMatch[1].trim();
1011	    // Check if it's near "Customer PO" in the text
1012	    const poIndex = text.toLowerCase().indexOf('customer po');
1013	    const matchIndex = standaloneMatch.index;
1014	    if (poIndex >= 0 && Math.abs(matchIndex - poIndex) < 500) {
1015	      if (po.length >= 2 && po.length <= 50 && 
1016	          !/^(no|yes|na|n\/a|order|date|packing|list|number|invoice|account)$/i.test(po)) {
1017	        return po;
1018	      }
1019	    }
1020	  }
1021	  
1022	  return null;
1023	}
1024	
1025	/**
1026	 * Extract VAT Amount from text
1027	 * Handles formats like "VAT£0.00" or "VAT: £0.00" or "VAT £0.00"
1028	 */
1029	function extractVATAmount(text) {
1030	  const patterns = [
1031	    // "VAT£0.00" or "VAT: £0.00" or "VAT £0.00"
1032	    /vat\s*:?\s*[£$€]?\s*([\d,]+\.?\d{2})/i,
1033	    // "VAT Amount: £0.00"
1034	    /vat\s+amount\s*:?\s*[£$€]?\s*([\d,]+\.?\d{2})/i,
1035	    // "Tax Amount: £0.00"
1036	    /tax\s+amount\s*:?\s*[£$€]?\s*([\d,]+\.?\d{2})/i,
1037	    // "Tax: £0.00"
1038	    /tax\s*:?\s*[£$€]?\s*([\d,]+\.?\d{2})/i
1039	  ];
1040	  
1041	  for (const pattern of patterns) {
1042	    const match = text.match(pattern);
1043	    if (match && match[1]) {
1044	      const amountStr = match[1].replace(/,/g, '');
1045	      const amount = parseFloat(amountStr);
1046	      if (!isNaN(amount)) {
1047	        return amount;
1048	      }
1049	    }
1050	  }
1051	  
1052	  return null;
1053	}

1062	    /goods\s*:?\s*[£$€]?\s*([\d,]+\.?\d{2})/i,

1070	  
1071	  for (const pattern of patterns) {

1080	  }
1081

1084	
1085	/**
1086	 * Detect supplier from filename
1087	 */
1088	function detectSupplierFromFilename(fileName) {
1089	  const upperFileName = fileName.toUpperCase();
1090	  
1091	  // Common supplier patterns in filenames
1092	  const suppliers = ['MAKITA', 'BOSCH', 'DEWALT', 'HILTI', 'STANLEY'];
1093	  
1094	  for (const supplier of suppliers) {
1095	    if (upperFileName.includes(supplier)) {
1096	      return supplier;
1097	    }
1098	  }
1099	  
1100	  return null;
1101	}
1102	
1103	/**
1104	 * Detect supplier from text content
1105	 */
1106	function detectSupplierFromText(text) {
1107	  const upperText = text.toUpperCase();
1108	  
1109	  // Look for supplier names in text
1110	  const suppliers = ['MAKITA', 'BOSCH', 'DEWALT', 'HILTI', 'STANLEY'];
1111	  
1112	  for (const supplier of suppliers) {
1113	    if (upperText.includes(supplier)) {
1114	      return supplier;
1115	    }
1116	  }
1117	  
1118	  return null;
1119	}
1120	
1121	/**
1122	 * Detect document type using supplier template patterns
1123	 */
1124	function detectDocumentTypeFromTemplate(text, template) {
1125	  if (!template.documentTypePatterns) {
1126	    return detectDocumentTypeFromContent(text);
1127	  }
1128	  
1129	  const upperText = text.toUpperCase();
1130	  
1131	  // Check each document type pattern
1132	  for (const [type, patterns] of Object.entries(template.documentTypePatterns)) {
1133	    if (Array.isArray(patterns)) {
1134	      for (const pattern of patterns) {
1135	        if (upperText.includes(pattern.toUpperCase())) {
1136	          return type; // 'invoice', 'credit_note', 'statement'
1137	        }
1138	      }
1139	    }
1140	  }
1141	  
1142	  // Fallback to content-based detection
1143	  return detectDocumentTypeFromContent(text);
1144	}
1145	
1146	/**
1147	 * Detect document type from PDF content
1148	 * Looks for keywords in the document text to determine if it's an invoice, credit note, or statement
1149	 */
1150	function detectDocumentTypeFromContent(text) {
1151	  const upperText = text.toUpperCase();
1152	  
1153	  // Check for credit note indicators (most specific first)
1154	  if (upperText.includes('CREDIT NOTE') || 
1155	      upperText.includes('CREDITNOTE') ||
1156	      upperText.includes('CREDIT NOTE') ||
1157	      (upperText.includes('CREDIT') && upperText.includes('NOTE'))) {
1158	    return 'credit_note';
1159	  }
1160	  
1161	  // Check for statement indicators
1162	  if (upperText.includes('STATEMENT') || 
1163	      upperText.includes('ACCOUNT STATEMENT') ||
1164	      upperText.includes('STATEMENT OF ACCOUNT')) {
1165	    return 'statement';
1166	  }
1167	  
1168	  // Check for invoice indicators
1169	  if (upperText.includes('INVOICE') || 
1170	      upperText.includes('TAX INVOICE') ||
1171	      upperText.includes('INVOICE NUMBER')) {
1172	    return 'invoice';
1173	  }
1174	  
1175	  // Default to invoice if no clear indicator (most common document type)
1176	  return 'invoice';
1177	}
1178	
1179	/**
1180	 * Extract account number from text
1181	 * Looks for common patterns like "Account Number:", "Account:", "Acc No:", etc.
1182	 * IMPORTANT: Excludes bank account numbers (usually longer, near "Bank Details")
1183	 */
1184	function extractAccountNumber(text) {
1185	  // First, try to find customer account number (usually shorter, near top)
1186	  // Exclude bank account numbers which are usually longer and near "Bank Details"
1187	  const bankAccountSection = text.toLowerCase().indexOf('bank details');
1188	  const textBeforeBank = bankAccountSection > 0 ? text.substring(0, bankAccountSection) : text;
1189	  
1190	  const patterns = [
1191	    // "Account No.: 999999" or "Account No.999401" or "Account No: 999401" or "Account No 999401"
1192	    // This pattern handles the colon after "No." and captures the number
1193	    /account\s+no\.?\s*:?\s*(\d{4,}[A-Z0-9\-]*)/i,
1194	    // "Account #999401" or "Account #: 999401"
1195	    /account\s*#\s*:?\s*([A-Z0-9\-]+)/i,

1199	    /acc\s+no\.?\s*:?\s*(\d+[A-Z0-9\-]*)/i,
1200	    // "Account Code: 999401"
1201	    /account\s+code\s*:?\s*([A-Z0-9\-]+)/i,
1202	    // "Customer Account: 999401"
1203	    /customer\s+account\s*:?\s*([A-Z0-9\-]+)/i,
1204	    // "Account ID: 999401"
1205	    /account\s+id\s*:?\s*([A-Z0-9\-]+)/i
1206	  ];
1207	  
1208	  // Search in text before bank details section first (customer account is usually at top)
1209	  for (const pattern of patterns) {
1210	    const match = textBeforeBank.match(pattern);
1211	    if (match && match[1]) {
1212	      const accountNumber = match[1].trim();
1213	      // Validate it looks like an account number
1214	      // Must be at least 4 characters (to avoid matching "No")

1216	      if (accountNumber.length >= 4 && accountNumber.length <= 20) {

1219	          return accountNumber;

1222	    }

1224

1237	        if (potentialAccount.length >= 4 && potentialAccount.length <= 20) {
1238	          return potentialAccount;
1239	        }
1240	      }
1241	    }
1242	  }
1243
