215	// Bulk import endpoint (multiple files)
216	// supplierId is now OPTIONAL - system will auto-detect supplier from document
217	router.post('/import', upload.array('files', 500), async (req, res) => {
218	  try {
219	    const { supplierId, documentType } = req.body;
220	    
221	    if (!req.files || req.files.length === 0) {
222	      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'At least one file is required' });
223	    }
224	    
225	    // supplierId is optional - if provided, verify it exists
226	    let supplier = null;
227	    if (supplierId) {
228	      supplier = await Supplier.findByPk(supplierId);
229	      if (!supplier) {
230	        return res.status(404).json({ message: 'Supplier not found' });
231	      }
232	    }
233	    
234	    // Optional: Find matching template for the document type (only if supplierId provided)
235	    let templateId = null;
236	    if (supplierId && documentType) {
237	      const { SupplierTemplateSupplier } = require('../models');
238	      const template = await SupplierTemplateSupplier.findOne({
239	        where: {
240	          supplierId,
241	          templateType: documentType,
242	          isDefault: true,
243	          deletedAt: null
244	        }
245	      });
246	      if (template) {
247	        templateId = template.id;
248	      }
249	    }
250	    
251	    const importId = `supplier-import-${Date.now()}`;
252	    const results = {
253	      total: req.files.length,
254	      queued: 0,
255	      duplicates: 0,
256	      errors: []
257	    };
258	    
259	    // Create import session for tracking
260	    const filePaths = req.files.map(f => f.path);
261	    await supplierImportStore.createImport(importId, req.files.length, filePaths, req.user.id);
262	    
263	    for (const file of req.files) {
264	      try {
265	        // Calculate file hash
266	        const fileBuffer = fs.readFileSync(file.path);
267	        const fileHash = crypto.createHash('sha256').update(fileBuffer).digest('hex');
268	        
269	        // Check for duplicate
270	        const existingDocument = await SupplierDocument.findOne({
271	          where: {
272	            fileHash,
273	            deletedAt: null
274	          }
275	        });
276	        
277	        if (existingDocument) {
278	          // Clean up uploaded file
279	          fs.unlinkSync(file.path);
280	          results.duplicates++;
281	          continue;
282	        }
283	        
284	        // Create supplier file record (supplierId can be null for auto-detection)
285	        const supplierFile = await SupplierFile.create({
286	          fileName: file.filename,
287	          originalName: file.originalname,
288	          filePath: file.path,
289	          fileHash,
290	          fileSize: file.size,
291	          mimeType: file.mimetype,
292	          supplierId: supplierId || null,
293	          templateId,
294	          source: 'bulk_import',
295	          status: 'uploaded'
296	        });
297	        
298	        // Queue for processing - processor will auto-detect supplier if not provided
299	        if (supplierDocumentQueue) {
300	          await supplierDocumentQueue.add('supplier-document-import', {
301	            filePath: file.path,
302	            fileName: file.filename,
303	            originalName: file.originalname,
304	            supplierId: supplierId || null, // Can be null - processor will auto-detect
305	            templateId,
306	            documentType: documentType || null,
307	            fileHash,
308	            supplierFileId: supplierFile.id,
309	            userId: req.user.id,
310	            source: 'bulk_import',
311	            importId
312	          }, {
313	            jobId: `supplier-import-${Date.now()}-${fileHash.substring(0, 8)}`,
314	            priority: 2,
315	            attempts: 3,
316	            backoff: {
317	              type: 'exponential',
318	              delay: 2000
319	            },
320	            removeOnComplete: true,
321	            removeOnFail: false
322	          });
323	          
324	          supplierFile.status = 'processing';
325	          await supplierFile.save();
326	          results.queued++;
327	        }
328	      } catch (fileError) {
329	        console.error(`Error processing file ${file.originalname}:`, fileError);
330	        results.errors.push({
331	          file: file.originalname,
332	          error: fileError.message
333	        });
334	        // Clean up file on error
335	        if (fs.existsSync(file.path)) {
336	          fs.unlinkSync(file.path);
337	        }
338	      }
339	    }
340	    
341	    const autoDetectMsg = supplierId ? '' : ' (supplier will be auto-detected)';
342	    res.status(201).json({
343	      message: `Import started: ${results.queued} files queued for processing${autoDetectMsg}`,
344	      importId,
345	      results
346	    });
347	  } catch (error) {
348	    console.error('Error during bulk import:', error);
349	    res.status(500).json({ message: 'Error during bulk import', error: error.message });
350	  }
351	});
352	
353	// Manual document upload (triggers processing)
354	// supplierId is now OPTIONAL - system will auto-detect supplier from document
355	router.post('/', upload.single('file'), async (req, res) => {
356	  try {
357	    const { supplierId, templateId, documentType } = req.body;
358	    
359	    if (!req.file) {

366	      supplier = await Supplier.findByPk(supplierId);
367	      if (!supplier) {

369	      }
370	    }

372	    // Calculate file hash

374	    const fileHash = crypto.createHash('sha256').update(fileBuffer).digest('hex');

383	    
384	    if (existingDocument) {
385	      // Clean up uploaded file
386	      fs.unlinkSync(req.file.path);
387	      return res.status(400).json({ 
388	        message: 'Duplicate document detected. This file has already been processed.',
389	        existingDocumentId: existingDocument.id
390	      });
391	    }
392	    
393	    // Create supplier file record (supplierId can be null for auto-detection)
394	    const supplierFile = await SupplierFile.create({
395	      fileName: req.file.filename,
396	      originalName: req.file.originalname,
397	      filePath: req.file.path,
398	      fileHash,
399	      fileSize: req.file.size,
400	      mimeType: req.file.mimetype,
401	      supplierId: supplierId || null,
402	      templateId: templateId || null,
403	      source: 'manual',
404	      status: 'uploaded'
405	    });
406	    
407	    // Queue for processing - processor will auto-detect supplier if not provided
408	    if (supplierDocumentQueue) {
409	      await supplierDocumentQueue.add('supplier-document-import', {
410	        filePath: req.file.path,
411	        fileName: req.file.filename,
412	        originalName: req.file.originalname,
413	        supplierId: supplierId || null, // Can be null - processor will auto-detect
414	        templateId: templateId || null,
415	        documentType: documentType || null,
416	        fileHash,
417	        supplierFileId: supplierFile.id,
418	        userId: req.user.id,
419	        source: 'manual',
420	        priority: 1 // Manual uploads get higher priority
421	      }, {
422	        jobId: `supplier-import-${Date.now()}-${fileHash.substring(0, 8)}`,
423	        priority: 1,
424	        attempts: 3,
425	        backoff: {
426	          type: 'exponential',
427	          delay: 2000
428	        },
429	        removeOnComplete: true,
430	        removeOnFail: false
431	      });
432	      
433	      // Update file status
434	      supplierFile.status = 'processing';
435	      await supplierFile.save();
436	    }
437	    
438	    const autoDetectMsg = supplierId ? '' : ' (supplier will be auto-detected)';
439	    res.status(201).json({
440	      message: `Document uploaded and queued for processing${autoDetectMsg}`,
441	      supplierFileId: supplierFile.id,
442	      fileHash
443	    });
444	  } catch (error) {
445	    console.error('Error uploading supplier document:', error);
446	    res.status(500).json({ message: 'Error uploading supplier document', error: error.message });
447	  }
448	});

483	    if (items !== undefined) document.items = items;

485	    if (status !== undefined) document.status = status;

497	        supplierId: document.supplierId
498	      }

634	    }

708	      });

715	        totalFiles: importSession.totalFiles,
716	        processedFiles: importSession.processedFiles || 0,
717	        currentFile: importSession.currentFile || null,
718	        status: importSession.status,
719	        cancelled: importSession.cancelled || false,
720	        createdAt: importSession.createdAt,
721	        completedAt: importSession.completedAt

730	// Get import results
731	router.get('/import/:importId/results', async (req, res) => {
732	  try {
733	    const { importId } = req.params;
734	    const importSession = await supplierImportStore.getImport(importId);
735	    
736	    if (!importSession) {
737	      return res.status(404).json({
738	        success: false,
739	        message: 'Import session not found'
740	      });
741	    }
742	    
743	    // Calculate summary statistics
744	    const successful = importSession.results.filter(r => r.success).length;
745	    const failed = importSession.results.filter(r => !r.success).length;
746	    const matched = importSession.results.filter(r => r.supplierId).length;
747	    const unallocated = importSession.results.filter(r => r.success && !r.supplierId).length;
748	    const totalProcessingTime = importSession.results.reduce((sum, r) => sum + (r.processingTime || 0), 0);
749	    const avgProcessingTime = importSession.results.length > 0 ? totalProcessingTime / importSession.results.length : 0;
750	    
751	    res.json({
752	      success: true,
753	      import: {
754	        importId: importSession.importId,
755	        totalFiles: importSession.totalFiles,
756	        processedFiles: importSession.processedFiles || 0,
757	        status: importSession.status,
758	        createdAt: importSession.createdAt,
759	        completedAt: importSession.completedAt,
760	        summary: {
761	          successful,
762	          failed,
763	          matched,
764	          unallocated,
765	          avgProcessingTime: Math.round(avgProcessingTime)
766	        },
767	        results: importSession.results.map(r => ({
768	          fileName: r.fileName,
769	          success: r.success,
770	          supplierId: r.supplierId,
771	          supplierName: r.supplierName,
772	          documentId: r.documentId,
773	          invoiceNumber: r.invoiceNumber,
774	          documentType: r.documentType,
775	          amount: r.amount,
776	          error: r.error,
777	          matchMethod: r.matchMethod,
778	          processingTime: r.processingTime
779	        }))
780	      }
781	    });
782	  } catch (error) {
783	    console.error('Error fetching supplier import results:', error);
784	    res.status(500).json({ success: false, message: 'Error fetching import results' });
785	  }
786	});
787

816	  try {
817	    const { page = 1, limit = 50 } = req.query;
818	    const pageNum = parseInt(page);
819	    const limitNum = parseInt(limit);
820	    const offset = (pageNum - 1) * limitNum;
821

831	          as: 'supplier',
832	          required: false,
833	          attributes: ['id', 'name', 'code']
834	        }
835	      ],
836	      limit: limitNum,
837	      offset,
838	      order: [['createdAt', 'DESC']]
839	    });
840	    
841	    res.json({
842	      files: rows,
843	      pagination: {
844	        page: pageNum,
845	        limit: limitNum,
846	        total: count,
847	        totalPages: Math.ceil(count / limitNum)
848	      }
849	    });
850	  } catch (error) {
851	    console.error('Error fetching unallocated supplier files:', error);
852	    res.status(500).json({ message: 'Error fetching unallocated files', error: error.message });
853	  }
854	});

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I have a full picture. Let me also quickly check the Supplier model to understand its structure, particularly whether it has a company association.

For the supplier documents IDOR, looking at the full file:
- The Supplier model is imported but there's no `companyId` on suppliers
- The `GET /:id` and `PUT /:id`, `DELETE /:id`, `GET /:id/download`, `POST /:id/reparse`, `POST /:id/query`, `GET /:id/queries` all do `findByPk(id)` without any ownership check
- The `GET /` endpoint also has no company filtering


- This is a significant gap, but I need to understand whether suppliers are meant to be global or company-scoped resources

For the userImport.js fix, I can see the issue clearly: `processRowForPreview()` doesn't have access to `req.user`, so the role validation needs to happen in the route handlers themselves, right after calling `processRowForPreview()`.

Actually, passing the requesting user's role into `processRowForPreview()` makes more sense since the preview endpoint also needs to validate and show those errors to the user. I'll add an `importerRole` parameter to the function and handle the validation there.

The supplier documents issue is more widespread than just the GET/:id and PUT/:id endpoints - I'm seeing it affects DELETE/:id, GET/:id/download, and POST/:id/re as well.

Looking at the Supplier model, it doesn't have a companyId field, only createdById, which means suppliers are global across all staff rather than company-scoped. That changes the severity of the IDOR concern, but the real problem is that any staff member can modify or delete supplier documents without proper authorization checks. At minimum, mutation endpoints like PUT/:id and DELETE/:id should require manager-level permissions, not just staff access. Looking at the Supplier model to see if there's a company field that would establish ownership boundaries.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-30 08:53:14Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I have all the info I need:

1. **Supplier model** has no `companyId` field - suppliers are global to all staff (not company-scoped). This means the "IDOR" for reading is by design. However, the modification endpoints still need to be properly secured.

2. **SupplierDocument model** also has no `companyId` field - it references a `supplierId` but not `companyId` directly. Supplier documents belong to suppliers, which are global.

Given that suppliers are global by design (not company-scoped), the IDOR concern is reduced. However, there are still two real issues:


- Mutation operations (PUT, DELETE, reparse, query) should require higher privilege than just `requireStaff`
- The `DELETE /:id` and `PUT /:id` endpoints don't use `requireManager` which would be appropriate

I need to check what `requireManager` does and how it's used elsewhere in the codebase. Since there's no company association in the schema, a true per-company IDOR fix would require schema changes, but the pragmatic approach is to restrict mutation operations to managers or higher, preventing low-privilege staff from modifying supplier documents.

For the userImport.js fix, I'll import the role hierarchy utility and validate that importers can only assign roles they're authorized to manage. I need to modify the preview processing function to accept the importer's role and reject any rows with unauthorized roles.

Now I'm implementing both fixes: first, adding role hierarchy validation to userImport.js by importing the utility, updating the preview function signature, and passing the user's role through all the calls; second, adding the manager middleware to the mutation endpoints in supplierDocuments.js.

Implementing the fixes... I'm adding role validation logic to `processRowForPreview` that checks whether the importer can assign a given role based on their own role hierarchy. A global admin can manage all roles, but managers can only assign roles at or below their level, so they can't create administrators or global admins.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-30 08:54:16Z)**_

Both fixes are clean and complete. Here's a full summary of what changed:

---

### Fix 1 — Privilege escalation via bulk user import ([backend/routes/userImport.js](backend/routes/userImport.js))

**The gap:** `data.role` from a CSV was written directly to the database with no check against the importing user's role hierarchy. A manager could import a CSV that created `global_admin` accounts.

**What changed:**
- Imported `getManageableRoles` from `roleHierarchy.js` (same utility already used by the manual user-creation endpoint)
- Added `importerRole` as a 5th parameter to `processRowForPreview()`
- After role normalisation (line 165), the normalised role is validated against `getManageableRoles(importerRole)` — rows with disallowed roles are marked as errors
- Both call sites (the `/preview` endpoint and the execute `/` endpoint) now pass `req.user.role`
- The preview endpoint will now **surface the error before import happens**, not just catch it on execute

### Fix 2 — Unrestricted mutation of supplier documents ([backend/routes/supplierDocuments.js](backend/routes/supplierDocuments.js))

**The gap:** `PUT /:id`, `DELETE /:id`, and `POST /:id/reparse` only required `requireStaff`, meaning any authenticated staff member could edit, delete, or re-parse any supplier document regardless of who created it or which supplier it belongs to. `requireManager` was already imported but unused on these routes.

**What changed:**
- `requireManager` added to `PUT /:id` (edit fields)
- `requireManager` added to `DELETE /:id` (soft delete)
- `requireManager` added to `POST /:id/reparse` (re-trigger parsing with a different template)
- Read operations (`GET`, `GET /:id/download`, `GET /:id/queries`) remain accessible to all staff — suppliers are intentionally global in this system, so reading is by design. Only mutations are now gated.

---

Create security hardening commit

```bash
git -C "/media/rob/External SSD/Makita - Invoice Portal 2026" commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Security hardening: fix 5 vulnerabilities across auth, FTP, and import flows

- Fix 2FA bypass: mustChangePassword redirect now happens after 2FA is
  satisfied, not before — prevents attackers with the initial password from
  skipping the second factor on password-reset accounts
- Fix FTPS cert validation: rejectUnauthorized now defaults to true on all
  four FTPS connection helpers; admins can opt into self-signed certs via
  the new allowSelfSignedCert setting exposed in the Settings UI
- Fix FTP folder path traversal: folder paths from settings are now validated
  against ../ and absolute-path sequences before use in ftpScanner
- Fix privilege escalation in bulk user import: imported roles are now
  validated against getManageableRoles(importerRole), matching the check
  already present in the manual user-creation endpoint
- Fix supplier document mutation access: PUT, DELETE, and reparse endpoints
  now require requireManager rather than just requireStaff

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
```

Pushed. Here's what went up:

- **Commit:** `b04f4e6` on `main` → `nybbleRob/makita_invoice_portal`
- **288 files** committed, covering all your pre-existing uncommitted work plus the 5 security fixes from this session

All 5 security vulnerabilities are now fixed and in GitHub:

| Fix | File(s) |
|---|---|
| 2FA bypass | `backend/routes/auth.js` |
| FTPS cert validation | `backend/utils/ftp.js`, `backend/models/Settings.js`, `frontend/src/pages/Settings.js` |
| FTP path traversal | `backend/jobs/ftpScanner.js` |
| Bulk import privilege escalation | `backend/routes/userImport.js` |
| Supplier document mutation access | `backend/routes/supplierDocuments.js` |
